# Thread: Question about Playa del Carmen
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-playa-del-carmen.27992/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Evee — 2023-07-21T18:25:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-playa-del-carmen.27992/post-324081>

Is the population density of Playa del Carmen too high to make it a good choice to stay in Quintana Roo or do the natural magnetic fields and light still make it a decent choice?

I’m planning to head to Mexico for fall but I need to work remotely and I’ll be traveling alone so PDC seems like an appealing choice for those reasons, but just wondering if it’s a poor choice for pop density?

### OptimalSkr — 2023-07-22T16:47:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-playa-del-carmen.27992/post-324123>

Great question! Maybe [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) can comment

Do you need to be at the beach daily to get the full effects of PDC's (light and magnetism) or can you get these just from living in the city
and going outside in a park or yard?

### Anne V — 2023-07-22T17:03:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-playa-del-carmen.27992/post-324125>

> [OptimalSkr said:](/goto/post?id=324123)
>
> Great question! Maybe [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) can comment
>
> Do you need to be at the beach daily to get the full effects of PDC's (light and magnetism) or can you get these just from living in the city
> and going outside in a park or yard?

You just go on the beach in PDC

### Anne V — 2023-07-22T17:06:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-playa-del-carmen.27992/post-324126>

> [Evee said:](/goto/post?id=324081)
>
> Is the population density of Playa del Carmen too high to make it a good choice to stay in Quintana Roo or do the natural magnetic fields and light still make it a decent choice?
>
> I’m planning to head to Mexico for fall but I need to work remotely and I’ll be traveling alone so PDC seems like an appealing choice for those reasons, but just wondering if it’s a poor choice for pop density?

I will go to PDC for a while, just to be able to choose where I want to rent .

### OptimalSkr — 2023-07-22T19:39:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-playa-del-carmen.27992/post-324130>

> [Anne V said:](/goto/post?id=324125)
>
> You just go on the beach in PDC

Yes - But if you don't live close to the beach and cant always go is PDC still worth it? - can you still get the benefits?

Also even if you live close the seaweed (sargasso) is becoming a problem - people say that a lot of times it is so disgusting there and not a good experience.

### Anne V — 2023-07-22T20:36:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-playa-del-carmen.27992/post-324133>

> [OptimalSkr said:](/goto/post?id=324130)
>
> Yes - But if you don't live close to the beach and cant always go is PDC still worth it? - can you still get the benefits?
>
> Also even if you live close the seaweed (sargasso) is becoming a problem - people say that a lot of times it is so disgusting there and not a good experience.

I do not know. I live in the UK, I have been to puerto Aventuras for a month 1n March2019.

I went back in March 2020 after the open heart op. there was no seaweed then.I think it gets clean early morning

at least in places.

### Ronald Waters — 2023-07-23T00:18:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-playa-del-carmen.27992/post-324136>

I think the seeweed problem is somewhat seasonal but yes in many places they are out cleaning it up and hauling it out. I've Heard it's a problem coming up from Brazil so the weather could shift next year and the problem disappear. It's anybody's guess.
In my opinion, take along some meters and test a place before you make a long-term decision. nnEMF can vary from house to house..

This looks like a heat lamp which is Far-InfraRed 2000nm - 3000nm +/- (Obvious some red and NIR)
Near-InfraRed is about 850nm +/-
Red light (visible) is 660nm +/-
What are you trying to achiev?

### ateale — 2023-07-22T15:53:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-or-ir-light-lamp-or-panel-for-home-use.27994/post-324119>

[@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/) thanks for replying to my post, I'd appreciate any comments that you might have.
To be honest I'm not completely sure about what i am trying to achieve apart from getting as healthy as I can be and hopefully set a good example for my kids. I spend most of the day inside working at a desk+computer so I am trying to compensate for that I guess.

I understand that exposure to red light in the early hours of the day preconditions our skin for UV - obviously the idea is to get out to see the sunrise and get grounded but some days of the week I'm unable to do that so I figured it wouldn't hurt to have something that I could setup in the bathroom or bedroom to get some exposure.
I'm considering to also replace the white lightbulbs i have in my bathroom with red ones or similar to reduce that exposure to the blue spectrum in the morning

# Thread: Grounding shoes/NYC
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-shoes-nyc.27996/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TaylorF — 2023-07-22T16:55:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-shoes-nyc.27996/post-324124>

Is it harmful to wear grounding shoes in a city like NYC due to the high nnEMF levels?

OR is it always helpful to wear grounding shoes on something like concrete no matter where you are?

### TaylorF — 2023-07-23T11:47:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-shoes-nyc.27996/post-324168>

I go one day every few months to check in on the school where I teach online. I don’t understand why you’re saying still? Do you know anything about me?

### TaylorF — 2023-07-23T11:51:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-shoes-nyc.27996/post-324169>

I’ve been in this forum for 4 years. Looks like you’ve been for 4 months. Here for support, and getting an answers to my questions. Not judgement.

### Sue-UK — 2023-07-23T12:58:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-shoes-nyc.27996/post-324172>

> [Taylor Freeman said:](/goto/post?id=324124)
>
> Is it harmful to wear grounding shoes in a city like NYC due to the high nnEMF levels?
>
> OR is it always helpful to wear grounding shoes on something like concrete no matter where you are?

Jack has posted a video about dirty electricity such as

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-time-line-of-tech.23126/#post-323817](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-time-line-of-tech.23126/#post-323817)

### ShaneR — 2023-07-24T04:29:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-shoes-nyc.27996/post-324224>

grounding to the soil in a population dense area is not recommended. Somewhere around 60% of the return current in North America's electrical grid goes through the soil. It is like a huge circuit. When you ground in any big city, even in a city park or green space, you have significant potential to become part of this circuit. That's no bueno.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-07-24T09:56:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-shoes-nyc.27996/post-324234>

> [Taylor Freeman said:](/goto/post?id=324124)
>
> Is it harmful to wear grounding shoes in a city like NYC due to the high nnEMF levels?
>
> OR is it always helpful to wear grounding shoes on something like concrete no matter where you are?

Our bodies run on DC voltage as does nature. Grounding makes you part of the circuit and in nature this is good. In any man made nnEMF AC field the AC field is induced thru your body. If you are in any city close to buildings and power lines grounding is probably not healthy. Get out in nature and ground.

### TaylorF — 2023-08-09T03:55:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-shoes-nyc.27996/post-324872>

I wonder if this is safer in small towns where electric power lines are above ground vs under. I would think it is safer for the lines to be above ground.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-09T22:41:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-shoes-nyc.27996/post-324892>

> [TaylorF said:](/goto/post?id=324872)
>
> I wonder if this is safer in small towns where electric power lines are above ground vs under. I would think it is safer for the lines to be above ground.

When power lines are above the ground it is easier to avoid them and find a “clean” place to ground/Earth

# Thread: NIKKI's journal 2023 onward
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nikkis-journal-2023-onward.27997/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 84
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### NikkiYork — 2023-07-22T20:13:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nikkis-journal-2023-onward.27997/post-324132>

I honestly don't know where to start. when it comes to disease, I am hesitant to write things down, because i believe that the written word is stronger than the spoken word. I don't want to give a disease more of a reality than it deserves.
One of my former mentors, farmer Jack, who taught me about soil making, got so upset with me, because I didn't journal properly, he stopped talking to me for a while and then nothing was the same. Alas, I've always journaled and I started with a Jack journal independently from this forum. It has the form of "Dear Dr. Jack", so I'll start with that and add.

Dear Dr. Jack.
Today is May 21st, 2023. My husband David recommended listening to your Huberman podcast last week and ever since I have been going down the rabbit hole as you would probably phrase it.

Thanks for that! Not thanks for being really annoying to listen to , gladly I already got somewhat used to it, so it’s getting easier. English is my second language, so that doesn’t really help. However, if you are correct, Dr. Jack, and so far I see your points, it is immensely stupid of all humans to not listen to you and immediately stop what we are doing.

I am putting you to the test. I will try out all you are saying, and if it means we need to move, I sure as hell will move the family. But this is only day… maybe 10, so we will see about that.

As of now I bought an RF, electric, magnetic meter device, what a scary machine. I will now measure everything.

I started sleeping with a shirt over my head for darkness and its good. I also bought orange uvex glasses for the whole family. I am contemplating taking the house off of the grid over night.

I am turning 51 in 5 days. And all my life I have been trying to figure things out. So here we go. Maybe this is it. I very much like principles, because if we don’t go to the principles and deduct from there, all is wrong.

June 12th
I am still with you and listening to your info. I got your book. I am making sure that we are outside as much as possible, especially in the mornings to build up melanin and melatonin.
It’s been going well.

We call it solar panelling and sit in the sun as naked as our deck allows. For 30 minutes or an hour, its been so cold, it’s gnarly.

June 27th

the sun has been out recently. I got a tan by now. I have a bit of sun allergy, or whatever the itchy stuff is called, but, and this is amazing: the sensation on the back of my left hand is starting to come back. I have had none in it and doctors said there is nothing to do about… other than an epidural. The cortisol pack I was prescribed had not worked, and supposedly it’s from stenosis.

My midlefinger is completely back, all else is way less numb, than it used to be. If this comes from the sun alone, I am so impressed.

I am also feeling a lot better. I cut down on screen time a lot. Been getting up early. Eating high protein in the mornings, my diet could be better.

Yesterday and today you actually answered my questions on twitter, which is amazing. Yay!!!I am still figuring out where to move, since you said LA is bad.

Well. We are probably staying till James finishes high school, which would be 8 years. Unless it gets so very bad of course. I have not yet figured out why it is so bad here, other than the film industry and 5g… which might just be exactly it… however, where is it better?

I checked a light pollution map. I checked a 5g map. I checked a starlink map. I’m on it. Florida, Texas and Hawaii might be good, but Florida is bright white on the light pollution map. Hawaii is a place to get stuck at. We used to have a condo there. They didn’t let you fly without the vaccine. So that’s bad.

July 11th

Hi Jack, we finished our road trip. I can feel how the light is not there in higher latitudes. We went up as far as Vancouver. Also, the cities stink like piss and I saw at least 20 people shooting up heroine in the middle of the streets, who wants to live there? We are in our little bubble here, next to all the rich people.

It is starting to hit me, I’m starting to understand your science part. A little, hahahaha.
I know that you can put the truth in front of everybody and they will not see it. It’s always been like that, because the truth has been around for thousands of years. Some can see it. Some can feel it, some can grasp more of it.

From your getting emotional parts in your podcasts, it seems to me that you have been spiritually hit by being the knowledge. I don’t know how to better put it. And interesstinly enough your state doesn’t seem to vanish, which is great. Leeds me to a question: How can I help?

July 12th

So far the effects of morning sun therapy led to: smaller eyes. It does not look attractive, but I find my eyes got a lot smaller.
Why are big eyes attractive? I guess because it’s childlike and means great future breeding.

I gained weight. Like a lot. Why? Maybe because I also started eating fat. My issue was, that I was down to 800 calories a day to simply sustain my weight, and every calorie more, made me go fat. Maybe, because I eat too much blue light. For what it’s worth, it is food. Just not a great one I guess.

I am starting to understand things. Where do we go from here?
I did fall apart over the last years and it has been getting better. I need to understand all the testing and labs. I need to get the starting point. I have overcome severe ecxema from childhood on. I’ve had asthma in my 20s and the doctors said, I would never be able to heal it and they were wrong. It took me 7 years, and I don’t know how I did it. So it would be great to know. Now i just have crazy things, that make me look crazy.

I need to figure out my haplotype. I will.

17th of July

You really are a great doctor from what I can tell. I would love to get on your patient rooster. I have so much to say, but why all the noise?
I’ve been trying to get into your forum, but it hasn’t technically worked yet. It’s probably a better place than twitter.

22nd of July.
Haha,I made it into the forum

### NikkiYork — 2023-07-23T21:30:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nikkis-journal-2023-onward.27997/post-324197>

O.K. so what is my problem? My problem is that I am falling apart and the doctors are saying that I have nothing - which is great. But I am still falling apart in a way I don't like.

It started on two separate occasions, well three, if you count giving birth, because pregnancy took a literal life out of me, but lets stay current. My son turns 10.

1\. In June 2020 an unfortunate event occurred, it hit me like a lightening bolt that went from my head to my toes. I got serious tooth aches, went to the dentists a month later, actually they sent me around to 3 doctors as it seemed my teeth were actually ok. A root specialist with heavy visual machinery and a periodontist. I was in serious pain and my right cheek was swollen.

They ended up pulling two teeth, and over the course of the year did 6 oral surgeries on me for gum disease (its my second round of that, had it done 17 years ago already) and two bone grafts - one in the nose for the fake tooth. Yay, my teeth still hurt (much, much less though), but supposedly it can't be true, and all they could do according to the surgeon is pull all my nerves. What a great answer. I am no longer going there and I obviously didn't take that option seriously. I got 6 or 7 rounds of antibiotics, and couldn't exist unless I took ibuprofen and a half a vicodin at night.

I don't take meds, unless I really have to, but this was different. Anyways, no whining. To the day nobody told me what was wrong with me, because the teeth they pulled were actually not broken, one had a gold filling though. if that matters. When the swelling wouldn't come down, the doctor said I must have bit my cheek. Well, want to see a photo? I didn't bite my cheek, I swear.

Anyways, I am going into Covid with a semi-weakened system. I'm 5'7" and weighed 140lbs. I had a hair salon, and the politicians really destroyed the hair industry and all salon owners took massive hits, I ended up closing mine. F that, let's do something different.

I admit, I started drinking a beer every evening that I wasn't allowed to work - no bueno - and I ate all the ice cream for fun, like, hey, it's so much fun, if the state tells you that you can't go to your workplace, but you need to pay rent for it. Yeah, I'm angry about that, but it also had a lot of good in other respects. I made it to weighing 170lbs before I pulled the break.

2\. Anyhow. I used to go to the sauna twice a week, sauna closed down for Covid. I used to brisk power walk 3 miles with my friend once a week. And then it happened after one of those walks: I wasn't able to do exercise of any kind any longer, or else my body would get so sore, that I could not walk. It happened from one day to another. I'm not lying. Like I ran a marathon.

I would want to do stretching instead, and I immediately got a stiff neck. My hands and feet started tingling and going numb in and out. It would take me an hour sometimes, just to get out of bed, and then it was usually getting better quickly. But in the mornings I couldn't hold a cup, I fell 3 times in the street, because my feet wobbled away.

I really freaked out when I had no shins anymore, like they weren't there. My lower legs didn't go numb, it felt like I didn't have them. I would hit them, but they weren't there. It took 6 weeks to get an appointment with a neurologist. I look good for 50, so at some point he said "why are you even here? because you get sore from exercise?" He was just laughing at me.

I asked him, if the numbness could come from the vaccine. I got the J&J single shot, knowingly that it could be bad (I had reasons I can explain, but not here). Doctor said "nope, can't be the vaccine.". I guess I didn't ask him the right questions or didn't talk to him the right way. My general practitioner said it was fibromyalgia and menopause without examining me.

So I sat at home crying, and finally my husband found another doctor in that practice. I told the new doctor, that everybody says it's in my head, and I'm ok with that, if that is so, but he please would need to help me. He prescribed gabba(pentin?), I took it for 4 days, it did nothing, I said no thanks. He ordered a brain MRI to see, if i had an onset of MS. I do not. He checked my heart, all good, he did the blood work, well high cholesterol, so he wants to put me on statins, but I said no.

I kinda get, why a doctor doesn't like me so much, if i don't want it, but I don't. I got a lower back MRI and a cervical one. I have moderate stenosis, and two 10mm nodules on my thyroid that are benevolent. That doctor also concluded that i was healthy, and he was nice about it.

I spent a year with daily pain between 4 and 7, my feet started getting better, but my hands did not. I had signed up with a nutritionists, but they didn't help me, and because none of their stuff really worked they started projecting it on me. I logged all my food daily for 400 plus days, I tried all they said, I walked as much as my pain could tolerate, which was 6000 steps, but since I made no real progress, they started saying I wasn't 100percent in compliance. Not true, i did everything to the t, and they actually know it. So I waited till i could get out of that contract and went to osteostrong instead.

I was looking into frequency medicine at the time - because I had the feeling my issues are electrical - and I signed up at osteostrong, because they have a biocharger, red light wall, PEMF mat and I thought, sure, I take the osteogenic loading also. I did a dexa scan and turns out I am osteopenic, and I really didn't think I was. Man, the surprises. So I did that for 6 months religiously 3 times a week. I didn't notice any bettering in my hands. i stopped it after 6 month.

I talked to the people from bio charger by the way, and they don't tell you anything scientifically valuable about their device. I talked to a third party scientist, their melange of tech must cancel each other out, just fyi. Still a very interesting field.

So the pinky, ring, and middle finger were bad every morning, edema-ish type swollen, nothing visible in my blood. When I tried playing piano, my thumb started swelling up badly, so I had a photo to show the doctor (because, I'm crazy, I have nothing), so he ordered a x-ray. x-ray came back with that my ulnar is too long, which justified sending me to an orthopedic surgeon. The orthopedic surgeon said my finger issues came from sleeping with bent arms. I liked the doctor, but that is not the case.

I have a friend who is a computer guy, he fed all my symptoms into some AI and came up with the idea that I have TOS (thoracic outlet syndrome), I looked it up on youtube and there was a guy who said to massage three spots, one on the neck, one under the clavicle and one under the arm pits. I did that twice for a 15 minutes. My fingers started tingling big time and then it was gone. I kid you not. I mean, i believe THAT is crazy. I have had these hand issues for 1+years, and they are gone simply by self-massaging twice? What kind of sorcery is that? This was last April 2023. It didn't come back. Explanations welcome.

The back of my left hand lost sensation, but that is different, not painful, just annoying. That sensation is almost back, thanks to sitting in the sun I believe, thanks to Dr. Jack.

I fear that my redox potential sucks, or I somehow else got myself into a bad spot and messed up, and I am feeling pretty ok now. i still can't really do the gardening i want without excessive pain, but it's much, much better. I want to know and I want to fix it and I want to change for the better, because i don't want this to happen to me again or worse.

I have another one freak thing to report - to complete the picture, that gladly is also gone hopefully and that I don't wish for my worst enemy. For about 2 years in that time I woke up almost every night around 4, because it was like a fridge turned on in my chest. Or somebody put a vibrating phone into my chest. I am not exaggerating, I however understand, that it must sound totally stupid. The sound of large speakers that don't have sound on them is also describing it well.

It came with a sound type situation and the vibration was way stronger than the alarm on a wrist watch. It woke me up. Way, way stronger and really kind of scary, because it was inside of my chest. It eventually moved locations from my upper chest to more of my upper belly. The neurologist said that it would come from meditating and listening to your own body too much. I'm sorry, that is such a bullshit answer, yes, i do meditate often, but that's only been a good thing.

Anyways, one night I had the vibrating and was hoping for it to stop when out of the blue it stopped and went back on and stopped. That was so odd. And it never came back. That was April 2022 and I had it for more than a year, maybe 2 years.

I don't have depression or anxiety, it's hard to know me via internet and I am hoping that this post doesn't sound too crazy. I'm an optimistic, high energy person.

### NikkiYork — 2023-08-05T20:04:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nikkis-journal-2023-onward.27997/post-324736>

I’m doing the sun paneling every day, I’m studying as much as I have time to do it.

noteworthy for health: we went to six flags for our son’s bday. Like every penny was wasted on me. But anyways, at some point I was going to be a good sport and got onto a roller coaster that we thought was a bit less horrible.

Right when I decided that loops are fun, that thing sped up and I started seeing stars, oddly they were green, and then I was just occupied trying to keep conscious because my sight wasn’t there anymore. I probably passed out for a half a second.

I know, Darwin, who in a sane mind goes on these things, but I really didn’t think they would be able to permit something like that. Anyways. Great for an experiment.

So the Mach pushed the blood out of my circulation I suppose. It felt like my blood pressure went all the way down. My blood pressure does that by itself and since I was born, so I know the feeling. This was pretty special though.

Anyways, why was the light green? Blood is red. So was that all that my nerves could capture, because the red was absorbed? If the red was absorbed, by what was it absorbed? And what caught the green?

Next deal, I got out of that thing walking on eggs and later in the day my lower legs and shins had one cramp after the next, like I had the hardest time walking…. Soooo, that could tell me something about my issues with circulation in my extremities and muscle issues. I will contemplate this.

### NikkiYork — 2023-08-18T04:03:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nikkis-journal-2023-onward.27997/post-325262>

I’m almost done packing for Burning Man, and then I will be off grid for 2 weeks. I’m going via Vegas to a party at the punkrockmuseum, which is going to be some great pampering for my ego, so I’m excited.

David and James are flying a week later. I am hauling all the stuff.

I also figured out a way of how to sleep in my car with all the stuff in it, that saves at least 200 bucks in hotel costs and hey, I thrive on little things like that.

I had my first colonoscopy done and all was fine, two polyps removed. TMI hahaha.

I had gone back to my farm in Malibu (I help out working there) three times, after a year of pause, and I felt like crap after. Couldn’t get out of bed again.
It barely makes sense to me, but possibly that farm had a bad effect on me. I will test it out in a month. I don’t think it’s from the farm work, because that’s light work.

I’m doing great now, except for a really stiff back, but that comes and goes, by stiff I mean that I can’t sit up with my legs flat on the ground. Pain is really not as bad.

My feet are at a 2 to 3 these days which is great.

I’m plotting out where to get a house or apartment or land. It could be ES. I need to take the family there to look.

My son goes to the best school in the area and they keep implementing odd stuff like car pool nazis. It seems that rules are becoming more and more attractive to people. Not me, but I also don’t feel safe with a rule. Opposite.

I’m lying in the sun as we speak. I have a great life.
I had some Japanese eel today, trying out all kinds of new foods, so much fun.

Stressful is seeing the Maui fires - and hearing that the people there had no water coming out of their hoses, that they saw the white vans, and that they closed up the zone around Lahaina.

Well, the 2019 Malibu fires changed my world. Because I was right there. And there was no water coming out of the hoses, I personally saw the white vans, making 180 degree turns in the middle of the high way at the same time. And they blocked off the area for weeks. There was more, but being right there when something happens is not a conspiracy theory. It then is about remembering things how they were instead of forgetting or letting other say how it was.

It’s been an ongoing thing, the: “but I was there, I saw it, with my own eyes.” And then the narrative says something else. It is highly concerning. And for that reason I am on my tippie toes.

I am seeing friends at Burning Man whose two adult children burned to death in the wild fires up north when they all tried fleeing. The parents made it. Yes, very horrible story.

We will also have a ceremony for the husband of our former head of camp, who died the first night of their honey moon in Costa Rica. 42, blood clots to the heart. Had just started a new job at a bank and needed the shot for it. Not that it could be related. I went straight to Costa Rica and helped last year.

Whoever reads this, it just got morbid, but things are only real when people really die, and they do. Health is wealth and decentralization is in deed the way, at least it’s a desirable one and a practically doable one.

That’s why I’m here. And I also want to meet people who get it.

### Branden G — 2023-09-04T05:13:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nikkis-journal-2023-onward.27997/post-325907>

Please provide an update from Burning Man at your earliest convince. I hope all is well Nikki.

### NikkiYork — 2023-09-05T23:45:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nikkis-journal-2023-onward.27997/post-325997>

> [Branden Gatewood said:](/goto/post?id=325907)
>
> Please provide an update from Burning Man at your earliest convince. I hope all is well Nikki.

All is well  thanks for caring!!

It’s only the press that makes it a big deal, there was no emergency at any point.

This was certainly the most rain there ever was - it rained in 2014 as well, but was only muddy for a day. And it does suck that you can’t drive, not even ride a bike, so basically you can only walk and it is really hard with stacks of clay under your feet.

It was all in all great though. Not the expected event, but neat. There were way too many spectators there this year, so they got a taste of reality you know, way less artists came, way less good art, and many people who you would also see on a tour bus to Zion or Bryce wanted to bucket list the event. Burning man decays for a while now to bars and food, and more and more rules, because the makers had to incorporate (to deal with the state of Nevada and washoe county. Not a good development).

There was no danger to anybody’s life or even well being.

The dead guy I believe tried using a generator while standing in a puddle, that’s what we were told, now they say in the media that it’s investigated. I don’t know, but that would be on him. If one person dies within 1 week in a population of 75.000, that’s pretty normal.

My husband and son were separated from me when it started pouring. They had gone on a ride about 1.5 miles on the other side of the city, so they stayed there over night. I was a tad worried, because there is no communication, no phones or internet, but I was also pretty sure that they were well. They got a nice bed there and played dungeons and dragons - with the smartest dungeon master ever, according to my son. They got Chinese food even, while I slept in a yurt that was under water  but my air bed was not.

I was briefly thinking that some idiot could set electricity to the puddle I was sleeping in, but resorted to the air mat being rubber so I should be fine.

The toilets were gross not being emptied, but that’s only inconvenience. Anybody who ever camped off grid knows how to manage a toiletless situation.

Long story short, it’s only the media, none of it is true. People who needed to work walked off the playa and hitch hiked to town. Inconvenient, but doable. Costs money to take a flight you didn’t plan on taking, but really that’s just a lesson to not be attached to money - or figure out a better work situation.

There was never a shortage of water or food. Burner style is to share everything anyways, so whoever was not prepared or had shelter malfunctions was taken care of.

It was also inconvenient to get out of the desert once you could drive, but that was basically a matter of being quick. We broke down our Hexayurt and everything in under an hour to beat the lines and made it “to asphalt” in 3.5 hours. Drove till last night at 12 and the kid is in school today.

Whoever sits in those lines for 9 hours is just stupid respectively mismanages their situation.

The event is about radical self reliance and many people got a check up on their status in those regards.

I have to say that the dust storms, white outs, and some heat we had in other years was a lot worse.

P.s.: my highlight was the Brodega. Some kids put together a funky version of a New York bodega and the two guys behind the counter were just such great actors.

They had a back door through which you were led to see cock fights. And they just did such an amazing job. They build a dingy boxing ring there with all sorts of pimp characters. People were picked to play silly games, like dildo throwing. It was all enacted in some sort of immersive theatre and it was just so well done. Then the place was raided by police that was paid off with drugs and everybody was put back on the street through the back door. Really, really great, hilarious comedy and whoever knows New York probably had even more fun in the details.

### NikkiYork — 2024-01-31T19:09:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nikkis-journal-2023-onward.27997/post-330789>

2 days ago I did 3-5 sit ups. Yesterday I had sore belly muscles, today it feels like I have a knife stuck in my belly, which makes walking a chore.

2 month ago I tried working back at the farm, I didn’t think I was working very hard, but felt so crap after, that I’m no longer going.

2 weeks ago I lifted one flower pot together with David, and it made my hands sore for… more than a week. 1 pot.

My body is in my opinion overreacting to any kind of movement that is just a tad more than what I need to live.

For the 2 or 3 years that’s been going on I walk as much as I can to get in better shape, but that is not happening.

I’ve been wondering, if there is a chemtrail correlation. Because it’s been catastrophic for the last days. A correlation with sleeping with an open window? The onset was 30 days after the vaccine to my knowledge, so maybe it’s that.

I am doing a lot better than 2 years ago, so that’s a plus.

Two general doctors gynecologist and neurologist say it’s in my head, it’s age, it’s menopause, it’s fibromyalgia.
I agree that the diagnosis “complains about sore muscles after working out” sounds stupid. It’s really more than that and I’m not so sure that they are right about that I need to get used to that.

Since I here and there develop swellings, so I can document it’s not made up, they have ordered various x-rays or mris and tests, but I have nothing, so that s “great”.

Sun helps. At least so I think.
I’m trying to book my next health trip down south to El Salvador and figure out real estate. I can’t wait for winter to be over.

Down below is the sun right now. The other side of the sky is bright blue. Whatever is in the air is sun reactive and it’s reacting to a frequency that in my opinion is not just wind. Tomorrow atmospheric rivers.

### NikkiYork — 2024-02-01T00:06:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nikkis-journal-2023-onward.27997/post-330803>

Ok, great. I found a good helpful article via quantum entanglement with Jack.

He writes about chronic fatigue syndrome - and I believe I could have a lighter form of post exertional malaise:
„It is about the loss of electrons, loss of proton recycling, in the mitochondria of muscles and collagen that causes a loss of organizational structure and function in those cells. That is why muscles and fascia don't work properly in these patients to distribute energy throughout the body through the cytoarchitecture. You must renovated melanin, via alpha MSH, in your interior and exterior before you employ Cold therapy or grounding or you can "short circuit" your recovery.“

I have been grounding and ct-ing a lot recently, because I thought winter makes my life worse. David and I spend the mornings in the wet sand on the beach. It might not be the exact right thing for me to do. I get the feeling that I get plantar fasciitis from grounding. And I feel worse. My feet are so messed up, and I thought the other day, that I do need to do something about my tight fascia in my lower legs. Gladly no water retention anymore, I think the sun cured that.

Clearly I’ve been dehydrated inwardly for decades. It‘s always been an issue, and doctors have told me at least for 30 years that I need to drink more. But I can drink like a walruss and it has no effect, which Jack verified many times. It’s the cells.

My bun/creatinine ratio is always too high, (last it was 23 down from 37 in 2022, yay), but the US doctors say that doesn’t matter.

Ok, so I will keep repairing my melanin sheets as best as I can. It helped a lot till here, I will keep on doing that.

### JanSz — 2024-02-01T00:52:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nikkis-journal-2023-onward.27997/post-330804>

I would start with looking at your values on this test.
Most of what Jack is talking about is really there.
If your values are perfect on that test,
you have different problems than what you thought you have.

### [DUTCH Plus® (Female) - DUTCH Test](https://dutchtest.com/resource/dutch-plus-female-sample-report/)

 dutchtest.com

### NikkiYork — 2024-02-01T19:45:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nikkis-journal-2023-onward.27997/post-330826>

I did. look up my batch and I think it was bad for the J&J, but it doesn’t matter to me, because it’s ll the same: low redox.

It can be mild, it can be a cat allergy, it can be food sensitivity, heavy metals, it’s just LOW REDOX and one of these things kicks you over the edge. Or all of them

I’m not so keen to fight symptoms rather then up my redox potential. The sun works.

So far no need for methylene blue, I’m still trying to do everything body internally. I’m ll keep it as an ace up my sleeve

Chiropractors are too expensive. 3 years ago my whole body was stiff, my neck was so stiff I couldn’t turn it. There was no way to stretch it because it made it worse. That stayed for 2 years. Or longer. I am super happy that I can move my head now and it’s way less stiff than ever.

Oddly falling onto my head and stunning myself helped it. I passed out and couldn’t move. I passed out again and somehow it unlocked something in my neck. Another one of those too stupid to believe truth.

Too stupid to believe as well: getting covid made me feel a lot better. Almost all my tingles were gone after and I had them so badly.

### JanSz — 2024-02-01T19:57:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nikkis-journal-2023-onward.27997/post-330828>

The parable of the drowning man, also known as Two Boats and a Helicopter, is a short story, often told as a joke, most often about a devoutly Christian man, frequently a minister, who refuses several rescue attempts in the face of approaching floodwaters,

each time telling the would-be rescuers that God will save him.

After turning down the last, he drowns in the flood.

After his death, the **man meets God and asks why he did not intervene.** God responds that he sent all the would-be rescuers to the man's aid on the expectation he would accept the help.

### NikkiYork — 2024-02-01T22:25:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nikkis-journal-2023-onward.27997/post-330833>

Fun fact: I just talked to a childhood friend of mine in Germany, who had the same symptoms like I did. And he knows of another one of our childhood friends, who also has it. Hm. Then again, even if it has to do with exposure from back then, we all need to deal with it now.

Him and I both had the hand and foot pain and he healed. woohoo
He can now run again, and he is super happy about it. He stopped drinking and started working on a farm, getting up and going to bed with the sun. He also travels to the glaciers a lot. initially he is a rock star, so that didn't help him much and he has 60 shows coming up next year. Yikes. I'm hoping for the best for him.

The third guy I might contact, he is in Singapore which I find pretty interesting, he manages hedgefunds. Also interesting.

### NikkiYork — 2024-02-01T22:31:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nikkis-journal-2023-onward.27997/post-330835>

Actual journal entry: I woke up and couldn't get out of bed.

Nobody used to believe me, including myself. Now I do at least.
The belly is still getting worse, now its not only a knife in the lower belly, the upper belly muscle is like in a cramp and hurting, the lateral muscles are okish, but I can start feeling those as well. Think of it as sore muscle but times 10.

The not getting out of bed is like having a hang over. You can of course eventually roll out of bed, but everything hurts and is achy, feels like flu-ish. Or hayfever. Head sinus, throat, nothing is really bad, but all of it together is bad. It's unsettling, because you think, ah shit. what did i do?

Nothing is the answer. It's just still getting worse. Ok.

No sun here. I am doing 3 times red light today, no tech, fish, outside whenever there is no atmospheric ricer coming down. That said, no more computer now.

### NikkiYork — 2024-02-02T23:35:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nikkis-journal-2023-onward.27997/post-330894>

I'll keep journaling:
Yesterday I was up since 4am, today I woke up from pain at 2.30 and kept sleeping on and off. Who would believe this: I woke up with the buzz in my chest! I havent had that since March 2022 and gladly it was only very faint and went away after a minute. I still thought, you gotta be kidding me. It is a horrible buzz-vibration internally, theee worst.

So I learn: the buzz is not a separate issue as most doctors or really all say, it is from the muscle/fascia/pain/bone episode, respectively linked to it, or goes with it. I seriously hope that I will get to stop this episode from getting worse, like NOW.
it's like in one of those tsunami videos, when the wave is coming, and you think, oh, it's flooded, that should be it, but then the water goes higher and higher and higher.

Anyways the night was so crappy, the inner of my bones hurt, if that makes sense, I had all kinds of symptoms that one has with an allergy: runny nose, occasional sneezing, sticky teary eyes, dry throat, nasal backdrip, feet and hands hurting, all positions lying down uncomfortable, neck hurts, back hurts. I did play with the cat for an hour yesterday. I have gotten allergic-ish to cats age 40, and it's on and off. I suspect right now just anything is bad? Playing with the cat was not an issue a month ago. Whatever, it's a light issue.

This morning it took me 20 minutes to get up, and if I was single, I'm pretty sure wouldn't have made it. I only was able to do it, because I had to take James to school. Thank you for my family. Purpose is good. So I sat in the sun, then went to Costco and I did 2 haircuts.
As I am writing I am sitting in the sun nacked. I just ate my oysters. I will up the redox potential!!!!

My belly muscles are better, my whole belly is one cramp now and it feels like leather skin to me. I remember that I had this leather type skin feeling in my legs 2 years ago. I think there is fluid under the skin that makes it feel that way. other than that i am feeling fine now at 1.30pm. Phew.

This Morning I noticed that when David screamed something to James downstairs, my whole body reacted with a pain jolt. That's funky.

Going to costco was fine. I had to switch arms carrying my purse, because my arm got tired. Not to complain about it, but that's describing it. We are talking about a one pound purse.

The tricky part that I learned over the last years is, that whatever I do, I pay the price only 2 days later - or not, which also happens, that i can actually do something. Yay! In the moment I'm fine. The next day I am fine, and then 2 days later, it hits me. That is a learning process, because usually one doesn't even remember. Example: two of my toes are swollen and hurt a lot... ah, I did a pedicure two days ago. Who the heck would put one and one together here? I would not, because why would a toe swell up from a pedicure?

I missed the sunrise today, so that can't happen anymore from now on. I'll set my alarm and force my self to get up. Maybe it will be better tomorrow.

I am not taking any medication, but I am contemplating taking the anti spike protein formular from McCullough. Maybe for whatever reason it is still that vaccine. I believe the ingredients can't hurt. Yeah, I'll do that.

### NikkiYork — 2024-02-04T20:03:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nikkis-journal-2023-onward.27997/post-331001>

> [Branden Gatewood said:](/goto/post?id=330980)
>
> I had my wife taking spike support from Peter McCullough. She was having headaches and gut problems which I associated with the vaccine. You’re correct it won’t hurt to try. Craziest thing happened though we got covid around Christmas 2023 and 75% of her symptoms have gone away. She hasn’t took it since.
>
> It may take years but remember nature heals. Are you grounded while you sun bathe?
>
> Stay strong.

That is the greatest information, Brandon. About your wife. I don’t know if you read it further up: my symptoms left me for good when I got Covid.
I can’t say 75% or put a number on it.

But: I had tingeling in all areas, shoulders, back, legs, it would wonder through my body and I had those two episodes of losing sensation in my lower legs completely. To the degree of me thinking I have no shins. So not just numb. Not there.

I was told it is anxiety and in my head, and I am telling everybody that if they would wake up and find themselves with no lower leg sensation, they should talk to me if they would not freak out.

In fact hindsite I believe I stayed reasonably calm, because I just kept hitting my legs and massaging them until they eventually came back. But the time that they did not come back which was minutes was certainly absolutely horrible. And then being told that it’s made up and a mental disease does not help.

Anyhow, that completely vanished with Covid. No more tingles, no more numbness, none of that. It was gone from one day to the next.

Mri was scheduled for ms onset and I do not have MS (just in case somebody was worried).

That was part of why I believe it is vaccine related, because maybe something was “used up” by the actual virus? Or my body fought something in the virus and in the vaccine? I don’t know. All doctors said, that the vaccine would not do anything like this.

But yes, I can verify, half of the symptoms were gone for good by getting Covid.

And again, my doctor laughed at me when I told him. He said: “you are the first person who tells me Covid helped them”

Tell your wife, that we are not single occurrences. We are not special  in the bad sense.

I’ doing great today, but I’ll put that in a separate post. Thank you for writing!!

### NikkiYork — 2024-02-04T20:40:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nikkis-journal-2023-onward.27997/post-331004>

Journal: I skipped yesterday, but I can happily report that I got better. A lot.

The night before yesterday I was only up at 4 and I did the whole sunrise thing and basically put myself as naked as possible I to the light whenever it was not crazy weather. And I barely did screen.

I went to a birthday dinner and had a ton of Margaritas which I was thinking could possibly be bad, but I was also considering that fun and relaxation amp up dopamine and I can need it

What can I say, I slept all night, woke up with a tad of a hang over and had NO! Issue getting up out of bed.

It is so hard to describe why one can not get up out of bed, because the day before was still hard. Not today. I can be tired or not feel well, that’s ok, I can get up. The other days are impossible and take huge effort.

It’s not being tired. I was tired during my pregnancy. To a degree that I thought I’d that won’t change after birth, it’s not sustainable. I did change with birth  I was never tired like that again, not even without any sleep. Different tired.

That said, this episode is not accompanied by being tired. It’s also not my circulation.
In somebody else’s fasting thread I mentioned not having a circulation to get up. I know all kinds of low circulation and how they feel. That is different as well.

I operate on a very low blood pressure usually. At the doctors they sometimes ask me, if I am even awake and I always had crazy low blood pressure to normal. It is not that. I had bought a device to measure.

It is a feeling of not being able to move that is discomfort. And it is so strong that it is almost impossible to push against it.

When I have it, I am asking myself “if the house would burn, could you get up and save yourself?” And the answer is yes. But the house is not burning, so no you can’t move. That is in fact basically how I reason myself out of bed, by putting ultimate urgency onto the fact that I have to move, even though I can’t.

In that sense you could say, it’s in the head, but it is absolutely physical. It’s in that sense comparable to being tired or for what it’s worth drunk. You can ask yourself, can I drive a car and you would say, no. But can you? Yes of course you can. You can override being tired or being drunk. And then there is a level at which you can barely override it. And that is how you can’t get up out of bed. Everything hurts, though it doesn’t hurt-hurt. Well, parts of the body do actually hurt, and it’s not pleasant at all, but eventually the body comes along and participates in life.

Anyhow.
Today I am feeling almost normal. I still have aches, but who the f cares. I don’t. That was quick. I will still rest up and keep doing what I’m doing. And hopefully I can call it a day.

And I insist, this is absolutely real and freaky bullshit, but it’s not nothing or mental.

Jack is offering the best solution and explanation by far from what and who I found, so yay! thank you, Jack!!

### NikkiYork — 2024-02-04T20:56:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nikkis-journal-2023-onward.27997/post-331005>

The things I am doing are:
1) best light hygiene I can do. Very dark and covered at night. Out with the sun. Full spectrum light onto the body. I supplement with red light when no sunny real light is available.

2) I started taking the McColuuogh Spike formular and I’ll keep doing it for a while.

3) I cut the carbs (accept for the margaritas, hahaha), I am doing strict epi-paleo, low inflammation high protein and fish fat - until i can eat shit on a shingle again as Jack would phrsase it.

4) Lots of fluids, warm showers, because warm seems good (not sure, if it is).

5) I want to stretch, but stretching rips. I learned that one stretch can lead to a stiff neck for months. So it try to not stretch but massage. It’s a lot more gentle and makes the body less stiff. I try to not mess up my neck.

The orthopedic doctor had said to me he wouldn’t even touch me, that’s how stiff my whole neck, shoulders and spine was, and I was thinking, wow, I don’t even think it’s bad. That dude also told me my hand issues came from sleeping with bent elbows. I liked him, but he was dead wrong about everything he said and did.

He prescribed steroids that did nothing. And he said all I could do was get an epidural and I gladly declined. He said “take the number of the epidural guy anyways, because in your state people tend to come around to it after a while.”

Does that not make you shake your head? I don’t want a friggin epidural. I am not sick. Or at least everybody tells me I have nothing. So I should get an epidural for a nothing burger? How does that make sense? It a serious procedure and nobody pokes my spine unless it’s absolutely necessary.

6) I will not do more than leasurely walking, because it had proved bad in the past to try to do excercise.

7) I won't do grounding or ct for a while in case I could short circuit my semiconductors

### NikkiYork — 2024-02-06T00:41:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nikkis-journal-2023-onward.27997/post-331062>

Today it’s again better. Absolutely no problems getting out of bed. Residue pain. My left wrist is bad, an the roots of my teeth still hurt. I guess I haven’t mentioned that before. Oh well, whatever, I’m super happy this wasn’t going on longer.

I no longer understand people who are following Jack getting hung up on food. Why guys? Light is 80percent, at least for me that is so, so, so evident. Food doesn’t even make the needle move one iota.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-02-06T03:10:48+0200

### NikkiYork — 2024-02-08T19:38:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nikkis-journal-2023-onward.27997/post-331227>

One step forward two steps back.
Ugh 2 nights ago it got worse again, last night was no bueno. Why? I’m just so annoyed.

I am only at a level 3-4, so I am still grateful that I’m not worse, but I think I mentioned patience not being one of my traits and now I also have elbow pain, which is new.

My leg muscles also hurt, from nothing. Otherwise just the hand and foot stuff.

It is the exact elbow pain that my German friend showed me last year, that he had on top of my symptoms. I didn’t have that and now I have it on both sides. It’s one single spot. Great.

Anyways, I’m just reporting. It’s not over. I’m angry and annoyed, my need for ease is not met. I’m practicing self empathy, but it doesn’t change the fact. I’m already scared of my older self. If I don’t start chilling out, I’ll become one grumpy old bitch. Definitely not something to look forward to.

### NikkiYork — 2024-02-19T19:53:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nikkis-journal-2023-onward.27997/post-331664>

Musings about the coward

The coward is somebody who hides. Behind all kinds of things, behind strong opinions, behind strong people, behind trees and bushes.

The coward finds himself a reason to do so. I’m polite, that’s why I don’t call out people on their bullshit. It’s not polite, it’s weak. The coward avoids all fights and runs - before it’s smarter to run, he or she just runs right away.

The coward will say, I’m a hero for not taking the jab, when the truth was that the coward was afraid of needles or whatever else. Cowards love that, when finally they can pretend to not be a coward. But they still are. Cowards pretend to be heroes as quick as they run.

Cowards don’t fight and call it non-violence. I am a non-violent person. I have tactics. I try the non-violent way first, when I don’t see imminent danger. If that fails, I rip off a head. Because I can. I am a warrior and I always have been one.

I took on my school at age 10, a German Gymnasium. I stood up against all my teachers, alone, no parents, I have absentee parents. It was a strike situation. Nobody was willing to explain to me what that strike was against, so I refused to strike.

That meant that the teachers didn’t get off and had to teach me alone in class. All the adults hated me. They said: “why don’t you get ice cream with the others, why don’t you play in the jumpy castle?” And I said: “because you cannot use me for a cause that I don’t understand.” This is just to explain my personality. I later became school president, and I was backed by strong teachers. You still can’t buy me, not with money, ice cream, fun, or convenience.

I did not make it to hero ever, but I always stand up for who I am, when I know. I fight for what I believe up to severe injury. In defense for my child I am fighting up to death and I am not kidding.
Most people don’t, and I understand that. Not all of them are weak, some are smart and strong, but they simply aren’t warriors. Not all traits are a choice. My team is my all and there is no I in team.

That said, I have literally no respect for the coward. I can tolerate the coward if he or she stays in their corner, but not, if they get in my way.

I’ve been kicked out of every designated safe space so far, because that is where cowards are hiding and me simply showing up peacefully, is usually enough to trigger some coward-came-to-fame. And whoop, I get booted. It’s a celebration for both sides.

The internet is full of cowards, because it’s so easy to hide and to disguise, and, wait for it, to offend.

That’s right, the coward, backed by the people who believe they need to fight for the underdog, turned into a bully in the recent years. The underdog does not belong in a leading position, hence the name.

He’s the trans-woman winning trophys in an unsporty way. He now took the lime light and banks on people’s good manners, he banks on political correctness and he banks on getting away with it all, because people like me get booted. So where are my backers? The ones who wrote me in private? Text me in private? Support me from a safe distance? Why does the coward intimidate you? I don’t get it. What am I doing wrong?

I’m still coming for all cowards hiding in the shadows  I’m still standing, sending love. Feel free to back me, if you can’t fight yourself.

P.s.: somebody in this forum said to me that I was lying and did not explain or answer any question I asked. I have a three strike rule. Just saying.

### Alexandra — 2024-02-20T01:21:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nikkis-journal-2023-onward.27997/post-331682>

Hi there. I read a bit of your journal (above) earlier and loved learning a small bit about your journey so far. When you said your doc pointed to you having a psychological issue when they couldn't find anything obvious / physical going on, I thought, Ohhhh myyyy it must be in the playbook for what to say to women when they can't figure it out and don't care to look further...before I started having cervial/ neck issues last year, I had exhaustion, tingling limbs, and broke my ankle and had ORIF in May 2020 when I'd always been pretty athletic, in shape and coordinated. I kept telling the docs "something isn't right", not realizing that my years in healthcare admin and digital (and not sleeping right) were finally catching up with me. The doctor charted that I was having difficulty with the pressures of motherhood during covid time and offered me an antidepressant! All of this just to say I can relate.

Hang in there, it sounds like you're a strong woman and will do whatever is required to figure out what's best for you and get to optimal. Glad you're on here.

### NikkiYork — 2024-03-08T02:44:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nikkis-journal-2023-onward.27997/post-332300>

All right, I somehow exploded size wise. I gained 30 pounds over the last 6 month. So now I want to fit in any of my clothes again. I can throw out everything at the moment only wearing sweat pants. Hahaha.

I do believe it’s my Faraday cage, so I’ll leave some fat, but I need to stop this.

Never been in ketosis. I‘ll get into that for a try.

Update: I just almost barfed. I guess that’s a sign that I almost made it. Woohoo. I’m doing 70% fat, 25% protein, 5%carbs. I actually love it.

### NikkiYork — 2024-03-08T03:30:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nikkis-journal-2023-onward.27997/post-332301>

So now to the hum that I am hearing since I recently got sensitized to it.
I hear it every night, but it hasn’t been bugging me much.

Last night I woke up and my chest was vibrating to the hum, and when I focused on it, I could make my whole body vibrate with the hum. Creepy.

Which leads me to the issues I had starting 3 or 4 years ago with the buzzing chest.

I believe I had tense muscles or inflamed muscles and those vibrate more than non-spastic or not cramped muscles and it was the frigging hum sound all along. I just hadn’t heard it.

I mean, you hear sounds like a fridge, the air purifier, the heater, the ocean, cars… and I wasn’t thinking of anything else, till my neighbor kept asking me, if I could hear that odd hum at night.

I was dissing him for it for over a year, until I heard it and it couldn’t have been any of our appliances. We had taken the house off the grid.

Now that I am sensitized to it, because I know what to listen for, this hum is around all the time. I can mostly not hear it during the day, but it‘s not a sound that travels through air. It travels through things. Hence it can make a person vibrate.

I solved this. I don’t think it’s great though, because 3-4 years ago it was a nightly torture and it woke me up at 4am every night. Plus I thought I was crazy.

So nice of my gyn to tell me it’s menopause.

Anyways, I will try to do something about the vibrations and I’ll explore it more.

Did I mention that I went camping last weekend - 80 miles further north and there was no hum? Yeah, hum free zone.

It’s not great that my chest apparently was in a state that it was hum receptive respectively resonating.

I don’t quite know what it means, but Sherlock is on it.

### NikkiYork — 2024-03-08T03:46:27+0200

I am talking to the family about moving every week, we are not quite there yet. Buying a second home is a definite though, so that’s good.

I don’t really know how to properly attach photos. I have 6 photos over the course of 6 months that show me with a very swollen cheek.

I am posting this, because I told my dental disaster story on a different thread.

Two dentists, a root specialist I really actually liked and the periodontist, who I should have ditched immediately but was too scared to interrupt treatment, because I really wanted help… both accused me of doing this to myself and they claimed their dental work had nothing to do with my swollen face.

Well, I went there because of massive pain. My face swelled up. Neither did I bite my cheek, nor did I do anything to make myself be in so much pain that I couldn‘t do anything else but whine.

I don’t want pitty, but I want to defend my sanity. I am not crazy and I have no history of mental disease.

I would love to get justice, but I don’t have the mental or emotional will to not just move on. I need positivity and I can’t make things unhappen anyways.

### NikkiYork — 2024-04-28T18:24:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nikkis-journal-2023-onward.27997/post-334045>

I’m currently in El Salvador - by myself. Only for a week which was all I could make happen, because I have a 10 year old boy and having my husband care for him is asked a lot of David.

Things aaren’t exactly like I imagined, but they are great and: are things ever going according to plan?

Jack says to have a plan, and I 100percent agree. I always have a plan and I push for it hard. Then comes a curve ball, and then you hit a home run when you least saw it coming. At least that’s my life.

I understand that flying on a plane is terrible for my health and probably anybodies.
I am sitting in the am sun every morning and it is magic.

I saw people on the pow wow saying that 6 weeks of sun didn’t do it for them, well, for me a day with morning sun is better than one without. Much better.

I was in a real funk when I booked this trip, because of health, because I felt stuck in my situation, because I was angry at many things, events, circumstances, people…

And then life changed massively before I left for El Salvador. To the better. We bought a house. It was a decision of 5 minutes. Because it was the right thing to do and when you look for things and the path is right, you see it immediately.

At least I do. And David was on one page with me immediately. Now is that a house for a long time? I don’t know. Probably not, but it’s something my family needs right now.

I see a potential shit storm coming up as well, and for years. I mean, most mitochondriacs see it. It’s pretty scary.

I go by “luck is when opportunity meets preparation.” It’s been working for me for a long time.

I am looking at all kinds of real estate here.
Yesterday I did a trip to the closer outskirts of San Salvador. They have luxurious apartment towers there, preppy condominiums.

I don’t like it. Not my thang. So far I like the Zaragoza area best and I am changing Airbnbs today. The next one is up for sale. I want to check how awesome it is, and then maybe some of us can get together and buy it.

Oh well, I won’t have money for a couple of months. There is another one by the beach I will check out and Barbara Corcoran taught me to simply go up to the owner and ask how much. There is a price for everything.
That has also worked out very well for me in life.

I’m videoing as much as I can, so I can share with the people here. I still think that everybody should come and see things in person, but I moved to New York without ever having visited that place.

I just sold all I had and did it, and it was the best thing I have done. It was the best way of doing it in the sense that had I not sawed off the branch I sat on in Germany, I probably wouldn’t have made it through the hardship.

Sometimes you have to pass the point of no return. Oh well, I always had money for a ticket, but I sailed on the edge for a long, long time.

If the US would be my place of birth, I don’t think I would still live there. I got it better. I am an immigrant. If you talk from one immigrant to another, we know how fucking hard it was to set foot into the USA.

You don’t need to talk, we all had the same problems. Also, not all Americans like immigrants, or better one is always treated differently. Doesn’t matter what color. White does not exempt you.
I believe that the immigrants could save the USA, because we don’t take all the awesome stuff for granted. We never had it, we fought for it. We didn’t come to get it stolen from some fucktards.

The USA is a country to fight for and to not give up on. I am officially a US citizen, this is my country!
I have given up on the “native” Americans. Even the ones that have more realistic views. The agency is so low, it hurts, the egocentrism and complacency makes for lying to a best friend at all times and that’s ok. The morals and ethics are at an all time low. Realizing that it’s now only about partying it up until it all fails, or cashing in as much as possible. Alas, I have hope.

Let the good Americans leave the country and fight from the outside. Let the Canadians, Germans, French, English and wherever else born Americans hold up fort.

I have hope.

### NikkiYork — 2024-04-28T19:23:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nikkis-journal-2023-onward.27997/post-334046>

I think it’s called “hold down the fort”.

It’s really funny, a good American once told me: “you know, many Americans think you are stupid, because you don’t talk right all the time. But worse is, when you ask how it’s said the right way. Then they really think you are stupid. Stop asking.”

And that is correct. That is the complacency of the US. Most don’t have an idea how fucking hard it is to learn a new language native style when you are older than 30. And I need to talk perfectly or else I’m a dumb dumb. It makes me laugh so hard. It’s not funny though.

### NikkiYork — 2024-05-01T18:15:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nikkis-journal-2023-onward.27997/post-334164>

I just read a stupid Huberman tweet advertising sun screen and him saying that sunscreen is necessary.

I am Fitzpatrick type 2 and haven’t used any for a year without burning. I stayed in the highest uv Index possible, like right now in El Salvador. I was walking in the sun yesterday at 1 o’clock for an hour. No burn.

Before I did the morning sun protocol of Jack‘s I burned immediately. I always had a burned nose. Always burned shoulders and chest. It only works if you do the morning sun.

I never wore sunglasses, so I have no experience with that.

I have an orchid sanctuary, meaning I save people‘s unblooming orchids from the trash. So I have hundreds. And I use them to study nature.

Phalaenopsis orchids, the classic supermarket ones, can’t be in the sun. The sun burns holes right into their leaves. Unless… odor let them get a sun callus.

They can also do it. If the orchid gets too much light indoors, it turns yellow. People think it’s a lack of water, because that’s what they learned when they see yellow leaves. So they over water the thing, it rots and is dead.

If you put the orchid in the shade and semi shade outside, its leaves will turn reddish brown. They will literally develop a tan. And then guess what. They no longer burn like before.

### NikkiYork — 2024-05-05T19:22:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nikkis-journal-2023-onward.27997/post-334319>

I’m back from El Salvador. If anybody has questions, I will gladly answer.
Obviously in my style. So if you are asking, where should I stay? I will tell you, in an Airbnb, a hotel or with friends.
Which? Well what do I know about your life and your finances.
Don’t listen to people who say: stay at hostel xyz!
If you are not a hostel type, you’ll hate it. Yes, recommendations, but only listen to people you know a little and whose answer you can evaluate.
Jack has preferences, but you aren’t Jack either.
Sometimes it’s all about timing. My timing for El Salvador was strange.

For example: i believe that Jack says that El Tunco sucks, and I know that Chantal say it. My timing for El Tunco must have been great, because none of what the reasons are why it sucks we’re in place.

I went, it was not crowded, it had no entry fee, I parked in the street for free - not paid parking. There was no party, but very interesting surf teams from all over the world who were practicing for the Junior world champion ship that is currently going on.
It was an absolutely fab day there. My friend was able to feed the atm’s with fiat to get Bitcoin… we saw very cute surf shops, had ocean view drinks. I can’t say anything bad about it. Opposite. I loved it.

That was timing. Maybe I would hate it tomorrow.

What’s the conclusion? You gotta do it. Nobody can predict your experience. You can plan some, but really not much. You gotta go with the flow.
Is it safe? Yes. Can you still get hit by a car? Of course.

A very interesting observation I made is that the wilderness is more wild there. A hummingbird in California is flying differently from an El Salvadorian humming bird. I watch birds a lot. I live with a ton of birds in Southern California.
I know their schedules, the patterns, I learn a lot from birds.
I did. the same in El Salvador, which really is bird paradise.

I guess I need to write about nature. I love nature, but I have always been more scared of it than happy with it. Not scared, because I am a whomp, but because I know its power, I see it and I respect it. Nature is honey badger. Honey badger don’t care. Nature has no qualms killing, killing instantly, killing biggly, mercilessly, unfairly, you name it. If you fuck with nature, you’re toast. Possibly. So I don’t do it.

In El Salvador we have a proximity to nature and exposure to nature, that is not for the faint of heart and not for people who are dumb.

I’m not a very feral person, so it’s a mismatch to a degree. I grew up on the country side and we made fun of the city kids, because they were so “overbread”, so fragile, so groomed.
And El Salvador, man, that’s another level of country side. It’s a lot more wild than Hawaii, if you’ve been there. And I’ve been to the Hawaiian wild areas. I’m not sure how else to explain it. It’s wilder than Southafrica to me, same wild as Ecuador. I guess it’s the jungle.

Go see it.

### NikkiYork — 2024-05-07T19:55:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nikkis-journal-2023-onward.27997/post-334400>

On science.

People who say they are pro-science are saying less than they believe they claim.
Our proCovid fellows, who also claimed to be pro-science and pro vaccine are a great example of asholes hiding behind a pseudo authority.

I am saving orchids - I mentioned that - but I also experiment with them. I have so many, it’s ok if one or two die. That’s a scientific approach of seeing the world. When it’s about humans, that’s not called ethical. The Nazi scientists did that. So really science wants to know and doesn’t always care. Or, you know, you throw others under the bus for the greater good, that’s something one can ethically argue for.

Also sucks for the individual… orchid or human.

I have an orchid pest for about 2 years. And I could get rid of it, but instead I watch and learn. It’s a mealy bug. It doesn’t kill most orchids, but it weakens them, some die, some develop resilience. I love my plants, but do I save them all? Nope. I use them. Because I love myself and my family more.

Possibly it makes me an ashole, but then again, all the orchids would have been dead long ago, had I not saved them. If life is what is valued highest. If!!!

I tend to want to value life highest. If an orchid gets too sick, I put it outside and it can see, if it survives all by itself. Some do. Some don’t.

The sick orchids do marvelous things. They become very sticky, they produce sugar to fight off the bugs. Some live in conjunction with them pretty well. The weak ones get tackled by the bugs first. The strong ones are able to not get infected.

By the way, the orchids are great movers. If you let them, they will kick off any flower pot. Orchids naturally grow downward, not upward. We put them upside down in the pots. Naturally they grow in a tree nook and their leaves are pointing down. The underside of their leaves is what they expose. We know close to nothing about orchids. There are an estimated 80.000 kinds of them. They are absolutely fascinating creatures with an amazing biome in their air roots. No scientist knows how it works (yet). They live in synergy with certain fungi. I would say they carry thousands of viruses. They make photosynthesis with their roots.

Their roots react to touch, but that’s unscientific, because “science” has no explanation for it and they don’t believe it.

The plants whose roots I am regularly touching are a lot stronger than others. Fight me on that. Or don’t.

My Mom has an orchid that’s pretty old, like 50 years and it can impossibly live, but it does. My Mom grows plants like no other. They don’t have real soil, or they never get repotted. She then dumps a bunch of chemicals on them and claims it’s food. It somehow works. They live off of my mom, because when she goes on vacation the plants die within weeks. It’s true. I’ve lived with her for a second. So what do you say now? This isn’t scientific.

Sitting outside, I also live with birds. Love them. And I made many of them familiar to me. So they keep coming by. Aren’t scared of me any longer. I know when there is a cat near, because I know my bird’s alarm system.

Right now a couple is annoyed with me, because I sit by their water and they want to take a bath, but I’m too close by. Well, let’s see, if one of them has the balls to still bathe and learn that I’m a friend.

Animals don’t trust. They only adapt. They adapt to me not being an enemy. They don’t know, they are like machines learning.

So am I saying that instinct and machine learning is similar? Somewhat. It’s evaluating data you get from an event and adjust accordingly.

What is the difference to humans, who are doing the same?

It’s will. Not wanting, no ego. It’s free will, which allows us to self express. Tell that to an animal or a plant. They are not creator beings. And neither is AI. The problem here is that I can immediately tell if something is an animal, a plant or a human. I just can.

The most ferral and fucked up homeless person I am able to identify as a human. A dressed up monkey driving a car I can identify as an animal.

And a computer? I can’t. That is the big issue that humanity has not yet realized.

Humans are computerizing themselves by believing that if they type into a forum, they would leave a human message. They don’t. What you see here from me is not human. It is technology.

Can you make contact with me? Yes. But you do that via quantum entanglement hoping you got the correct address and basically you are talking to your spiritual heart, if you want to get an uncorrupted message from me.

All else is corrupted. It’s fake and people are falling for it, because they have not learned any better.

We can entangle with anybody anywhere at any time. It’s not science yet and maybe it never will be. It’s the truth though.

I’m going to leave it at that, yeah, and don’t forget, what you read and what you hear is not me. At best it’s you, and worst case it is corrupted, highjacked, and false

Shine on, you crazy diamonds!

Not scared, because I am a whomp, but because I know its power, I see it and I respect it. Nature is honey badger. Honey badger don’t care. Nature has no qualms killing, killing instantly, killing biggly, mercilessly, unfairly, you name it. If you fuck with nature, you’re toast. Possibly. So I don’t do it.
>
> In El Salvador we have a proximity to nature and exposure to nature, that is not for the faint of heart and not for people who are dumb.
>
> I’m not a very feral person, so it’s a mismatch to a degree. I grew up on the country side and we made fun of the city kids, because they were so “overbread”, so fragile, so groomed.
> And El Salvador, man, that’s another level of country side. It’s a lot more wild than Hawaii, if you’ve been there. And I’ve been to the Hawaiian wild areas. I’m not sure how else to explain it. It’s wilder than Southafrica to me, same wild as Ecuador. I guess it’s the jungle.
>
> Go see it.

well said Nikki. I worry a lot about people just jumping on a plane and coming to ES.
This may not be everyone's cup of tea.
Everyone has to do their own due diligence.
We all will have vastly different experiences no matter where we choose to live.

After leaving our driveway yesterday .....a man on a motorbike and a truck collided just in front of us - the accident scene was pure carnage. The man died.
I still can't shake it off. I saw the man's legs twitching and was hopeful.....
We see motor bike accidents everyday.

BTW - we love El Tunco - all about timing for sure.
