### Cadoia — 2023-07-31T16:09:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-makes-me-tired.28016/post-324523>

> [Neurotic Farmer said:](/goto/post?id=324519)

just spoke in private with a guy who basically is outside all day every day.
He said 7am prepares you for 8 am. 8 am prepares you for 9 am. and so on.
It makes sense to me.
However, I cant be outside all day. Have to work etc. Also sometimes its cloudy here for a whole month in a row.
Ill try increase sunrise time to 30 min. and see if it helps with my noon sun.

# Thread: NOSTR : a new social media protocol
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nostr-a-new-social-media-protocol.28018/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 59
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rusty — 2023-07-25T20:41:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nostr-a-new-social-media-protocol.28018/post-324304>

Hello all,

[NOSTR](https://nostr.com/) is a social media protocol being developed right now. Think of it like bitcoin but for social media.

NOSTR makes use of lightning network for incentivising users which is a layer 2 protocol built on top of Bitcoin. (layer 1)

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Users have a private key that gives them control over their identity on the network. This means users can switch between different NOSTR software services while retaining the same identity, similar to how phone numbers can transfer between cell providers.

-   NOSTR apps can connect to multiple servers at once. This allows users to see content from different servers in one feed.
-   If one server bans a user, they can still post on other servers. Their content won't disappear completely.
-   There is no central company or server in charge of NOSTR. Anyone can run a server or build an app on top of the protocol.
-   This decentralized design means no single entity controls user identities or content. The users have more control through their private keys.

You can read more about it on : [nostr.com](http://nostr.com)

I'm making this thread to increase awareness about it.

You can find me using my public key : **npub16guycjkxy04e79dwfjejaeafkqlqyq5q94f0yw2l9m50vse32x4qsyulsy**

If you have an account or make one (I strongly suggest you should), do post your public key so we all can be follow each other.

**How to get started :**

Get started at [nosta.me](https://nosta.me/)

They have a pretty good guided tutorial.

Will try to keep this thread updated with learning resources I encounter.

Looking forward to more discussions around it.

### Rusty — 2023-08-08T00:50:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nostr-a-new-social-media-protocol.28018/post-324821>

> [Mal Pal said:](/goto/post?id=324819)
>
> I'm not too sure about this. They have to figure out the incentive model for public relay nodes cause cost goes up with scale.
> Most people aren't going to pay.
> Probably will need ads to make this work.

Private relay is for people who want to cut down on the spam.

Second, anyone can run a private relay on a basic computer.

Nostr Relay’s aren’t like Bitcoin full nodes. They’re neither resource intensive, nor do they rely on peer 2 peer, it works via broadcasting.

Just dumb stuff.

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### Rusty — 2025-12-01T08:33:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nostr-a-new-social-media-protocol.28018/post-363430>

> [DLO said:](/goto/post?id=363388)
>
> If the gov decides to lean on a VPN they can pull all your records from them. With ZKP as the base layer of vp.net thats not even possible.

Obscura is the more serious attempt in the history of internet to minimize what can be logged.

There ARE ABSOLUTELY NO RECORDS because of the way it works.

Obscura uses a 2-hop setup: your device first connects to Obscura’s entry node, then gets passed through a second exit node (run by Mullvad VPN).

That way, no single server ever sees both who you are and what you’re doing.

### coho3131 — 2023-07-25T22:24:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spectroscope.28019/post-324309>

Does anybody have any recommendations for a spectroscope to test blue light blocking glasses and Iris computer screen settings?

### jackmck — 2023-07-25T23:29:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spectroscope.28019/post-324311>

Hey Mike, I recently looked into brands and prices of spectrometers. It looks like UPRtek is the most accessible brand. Still searching in case there's a cheaper alternative. I get the feeling anything more affordable would not be quite the same quality.

### coho3131 — 2023-07-25T23:41:01+0300

Thanks. Would that test blue light blocking glasses, or do I use a spectroscope for that?

### jackmck — 2023-07-26T01:42:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spectroscope.28019/post-324317>

> [coho3131 said:](/goto/post?id=324312)

> Thanks. Would that test blue light blocking glasses, or do I use a spectroscope for that?

# Thread: What are the recommended Blue Light Blocking Glasses?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-the-recommended-blue-light-blocking-glasses.28020/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### grillosun — 2023-07-26T11:56:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-the-recommended-blue-light-blocking-glasses.28020/post-324334>

Hey everyone!

I'm new to all of this and trying to make some positive changes after years of harmful health choices. Being from Venezuela, health concerns sometimes take a backseat in our priorities.

I've been looking into buying blue light-blocking glasses. It seems there are "Daylight" glasses (amber) and "Night" glasses (red). The red ones supposedly block 90-99% of Blue light in the 385-485 wavelength range.

Is there really a difference between them? Should I use blue light-blocking glasses all day, or is it more effective to use them only at night?

I've come across these popular ones: [https://truedark.com/truedark-science/](https://truedark.com/truedark-science/)

Thanks in advance for any help or advice!

### Neurotic Farmer — 2023-07-26T13:05:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-the-recommended-blue-light-blocking-glasses.28020/post-324335>

Yes, there is a difference. The daylight ones typically allow some blue light in while the night ones block about 99% depending on the brand. I personally just wear the night ones all day when I'm in front of screens and artificial lights. When I go outside, obviously I remove them. Lucia Eyes or Ra Optics are good from my experience.

### grillosun — 2023-07-27T13:01:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-the-recommended-blue-light-blocking-glasses.28020/post-324363>

> [Neurotic Farmer said:](/goto/post?id=324335)
>
> Yes, there is a difference. The daylight ones typically allow some blue light in while the night ones block about 99% depending on the brand. I personally just wear the night ones all day when I'm in front of screens and artificial lights. When I go outside, obviously I remove them. Lucia Eyes or Ra Optics are good from my experience.

Thanks!!!!

### sunman — 2023-09-11T16:30:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-the-recommended-blue-light-blocking-glasses.28020/post-326236>

i love these i recently purchased; they're wraparound and block green/blue; [https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B085VMDK7H/ref=ppx\_yo\_dt\_b\_search\_asin\_title?ie=UTF8&th=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B085VMDK7H/ref=ppx_yo_dt_b_search_asin_title?ie=UTF8&th=1)

While during daytime indoors i wear the emr tek Cyclops daytime glsses;

### Jack Kruse — 2023-09-11T18:43:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-the-recommended-blue-light-blocking-glasses.28020/post-326241>

[www.luciaeyes.com](http://www.luciaeyes.com) LIGHT20 is the member code.

### Trippg — 2023-07-26T21:36:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.28023/post-324348>

Thoughts on grounding while indoors?

I understand that you cant do better than being outside with your bare feet on the ground (nature always wins), but what about grounded sleeping sheets, or grounding pads while in doors?

I currently have a copper plate I stand on (bare foot) wired to a grounding rod outside of a window.

Any good links/resources on this? Thanks!

### JanSz — 2023-07-27T00:13:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.28023/post-324354>

> [Trippg said:](/goto/post?id=324348)

>

>
> Any good links/resources on this? Thanks!

grounding rod outside
may be working or not.

One way to figure out if your grounding is acceptable is to hook it up to GFCI.
See if it trips the GFCI.

/

### Anthony Smith — 2023-07-27T03:10:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.28023/post-324361>

> [Trippg said:](/goto/post?id=324348)
>
> Thoughts on grounding while indoors?
>
> I understand that you cant do better than being outside with your bare feet on the ground (nature always wins), but what about grounded sleeping sheets, or grounding pads while in doors?
>
> I currently have a copper plate I stand on (bare foot) wired to a grounding rod outside of a window.
>
> Any good links/resources on this? Thanks!

Don’t need a link, think it thru. So many people in the Earthing community have this wrong, they know grounding is good outside so they thing if they ground to the outside - that’s good even while inside. Grounding make you part of the circuit. If you are inside, in a 60Hz AC electric field and you ground, that field is now following the path of least resistance to ground THRU you. Your body runs on DC and grounding outside in nature connects you to the natural DC of Mother Earth. Grounding while in an AC field is exacerbating the problem not solving it.

### Trippg — 2023-07-27T18:47:51+0300

Your body runs on DC and grounding outside in nature connects you to the natural DC of Mother Earth. Grounding while in an AC field is exacerbating the problem not solving it.

Interesting, I though that might be the case. Thanks.

Seems there are very few workarounds that solve the problem of being indoors.

### ekarge — 2023-08-01T06:59:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.28023/post-324560>

Yea very discouraging to hear here that grounding in the modern indoor world is actually detrimental, and equally hard to swallow that sitting in the backyard with my shoes off is according to the info laid out here, actually more deleterious than avoiding contact with the ground due to the dirty AC fields ..smh.

I've come to the conclusion the only thing I can do EMF wise in my house is sleep with an EMF blocking blanket (ideally EMF blocking bed canopy if you have the money) to at least block some nn EMF. I guess grounding indoors/at night just isn't a possibility if you live in a normal on the grid home surrounded by AC fields..

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-01T08:07:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.28023/post-324562>

> [ekarge said:](/goto/post?id=324560)
>
> Yea very discouraging to hear here that grounding in the modern indoor world is actually detrimental, and equally hard to swallow that sitting in the backyard with my shoes off is according to the info laid out here, actually more deleterious than avoiding contact with the ground due to the dirty AC fields ..smh.
>
> I've come to the conclusion the only thing I can do EMF wise in my house is sleep with an EMF blocking blanket (ideally EMF blocking bed canopy if you have the money) to at least block some nn EMF. I guess grounding indoors/at night just isn't a possibility if you live in a normal on the grid home surrounded by AC fields..

Hi ekarge,

#1 - Grounding/Earthing is a reconnecting with nature thing that needs to take place - in Nature. Your house ground in part of the modern electrical system which is part of the problem. If you are in a 60Hz AC electric field (inside your home) connecting to ground makes you part of the modern electrical system and is not a good idea.

#2 - Sitting in the back yard may well be perfectly ok if your back yard is not in the middle of a City or next to an industrial area. Grounding/Earthing in a high AC polluted area is NOT nature.

EMF is not a single thing. EMF is the electric field, the magnetic field and the radio frequency (RF). Each requires different equipment to measure and a different approach to mitigate.

#3 - an EMF blocking blanket/canopy can block RF from getting to you BUT being metallic could attract and increase the electric field in the room that you are exposed to. Controlling the electric field in your home is EASY . . . TURN OFF THE CIRCUITS and KILL the 60Hz (50Hz) electric fields, especially at night. Reconnect, ground/earth with Nature - in Nature - it is that simple.

### Trippg — 2023-08-01T20:13:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.28023/post-324579>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=324562)
>
> Hi ekarge,
>
> #1 - Grounding/Earthing is a reconnecting with nature thing that needs to take place - in Nature. Your house ground in part of the modern electrical system which is part of the problem. If you are in a 60Hz AC electric field (inside your home) connecting to ground makes you part of the modern electrical system and is not a good idea.
>
> #2 - Sitting in the back yard may well be perfectly ok if your back yard is not in the middle of a City or next to an industrial area. Grounding/Earthing in a high AC polluted area is NOT nature.
>
> EMF is not a single thing. EMF is the electric field, the magnetic field and the radio frequency (RF). Each requires different equipment to measure and a different approach to mitigate.
>
> #3 - an EMF blocking blanket/canopy can block RF from getting to you BUT being metallic could attract and increase the electric field in the room that you are exposed to. Controlling the electric field in your home is EASY . . . TURN OFF THE CIRCUITS and KILL the 60Hz (50Hz) electric fields, especially at night. Reconnect, ground/earth with Nature - in Nature - it is that simple.

Anthony,

I tested this, when I'm ungrounded indoors the electrical field around me is ~5V/m as soon as i touch the copper plate attached to a grounding rod, the electrical field jumps to 60-70 V/m. One thing that is interesting is that my body voltage tested with a multimeter does drop significantly when I touch the grounding plate.

ungrounded indoors = high body voltage (delta between body and ground), low electrical filed (V/m)
grounded indoors = low body voltage (delta between body and ground), high electrical filed (V/m)

Is this because I'm becoming an antenna for the ELF into the ground? Thanks.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-01T21:39:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.28023/post-324585>

> [Trippg said:](/goto/post?id=324579)
>
> Anthony,
>
> I tested this, when I'm ungrounded indoors the electrical field around me is ~5V/m as soon as i touch the copper plate attached to a grounding rod, the electrical field jumps to 60-70 V/m. One thing that is interesting is that my body voltage tested with a multimeter does drop significantly when I touch the grounding plate.
>
> ungrounded indoors = high body voltage (delta between body and ground), low electrical filed (V/m)
> grounded indoors = low body voltage (delta between body and ground), high electrical filed (V/m)
>
> Is this because I'm becoming an antenna for the ELF into the ground? Thanks.

Would need to know your set up. Are these Electric meter readings or body voltage readings? An electric meter gives a very different reading when being touched than when not touched (due to body electric field induction). This is why we prefer body voltage because it tells you what is going on with your body and offers consistency of readings. Being double grounded does not reduce the electric field you are in it just goes thru the other ground and not the meter.

Whatever your BV reading is inside, when you touch a second ground, the reading should go to zero because the field is no longer going thru the BV meter but follows the path of least resistance to ground (the ground pad or grounded appliance - touch a fridge). The reading goes down but the electric field you are in is exactly the same. Put a second meter on the ground pad and you will see what is happening.

To get rid of an electric field ground the appliance or unplug it, or a wire with MC cable. When you ground yourself you make yourself part of the circuit.

5V/m (5,000mV) is a high BV reading, 60,000mV is _#$@&%\*!_

### ekarge — 2023-08-02T06:31:37+0300

Hey Anthony. Thanks for the response and feedback, greatly appreciate it.

1\. Couldn't agree more nature is king and the ultimate solution.
2\. I live in densely populated South Florida adjacent to a plaza/restaurant/hotel and many neighboring houses. So I just assumed my backyard is marinating in dirty AC current but hard to know for sure of course unless measured. How might one go about measuring this?
Even the beach 5 minutes down the road is inundated with neighboring high rises/restaurants within a few hundred feet, making nature/clean grounding feel inaccessible. Would be nice to have a better understanding of the safe distances from dirty AC current sources that would be safe for grounding (I'm sure there's a multitude of factors but any resources/rough ball parks would be great to know).
3\. I wish it were as easy as just turning off the circuits at night but as I mentioned I'm down here in sweltering South Florida where central air is a necessity and I also use a CPAP to sleep at night, so I’m just trying to mitigate what I can with EMF blocking blanket and wrapping what electronics I can in excessive faraday material. Maybe grounding the blanket would help dispel the electric field a bit? Or would this be just grounding/exposing the blanket to more dirty AC fields making it more of a fishing net for dirty electric fields? How would I be able to tell if its helpful or just increasing the electric field and ultimately harmful?

How would I be able to tell if its helpful or just increasing the electric field and ultimately harmful?

Several things here but simply if you measure you know. A body voltage meter measures the electric field you are in that is being induced into your body. You can measure where you are and if the reading is too high you can start turning things off and see the results. Shielding from RF fields with blankets is good BUT you must be sure not to increase the electric field at the same time. By doing what you are doing you may actually be making the total nnEMF situation worse.

At night you only need to turn off the circuits that affect your bedroom. It is ok to flip the breakers for a few weeks. Leave the Air on. Run an extension cord in from a part of the house that is on for the CPAP machine.

Earthing/grounding outside in a built up area (as Dr Jack has pointed out) can be a problem. AC currents in the ground are a very real Issue. If you go to the beach down by the water I expect you will be fine.

You ask in regard to measuring “dirty” AC currents in the ground outside

> How might one go about measuring this?

Give me a couple of weeks and I may have an answer for you.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-03T02:15:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.28023/post-324634>

> [ekarge said:](/goto/post?id=324633)
>
> Thanks, Anthony, great suggestions.
>
> Though still a bit unsure exactly how I would ascertain if the EMF blanket is doing more harm than good? Body voltmeter? Compare EMF readings under blanket vs. no blanket?
>
> And will stay tuned for the rest. Appreciate your time/shared knowledge.

The EMF blanket is to reduce RF. It does this by being metallic and reflecting the RF away. If you are using it in an area that has a 60Hz AC electric field, being metallic, it will attract this electric field. An RF meter, I hope would show a reduced RF. A body voltage meter (BV), I expect, will show an increased electric field. Measure on the bed without the blanket then with. If you then ground the blanket your BV will show close to zero (because you would be double grounded) BUT the field would be exactly the same, except now you are part of the circuit and this 60Hz AC field is flowing thru you all night.

You are obviously trying a number of things to hopefully reduce your nnEMF exposure but you have no idea if what you are doing is helping or hindering unless you measure.

### ekarge — 2023-08-03T05:36:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.28023/post-324641>

Got it. So sounds like generally speaking, EMF blankets in many cases are futile and not really beneficial when they are being used in area with AC electric fields bc while it may be reflecting RF, it's still attracting the surrounding AC fields and making you more a part of that 'dirty' electrical field. The only seemingly positive roll it could play is if you can drastically/measurably eliminate this AC field by turning off circuits, in which case the blanket could serve its intended beneficial purpose of reflecting RF, without being a fishing net for AC electric fields.

Bed canopies are a better solution where there is no physical contact with the material but even then without turning off the electric field, problems can be created. People selling products tend to focus on the problem they are offering a solution to without consideration of the problems they may be creating.

The benefit of Grounding/Earthing is reconnecting with Nature and making yourself part of the natural DC energy circuit of the Earth. Being outside, away from man made nnEMF with your bare feet on the ground.

ALL the neutrals in your house are connected together in the breaker box. ALL the ground wires are connected together in the breaker box AND ALL the neutrals are connected to all the ground wires. All these wires also act as antennas picking up RF. If you ground to the house ground you are making yourself part of the AC circuit. If you use an outside ground you are still inside. You are not on the ground you are connected to a long copper wire (antenna).

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-19T20:06:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.28023/post-325334>

> [Elizabeth Leonard said:](/goto/post?id=325322)
>
> Is this measuring that we could do ourselves at a reasonable price(getting device), or do we need to hire expert?

Measuring for oneself is simple and does not cost that much (relatively speaking). Some people just don’t want to do it themselves so they higher in an expert. Others may have a more complicated situation and need the special equipment and expertise of a professional to resolve their issue. If you have a Building Biologist or EMF Consultant close by this need not be too expensive either but if they are several hours away the price goes up. A virtual consult can be a good compromise but you will still have to do the measuring yourself.

There are different schools of thought on this but you asked me. For in home 60Hz AC electric fields, which for most people is the largest single source of daily nnEMF exposure, we recommend a body voltage meter. This will tell you how strong the electric fields are throughout your home and help locate the source (hidden wires) so you can mitigate. The Building Biology Institute say a BV reading at night below 10 milli-Volts (mV) is of “no concern”, between 10mV and 100mV of “slight concern”, above 1,000mV “extreme concern”. I have seen readings in bedrooms of over 7,000mV before mitigation and most are above 2,000mV! Touching my iPad while charging 40,000+mV. We have a body voltage kit (BVK) on our web site, less than $150 with Kruse10 discount code.

For Radio Frequency (RF) - WiFi, cell phones, Bluetooth, “smart meters”, microwave ovens etc you need a RF meter. The goal is to first identify any problems, locate the source and then mitigate. The important number is the end number, the difference between the first reading and the mitigated reading, did the mitigation work? RF meters can get very expensive but a consumer grade meter many people use is the TriField TF2. This meter measures RF up to 6 GigaHertz (GHz) - covers microwave, 4G and lower frequency T-Mobile, AT&T 5G. By the way, microwave ovens, WiFi routers and Bluetooth devices ALL use 2.4GHz. The TF2 also measures magnetic fields, available on Amazon for $180+/-.

When you switch off ALL the circuits at night that are within 8 feet of your bed, you remove 100% of the electric field, magnetic field, “DE” and RF on those circuits. This creates an EMF mitigated sleep sanctuary and because we are more sensitive at night to the affects of EMF you could be reducing the negative affects of daily nnEMF exposure by as much as 70%. Many EHS people have told us this significantly improved their condition and they became less affected by the outside EMF When they went out.

From the info given the magnetic fields inside the incubators at 1.5 mG to 12.7 mG is very high. They don’t say what the 60Hz reading was on the skin. This could be a case where grounding the baby made a bad situation better. I expect they would have got better results by somehow reducing the magnetic field and grounding the incubators in such a way that they reduced/removed the 60Hz from being on the baby in the first place. Native Americans would dig a shallow pit and put babies in it to ground them (they had no 60hz). In Europe they put babies outside (away from 60Hz) in the sun every day. Modern science is just trying to catch up.

### Sue-UK — 2023-08-22T10:28:08+0300

Native Americans would dig a shallow pit and put babies in it to ground them (they had no 60hz). In Europe they put babies outside (away from 60Hz) in the sun every day. Modern science is just trying to catch up.

I've always felt better using a grounding mat at night connected to the outside, knowing where there is no pipes or wiring out there, and with no 5G. Pre the 2020 global meltdown after a long weekend in a 5G city and/or a flight, a night of grounded sleep made a huge difference to recovery and mitigation. (Finally able to go to the sun this coming winter for a UV top up. )

In Becker's book Cross Currents there is a discussion on the earth's rotation through the magnetosphere, and that any given spot on the earth's surface is in a constantly changing magnetic field. There's a diagram of the daily rise and fall in the strength of the magnetic field at one spot on the earth during a quiet period of solar activity. On the pineal, after acknowledging the retinal pathway, he says

_"It appears that, over 2 billion years of evolution, living things have taken advantage of the two portions of the electromagnetic spectrum that could be depended upon to always be present: the geomagnetic field and visible light. In this view, it is no more surprising that life developed specific organs to sense the geomagnetic field and to derive timing information from it than that it developed specific organs to sense and derive information from light."

"More recently, it has been shown that the pineal is also sensitive to the daily cyclic pattern in the Earth's magnetic field. **Melatonin secretion in human subjects may be changed at will by exposure to steady magnetic fields of the same strength as the geomagnetic field.** Apparently, nature determined that biological cycle activity was too important to be left to one environmental signal alone. "

"Nature intended the pineal to simultaneously receive the same signals from the daily pattern of day-night and the same rise and fall in strength of the geomagnetic field. Obviously, when one or both signals are abnormal, the pineal does not respond in the normal fashion, and the body's biological cycles become disturbed - with important clinical consequences."_

Figure 2 of the paper is interesting. If grounding has this vagal tone effect on vulnerable babies under hi tech conditions without removing the 60Hz exposure, then theoretically turning off the breakers to a bedroom at night, and using a grounding mat with an outside rod in a safe outside space (context as usual) could gain signalling benefits from the geomagnetic field when light is absent, as well as electrons. Doubt and fear changes the results of any experiment I do. Even after reading research on grounding, I've always preferred not using the sockets, but its more of a left brain/right brain thing. Intuitively the outside rod is the better option for my n=1, and by checking where its put in the outside environment, my logical brain gets out of the way.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-22T20:16:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.28023/post-325424>

Most of us go by our feelings when it comes to grounding, EMF mitigation etc. I am reminded of a lab experiment that was done with rodents. Two groups exposed to the same EMF, one group had a so called EMF “neutralizing“ devise in with them, the other didn’t. The group with the “neutralizer” ran around all day ”feeling good”. The second group stayed as far away from the source of the EMF as they could (feeling not so good?). The group that “felt good” and ran around ignoring the EMF (because the short term affects were covered?) lived half as long as the group that avoided the EMF. The affects of nnEMF on the body are the affects of nnEMF no matter how we feel. What nnEMF is doing at a cellular level in the background may take years to manifest despite an apparent short term benefit. Covering up nnEMF is never as good as removing it or being removed from it. Reduce nnEMF exposure and spend as much time in Nature away from it as possible. More research needs to be done on the short term and long term physical affects of Earthing/Grounding while in a 60Hz AC electric field beyond how people feel.

### Sue-UK — 2023-08-24T10:45:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.28023/post-325518>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=325424)

Two groups exposed to the same EMF, one group had a so called EMF “neutralizing“ devise in with them, the other didn’t. The group with the “neutralizer” ran around all day ”feeling good”. The second group stayed as far away from the source of the EMF as they could (feeling not so good?). The group that “felt good” and ran around ignoring the EMF (because the short term affects were covered?) lived half as long as the group that avoided the EMF. The affects of nnEMF on the body are the affects of nnEMF no matter how we feel. What nnEMF is doing at a cellular level in the background may take years to manifest despite an apparent short term benefit. Covering up nnEMF is never as good as removing it or being removed from it. Reduce nnEMF exposure and spend as much time in Nature away from it as possible. More research needs to be done on the short term and long term physical affects of Earthing/Grounding while in a 60Hz AC electric field beyond how people feel.

Nocturnal animals in the wild would be reliant on the geomagnetic field. If the neutraliser in the rodent study wasn’t grounding them to the geomagnetic field (in darkness, not running around under any light frequency they would normally sleep through) the study isn’t comparable to humans. If the (diurnal) human baby study had measurable effects - sure they would be better off if they were well without all the tech, but finding something to support their progress without risking the removal of lifesaving monitoring or oxygen delivering equipment etc- I'm curious as to how it did what it did.

Although nnEMF is bad, the question I have is, were the effects seen in the baby study because whatever nnEMF was present, grounding at the same time was better than nnEMF without grounding, (although avoiding nnEMF and being grounded would obviously be better?)

There's a lot about why grounding is good or bad depending on the viewpoint. But I haven't seen any studies looking at long term blocking of the native electromagnetic field at night on vagal tone, UV signalling, repair and regeneration, or sleep cycles including REM, with or without nnEMF, so what Becker said may be important.

_"Nature intended the pineal to simultaneously receive the same signals from the daily pattern of day-night and the same rise and fall in strength of the geomagnetic field. Obviously, **when one or both signals are abnormal,** the pineal does not respond in the normal fashion, and the body's biological cycles become disturbed - with important clinical consequences."_

Although what nnEMF is doing at a cellular level in the background may take years to manifest despite any short term benefits "felt" by grounding, by the same token it may take years to manifest what _not_ experiencing the _natural_ EMF is doing to us, particularly during sleep. Blocking nnEMF tied to an _absent_ geomagnetic field signal at night when its supposed to be the dominant signal to the biological cycles (12 hours at the equator, swinging between 8 and 16 at my latitude) doesn't seem anywhere close to optimal. Theoretically I could sleep like a log for 8 hours with no nnEMF but not regenerate, repair or enter the sleep cycles properly, and wouldn't know it until disease hits me.

Easier said than done for many I know, but if my environment was so bad I was considering faraday options or too frightened to put a grounding rod in an outside space and switch off the breaker at night I would move at the first opportunity, however "clean" from nnEMF I got my sleeping environment.

### JanSz — 2023-08-24T21:21:09+0300

> We are so far from nature with our modern lifestyles and technology

For quite some time I thought of myself as growing up in the really old fashion way, close to the way nature intended.

80+ years ago, my parents had a small farm far away from nowhere.
We had electricity. 2 or 3 40W bulbs.
During harvest 4 or 5 HP electric motor was running for 2-3 days.
Sometime latter my dad got radio.
Looks like a perfect picture except
rye and wheat were milled to get white flower
that was the only sins I was thinking about
when recently (finally) it dawned on me that
we had oil from rapeseed

In the high school and college, I was fed with margarine from their restaurants.

So, it is sick, but really there is practically no way to escape Linoleic acids.

[@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/)

I could use evaluation.
which one is more detrimental

nnEMF from couple of 40W incandescent bulbs or LA that when measured is 20 or 30 times greater from desirable?

////

Jan, I think we can all look back from our perspective and knowledge today and see our faults in the past. It is not an either/or Issue, then or now. Maybe back then the nnEMF from a couple of 40W bulbs did more harm (being a new thing) than far more exposure today, who knows? What difference does it make which was/is worse? The more we can clean up our act, in as many areas as possible, the better off we will be.

As in medicine, where people tend to focus on one area/condition and not realize that when treating one thing harm is caused elsewhere, the same with nnEMF mitigation. So many are focused on mitigating “DE” or RF and make the 60Hz AC fields worse in the process. The very 60Hz AC fields that are, for many, a far greater impact on health.

Which is more detrimental? I believe a holistic approach is best. Get as much natural light and little artificial light as possible. Eat as organic and close to nature as possible. Cut the power whenever you can, especially at night, and reduce as much in home originating nnEMF as possible. I believe the affects of both the good and bad are cumulative. Let’s get much more of the good and far less of the bad.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-28T22:24:02+0300

> [Kathirf said:](/goto/post?id=325650)

> Hi Anthony, have you heard of the Soma Veda device? If so what are your thoughts.

I had not heard of the Soma Veda device so I looked it up. It comes with a USB cord so I guess it is a powered device using some kind of crystal to put out a field. The claim is this field neutralizes the negative affect of 5G, 4G, WiFi, other nnEMF etc. have on the body at a cellular level.

So I ask you, with one of these devices do your cell phone, WiFi, coffee pot, lights still work? If they do then you still have electric fields, magnetic field and radio frequency throughout your space. The nnEMF has not been affected in ANYWAY either in strength or frequency. The nnEMF is still there, your phone and computer still work. Does this device stop, neutralize, reduce the harmful effects of the nnEMF on the body? I don’t know, but I do know the nnEMF that was there before is still there unchanged.

If I have taken some poison the person with the antidote is my friend, BUT if this person says don’t worry about poison, just keep taking it, I have all the antidote you need, they are not my friend. A friend would tell me to avoid poison, to cut off, remove, reduce the nnEMF I am exposed to. Having done that if you want to spend an extra $1,000 on some “woo” that may or may not do anything in the long run, go for it.

I wonder what the mechanism of action is on this procedure....

"Ectasia and keratoconus can now be treated with a procedure known as corneal cross-linking. The cornea is saturated with Riboflavin and then exposed to UV light which causes the fibers of the cornea to bond together, strenghtening the cornea and halting progression of this disease."

# Thread: August first incandescent light is BANNED from being sold in the USA...

### NikkiYork — 2023-07-27T20:42:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/august-first-incandescent-light-is-banned-from-being-sold-in-the-usa.28025/post-324369>

i learned that yesterday. Maybe i live under a rock, but when did this come about?
Anyways, I listened to Dr. Alexander Wunsch, who was recommended by Dr. Jack as a German doctor. Apparently in the EU you can't get any incandescent lighting for years. And while no artificial light is better than any, it is acording to Dr. Wunsch a lot better than LED.

You mke from it whatever you like, I just bought the last four bulbs at my supermarket. Who knows, maybe it will be a hot commodity among smart people one day.
P.S.: for the sake of the environment, I have not used incandescent lighting for 30 years now. None. Those are my first bulbs in 30 years. Never stop learning!!

### Anthony Smith — 2023-07-30T20:35:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/august-first-incandescent-light-is-banned-from-being-sold-in-the-usa.28025/post-324491>

On Monday night I think we should all light a candle in remembrance of the incandescent bulb.

Much has been written about bulbs and light. Incandescent are inefficient from a lighting point of view because 90% +/- of their energy consumption is heat (not light). That heat though is IR “light”, VERY beneficial in our nnEMF lit inside world.

I’m leaning toward #1 bed at sunset. #2 candles. #3 red lights. #4 In the winter when I need to see, 12V DC bulbs where I can (little to no flicker) or no flicker, full spectrum LED‘s (grow, aquarium, reptile lights) with a red and/or NIR/UV light in the same room.

Of course this ban is for energy saving reasons because electric stoves, heat, A/C and car chargers are all so efficient . . .

# Thread: Been to the dermatologist today... he had listened to the Huberman podcast, and guess what he told me :)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/been-to-the-dermatologist-today-he-had-listened-to-the-huberman-podcast-and-guess-what-he-told-me.28026/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### NikkiYork — 2023-07-27T21:15:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/been-to-the-dermatologist-today-he-had-listened-to-the-huberman-podcast-and-guess-what-he-told-me.28026/post-324371>

I love my dermatologist, but I am more educated than him, which is ok. I am more educated than the vast amount of people i meet in my life. They don't know it, because I'm only a hairdresser living in a mobile home park, which is hilarious to me.

Dermatologist: i love the confidence with which you are presenting me this serious tan.

me; you know, Dr. Jack Kruse, my husband told you about him a months ago.

D: ah, i forgot, tell him thank you, that was great. I listened to the whole thing and i don't have that sort of time. Yeah, Kruse is a genius. It was so entertaining. but, you know, he has great ideas, and... he is wrong in so many parts.

Me: oh, how so?

D: he needs to quantify the amounts of sun he wants people to be in, like a half hour. You are too tan with your haircolor and eyes.

Me; you mean because i am fitzpatrick 2?

D: what do you know about Fitzpatrick (like how does the dum dum know a word)?

me: well you are fitzpatrick 1. (touche, he still hates me now)

D (offended): i'm not. i'm a 2, 1 is almost albino.

Anyways, we talked, and whenever i wanted to explain something, he said, Yeah, i didn't undersand that part, he needs to prove it.

Right there you have the answer to his advice: Dermatologist is set on his believes, likes to be entertained by new ideas, doesn't want to dig deeper, doesn't want to do the work, aka, is happily raking in the cash the way it is.
He told me, that if i keep having a tan like i do, i would get skin cancer with 99,9 percent. Thanks for the spell, doctor, now my subconscious has to work on unhearing that. But that's all right. He means well, he's hooked to the money like they all are. If saying the opposite makes him money next year, that's what he is going to say.

### NikkiYork — 2023-08-06T20:31:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/been-to-the-dermatologist-today-he-had-listened-to-the-huberman-podcast-and-guess-what-he-told-me.28026/post-324765>

I'm not worried about skin cancer at all. There is no, if any evidence, that sun light causes skin cancer. How would the scientists even know?

What do the studies look like? Did they fry up some mice to conclude it? Did skin cancer people pass in light diaries? I don't think so.

Isaac Newton came up with the color indigo, that doesn't even exist on the visual spectrum, just to make it 7 colors like the days of the week, when there were only six colors visible. Essentially he used science to make nature fit according to his will. So he basically lied to make things go his way.

Nature does not go the human's way though. That hubris will bite people in their asses, always.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/been-to-the-dermatologist-today-he-had-listened-to-the-huberman-podcast-and-guess-what-he-told-me.28026/post-325414>

It is challenging?

Well, you are basically saying it’s challenging not to be a liar, it’s challenging to not make money you deserve to make, because it is withheld from you, if you don’t want to play by bad people’s rules. It is challenging to act ethically.

So what? If there are people who can do it, then it can’t be that challenging.

It’s challenging to get threats, and some people get threats not only to their livelihood, but also regarding their lives.

If you have family and you want to protect your family, that doesn’t have anything to do with your personal endeavors, then that is more challenging than being cancelled and it happens all the time.

What is challenging? Selling out? That’s not challenging.

An important point of my post is how little it takes before you are discredited. In our age, almost no deviation from the "the science is settled" straitjacket is allowed. You will be punished immediately. There is no room for debate or for critical examination and skeptical processes around "science". This is a challenge for everyone who works with research today, not just for Huberman. They risk losing titles, prizes, funding, jobs, publishing opportunities etc, etc... tragic.

I threaten them in one way or another, but if everything I say is nonsense, why do they feel threatened?
>
> There are many questions that come to mind after lifting the veil.

I migrated from Germany to the US and it became so very apparent how all of us, even the most independently thinking are influenced by - not only the media, but our cultures, our environments, obviously the press that comes with it, the hysterias, the denials…. It’s a lot more than just the propaganda machines.

My idea of decentralization is that a network of ideas, thought, people, energies for the lack of a better term will connect to build something that is stronger than the decaying old structure. For me that is more a societal change than financial or medical, which is obviously part of it.

We can create a sister bridge before the old ones fall apart or get burned. Alas, we need to do it. It’s not a secret, but if it’s done non-chalantly and without much ado, I’m confident that it will eventually hold. To circle back to the original point: not even the so called progressive people want that progress and really it’s very few people pulling and pushing.

### annelies_1 — 2023-08-24T14:02:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/been-to-the-dermatologist-today-he-had-listened-to-the-huberman-podcast-and-guess-what-he-told-me.28026/post-325523>

> I migrated from Germany to the US and it became so very apparent how all of us, even the most independently thinking are influenced by - not only the media, but our cultures, our environments, obviously the press that comes with it, the hysterias, the denials…. It’s a lot more than just the propaganda machines.

> My idea of decentralization is that a network of ideas, thought, people, energies for the lack of a better term will connect to build something that is stronger than the decaying old structure. For me that is more a societal change than financial or medical, which is obviously part of it.

It’s not a secret, but if it’s done non-chalantly and without much ado, I’m confident that it will eventually hold. To circle back to the original point: not even the so called progressive people want that progress and really it’s very few people pulling and pushing.

I agree.

I always feel a very strong tendency to fight the old, even though I know in my heart that that is not necessary. It will eventually collapse on its own. I feel that the best thing to do is to focus on building something new, which is challenging because you cannot trust upon the guarantees that the old system pretends to give. Guarantees like, if you study law you can become a lawyer and then you'll have money and status and blablabla (nothing against lawyers btw  )

Most people will say that they want change, but they are so codependent and disempowered that when presented with an opportunity for change, most will stick to what's known, because they're scared. Centralised systems offer a framework to stick to what's known. Centralisation eventually becomes so abusive that the pain of staying in the system becomes greater than the perceived pain of change. I feel that that is when change happens.

It's indeed more than financial or medical. Agriculture is becoming more centralised, real estate is becoming more centralised, I'm sure there's more examples. But the thing is that people THINK centralised. They want to look at something outside of themselves to take on responsibility for them. I feel that that is the societal decay that needs healing.

### Jack Kruse — 2023-09-05T05:16:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/been-to-the-dermatologist-today-he-had-listened-to-the-huberman-podcast-and-guess-what-he-told-me.28026/post-325961>

The conclusion from the paper: "Sun exposure is associated with increased survival from melanoma."

### [Sun Exposure and Mortality From Melanoma](https://academic.oup.com/jnci/article/97/3/195/2544082)

Abstract. Background: Melanoma incidence and survival are positively associated, both geographically and temporally. Solar elastosis, a histologic indicato

 academic.oup.com

### [Sunscreen and Melanoma: Is Our Prevention Message Correct?](https://www.jabfm.org/content/24/6/735.long)

Many primary care providers advise patients to use sunscreen as a means to reduce their risk for skin cancer, especially cutaneous malignant melanoma (CMM). Despite the availability and promotion of sunscreen for decades, the incidence of CMM continues to increase in the U.S. at a rate of 3% per...

 www.jabfm.org

# Thread: Reported Biological Effects from Radiofrequency Radiation at Low-Intensity Exposure

**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Trippg — 2023-07-28T02:04:34+0300

[https://bioinitiative.org/wp-content/uploads/pdfs/BioInitiativeReport-RF-Color-Charts.pdf](https://bioinitiative.org/wp-content/uploads/pdfs/BioInitiativeReport-RF-Color-Charts.pdf)

### NikkiYork — 2023-07-28T02:17:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reported-biological-effects-from-radiofrequency-radiation-at-low-intensity-exposure.28029/post-324398>

Hm, cool list, but how does it work? Are the references studies? Is it self reported? What do I make from this?
Thanks for posting.

### Trippg — 2023-07-28T02:45:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reported-biological-effects-from-radiofrequency-radiation-at-low-intensity-exposure.28029/post-324401>

Its got the radiation "doses" on the left and the studies on the right.

Good for convincing the uneducated about effects of RF exposure.

For example - Apple Watch is 2-4 W/kg - [https://www.apple.com/legal/rfexposure/watch1,2/en/](https://www.apple.com/legal/rfexposure/watch1,2/en/)

If you look at the bottom of the PDF you can see that this exposure level accelerates cancers.

### NikkiYork — 2023-07-28T04:39:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reported-biological-effects-from-radiofrequency-radiation-at-low-intensity-exposure.28029/post-324407>

Wow, thanks for clarifying. I took my Fitbit off for good yesterday. I do miss it, but much less after reading this. Thanks again.

### Trippg — 2023-07-28T15:07:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reported-biological-effects-from-radiofrequency-radiation-at-low-intensity-exposure.28029/post-324418>

Great!

Remember nnEMF (non native EMF) and light are part of the same electromagnetic spectrum, so you can think about them in roughly the same way. If you think about your light diet, you should consider your nnEMF “diet” as well.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reported-biological-effects-from-radiofrequency-radiation-at-low-intensity-exposure.28029/post-357851>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/car-windows-tinted-or-clear.28033/post-324410>

So I've been pondering this and not sure what to think.

Clear car windows are still blocking UV, and a lot of the red, and tinted windows are going to be blocking even more red, and creating a more blue rich environment, but with considerably less bright light.

# Thread: Raised age spots
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raised-age-spots.28034/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TaylorF — 2023-07-29T23:05:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raised-age-spots.28034/post-324453>

My dad has a ton of raised age spots on his back that bother him. The dermo obviously told him it’s from the sun, which is why I turn to the proper crowd to help me with him why he should avoid wearing sunscreen and SPF/zinc.

Any studies or information on how the sun is not the main cause of raised age spots? Any info is helpful!

### JanSz — 2023-07-29T23:36:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raised-age-spots.28034/post-324456>

> [TaylorF said:](/goto/post?id=324453)
>
> My dad has a ton of raised age spots on his back that bother him. The dermo obviously told him it’s from the sun, which is why I turn to the proper crowd to help me with him why he should avoid wearing sunscreen and SPF/zinc.
>
> Any studies or information on how the sun is not the main cause of raised age spots? Any info is helpful!

You are using the word **"raised"**.
Usually, they are called Age spots, and are small, **flat** dark areas on the skin.
Or liver spots, or sunspots.
They vary in size and usually appear on areas exposed to the sun.
\-----------
Sun should not be avoided.
Sunscreen should be avoided.
Likely it may help if you would post (good picture) and describe why this bothers your dad?
Is that mental or physical, say itch that he must scratch.
\---------------
When intentionally seeking the sunlight,
still
there are times for sun.
and
times for shade.

Just observe animals.
..

Hi all, the man burns in 34 days, I am going in 20 days, completely off grid.

If anybody who doesn't know (much) about Burning Man wants to get an inside scoop, I shall gladly provide. Ask me anything, I have been going since 2010, my husband since 2004, and it's our son's 5th burn.

No, not everybody is naked and yes, many people don't do drugs
In my opinion it is an amazing playground and practical experimentation station for all sorts of living, decentralized living being a huge one of it.

The amounts of human energy in their most intricate form and shape over there is stunning and overwhelming, and the sunrises are the most unique on the planet, since the Black Rock Desert part named "playa" consists of white alcaline dust from a dried out lake, that reflects the light in colors unlike anywhere else. Photos can't capture.

\>> waiting for the sunrise

# Thread: Windows "nightmode" bluelight block
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/windows-nightmode-bluelight-block.28036/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lindman112 — 2023-07-30T19:36:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/windows-nightmode-bluelight-block.28036/post-324488>

I'm not subscribed to any of the apps that are downloadable, im just using the natural windows function.

Does the normal "nightmode" function on screens actually help? I always have it turned to max. My screen is bright red and I can hardly see but it feels very relaxing on the eyes.

Any expert on windows computers can chime in? I feel like it helps but it might just be the placebo effect.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-07-30T21:52:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/windows-nightmode-bluelight-block.28036/post-324494>

Does your screen have a backlight? If so, no software filter will eliminate all the blue. Try viewing the screen with/without blue blockers as an easy test. If the screen filter is doing its job, you should not be able to tell a diff with/without the glasses.

I prefer to use F.lux

It's simple to download and set up and works really good.

### coho3131 — 2023-07-31T03:53:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/windows-nightmode-bluelight-block.28036/post-324503>

I tried F.lux. I like Iris even better. PWM Flicker is a factor also; I am not sure if F.lux helps with this now, but when I tried it years ago it did not.

### coho3131 — 2023-07-31T03:55:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/windows-nightmode-bluelight-block.28036/post-324504>

Brightness is also harmful to our circadian rhythm.

### Lindman112 — 2023-07-31T17:21:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/windows-nightmode-bluelight-block.28036/post-324532>

Thanks guys, I have blue light blocker glasses so i'll try that out.

Yeah, im having trouble sleeping right now in scandinavia and staring at the dark ceiling just makes my head race around so I thought i'd try staying up one night with blue light blockers + nightmode on my screen and get some work done and recover tomorrow.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-08-02T15:19:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/windows-nightmode-bluelight-block.28036/post-324614>

> [Lindman112 said:](/goto/post?id=324532)
>
> thought i'd try staying up one night with blue light blockers + nightmode on my screen and get some work done and recover tomorrow.

I wouldn't. Embrace darkness after sunset as much as possible.

Im curious, what is the length of your day in Scandinavia these days?

### Lindman112 — 2023-08-08T23:42:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/windows-nightmode-bluelight-block.28036/post-324857>

> [Joseph LeBlanc said:](/goto/post?id=324614)
>
> I wouldn't. Embrace darkness after sunset as much as possible.
>
> Im curious, what is the length of your day in Scandinavia these days?

Sorry for late response, yes the scandinavian day length is getting better now, from may to july it's been like 3 AM to 9 PM, then the sun "sets" but it doesn't really go dark, it's just semi-dark from 9 PM to 3 AM and it starts over again, now it rises around 4 AM and we actually have some darkness around 11 PM - 05 AM in august.

I've got my windows covered with pitch black sheets to block out most of the lights (Not only from the sun but also all the annoying street lights outside my apartment).

I try to sleep around 10 PM while it's still fairly bright but it's a "bit" dark around 2 AM before the sun rises and then back at it again.

I used to think the long winters was hard but my new-found obsession with darkness and sunlight has taken it to a whole new level.

In a month or two it will be the opposite, just darkness all day around.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-08-11T00:41:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/windows-nightmode-bluelight-block.28036/post-324941>

I honestly wouldn't even know what protocol to follow in that environment. That sounds tricky. My inclination would be to listen to your body for the proper bedtime and wake time, assuming as you are getting adequate time outdoors. I would also be CTing like crazy if I lived that far north.

I've focused only on the light pillar. Haven't even attempted bringing water and mag to them yet. I like to think I do a pretty good job explaining the concepts of light to them, but they just don't believe it, or don't care.

I started out just trying to explain everything. Now I have backed off a bit and am trying more indirect "lead by example" approach. Problem is I've never had major health probs, so they aren't going to physically see a big change in me through this approach.

I know I ultimately cannot make others understand or care about these topics, but I feel like I need to continue trying. It pains me to continue watching the ppl closest to me go on living like they do and continue falling apart healthwise. I am wondering if anyone else shares in this struggle and if there are any tips on different approaches/mindsets to this conundrum.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-07-31T04:23:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bringing-kruseology-to-friends-family.28038/post-324505>

Joseph, there is a good chance the people you know, especially family will not listen. Not personal, just a fact of life and as other have said here we each come to this in our own way and time. They will hear it better from someone else. The lucky amongst us didn’t have to “hit the wall” before paying attention but many did. Share what you know then shut up, be an example and wait for questions. Copy and share this if you want (or not).

My name is Anthony Smith, I am a professional EMF consultant and I talk to people all day about non-native ElectroMagnetic Fields (nnEMF) and the affects they are having on their health and how to mitigate them in the home. Some already get it but even they have family that don’t and it helps for their healing journey if they do. Here is what I share with them to help get their family on board.

Our bodies are electrical (think EEG, EKG) and they run on DC voltage, nature runs on DC voltage. Our modern world largely runs on AC voltage. We are surrounded by 60Hz AC electric fields (a Body Voltage Kit will physically show these) and 60Hz electric lights (vibrating 60x per second) as well as an assortment of nnEMF radio frequencies (RF), TV, mobile phone, micro waves etc. all going thru us, all the time.

During the day our brains mostly operate between 16Hz to 100Hz +/- (Beta & Gamma). At night our brains need to go thru a reset, to relax and drop down to 8Hz to 15Hz (Alpha) and then to sleep, deep sleep, heal & detox drop even further to 1Hz to 7Hz (Delta & Theta). In the evening the signal from nature to shut down is the light changing (sunset) and the natural EMF (from the sun) going away. So if in the evening we sit in 60Hz AC electric fields and look at artificial lights (the color spectrum of midday sun light) and then lay in 60Hz AC electric fields (from the wires in our walls) all night, our brains cannot relax and reset and our immune system cannot operate optimally because all the necessary body systems THAT ARE CHARGED BY BEING IN SUN LIGHT DURING THE DAY are not properly activated. All the signals are wrong and melatonin is suppressed.

Natures signal to get us going again in the morning is the light frequencies of sunrise. These also charge the batteries in our cells (mitochondria) to give us energy. The more full spectrum natural sun light we get during the day, to charge up all our systems and the less artificial light and nnEMF we get during the evening/night the more efficient our body/brain/system will work for us. Simple biology/physics/science.

It was many years ago that I had told people about the toxicity of sunscreens. Recently some of those same people are telling me that they have "heard" that sunscreens are toxic, and that they stopped using them. Maybe they were finally convinced because of the seed I planted years ago.

Frequency of pink light
The **color pink**, is not a part of **spectrum**, it does not have a particular frequency.
It's an extra-spectral color, and it has to be mixed to generate it
\===============

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-dangerous-pink-light-and-or-pink-filters-close-to-red-light.24376/post-280784](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-dangerous-pink-light-and-or-pink-filters-close-to-red-light.24376/post-280784)

I think one of the conclusions was that pinklight does not have particular frequency.
It is created in the brain.

### Cadoia — 2023-08-01T18:05:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-glasses-sold-in-the-world-that-lets-in-uv-light.28039/post-324575>

So found this online:
"Without any specific technology or coatings designed to block UV light, standard regular soft contact lenses typically offer limited UV protection. The percentage of UV light transmission through these lenses can vary depending on the lens material and its specific characteristics.

On average, it is estimated that regular soft contact lenses may block or absorb around 10% to 30% of UVB rays and 3% to 15% of UVA rays. This means that a significant portion of UV radiation may still reach the eyes when wearing standard soft contact lenses without additional UV-blocking features."

It makes me consider maybe using contact lenses instead of glasses as the block a lot more UV.
I just dont like the idea of putting this over my retina as it lowers oxygen flow to the tissues I would assume.
But I dislike the idea of having "window glass" covering my eyes all day just so I can see, even more.

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=324577)
>
> Wonder if you came across similar evaluation for my lenses.

I am assuming you mean you had an operation and they put lenses inside your eye correct?
Its made of what?

The amount of UV light blocked by intraocular lens (IOL) materials can vary depending on the specific type of material used. Generally, IOLs made from acrylic or silicone materials are designed to block a significant portion of harmful ultraviolet (UV) radiation to protect the eye's structures.
The UV-blocking capability of IOLs is typically measured by their UV transmittance percentage. For example:

1.  Acrylic IOLs: These IOLs often have UV transmittance values of around 10% or less, meaning they block at least 90% of harmful UV rays.
2.  Silicone IOLs: Silicone IOLs usually have UV transmittance values of about 5% or less, blocking at least 95% of harmful UV rays.

Considering in John Ott's own case he went into the sun all day every day in Florida with little to no effect on his arthritis and only by accident broke his glasses only to discover having the eyes exposed to sunlight directly healed his issue right away, I would say having UV blocking lenses operated into ones eyes is a bit of an issue.
This is why I started this thread because I wear glasses 98% of my time as I have very bad vision. Was born with it. So my story is: how to get glasses that transmit UV. Or buy "cheap" unprotected low tech contact lenses that will let the UV in.
But if you got one of these above mentioned materials firmly operated in your eyes its a whole different situation. Im no expert. Maybe what you got is different?

**UV Acrylic: Acrylic Sheet | Plastic Stockist**

Plastic Stockist offers **Sunbed grade acrylic sheet** in 2mm, 3mm and 5mm material thickness. Sunbed acrylic panels are for applications requiring transparency to UV rays and these acrylic sheets are produced without UV filters.

**Common uses for Sunbed Grade UV Acrylic Sheet

Sunbed grade UV acrylic sheet** is often used in sunbeds and acrylic tanks for reptiles that requires UV rays. These UV sheets are for internal applications.

### Cadoia — 2023-08-03T18:19:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-glasses-sold-in-the-world-that-lets-in-uv-light.28039/post-324658>

According to the web search results, contact lenses without UV filter will let in most of the UV radiation that reaches the eye, depending on their thickness and material. One study measured the UV and visible light transmittance of different soft contact lenses with and without UV filter, and found that contact lenses without UV filter had an average UV-A transmittance of 87.7% and an average UV-B transmittance of 88.5%.
Another study found similar results, with an average UV-A transmittance of 86.6% and an average UV-B transmittance of 87.8% for contact lenses without UV filter.
These studies also showed that contact lenses with a positive back vertex power (BVP) (+1.00 to +6.00 D) had slightly lower UV transmittance than contact lenses with a negative BVP (−1.00 to −12.00 D), because they were thicker in the center.

So it looks to me if one has reduced eye sight and need glasses or contact lenses the contact lenses is the better option.

The data for the UV transmittance of contact lenses at the 380 to 400 nm range is not very abundant, but I found some information from the web search results that might be helpful. One study that measured the UV and visible transmittance of soft contact lenses with and without UV filter reported the average UV transmittance for different brands and powers in the range of 280 to 380 nm, but not beyond that.
However, another source from ZEISS Vision Care stated that the gap between 380 and 400 nm accounts for 40% of total UV exposure at sea level, and that the most common lens material sold globally typically only fully blocks UV below 360 nm.
This implies that contact lenses without UV filter would let in a significant amount of UV radiation in the 380 to 400 nm range, unless they are made of a special material that blocks more UV.

### coho3131 — 2023-08-05T03:05:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-glasses-sold-in-the-world-that-lets-in-uv-light.28039/post-324712>

#1 Sunlight --> Contact Lens Filtering and Refraction --> nnEMF --> Retina ( No thank you )

#2 Corneal Hypoxia is a huge issue.

#3 Eyeglasses, especially small ones, allow natural sunlight to enter the eye in the regions not directly blocked by the lens.

#4 You can easily remove your eyeglasses outdoors when sitting or walking in a safe area.

#5 I would use "data and information" that is supplied by manufacturers, distributors, and merchants with caution.

\[ There might be some safety and performance advantages to wearing contact lenses for sports or other activities, but I would consider the risks versus the specific benefits in those situations. Competitive swimming gets mentioned as one of those activities, but there are now plenty of very affordable options for swim goggles for near-sighted or far-sighted people. Reggie Jackson, Kareem Abdul-Jabbar, Eric Dickerson, Bob Griese, and Billie Jean King wore eyeglasses while destroying their competition. \]

### Cadoia — 2023-08-06T04:07:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-glasses-sold-in-the-world-that-lets-in-uv-light.28039/post-324754>

> [coho3131 said:](/goto/post?id=324712)
>
> #2 Corneal Hypoxia is a huge issue.

According to the sources I found, the amount of oxygen that reaches the cornea depends on several factors, such as the type, material, fit, and wear time of the contact lenses. Some types of contact lenses are more breathable than others, meaning they allow more oxygen to pass through them. For example, gas-permeable hard contact lenses are more breathable than soft contact lenses, and silicone hydrogel contact lenses are more breathable than hydrogel contact lenses. The fit of the contact lenses also affects the oxygen supply to the cornea, as a tight-fitting lens can reduce the tear flow and create a stagnant layer of fluid under the lens. The wear time of the contact lenses is another important factor, as wearing them for too long or overnight can deprive the cornea of oxygen and increase the risk of hypoxia

One study measured the oxygen transmissibility (Dk/t) of different types of contact lenses and found that gas-permeable hard contact lenses had a Dk/t of 100 or higher, silicone hydrogel contact lenses had a Dk/t of 80 to 140, and hydrogel contact lenses had a Dk/t of 10 to 30. The Dk/t is a measure of how much oxygen can pass through a lens of a certain thickness. The higher the Dk/t, the more breathable the lens is. The study also estimated that the minimum Dk/t required to avoid corneal hypoxia is 24 for daily wear and 87 for extended wear. Therefore, gas-permeable hard contact lenses and silicone hydrogel contact lenses are more suitable for preventing corneal hypoxia than hydrogel contact lenses.

Now the question remains which kind of lenses blocks the least UV and are they the same that allow oxygen flow to the cornea?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-glasses-sold-in-the-world-that-lets-in-uv-light.28039/post-324865>

> [Cadoia said:](/goto/post?id=324575)
>
> So found this online:
> "Without any specific technology or coatings designed to block UV light, standard regular soft contact lenses typically offer limited UV protection. The percentage of UV light transmission through these lenses can vary depending on the lens material and its specific characteristics.
>
> On average, it is estimated that regular soft contact lenses may block or absorb around 10% to 30% of UVB rays and 3% to 15% of UVA rays. This means that a significant portion of UV radiation may still reach the eyes when wearing standard soft contact lenses without additional UV-blocking features."
>
> It makes me consider maybe using contact lenses instead of glasses as the block a lot more UV.
> I just dont like the idea of putting this over my retina as it lowers oxygen flow to the tissues I would assume.
> But I dislike the idea of having "window glass" covering my eyes all day just so I can see, even more.

Can you recommend any Contact lens brand to buy that does NOT block UV light? Or have you found any?
My son in law wears contacts and works in front of a computer for work. I'm giving him info from Dr Kruse to keep his eyes healthy.
I like your idea of wearing the soft contacts versus the glasses to get more UV around the eye. Makes sense.
Thank you.

> [NikkiYork said:](/goto/post?id=324621)

> Well, plastic by nature is UV blocking. The cheap 1 dollar glasses don’t say uv400 without a reason, respectively because somebody made the effort to put UV protection into that plastic. That’s what is does the way it comes.

> So if a vendor says “no UV protection added” that means nada.

How to see better. I've worn eyeglasses since 30 years old and am 72 now. I just let the stores sell me and I had no knowledge about materials, filter or blocking blue light etc.

I use glasses at the computer and to read and require to drive..
I have been using a very old progressive pair 3 or 4 prescriptions behind for many years.

**I should avoid UV** **coatings** **on** **prescription** **clear** **lenses** ... Right ?
I've been to 4 eyeglass vendors and none of them understand the concept of 'healthy' choices.
Visionworks and others are suggesting Polycarbonate **lenses?**
I have read that Polycarbonate lenses themselves block **UV** rays ?

**SO What material to use for the lenses ?** If Polycarbonate and Plastic block UV and I can hardly get any shop to consider glass and glass is so very heavy and unsafe...right?
They are saying UV causes cataracts and I my cataracts were mild until I started getting more sunlight. Just like they said would happen. I have cataracts in both eyes but my left eye is much worse?

**So many daily struggles trying to see best healthy options...

Any help appreciated.

Martha Ray**

### doctorwu85 — 2024-09-24T20:17:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-glasses-sold-in-the-world-that-lets-in-uv-light.28039/post-339969>

I've used prescription VivaRays for about a year and have been very happy with them. The basic eyeglasses let in about 60% of UV as others have pointed out, and they include three magnetic attachments with increasing levels of blue light blocking. If I'm doing sun and grounding I typically take them off, but if you're doing something outside that requires perfect vision they're the best I've found.

For the magnetic clip-ons, I wear the light amber ones at work/in front of the computer, the full amber ones after sundown, and the red ones about an hour before sleep (or if exposed to extreme bright LED light at night). I've noticed that my eyes have become much more sensitive to blue light, to the point where the fluorescent lights in the main office actually hurt my eyes if I don't wear one of the sets of lenses. I assume being more sensitized to blue light is probably a beneficial thing.

Golden Glo daytime lenses block harmful short wave 430-450nm dark blue light from screens that contributes to digital eye strain, fatigue, and other negative health outcomes, while selectively allowing beneficial **Violet** and **Cyan** light to stimulate the **Opsins**, supporting focus, mental energy, and a healthy circadian rhythm.

Unlike traditional blue light blockers, **Golden Glo** lenses preserve the healthy light your body needs for daytime alertness, energy, and natural skin health. 400-420nm violet light is known to promote cellular activity that enhances your skin’s resilience, allowing you to stay focused while subtly contributing to a healthier appearance.
