# Thread: The effect of nnEMF on brain waves
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-effect-of-nnemf-on-brain-waves.28040/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-01T04:41:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-effect-of-nnemf-on-brain-waves.28040/post-324556>

I am so not qualified to discuss this in detail (there is a Neurosurgeon in the room), all I know is what I have read, the brain wants to (need to) reset each night which means down regulating first to Rest/Alpha wave (8Hz to 12Hz) then to Sleep/Theta (4Hz to 7Hz) and then to Deep Dive/Delta (.5Hz - 4Hz.) The Earths natural background frequency is 7.83Hz - Schumann Resonance (this can and does jump around).

The in home AC electric field in USA & Canada resonates at 60Hz (50Hz in other countries). In many of our bedrooms, before mitigation, this field is 1500 - 3000 Milli-Volts). It stands to reason that it is difficult for our brains to find the whisper or Schumann (~300 microVolts) in this 60Hz ”Rock Concert”. There are 1,000 micro Volts in 1 milli-Volt - 3,000 milli-Volts is 10,000x the “volume” of 300 micro-Volts.

I say turn off, or at least turn down, the 60Hz and give peace (Schumann) a chance.

Add to this the very presents of nnEMF makes the brain (Pineal Glad) think it is still day time. Back in the day when the “Big Ball” went bye bye there was no more EMF which is one of the signals to release the melatonin. Any nnEMF interferes with the natural process. Add to that the 60Hz (50Hz) frequency and you have a double whammy.

### NikkiYork — 2023-08-01T05:29:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-effect-of-nnemf-on-brain-waves.28040/post-324558>

Thank you so much, Anthony.

I think some people live awake in alpha a good amount of their day, calmly. And sleep? it would be interesting to refine it, or define it. Theta is usually unconscious, but it doesn’t have to be.

It never occurred to me that an electromagnetic field of the type bedroom electricity could have an impact on my brain waves. Hm. I need to reassess.

On a cellular, respectively mitochondrial level that is different, as I can see the sensitivity of those little guys. And that would obviously also count for the brain tissue mitochondria. But are those in charge of forming what we call brain waves? I don’t know.

I do know that when I started reading about the brain and its uses and functions, I was 8 and I tricked my brain into learning stuff over night, BUT, mostly all that was written about how our brain works that I could find in the times before we had an internet was bs. I read so much, and it was useless information.

It still is probably a ton of bs, because scientists are not as great as most of them believe that they are and unfortunately they clog up the pathways for people like Dr. Jack or anybody who wants to speed past them.

That said, we are on our own. And experimenting on humans has its healthy limitations. Training Gama is a thing.

I talked to the people from Dave Asprey who have brain wave modulation pods or whatever they call it. Unpleasant.

It’s unbelievable, that they charge like 20k for training and adjusting brainwave patterns according to a mishmash of what they figured out successful people are displaying.

I’m not saying it cant work. And I believe in biofeedback, of any sort… like a good tan, that’s good biofeedback

So ok, Schuhman, that’s grounding, and the frequency is 7hz -8hz. So wait, would we sync up with 7hz brainwaves sleeping? No.
Sleep is lower, much lower.

I don’t understand the Milli Volt part, you are talking about.
I get that the electro noise is unfortunate and omni-present.

Do I need a certain voltage in my mitochondria? I am not getting this together.

Thanks again, I find this so interesting.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-01T08:47:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-effect-of-nnemf-on-brain-waves.28040/post-324563>

There is awake, conscious (to various degrees), then there is relaxed, prayerful, meditative, then there is sleep (dream state), then there is deep dive sleep (dreamless, healing). Obviously there are overlaps and areas that a disciplined mind can access and take advantage of.

The Earths natural frequency is what we call Schumann 7.83Hz +/- and the brains natural reset is also 7.83Hz. There are various states of consciousness all of which have a purpose. As Nicola Tesla said: “If you want to find the secret of the Universe, think in terms of energy, frequency and vibration.”

I’ve done biofeedback and found it interesting and useful. I’ve also done prayer and meditation and would lean in that direction. Taping into the natural process seams . . . more natural and I get that is where Jack is coming from also.

Milli-Volts vs Micro-Volts is just a way to measure the strength of the signal/field (power). People rant and rave about “something” that is measured in micro-Volts (not that it is not important) BUT ignore something else (saying it doesn’t matter) that is 1,000x or 10,000x stronger. Context and perspective are vital.

But here goes:

This is a house opportunity some of you might find interesting. why?

Location: Right at the equator in Ecuador. In a town called vilcabamba. at 1400 meters altitude in the Andes mountains.
I build it with biohacking in mind. Plenty of UV light.
Known as the valley of longevity. Try do some investigation on this place. Fresh water. Fresh air. organic food. cows grazing outside etc.
lots of foreigners live here.
Place has Ice bath. Private sun bathing area. Sauna and much more.
Electric has been wired by building biology standard. there is 0 dirty electricity. All wired with ethernet cables in every room. no wifi. fiber optics. all organic building materials.
Perfect view with great sunrise and sunsets. lots of space for growing your own food garden.
You can see the place here: [https://www.abecuador.com/beautifulhealth-mindedhome](https://www.abecuador.com/beautifulhealth-mindedhome)
Contact me directly through here. Just PM me.
I thought someone here might be looking to relocate and this could be an option.

### NikkiYork — 2023-08-02T04:51:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-something-spiritual-to-light-exposure-or-just-neuro-chemicals.28043/post-324601>

I believe there is a chemical correspondence to a spiritual state.

I once read a dissertation online, probably 20 years ago, in which the student compared the PET scans of schizophrenia with that of spiritual enlightenment. And they looked pretty much the same. I wish I could find that paper again.

So that would prove that the chemistry alone doesn’t do the trick and you also need context.

The whole subject regarding hallucinations is very dubious and not quite researched. Group hallucinations of all sorts exist, with and without drugs.

When I grew up, the thesis was considered true that the brain basically does not develop after 20. I always said that’s wrong, but I also was too lazy to walk the walk and prove it. Others have done this work now and at least we are at 50 I believe. Like they can prove that we are still forming all kinds of things at 50 in our brains, new synapses,why thank you.

Why would it stop?
I’ve been following the psychedelic and you know, mostly recreational drug use if people would be more honest, for expanding human potential. It’s problematic, because the people who believe in drugs like to go overboard, and they also need, you guess it: money.

This ruins pretty much everything, because you need to be a strategist more than a researcher. Dr. Shulgin, (phical) who I truly believe did it for research and exploring the human condition found his lab seized constantly, then always a foot in jail. And that’s just a drug guy.

There is so much that is being researched by people who prefer to stay off the grid or under the radar, because the sanctions are super high. It’s not a good thing, because all those idiots, who obviously are also out there, can claim that that’s what they do: research. But they don’t, they are just the guru type with ego issues. most people in the esoteric and spiritual scene are unfortunately like that. Very hard to find an honest person.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-08-04T22:05:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-something-spiritual-to-light-exposure-or-just-neuro-chemicals.28043/post-324700>

I think this all depends on perspective and your thoughts about light. Light certainly has a plethora of biophysical/biochemical effects on the body, but let's say you aren't aware of these direct effects. You might be inclined to believe there is some mystical healing powers to light. That simple thought alone can alter your neurochemical state. Or let's consider the opposite, say you believe that sunlight is truly toxic. That thought alone might change your brain state in a different way if your are in the light. Might cause you to experience a stress response when in sunlight.

At the end of the day, all of these outcomes are biophysical/biochemical in nature, but I think different ppl can experience different outcomes based on what they think about light.

You can always take parts from people and if he inspired you to something, that’s great.
I’ve seen the first couple of episodes and thought ok, and then he turned wrong for me. I forgot about what it was though, because I already decided that it was a waste of my time.

Of course thoughts influence your reality and your whole being. That’s not really particularly…particular.

Here is how you find the truth: you ask yourself and you listen to yourself. Nobody can do that trick for you. If you would like me to be more specific: you listen to your heart. Some say gut, but I would say that as great as the gut is, it is a more practical organ in that sense and the heart (that is still a metaphor) is the reliable resource.

All other pathways can be corrupted. They don’t have to be, but they can be, as by their nature. The connection to your heart is as direct as can be.

If you can’t hear your heart, well that is something that can be practiced. And the best thing to ask is “who am I ?”

I cannot correct you, if you are wrong. I mean, I can, but ideally you do that yourself

### markthesunbather — 2024-03-07T19:54:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-something-spiritual-to-light-exposure-or-just-neuro-chemicals.28043/post-332287>

So religion has lifted humanity to incredible heights, producing amazing art and culture, and it does tell you it's about light, but the religious still wear sunglasses, headcoverings, and you never get told to see the sunrise, and when they do their praying and theologizing or icon painting or what have you, they're doing it indoors in isolated blue light or under artificial light at night.

And so they'll talk lofty things about metaphysical light, but it becomes hard to take any of it serious once you see they'd have women covered up at the beach, and all the intellectuals out there wanting to debate it are super pale looking.

# Thread: Magnetism and geographic location
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetism-and-geographic-location.28044/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Evee — 2023-08-02T04:50:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetism-and-geographic-location.28044/post-324600>

Can someone help me out - I was researching areas in the Yucatán to stay and came across an article that says the magnetic field is different on the east and west side of the peninsula (lower in parts of the east and higher in parts of the west)
It goes on to say that NATURAL low magnetic field have been shown to be beneficial against cancer, while NATURAL high ones are better for things like neuro degenerative diseases. (Not talking about NNemf!)
I’m new as a member and working my way through the blogs (and don’t feel like I fully understand the magnetism piece as it relates to geography yet) so does anyone know if this article tracks with what Jack says about magnetism and where we should be looking to stay? If I’m in my 40s and concerned about hereditary cancer risk, is a place like Merida (should be lower natural magnetic field according to article) a smarter choice than Isla de las Mujeres (higher)?

Thanks!

### [Deuterium, Secret of Yucatán](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/deuterium-secret-yucatán-doris-loh)

When discussing the role of deuterium in the origin of life, it is impossible to exclude the mass extinction event that extinguished over 75% of species on earth. The Chicxulub impact crater in Yucatán, Mexico is the only known terrestrial impact structure that has remained intact.

 www.linkedin.com

I cannot speak to the magnetic geography part of your question but in regard to your statement:

> If I’m in my 40s and concerned about hereditary cancer risk,

It is my understanding that “hereditary” is a TINY part of cancer risk. Over 90% of cancer “RISK” has to do totally with environment. Your parents cancer problems are not your cancer concerns UNLESS you are in exactly the same environment.

### Evee — 2023-08-02T14:36:24+0300

# Thread: Water Softener, Potassium Chloride or Sodium Chloride?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-softener-potassium-chloride-or-sodium-chloride.28045/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gabriel L Rosenfeld — 2023-08-02T18:57:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-softener-potassium-chloride-or-sodium-chloride.28045/post-324622>

Hello,

I hope everyone is doing well. A couple of clarifications on water. Jack says to not add salt to water.

I have a water softener for hard well water. Is the small amount of sodium chloride added fine? Or should I switch to potassium chloride?

Also, I saw somewhere that Jack said to not add halides to water. I add iodine to my water bottle everyday, is that alright or not recommended?

Thank you so much for your help!

### coho3131 — 2023-08-16T03:19:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-softener-potassium-chloride-or-sodium-chloride.28045/post-325123>

Welcome Gabriel!

You are not required to use a water softener for well water.

If you do comprehensive testing (unfortunately it is not cheap) of the well water, and the water has undetectable or low enough levels of any toxic substances that you are concerned about, I think this water is much better for drinking and bathing than city water that has been "treated" and recycled. If your property is near, or downstream of, current or previous farmland, I would add Glyphosate to your testing.

Is there another animal photosynthetic pathway that links the skin, gut, and brain?

Who packs your medical parachute? In this podcast below, you are lead to believe you are hearing groundbreaking ideas on neurodegeneration. I am going to tell you-you are not hearing anything truly worthwhile if you're a Black Swan. Why do I give this scathing criticism?

What do most clinicians and biochemists understand fail about the biophysics of nitric oxide(NO) production and physiology?

How light and NO work in us. Share this with your clinicians to upgrade their knowledge so they can help you get to optimal. Nitric oxide and LLLT are interesting bedfellows. Light-mediated vasodilation was first described in 1968 by Furchgott, in his nitric oxide research that leads to his receipt of a Nobel Prize thirty years later in 1998. Later studies conducted by other researchers confirmed and extended Furchgott's early work, and demonstrate the ability of light frequencies to influence the localized production or release of NO, and to stimulate vasodilation through the effect NO on cGMP. This finding suggests that properly designed illumination devices may be effective, noninvasive therapeutic agents for patients who would benefit from increased localized NO availability. However, the wavelengths that are most effective on this light-mediated release of NO are different from those used in LLLT, being in the UV-A (320-400 nm) and in some cases light frequencies present in the low blue range just above UVA.

UVA light also marks the time of the day where PER1 gene is transcribed and found in its HIGHEST CONCENTRATION in the blood plasma to affect all tissues ability to tell time well. Without this frequency of sunlight, PER1 activity is poor in the plasma and cannot turn out the proper endogenous cycles in the cytoplasm of cells to optimize the timing of metabolic pathways in biochemistry. Just knowing the pathway matters little in this situation because you need to understand what is controlling its kinetics because altered kinetics come from bad circadian timing in cells. This = broken circadian mechanism = poor redox state = poor solar exposure. This is why food gurus and biochemists continue to trip over the dogma.

Some wavelengths of light are absorbed by hemoglobin, and that illumination can release the NO from hemoglobin (specifically from the nitrosothiols in the beta chain of the hemoglobin molecule) in red blood cells (RBCs) Since RBCs are continuously delivered to the area of treatment, there is a natural supply of NO that can be released from each new RBC that passes under the light source and is exposed to the appropriate wavelength of photon energy. Since the half-life of the NO released under the area of illumination is only 2 to 3 seconds, NO release is very local and focal, preventing the effect of increased NO from being manifested in other portions of the body. Vasodilation from NO is based on its effect on the enzyme guanylate cyclase (GC), which forms cGMP to phosphorylate myosin and relax smooth muscle cells in the vascular system. Once available levels of GC are saturated with NO, or once maximum levels of cGMP are achieved, further vasodilation through illumination will not occur until these biologic compounds return to their pre-illumination status. This system is designed to feedback upon itself. Again, the wavelengths that have been shown to mediate this effect tend to be in the UV-A and blue ranges, not the red and NIR wavelength ranges that are mainly used for LLLT.

Many people are unaware that cytochrome c is a heme related protein and contains 4 red light chromophores. This is critically important in understanding how light and mitochondria work at the most fundamental level. The activity of cytochrome c oxidase is inhibited by nitric oxide (NO). This was a surprising discovery to those who do understand how mitochondria work with light and sleep. They found it 'shocking' that the body could and WOULD poison one of its own enzymes; in fact, it was initially shrugged off as an imperfection of experiment, but a few years later, several groups reported that mitochondria produced an enzyme that synthesizes NO, that was identified as the neuronal isoforms of NO synthase.

It was proposed that evolution crafted cytochrome c oxidase to bind not only oxygen but also to use the NO as a braking mechanism for electron flow and power in the mitochondria. The effect of slowing respiration and electron flow in some locations was to divert oxygen elsewhere in cells and tissues where mitochondria were working better. Since O2 is paramagnetic and drawn to magnetic fields, O2 will naturally flow without much effort of extra needed energy to move toward mitochondria where magnetic fields are generated due to good redox power. What biochemists do not realize is that UVA light is never present when IRA light is not. This means that the NO-CCO mechanism is a shunt to get rid of poor mitochondrial engines using Carnot Thereom and the chiral heat effect. This all assumes RED light from the sun is present to drive autophagy for engine replacement. This is why indoor living is devasting to those with high heteroplasmy. This also explains why reduced NO production due to a lack of UVA sunlight also leads to the suppression of mammalian targets of rapamycin (mTOR) = 380nm signal. This is the anabolic/catabolic switch for mtDNA.

Since NO blocks respiration in the endothelial cells lining blood vessels, and this helps to transfer oxygen into smooth muscle cells in these vessels where it is needed and required by physiologic demands. This effect is very misunderstood in the etiology of PAD and atherosclerosis damage. A lack of sunlight with UVA light is the FIRST STEP in these diseases. This is why most people with PAD and atherosclerosis have low Vitamin D status and poor redox.

This inhibition of mitochondrial respiration by NO can be explained by direct competition between NO and O2 for the reduced binuclear center CuB/a3 of cytochrome c oxidase and is reversible. Many people do not even realize that cytochrome c is inducible. Details matter in quantum biology. It was later proposed that laser irradiation could reverse the inhibition of cytochrome c oxidase by NO by photodissociating NO from its binding sites. Because this coordinate binding is much weaker than a covalent bond, this dissociation is possible by visible and NIR light that has insufficient energy to break covalent bonds. This ability is used in optogenetics. This ability is also used in PBM.

The dissociation of NO from COX will thus increase the respiration rate ("NO hypothesis"). The experiment has now proved, light ALONE can indeed reverse the inhibition caused by NO binding to cytochrome oxidase, both in isolated mitochondria and in whole cells. Most clinicians are unaware of this ability of UV and IR light. **This is incredibly important in CAD, PAD, atherosclerosis, and all neurodegeneration because this is HOW THEY ALL BEGIN.**

When you do not get out in the sun in nature it alters the redox state of the arterial wall and the RBCs in the artery. This ruins the peroxiredoxin heme proteins in the RBC's and they lose their DHA, Vitamin C, and ability to induce the COX enzyme. All of these things age the blood cells faster and lower their ability to carry oxygen and CO2 while keeping the innate immune system activation status in the blood plasma active. This ruins how Toll receptor proteins work and also can lead to autoimmune conditions like Crohn's disease and neurodegeneration like PD.

Most of the beautiful things in nature appear to be done effortlessly. Do you think nature worked hard to freeze Lake Michigan to create a work of art from above?

Grass doesn't strain to grow, does it? It is effortless because it is designed well. Nature never tells us about her efforts. She just shows us continual results of her designs. Looking at the macroscopic anatomy of the viscera in the gut would never give you a clue how these organs work with the skin and brain. Solar light links them all. For humans, depth and substance can be easy to obtain.......it's just not effortless to attain in life. Choice of the light you use determines that journey.

**You should no longer be surprised how effortless nature really is below your level of understanding.** Light can also protect cells against NO-induced cell death. These experiments used light in the visible spectrum, with wavelengths from 600 to 630 nm (and others in NIR). NIR also seems to have effects on cytochrome oxidase in conditions where NO is unlikely to be present. This is why parabiosis studies show the effects they do. These are the things we do for our clients at Kruse Longevity Center. [https://peterattiamd.com/franciscogonzalezlima/](https://peterattiamd.com/franciscogonzalezlima/)

### [Vasodilation: What Causes Blood Vessels to Widen](https://my.clevelandclinic.org/health/diseases/23352-vasodilation#SnippetTab)

Vasodilation is the medical term for when blood vessels in your body widen, allowing more blood to flow through them and lowering your blood pressure.

 my.clevelandclinic.org

## OVERVIEW​

Vasodilation is the process that widens blood vessels.

### What is vasodilation?​

Vasodilation is the medical term for when [blood vessels](https://my.clevelandclinic.org/health/body/21640-blood-vessels) in your body widen, allowing more blood to flow through them and lowering your blood pressure. This is a normal process that happens in your body without you even realizing it. It’s also something that can be caused by things you eat or drink, and medications that you take. Vasodilation can also be a symptom of certain diseases.

#### How does vasodilation work?​

Your blood has many different purposes in your body, including:

-   Carrying oxygen and nutrients.
-   Helping your body manage its temperature.

The blood vessels in your body aren’t just tubes that stay the same size all the time. Blood vessels also contain muscle, and that muscle controls just how wide or narrow your blood vessels are at any given time. When blood vessels get wider, that’s vasodilation. The opposite process, when blood vessels constrict and get narrower, is called [vasoconstriction](https://my.clevelandclinic.org/health/symptoms/21697-vasoconstriction).

\======================================

### Can anything I eat or drink cause vasodilation?​

Yes, there are many things you can eat or drink that can cause vasodilation and, by extension, a decrease in blood pressure.

-   **F**oods that are rich in nitrates: **Leafy greens like spinach and lettuce, radishes and bok choy are all rich in nitrates.** Your body converts nitrates into nitric oxide, which is very effective at causing vasodilation.
-   **Alcohol:** When you drink alcohol, vasodilation is one of the things your body does right away. The effect is temporary and your blood pressure rebounds shortly after. Drinking too much also causes higher blood pressure, which is just one reason (of many) why healthcare providers recommend against drinking too much.

### What medications cause vasodilation?​

There’s a wide range of medications that cause vasodilation, either as their main purpose or as a side effect. Many types of [blood pressure medications](https://my.clevelandclinic.org/health/treatments/21811-antihypertensives) cause vasodilation because that’s their main purpose. Another common medication that causes vasodilation is nitroglycerin, which makes it useful in the treatment of [heart attack](https://my.clevelandclinic.org/health/diseases/16818-heart-attack-myocardial-infarction) and chest pain. **Erectile dysfunction medications like [sildenafil](https://my.clevelandclinic.org/health/drugs/20690-sildenafil-tablets-viagra) (Viagra®) also rely on vasodilation.**

### [Nitric oxide cream in pipeline](https://www.dermatologytimes.com/view/nitric-oxide-cream-pipeline)

A nitric oxide-releasing cream to treat psoriasis and atopic dermatitis is in development.

 www.dermatologytimes.com

\-------------

### Nitric oxide cream in pipeline​

Jul 12, 2017
[Bob Kronemyer](https://www.dermatologytimes.com/authors/bob-kronemyer)
_Article_

A nitric oxide-releasing cream to treat psoriasis and atopic dermatitis is in development.

A nitric oxide-releasing cream to treat psoriasis and atopic dermatitis is in development.
SB414 cream (Novan, Inc.) is a mixture of the stable ointment containing NVN1000 (Novan’s new chemical entity, which is the active pharmaceutical ingredient) and a hydrogel.
The first clinical study of SB414 for the treatment of psoriasis plans to use two separate tubes, but by the time of commercialization the drug product is targeted to be dispensed using a dual-chamber pump.
“When the ointment and hydrogel are dispensed together and mixed, a self-emulsifying cream formulation is created that immediately starts release of the nitric oxide from the polysiloxane macromolecule that harnesses the nitric oxide,” says Stanley Hollenbach, senior vice president of research and development at Novan.
NVN1000 is also the active ingredient for three other nitric oxide-releasing drug products by Novan in development, all of which are different gel formulations as opposed to a cream: SB204 for treatment of acne vulgaris, SB206 for external genital and perianal warts, and SB208 for tinea pedis and onychomycosis.
SB414 differs from these three gels in the release rate of the nitric oxide, concentration of the active ingredient and variances in pH, as well as cosmetic properties.
“What is so exciting about SB414 is that we have a stable drug product capable of delivering nitric oxide concentrations that can provide an anti-inflammatory pharmacological effect, with little or no toxicity,” Hollenbach tells Dermatology Times.
In a widely used psoriasis mouse model, SB414 significantly reduced composite psoriasis scores and inhibited the production of proinflammatory cytokines.
For psoriasis, “nitric oxide directly inhibits NF-kB and suppresses proinflammatory cytokines like IL-1β. Additionally, there is direct inhibition of the NLRP3 inflammasome assembly via S-nitrosation of the NLRP3 protein components,” Hollenbach says. “And in so doing, nitric oxide also suppresses IL-17a and IL-17f, which are known to be very influential in the psoriasis disease pathology,”
For treating atopic dermatitis, SB414 works in a similar fashion. “Nitric oxide downregulates relevant cytokines,” Hollenbach says.
The antibacterial properties of nitric oxide are also of benefit in treating atopic dermatitis. “All of our creams and gels have been able to demonstrate anti-microbial effects, with SB204 and SB414 demonstrating potent bactericidal effects against Staph. aureus,” says Hollenbach, who spoke on SB414 in April at the annual meeting of the Society for Investigative Dermatology in Portland, Oregon
“We believe S. aureus plays a significant role in the initiation and severity of the disease,” Hollenbach says. “Typically, the affected sites of atopic dermatitis patients are populated with an inordinate amount of S. aureus.”
Hollenbach and his colleagues at Novan are intrigued by the preclinical data published recently by Gallo and co-workers at the University of California, San Diego, and the hypothesis that S. aureus penetrates down below the epidermal layers and into the dermis and adipose tissue, as a clinically relevant stimulus of atopic dermatitis disease.
“Nitric oxide is a gas that can penetrate through the epidermal layers,” Mr. Hollenbach says. “With SB414’s extraordinarily strong and powerful antibacterial effects, we expect that we will be able to eradicate the bacterial component of atopic dermatitis disease, as well as modulate the inflammatory components of the disease.”
For atopic dermatitis, SB414 was evaluated in a mouse inflammation model of delayed hypersensitivity and in a minipig model of partial thickness skin wounds infected with a methicillin-resistant S. aureus strain isolated from a patient with atopic dermatitis.
“SB414 treatment demonstrated anti-inflammatory effects equivalent to a high potency corticosteroid in the mouse model and, with only five daily treatments, was able to essentially eradicate 99.9% of the Staph infection in the porcine infected wound model,” Hollenbach says.
Novan plans to initiate clinical development of SB414 by the end of June with the filing of an investigational new drug (IND) application, followed by a psoriasis clinical study for mild to moderate disease.
The Phase 2 proof-of-concept trial will likely enroll about 120 patients. At least two SB414 doses will be evaluated against an active comparator and placebo, with therapy administered twice a day for 8 weeks.
A clinical trial for atopic dermatitis could soon follow.
“We believe SB414 could be an effective treatment for psoriasis and atopic dermatitis patients with mild to moderate disease and low body surface area involvement,” Hollenbach says. “And due to the safe, localized delivery, we think it could be used concomitantly with systemic treatments for more severe cases.”
Considering the tolerability that Novan has shown in all of its existing topical products, “we think that the safety of SB414 will be a key differentiator to the other corticosteroid and calcineurin inhibitor topical agents in the marketplace,” Hollenbach says. “However, until we have some clinical data, it is difficult to gauge how SB414 will fit in the total armamentarium of topical anti-inflammatory products.”
Disclosure: Mr. Hollenbach is an employee and shareholder of Novan.

### [Vascanox HP • Calroy Health Sciences](https://calroy.com/product/vascanox-hp/?lp=new-frontiers-3-pack)

Next-generation nitric oxide support. Vascanox HP targets multiple pathways to support the natural production, storage, and release of nitric oxide.\*

 calroy.com

Nitric oxide is a messenger molecule that is produced in the body through both nitric oxide synthase (NOS) pathway and nitrate-nitrite-nitric oxide pathway. Vascanox HP supports the entire biotransformation cycle for nitric oxide, including both pathways.\*

## Nitric Oxide Synthase Pathway​

Nitric oxide synthases are a group of 3 enzymes catalyzing production of nitric oxide in the body. **Endothelial NOS (eNOS) is primarily responsible for the generation of nitric oxide in the vascular endothelium.** The mechanical force of blood flow triggers a response in the endothelial glycocalyx, which in turn signals the endothelium to generate nitric oxide.

//////////////////////////////////////////
Nitrate -> Nitrite -> Nitric Oxide Pathway
This pathway starts when inorganic nitrate, such as dietary nitrate in green leafy vegetables is ingested and absorbed into the circulation. Circulating nitrate is transported into the saliva gland and concentrated in the saliva. If secreted in the mouth, nitrate in the saliva is reduced to nitrite by nitrate reducing bacteria. Once swallowed, part of the nitrite is chemically reduced to nitric oxide in the stomach and the rest can be reduced to nitric oxide by several different enzymes after being absorbed.

FAQ

### Frequently Asked Questions​

#### Why should I use N1O1 Nitric Oxide Activating Serum?​

By the time we reach 40, studies have shown that we only produce about half or less of the Nitric Oxide (N.O.) we did at age 20, and it continues to decline as we age. Our ability to produce and generate N.O. within the body is a key element of healthy and youthful-looking skin. Pneuma Nitric Oxide Activating Serum delivers N.O. to the skin like no other product available today.

#### Can I use N1O1 Nitric Oxide Activating Serum with my other products I currently use?​

Yes, you can use our Nitric Oxide Activating Serum with other cosmetic products.

#### Is Pneuma Nitric Oxide Serum safe to use on people with sensitive skin or have experienced reactions to other skin serums?​

Yes, N1O1 Nitric Oxide Activating Serum contains high-quality, non-allergenic ingredients. Our serum is designed to restore the natural loss of Nitric Oxide in the skin. In addition, our unique formulation contains a powerful antioxidant defense system that is safe for even the most sensitive skin.

#### How is this different from other skin serums that are currently on the market?​

There are a number of skin serums available to consumers. However, only N1O1 Nitric Oxide Activating Serum is an innovative Nitric Oxide delivery system. It works down below the surface on a cellular level to improve the way skin cells function. Our serum is both corrective and protective.

#### My face is turning slightly pink or red. Is this normal?​

Yes, this is a normal response to Nitric Oxide. It’s actually a sign that the product is working.

### JanSz — 2023-08-29T03:19:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/man-woman-nitric-oxide.28046/post-325679>

Nitric Oxide Depletion Is Responsible For Accelerated Aging Skin!
Starting in your 20’s, your body slowly loses the ability to produce the anti-aging molecule—nitric oxide. By age 40, your body loses 50% of the nitric oxide it needs to function at peak efficiency. As a result, skin cells become dysfunctional, your skin ages, and wrinkles appear.

N1O1 replaces the age-related loss of nitric oxide to repair wrinkled or damaged skin and restore a youthful appearance.

#### What Is Nitric Oxide?​

Nitric oxide (NO) is known as the anti-aging molecule and is one of the essential molecules naturally produced in humans. This simple molecule regulates cellular communication by aiding oxygen and nutrient delivery to every cell in the body. When you don’t produce enough nitric oxide, your cells can’t maintain optimal health and start to malfunction.

#### How Nitric Oxide Loss Affects Your Skin​

Cells malfunction or age when they do not get enough oxygen and nutrients. Skin cells are no different. Healthy cells are hydrated and have sufficient collagen. However, unhealthy cells lose collagen and hydration, creating fine lines and wrinkles on the skin. Nitric oxide helps the body provide nutrients and oxygen so the skin cells can restore their function. This includes collagen deposition, improved hydration, the loss of fine lines and wrinkles, and improved skin tone, quality, and texture.

#### Why Pneuma Nitric Oxide?​

Healthy skin cells are like little buckets filled with nutrients, oxygen, and water. Keeping the tone, quality, and texture of your skin makes you appear youthful and healthy. As you age, your body loses its ability to produce enough nitric oxide. Your little buckets spring holes allowing the nutrients and moisture to leak from the cells. Other anti-aging skincare products try to keep filling a leaky bucket. But without fixing the holes, they can never keep up even with continuous use. You have to use more or more vital anti-aging skincare or resort to injections, fillers, and surgery. N1O1 works by fixing the holes in your buckets so that skin cells can retain their vital nutrients, moisture, and collagen production. N1O1 essentially reverses the effects of the aging process to visibly reduce wrinkles and improve the tone, quality, texture, and clarity of your skin.

### JanSz — 2023-08-29T03:19:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/man-woman-nitric-oxide.28046/post-325680>

## TEN FACTS ABOUT NITRIC OXIDE (NO)​

1.  Nitric oxide is one of the most important molecules produced in the human body.
2.  NO is a potent vasodilator that keeps your arteries open and blood flowing to every cell in your body.
3.  Nitric oxide is made by every cell in your body; however, production declined by 10-12% per decade starting in the early 20s. By age 40-50, we make only 50%.
4.  Loss of NO production is associated with the normal aging process.
5.  Prescription drugs, such as proton pump inhibitors, NSAIDS, antibiotics, antiseptic mouthwash, and cholesterol-lowering statin drugs block the production of NO.
6.  Most people do not know what nitric oxide is or why it is critical that they employ strategies to restore nitric oxide.
7.  NO has been shown to dilate blood vessels and recruit new blood vessels, improving circulation to regions as it is produced or supplied.
8.  NO has potent anti-inflammatory properties that help combat free radicals that damage cells and cause premature aging.
9.  NO is responsible for cellular energy production and cell metabolism that keeps cells healthy and functional.
10.  Due to diet, lifestyle, and many drug therapies, most Americans are deficient in nitric oxide.

### CREATING NITRIC OXIDE ON THE SURFACE OF THE SKIN WITH N1O1​

Nitric Oxide (NO) is considered one of the most important molecules **naturally produced in humans**. This simple molecule controls oxygen and nutrient delivery to every cell in the body, it regulated cellular communication. All of which affects the signs of aging. In fact, this molecule is so important it was named “Molecule of the Year” by Science Magazine in 1992 and in 1998 a Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine was awarded to the three US scientists responsible for its discovery. Loss of nitric oxide production is part of the normal aging process.

Orally Disintegrating Nitric Oxide Generating Lozenge

cardiovascular disease.

Nitric Oxide Generating Topical for Wound Care
NOi has developed and clinically tested a wound care topical. The nitric oxide generating topical improves vascularity which promotes improved blood flow and oxygen to the injury site. Rapid healing of skin injury, surgical sites with reduced scarring and the prevention and treatment of stages I-IV tissue injury have all been noted The anti-bacterial and anti-viral properties are essential to prevent potentially fatal infections such as MRSA.

Nitric Oxide Generating Topical for Dermatology

NOi has developed and clinically tested skincare a topical to be used by Dermatologists. The nitric oxide generating topical features anti-bacterial and anti-viral properties that are essential to prevent and heal acne. Extensive testing has shown the skincare topical is very effective in the treatment of skin conditions such as Rosacea and Eczema. The nitric oxide improves the blood flow to the microvascular structures of the skin leading to rapid healing and scar prevention.

Slow healing and restricted blood flow to the extremities are commonly found in people with Diabetes and conditions such as Raynauds Syndrome. Use of the topical on hands and feet have shown a rapid return of blood flow. Patients with Raynauds Syndrome report marked improvement with hand color changing from blue to a healthy pink within 2 - 3 minutes. Nitric oxide promotes improved blood flow and healing of diabetic ulcers, bruising and associated skin conditions.

>
> The intravascular half-life of NO is about 2 milliseconds.
> Consider this when thinking about exogenous NO supplementation.

Indeed 2 milliseconds is likely correct.
But concentrating on it gives wrong impression about what is happening.
To have this 2 milliseconds NO must first be present and dissolved in the blood and available when those 2 milliseconds are needed to come about.

To check if the supplementation is actually working, I think it would be better to look at different example.
Say someone with high blood pressure starting to take it.
After xx while see if his/her blood pressure is any lower (without blood pressure medications).

There is already testing available. It is called Endo-PAT.
But that test actually check veins if they increase volume under stress.
So basically, to confirm availability of NO in the blood.
I think 10% volume increase is the maximum expected, or maybe it is the desirable optimum.

### Bryan Therapeutics, Inc. (BTI) Acquires Nitric Oxide Innovations(NOi) LLC In Stock Merger Positioning BTI For Investments to Accelerate Nitric Oxide Drug Development​

NewMediaWire
**Thu, August 31, 2023** at 10:30 AM EDT·5 min read

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

BEVERLY HILLS, CA - ([NewMediaWire](https://www.newmediawire.com/)) - August 31, 2023 - Announced today: Bryan Therapeutics, Inc (BTI) has acquired Nitric Oxide Innovations (NOi) LLC, a clinical stage biotechnology company in a stock acquisition. BTI is a newly formed Corporation out of Texas that is singularly focused on nitric oxide therapeutics. BTI now assumes all of NOI's assets including its license from the University of Texas Health Science Center at Houston for nitric oxide-based therapies and ongoing FDA clinical trials. NOi is now a wholly owned subsidiary of BTI.
Bryan Therapeutics, Inc. (BTI) is represented for Media Strategy Positioning by Creative Management Partners LLC (CMP), Beverly Hills, CA. Additionally, Dr. Bryan's Authored book "Nathan S. Bryan, Ph.D, **_The Secret Of Nitric OxideBringing Nitric Oxide To Life"_**, sold to J. Boylston & Company Publishers, to be released in early 2024, represented by Agent Alan Morell, Creative Management Partners LLC (CMP) to support BTI endeavors.
Said Dr. Nathan Bryan Ph.D., Founder, Chairman and CEO of BTI, "During the past few years, we have outgrown our previous corporate structure. Our objective has not changed but our strategy has changed. BTI is ideally positioned to continue to advance the science of nitric oxide into safe and effective drug therapies. Our recent COVID drug study established the safety of our oral nitric oxide drug and now we are moving into the next phase of clinical trials for several indications. This requires additional capital and a broader team with experience in global distribution and drug development. We have assembled the best team in the business to get our drugs through clinical trials, approved in major markets and in the hands of physicians and patients to improve lives around the world. Our strategy with NOI was to move our nitric oxide technology into clinical trials, de-risk it and then sell or license to a larger company. Now that we have established safety of our oral formulation, we can quickly, efficiently, and cost effectively advance our drugs through clinical trials for approval. BTI's strategy is to see drugs through FDA approval and be the market leader in nitric oxide-based drugs and therapeutics. We are confident we have the right people, the best technology and sufficient capital to get our drugs approved for many poorly managed chronic diseases."
BTI has plans to submit investigational new drug applications (INDs) for **ischemic heart disease** in the coming weeks. BTI has active drug programs for **Alzheimers' disease and a topical drug for diabetic/pressure ulcers.** Additional development plans for **ischemic, non-obstructive coronary artery disease, pulmonary hypertension, heart failure with preserved ejection fraction (HFpEF). David Perlmutter,** M.D., Director of Neuroscience Research says, "BTI is uniquely positioned to implement novel therapeutic strategies utilizing the emerging understanding of **the central role of nitric oxide in regulating human metabolism.** This is incredibly relevant across a wide spectrum of disease processes, including **Alzheimer's disease, and cardiovascular disorders**."
Said John Somberg, M.D., President of Academic Pharmaceuticals and Professor Emeritus of Medicine Cardiology in Pharmacology Rush Medical College, Chicago; **"I've conducted more than 40 FDA clinical trials for many different therapeutics. I believe that nitric oxide has a major place in therapeutics**, and I think if anyone is going to bring it to fruition it is Bryan Therapeutics, Inc. The basic science behind nitric oxide therapies is solid. Now, it is time for the development of safe and effective nitric oxide therapeutics. Dr. Bryan's science and discoveries have great potential and I'm excited to be involved."

For investment opportunities and to receive a private placement memorandum, please contact Dr. Nathan S Bryan at [[email redacted]](mailto:[email redacted])
**ABOUT BRYAN THERAPEUTICS, INC. (BTI) AND NATHAN S. BRYAN PH.D:**
Bryan Therapeutics, Inc (BTI) was established to commercialize clinically, and scientifically proven Nitric Oxide (NO) based technologies for a **wide range of human disease and conditions**. Our mission is to be the industry leader and innovator in nitric oxide-based technologies in order to bring to market safe and effective therapeutic solutions where NO may be involved. Our vision for BTI is to be the market leader in nitric oxide-based therapeutics. BTI has **drug programs for ischemic heart disease, Alzheimers' Disease, topical drugs for diabetic and pressure ulcers as well as a novel antiviral in the event of another pandemic.**
Dr. Bryan earned his undergraduate Bachelor of Science degree in Biochemistry from the University of Texas at Austin and his doctoral degree from Louisiana State University Health Sciences Center in Shreveport where he was the recipient of the Dean's Award for Excellence in Research. He pursued his post-doctoral training as a Kirschstein Fellow at Boston University School of Medicine in the Whitaker Cardiovascular Institute. After a two year post-doctoral fellowship, in 2006 Dr. Bryan was recruited to join faculty at the University of Texas Health Science Center at Houston by **Ferid Murad, M.D., Ph.D., 1998 Nobel Laureate** in Medicine or Physiology. Dr. Bryan has been involved in nitric oxide research for the past 20 years and has made many seminal discoveries in the field. His many seminal discoveries have resulted in dozens of issued US and International patents and the product technology resulting from his discoveries and inventions has improved patient care worldwide and is responsible for hundreds of millions of dollars in revenue globally. Dr. Bryan is the inventor of **Superbeets, Neo40, BeetElite, NO2U, N1O1** nitric oxide activating serum. His nitric oxide products are the global market leaders. Dr. Bryan is an international leader in molecular medicine and nitric oxide biochemistry.

I use curing salt when making charcuterie. Usually just sausage.
Curing salt (below, that I use) contains 6.25% of sodium nitrite.

Per label below, other curing salts may contain
[sodium nitrate](https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=71ec325f94a1eb4bJmltdHM9MTY5MzUyNjQwMCZpZ3VpZD0xOTVjMjNiOC03YmZjLTZhM2EtMDE2OC0zMGMxN2E1NDZiZDcmaW5zaWQ9NTU1MA&ptn=3&hsh=3&fclid=195c23b8-7bfc-6a3a-0168-30c17a546bd7&u=a1L3NlYXJjaD9xPVNvZGl1bStuaXRyYXRlJkZPUk09U05BUFNUJmZpbHRlcnM9c2lkOiJmOTZkMzgyMy1mMzBjLTkwMjktMDkxOC0zYjc3MjhhMmU2MmEi&ntb=1) / Formula
NaNO₃
instead of nitrite.
\---------------------------------------
question:
Who and how breaks **Na** out of **NaNO₂** is that work for bacteria on the back of my tongue?
Gastric acid is taking one **O** out of **NO₂** so then we have the desired ****NO****

According to Dr. Nathan Bryan cured meats are source of no more than 15% of our No, Nitric Oxide.

Ah, now this is what I was looking for. Somehow I decided it's high time to explore and capitalize on NO. As you and I both appreciate, if we can test it then we can see what we need. I'll be ordering test strips today. Then selecting a supplement, based on my expectedly low levels of NO.

By the way, Nathan Bryan is a gold mine of information on NO, so thanks for all those video links. I don't really like to watch videos much - too much verbiage with too little actual information. Same goes for podcasts. However, when a specialists claims a raft of scientific articles, I'm on it. (Some claim them and don't actually have any - hmm.) Bryan clearly walks the talk. His list of articles on PubMed is impressive (see my search today, here: [https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/?term=bryan-ns&size=200](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/?term=bryan-ns&size=200)). His work is a wonderful starting point for me to understand what to do about NO and what I can expect. A search on "nitric oxide" at JackKruse.com also yielded results for wherever Jack mentions it ([https://www.google.com/search?q=site%3Ajackkruse.com+%22nitric+oxide%22&sca\_esv=562194575&sxsrf=AB5stBhPSRhlTDN9WZOXvNaVYMFpnOB\_Ew%3A1693665815920&source=hp&ei=F0rzZKOONbbDkPIPj5SdmAg&iflsig=AD69kcEAAAAAZPNYJ20th9fyMjuLvhvQEccWIpzLzHK-&ved=0ahUKEwjj9crqlIyBAxW2IUQIHQ9KB4MQ4dUDCAo&uact=5&oq=site%3Ajackkruse.com+%22nitric+oxide%22&gs\_&sclient=gws-wiz#ip=1)](https://www.google.com/search?q=site%3Ajackkruse.com+%22nitric+oxide%22&sca_esv=562194575&sxsrf=AB5stBhPSRhlTDN9WZOXvNaVYMFpnOB_Ew%3A1693665815920&source=hp&ei=F0rzZKOONbbDkPIPj5SdmAg&iflsig=AD69kcEAAAAAZPNYJ20th9fyMjuLvhvQEccWIpzLzHK-&ved=0ahUKEwjj9crqlIyBAxW2IUQIHQ9KB4MQ4dUDCAo&uact=5&oq=site%3Ajackkruse.com+%22nitric+oxide%22&gs_&sclient=gws-wiz#ip=1)

I don't know whether that godawful link will work here - you can do the same search on Google as: \[site:JackKruse.com "nitric oxide"\] (without brackets) in case it doesn't.

I also like to start out a new topic by looking to see what of one of my favorite NDs has to say about it. Regarding NO it's here: [https://draxe.com/nutrition/nitric-oxide-benefits/](https://draxe.com/nutrition/nitric-oxide-benefits/).

The most common questions I always have about supplements entail claims about this 'n' that being superior to competitors' products. Bryan mentioned in one of his videos that 99+% of NO supplements don't work. Again, my old lab rat days come to the fore and drive me to want evidence for such a claim.

Time to get studying!

Cheers,
Dennis

Oh, one more thing: Apparently NO can be produced by the arginine pathway. However, from what I've read about that pathway cites its inefficiency or complete failure in seniors. I think that what you choose to do in your 80s is a different ballgame to much younger people thinking that using NO lozenges is normal. I don't buy the not enough nitrate in veges now, and even if that's true, that's just one route to making NO. I also don't buy the supplementing with l arginine or l citrulline she mentions in the video. The interesting bit is the PBM - not for any man made light, but because its possibly the frequencies nocturnal mammals rely on, together with any nitrates, arginine and citrulline in the seasonal foods they eat, to generate NO. (I'd suspect grounding and cold are in the mix too, but looking for the mechanisms still ....) Wild nocturnal mammals are not all keeling over with heart disease etc, or reaching for the viagra. I think the nocturnal NO pathway exists in all mammals, and that tapping into that could be useful, especially for shift workers etc.

Theoretically a lozenge could make someone "feel" better, but by not following the normal signalling pathways to generating it, what hasn't been done that should have been? What else would have been beneficial in the food that someone didn't eat because they popped a lozenge? Whatever its clinical benefits if someone is already in a deep hole, this risks becoming an expensive lifestyle choice, just like DDW has. It also - like DDW - becomes the playground for those that can afford it. I don't have a problem with that, but I do have a problem with doom mongering about nitrate levels in foods or what the average 30-45 year old's NO levels are, if the belief system is that nitrates are the be all and end all of NO formation, or the NO levels in the 30-45 year old's were derived from people living indoors like zoo animals, rather than say the Hazda still living traditionally, or those living traditionally in blue zone areas.

The above is nicely written dissertation.
Wish I was able to do something similar.
Some people are more convincing than others.
How to figure out what to do?
My way is test and verify what works.
Indeed, money is an issue for most.
DDW-25 when I found it the very first time was indeed super expensive.
Now a 1/2liter bottle is about $7.
I have drunk it for three years and tested and posted, my results multiple times.
I have my doubts about drinking plain DDW-25 or even stronger versions of it.
Now about NO and
these N1O1 lozenges.
I eat fermented red beets and I was eating L-arginine and L-Citruline and actually I am continuing on eating them.
Nothing outstanding happened.
Started N1O1 and one can say "All Hell broke loose" but I think and hope it is in very good way.
There is ABSOLUTELY no mistake that they work.
I am a man, have a working "indicator".
When I walk my 5 miles daily, I have increased tempo and really thinking of going back to running.
Whole day I feel more energy in me.
\-----
I have a very clean heart and arteries. Agatston score, Calcium score 1.
Couple years earlier it was 2.
My GP asks me how is this possible.
I had no clue, but now have an idea.
Since invention (1998??) of Viagra and Cialis I am eating half a 20mg pill a day of Cialis.
So that must be the L-arginine and L-Citruline working at making NO, Nitric Oxide.
But
by going by how I feel after eating N1O1 pills I must have been slowing down with that part of NO production process.
I was thinking about getting Endo-PAT testing.
I have that available in NYC, paid by my insurance.
But just eating the N1O1 lozenges have given me if not all, then most of the answers.
\================
[@Sue-UK](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9658/)
In this area of interest, I still have at least one unsolved problem.
**Possibly you have some idea worth trying.**
No problem with getting erection, almost any time now, but big problem in getting orgasm during sex.
I am able to get orgasm no more than twice a week.
\-------

If you were my DH at over 80, I'd be asking him to be careful about starting up the running, because he might know a couple of test parameters, but what about heart heteroplasmy rates? What does having needed or wanted to take cialis since 1998 say about heteroplasmy? A man getting an erection using cialis or an NO lozenge is not the same as it happening naturally, no judgement about that, but what if its forcing the body to do something it doesn't have the resources to do, or replenish the compounds lost via sperm such as spermidine? What if the WHY for a younger man is because of aluminum deposits in the sperm, affecting not only reproduction, but the health of a partner's reproductive organs? If my DH is giving me great sex twice a week when we reach 80, I'd be happy with that. I'd find nothing sexy about DH at 80 worrying about how many erections he gets a week, and I won't be lying back and thinking of England if he starts popping the cialis or N101 lozenges without considering the risks.

 drnathansbryan.com

MENU

### NEW YEAR, NEW YOU? NOT IF YOU ARE TOXIC​

January 2, 2023[Blog](https://drnathansbryan.com/category/blog/)
Well, it is a new beginning. A new year where it gives us a chance to reflect on the past year, learn from our mistakes and then resolve to make changes to improve our lives.
For the next few months, gyms will be full, people will try to make diet and lifestyle changes only to give up and fail. Statistics tell us most Americans’s health continues to decline rather than improve.
One of the major reasons, I believe, is that most Americans are toxic and do not take the proper steps to detoxify. Most people who are unhealthy are toxic. Toxicity makes it difficult to exercise and suppresses any real results.
I’ve said this many times but people are unhealthy or sick for two reasons and two reasons only:
1\. People are missing essential nutrients needed for the body to heal and perform.
2\. People are exposed to toxins that inhibit the body to heal and perform.
The first is simple. All you have to do is take supplements that replace any missing nutrients from your diet.
The second is more tricky since the world we live in is toxic. The food we eat is toxic. The water most people drink, bathe in and cook in is toxic. The air we breathe is toxic and the vibrational energy of electromagnetic frequency (EMF) from wifi is toxic is to many people.
Have you ever wondered why you don’t lose weight despite dieting and exercising or feel better despite massive changes in diet and lifestyle? It’s because we do not take proper steps to detoxify our bodies.

### TOXINS FOUND IN OUR BODIES​

Toxins build up over time and cause a disruption in normal metabolism. If we don’t take steps to limit exposure to toxins or remove the body of toxins, they build up and over time and make people sick.
There are three main types of toxins that the body deals with:
1\. Toxins produced from bacterial and viral infections. If you have an infection, either from a bacterium or virus, these pathogens secrete toxins as part of their metabolism. Therefore if you have an infection i.e. root canals or any viral infection, then you are toxic and must rid the body of the infection to rid the body of the toxins. We are recovering from COVID. The spike protein is toxic.
2\. Heavy metals found in the environment, food we eat or metals exposed from contrasts, dyes or metals found in our body such as mercury amalgam fillings. We must find the source of exposure to metals and remove the source.
3\. Environmental toxins such as glyphosate, herbicides, pesticides, petroleum chemicals, etc. These toxins are primarily fat soluble and stored in our fat cells. If you have a lot of fat, you likely have a lot of stored toxins.

### 4 MAIN ROUTES FOR REMOVING TOXINS​

The human body has four main routes of removal of toxins. Removal of waste products and toxins occurs through sweating, urination, defecation and breathing.
If you are not sweating and breathing deeply you build up toxins. If you do not drink lots of clean water to promote urination, you are toxic. If you don’t have at least one good bowel movement a day, then you are toxic.
If you have poor circulation due to insufficient nitric oxide production, you become toxic because you cannot get the good stuff in to our cells and are unable to remove the metabolic waste products from tissues and cells.
To remove toxins and help your body to detoxify, you must do the following:
1\. Restore nitric oxide levels **so your circulatory system can actually do its job.**
2\. Remove all root canals, remove mercury amalgam fillings and clean up infection – root canals are infected and cause many systemic problems.
3\. You must sweat – exercise is best. If you are not sweating while you exercise you are not working hard enough.
4\. Use an infrared sauna – this provides **light therapy** but also generates heat so you sweat out toxins. Take 750 mg niacin 20 minutes prior to sauna to help liberate fat soluble toxins. Also consider taking a binder such as activated charcoal or algae prior to workout or sauna. You want to be able to bind and excrete the toxins you liberate through sauna. If not, you will just mobilize the toxins and you may feel worse if not properly bound and excreted.
5\. Drink, cook and bathe in good clean water. There is no substitute for pure, clean water. Shoot for drinking at least half a gallon of purified water every day. Avoid drinking municipal water. This will get your kidneys working great to help dilute and excrete toxins.
6\. Take fiber every night before bed – This will **bulk up your stool** and help absorb and eliminate toxins in your stool.
7\. Practice deep breathing exercises – take 10 deep breathes at least 3 times per day. Try cold plunging. If you have ever done this, you know it will make you breathe deeply.

### MAKE THE TIME, HAVE DISCIPLINE AND BE CONSISTENT TO DEVELOP A ROUTINE​

This may seem overwhelming but it is quite simple.
If you wait until you have time to make these changes, it will never happen. You must make time, have discipline, be consistent to develop a routine. Once it becomes routine, it is part of your daily life. Your body will thank you for it.
So before you decide to join a gym, go on a diet or make any other change, focus first on detoxifying. The results from everything else you do will be much better.
I wish you all a very happy and properous new year. Our health is our most important asset and should be a top priority.

* * *

Dr. Nathan S. Bryan is a biochemist and physiologist that has more than 20 years of basic science and clinical research. His many seminal discoveries have resulted in dozens of issued U.S. and International patents. He is an international expert in nitric oxide and molecular medicine. Dr. Bryan is an innovator and successful entrepreneur who product technology is responsible for hundreds of millions of dollars in revenue worldwide. ### [Biohacking Your Way to Wellness - Small Biohacks That Make a Big Difference](https://drnathansbryan.com/biohacking-your-way-to-wellness/)

Discover the role of nitric oxide in biohacking and how we can optimize our own biology to combat disease and stay healthy.

 drnathansbryan.com

[](https://drnathansbryan.com/)

### DISPELLING CONFUSION REGARDING METHYLENE BLUE​

Let me dispel some confusion regarding a popular biohack called **methylene blue.** We have used methylene blue in the research lab for decades **to inhibit an enzyme called soluble guanylyl cyclase (sGC)**. sGC is a primary target for nitric oxide that leads to an increase in a second messenger called cGMP.

**This is the messenger that allows for smooth muscle relaxation and dilation of blood vessels.
This is also the target of drugs like Viagra for erectile dysfunction.

Inhibiting nitric oxide based signaling with methylene blue is not a good idea, especially long term or on a daily basis.** Obviously, these effects **are dose dependent** and micro-dosing with methylene blue may not have these effects but the mechanism of action is clear.

Anything that inhibits nitric oxide based signaling will lead to reduced blood flow, increased inflammation, oxidative stress, immune dysfunction and the early onset and progression of chronic disease.

Low dose methylene blue may work by hormesis. Hormesis is defined as a phenomenon in which a harmful substance gives stimulating and beneficial effects to living organisms when the quantity of the harmful substance is small.

The concept of hormesis is to periodically dose and this is not designed to be done daily or else the body does not have a chance to respond and upregulate dependent pathways. So, take caution in the use of methylene blue and don’t overdo it.
**I avoid it all together.**

## MY 10 PERSONAL DAILY BIOHACKS​

Many people ask about my personal routine or biohacks. I travel weekly, run 3 companies, manage and operate a working ranch with several hundred head of cattle and have two teenage boys involved in nearly every sport and somehow seem to manage a level of stress that would likely debilitate many others. Below is a list of my (mostly) daily routines.

1\. 16 hour daily fast
2\. Daily nitric oxide supplement ([www.NO2U.com](https://no2u.com/))
3\. At least half hour of physical activity, exercise and/or weights
4\. Infrared sauna every morning (160 degrees for 20-25 minutes)
5\. Daily supplements **including iodine** and many others
6\. Use of voltage/electrical devices ([www.senergy.us](https://senergy.us/))
**7\. Biweekly chiropractic** (I JanSz am doing this since I learned about chiropractors in about 1984)**8\. Weekly or biweekly massage** (I am still just thinking about, one day have to start)
9\. 6-8 hours of sleep every night
10\. Daily prayer

These simple biohacks seem to work for me.

I have also harvested my own stem cells, banked them and then had then injected back intravenously to facilitate healing and cellular regeneration. I have a hyperbaric oxygen chamber in my home so I also occasionally do hyperbaric oxygen.

All this may seem time consuming and burdensome and it is but I make time to invest in my own health. If I waited until I had time, it would not get done. I have not been sick or lost a day of work due to illness in over 20 years.

Everyone is different and I encourage all of you to explore what may work best for you. I also encourage you to attend some of these biohacking conferences. I’ll be attending and speaking at the [Biohacking Conference](https://biohackingconference.com/) in Orlando in June. Subscribe to biohacking focused podcasts such as [The Human Upgrade](https://daveasprey.com/category/podcasts/). Tune in this Friday April 7 for my interview with Dave Asprey.

The current health care system in America is not working and the management of chronic disease is worst than ever. We must learn how to take care of ourselves and avoid getting sick and going to the doctor.

Be pro-active instead of reactive. Our greatest asset is our health. The information and technology is available for all those who desire a change in their health. Educate yourself, ask questions and take action.

We are not promised tomorrow so make the best of every day.

—

### JanSz — 2023-09-05T00:01:04+0300

### TOP TEN REASONS HOW NITRIC OXIDE IMPROVES WOMEN’S HEALTH​

February 1, 2019
_By Dr. Nathan S. Bryan_

February is American Heart Health Month and it offers a critical education opportunity on how to address the number one killer of women worldwide. In the United States, almost one in four women dies from heart disease. Heart disease is the leading cause of death for women in the United States, even outpacing breast cancer. Although heart disease is sometimes thought of as a “man’s disease,” around the same number of women and men die each year of heart disease in the United States. Some types of heart problems are more common in women.

The fact that heart disease remains the number one killer of both men and women worldwide is simply unacceptable. We now have the tools to diagnose nitric oxide (NO) deficiency and we have the technology to restore NO production in all patients. The problem then is not lack of knowledge or lack of resources. The problem is lack of education and awareness of nitric oxide by both physicians and patients and not utilizing the tools and therapies that are readily available.

There are several known risk factors for developing cardiovascular disease (CVD). Risk factors are conditions or habits that make a person more likely to develop CVD and/or increase the chances that their existing disease will get worse. Known and important risk factors for CVD are high blood pressure (hypertension), hyperlipidemia (increased levels of certain lipoproteins and not cholesterol), diabetes and prediabetes, smoking, being overweight or obese, physical inactivity, family history of early heart disease, unhealthy diet, periodontal disease and age (55 or older for women i.e. post-menopause). There are others but these are the primary risk factors.

All the known risk factors all have a common denominator that is responsible for increased risk of CVD. That commonality is loss of nitric oxide (NO) production. It is known that if you can eliminate these known risk factors, your chances of developing CVD are much less and in fact perhaps even eliminated. Therefore, if you can restore the production of nitric oxide, you can offset the reasons these risk factors cause or accelerate heart disease.

Top Ten of How Nitric Oxide Improves Women’s Health — From preventing CVD breast cancer, better sex to more restful sleep to bone health and mental cognition, nitric oxide is the answer.

**10\. Nitric oxide improves sexual function and orgasms in women.** NO is responsible for regulating blood flow to the sex organs. For women to have an orgasm, there must be an increase in vaginal and labial pressure. This pressure is controlled by an increase in blood flow to the clitoris. Without nitric oxide, there cannot be an increase in blood flow to the clitoris and consequently no increase in pressure and no orgasm. Therefore, restoring NO production can improve blood flow to the pelvic region and sex organs and improve lubrication, desire and orgasms.

**9\. Improve the cardiovascular effects of estrogen**. One of the jobs of estrogen is to activate the production of nitric oxide through an enzyme called nitric oxide synthase (NOS). However, when the NOS enzyme becomes dysfunctional, it can no longer produce NO when activated by estrogen. As a result, cardiovascular problems begin to occur from an increase in blood pressure to loss of sexual function. Restoring the function of the enzyme and providing a source of NO will maintain and preserve the cardio-protective effects of estrogen even before menopause.

**8\. Improve athletic performance and exercise capacity.** It is known that moderate physical exercise can prevent heart disease and many other diseases. This is due to the production of NO. This is why exercise is medicine. However, when women do not exercise, they can develop endothelial dysfunction or loss of NO. When you lose the ability to produce NO, you become exercise intolerant and are unable to maintain any exercise regimen. Many people even get winded walking up a flight of stairs. This is due to loss of NO production. Restoring NO production will allow the blood vessels to deliver more oxygen and remove waste products to working muscles and improve exercise capacity.

**7\. Improves sleep.** Nitric oxide is produced within neurons of the brain that signal sleep and sleep patterns. However, when you lose the ability to produce NO, which occurs with age, stress, menopause and the presence of all the other risk factors mentioned above, sleep becomes disturbed. Restoring the production of NO will help restore healthy sleep patterns.

**6\. Improves healing and recovery from injury.** Our own resident stem cells are responsible for repairing injured or worn out cells and tissues. Nitric oxide is the signal that tells stem cells to mobilize and go to the site of injury and differentiate and repair dysfunctional cells and tissue. If your body can’t make NO, then the body loses the signal to repair itself. Restoring the production of NO will allow for much faster healing and quicker recovery from injury.

**5\. Puts out the fires of inflammation.** Inflammation is the root cause of many diseases including CVD and auto-immune disease. Nitric oxide is part of the acute immune and inflammatory response and is necessary for healing and combatting infection. The problem is that during chronic inflammation, NO is overproduced. The local overproduction of NO (either in the gut in auto-immunity or joints of rheumatoid arthritis) causes a feedback inhibition and shuts down NO production in all other tissues. The scientific literature is clear that restoring normal production of NO leads to anti-inflammatory responses. Any chronic inflammatory disease will benefit from restoring normal NO production.

**4\. Improves mental clarity and cognition**. Nitric oxide modulates norepinephrine, serotonin, dopamine, glutamate, the major neurotransmitters involved in the neurobiology of major depression. Without enough NO production, these important neurotransmitters are not properly regulated. On top of that, NO controls cerebral blood flow and regulates blood flow to specific regions of the brain when activated. It is known that vascular dementia, or Pre-Alzheimer’s, is due to insufficient blood flow to the prefrontal cortex of the brain due to insufficient NO. Restoring NO production will not only make you feel a better sense of well-being but also improve your memory and cognition.

**3\. Improve bone strength and prevent bone loss.** Bone is constantly being broken down and built back up through cells called osteoclasts and osteoblasts respectively. Nitric oxide controls the activity of both cell types. Post-menopausal women are at risk of developing osteoporosis due to loss of estrogen and loss of nitric oxide production. Restoring the production of NO will enable the osteoblasts and osteoclasts to perform their function and maintain healthy bones.

**2\. Maintain proper oxygenation and mitochondrial energy production.** Nitric oxide controls oxygen delivery and mitochondrial function for cellular energy production. Cancer is a disease that thrives under conditions of low oxygen and inefficient mitochondria. All cancers, including breast cancer, have dysfunctional and uncoupled mitochondria that inefficiently generate ATP or cellular energy. Cancer also lives in a low oxygen or hypoxic environment. Maintaining proper NO production will ensure proper oxygenation of cells and functional/coupled mitochondria that will efficiently generate cellular energy.

**1\. Prevent the number one killer, heart disease.** It is crystal clear that maintaining endothelial function and proper NO production can not only prevent heart disease but reverse and cure existing heart disease. NO prevents the inflammation, oxidative stress and immune dysfunction that occurs in heart disease.

If there is one thing you do, either as a New Years Resolution or as an action item for Heart Health Month, it should be to take steps to improve and restore your nitric oxide production. There is not a more important thing you can do for your own health. Please remember, prevention is much easier and cheaper than treatment. Maintaining proper NO levels addresses the five most common health concerns for women but also addresses the number one killer of men and women worldwide. Invest in your health and act to restore NO production now.

//////////////////////

### coho3131 — 2023-09-05T01:19:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/man-woman-nitric-oxide.28046/post-325945>

There are only two studies about Sodium Nitrite supplementation, and both are sponsored by the company that sells them, and authored by the owners and CEO of the company.

Tony Giordano is the president and CEO of TheraVasc, and Nathan Bryan is cofounder and CSO of Neogenis Labs. Nathan Bryan also receives royalties from patents from the University of Texas Health Science Center in Houston.

Sodium nitrite and placebo capsules were provided by Tony Giordano at TheraVasc. Nathan Bryan is an inventor on dozens of issued U.S and international patents on nitrite and nitric oxide compositions and receives royalties from these patents from the University of Texas. Nathan Bryan is also a Founder and shareholder in HumanN, Inc. Pneuma Nitric Oxide, LLC and Nitric Oxide Innovations, LLD.

### coho3131 — 2023-09-05T01:34:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/man-woman-nitric-oxide.28046/post-325948>

Industrial food companies add Sodium Nitrite to cured meats, bacon, sausage, ham, and smoked fish as a preservative to kill bacteria and fungus. There are established guidelines to limit the amount of nitrites that can be used by the food industry because of their potential toxicity, including increased risk of diabetes and cancers.
