> ## MY 10 PERSONAL DAILY BIOHACKS​
>
> Many people ask about my personal routine or biohacks. I travel weekly, run 3 companies, manage and operate a working ranch with several hundred head of cattle and have two teenage boys involved in nearly every sport and somehow seem to manage a level of stress that would likely debilitate many others. Below is a list of my (mostly) daily routines.
>
> 1\. 16 hour daily fast
> 2\. Daily nitric oxide supplement ([www.NO2U.com](https://no2u.com/))
> 3\. At least half hour of physical activity, exercise and/or weights
> 4\. Infrared sauna every morning (160 degrees for 20-25 minutes)
> 5\. Daily supplements **including iodine** and many others
> 6\. Use of voltage/electrical devices ([www.senergy.us](https://senergy.us/))
> **7\. Biweekly chiropractic** (I JanSz am doing this since I learned about chiropractors in about 1984)
> **8\. Weekly or biweekly massage** (I am still just thinking about, one day have to start)
> 9\. 6-8 hours of sleep every night
> 10\. Daily prayer
>
> These simple biohacks seem to work for me.
>
> I have also harvested my own stem cells, banked them and then had then injected back intravenously to facilitate healing and cellular regeneration. I have a hyperbaric oxygen chamber in my home so I also occasionally do hyperbaric oxygen.
>
> All this may seem time consuming and burdensome and it is but I make time to invest in my own health. If I waited until I had time, it would not get done. I have not been sick or lost a day of work due to illness in over 20 years.
>
> Everyone is different and I encourage all of you to explore what may work best for you. I also encourage you to attend some of these biohacking conferences. I’ll be attending and speaking at the [Biohacking Conference](https://biohackingconference.com/) in Orlando in June. Subscribe to biohacking focused podcasts such as [The Human Upgrade](https://daveasprey.com/category/podcasts/). Tune in this Friday April 7 for my interview with Dave Asprey.
>
> The current health care system in America is not working and the management of chronic disease is worst than ever. We must learn how to take care of ourselves and avoid getting sick and going to the doctor.
>
> Be pro-active instead of reactive. Our greatest asset is our health. The information and technology is available for all those who desire a change in their health. Educate yourself, ask questions and take action.
>
> We are not promised tomorrow so make the best of every day.
>
> —

Apart from something like aluminum influencing testosterone

[https://www.researchgate.net/publication/51515785\_Aluminum\_induced\_suppression\_of\_testosterone\_through\_nitric\_oxide\_production\_in\_male\_mice](https://www.researchgate.net/publication/51515785_Aluminum_induced_suppression_of_testosterone_through_nitric_oxide_production_in_male_mice)

whatever the response to the initial acute exposure of something, there's also the possibility that not producing NO becomes protective, for example to keep toxins out of the brain, or to stop the release of damaged or cancerous stem cells, or to suppress reproduction because the conditions aren't right, whether that be caused by psychological stress, aluminum or other toxins, or whatever else.....

No my limit isn't 80, that was just a comparable age to you. DH's mother died of a heart attack in her 50s, and his father lasted into his 80s but had end stage congestive heart failure, vascular dementia and the last diagnosis was a small tumour on the surface of the brain. His parents never owned a computer or a mobile phone. But with those added risk factors, I think DH would be nuts to start messing around with cialis or the N101 lozenges whatever his age. As for the list of foods, he could eat the right foods, absorb the right aminos, but if signalling is screwed from nnemf or aluminum (or stress from something else), the probability of collagen restoration or repair drops, and he may age faster from causes he wasn't aware of. Chances are that reproductive fitness would go first, which matches other wild mammals.

Your frontal lobes may be asking for more orgasms, but perhaps that doesn't match what the rest of your system (the bodymind) considers healthy.

>
> February 1, 2019
> _By Dr. Nathan S. Bryan_
>
> **2\. Maintain proper oxygenation and mitochondrial energy production.** Nitric oxide controls oxygen delivery and mitochondrial function for cellular energy production. Cancer is a disease that thrives under conditions of low oxygen and inefficient mitochondria. All cancers, including breast cancer, have dysfunctional and uncoupled mitochondria that inefficiently generate ATP or cellular energy. Cancer also lives in a low oxygen or hypoxic environment. Maintaining proper NO production will ensure proper oxygenation of cells and functional/coupled mitochondria that will efficiently generate cellular energy.

### JanSz — 2023-09-05T20:06:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/man-woman-nitric-oxide.28046/post-325986>

[https://twitter.com/DrNitric](https://twitter.com/DrNitric)

[https://twitter.com/DrNitric](https://twitter.com/DrNitric)

### NITRIC OXIDE – MASTER HORMONE AND HOLY GRAIL OF ANTI-AGING​

June 7, 2023[Blog](https://drnathansbryan.com/category/blog/)
It is the beginning of summer and many people are taking time for vacations. This also becomes a very busy time for medical and scientific conferences around the world.
I have just returned from speaking at the American Academy of Anti-Aging Medicine (A4M), one of the largest anti-aging medicine meetings and also the International Cell Surgical Conference, a major stem cell and regenerative medicine meeting. This year’s theme of A4M was inflammation, the major driver of chronic disease. The Cell Surgical meeting was everything about regenerative medicine and stem cells.
As I preparing my lectures for both meetings, it reminded me how critical and important nitric oxide is for anti-aging, inflammation and the ability to heal from our own stem cells. Therefore, I thought it was relevant to cover the topic in this month’s blog.

### NITRIC OXIDE IS THE MASTER HORMONE​

Most Americans know the importance of hormones. In fact, there is a huge industry of hormone replacement clinics around the world.
Everybody knows the older we get, the less our body produces natural hormones including testosterone in men and estrogen in women. The time the body stops making these hormones is called andropause in men and menopause in women.
There are amazing health benefits of hormone replacement when done correctly. There are many other important hormones that we should also consider such as thyroid hormone, cortisol, and many others.
**Most people do not think of nitric oxide as a hormone but back in 2007, my lab was the first to demonstrate that nitric oxide acted as a hormone.**
Hormones are chemical substances that act like messenger molecules in the body. After being made in one part of the body, they travel to other parts of the body to affect specific activities. Prior to our study in 2007 it was thought that nitric oxide could only act in the cell in which it was produced (autocrine) or a neighboring cell (paracrine).
Our seminal paper showed that nitric oxide produced in a single organ or compartment had systemic effects that could signal and protect all other organs. This was a game changer in nitric oxide biochemistry and physiology. But the question was how could it do this since nitric oxide, once produced, is gone is less than one second.
The answer is in the more stable metabolites it forms once produced in the body. Nitric oxide is converted to nitrite and also binds to glutathione once produced to form a molecule called S-nitrosoglutatione. **These two molecules extend the biological half life of nitric oxide from 1 millisecond out to tens of minutes and hours.** This allows nitric oxide to affect many other organs systemically and act as a hormone.

### THE ENDOTHELIUM, OUR LARGEST ORGAN AND ENDOCRINE GLAND​

The inner most cells of our blood vessels is called the endothelium. The endothelium is our largest organ and the main site of nitric oxide, making it also an endocrine gland that secretes NO gas upon stimulation.
Every organ is the body is controlled by its own blood supply. The blood supply to every organ is regulated by the function of its own endothelial cells and its ability to produce nitric oxide.
We’ve known for centuries now that every major chronic disease is characterized by a diminished blood supply and reduced circulation to that organ. We see it in Alzheimers’s Disease, heart disease, kidney disease, lung disease, gastrointestinal disease and others. Without sufficient circulation, oxygen and nutrients cannot be delivered to these organs and they begin to fail. There is increased inflammation, oxidative stress and immune dysfunction that results from decreased blood flow and decreased nitric oxide production.
Similar to decreased testosterone production in the testes of men or decreased estrogen production in the ovaries in women, if there is decreased nitric oxide production in the endothelium, we get symptoms and the onset of many diseases.
Also similarly, if we replace missing hormones and restore the body’s normal production, we can reverse many symptoms and actually stop the progression of disease.
That has been my objective and strategy for the past 20 years. We must replace and restore nitric oxide production in order to combat the effects of aging and age-related diseases.
The question has been, how do we do that safely and effectively?

### PROVIDE THE BODY WITH NITRIC OXIDE​

Based on our 2007 study in the Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, we proved that all we had to do was generate nitric oxide in a single biological compartment and then the hormone effects would take effect. We don’t have to give testosterone shots in the testes for it to work. We can give it in the muscle and then it will do its job.
Similarly, we don’t have to only produce nitric oxide in the endothelium, we can produce it anywhere in the body and it will have its effects. That is why I chose the mouth or oral cavity as a compartment to generate nitric oxide gas.
I was the first and only person in the world to produce a solid dose form of nitric oxide gas. The basis is that an orally disintegrating lozenge would slowly dissolve in the mouth and as it is dissolving active ingredients would come together to produce nitric oxide gas. Once we do that, the nitric oxide and signaling molecules produced from NO gas go systemic and nitric oxide provides its hormone function.
The health benefits are measurable and profound. We get better circulation, better oxygen delivery, better stem cell function, reduced inflammation, reduced oxidative stress and better immune function. It basically reverses all of the known causes and consequences of all major chronic diseases.

### HOW DO WE REPLACE NITRIC OXIDE HORMONE?​

There are hundreds of so-called nitric oxide products on the market. Nearly all of them provide no therapeutic benefits since they do not produce nitric oxide themselves. That is the basis of my product technology that I termed “NITRICEUTICALS”.
My products produce nitric oxide gas providing benefits. This is only achieved through an orally disintegrating tablet or through our fermented beet powder that produces nitric oxide gas when placed in water. This cannot be achieved through capsules, gummies, chews that many other companies are selling.
If you want the benefits of nitric oxide, then you must find a product that produces it for you. If your body cannot make nitric oxide, then my product technology does it for you. It’s that simple. Remember the quote by Stephen Hawking “The greatest enemy of knowledge is not ignorance, it is the illusion of knowledge.” Many companies out there have an illusion of knowledge and yet others deliberately try to deceive their customers.
Ignorance is curable through education and the correct information. That is my goal to educate and inform you so you can make informed decisions on what is best for your health and the health of your family. After all, there is nothing more important than our own health.
—
Dr. Nathan S. Bryan is a biochemist and physiologist that has more than 20 years of basic science and clinical research. His many seminal discoveries have resulted in dozens of issued U.S. and International patents. He is an international expert in nitric oxide and molecular medicine. Dr. Bryan is an innovator and successful entrepreneur who product technology is responsible for hundreds of millions of dollars in revenue worldwide. This content is not intended to replace medical advice from a physician.
More information:

[www.drnathansbryan.com](https://drnathansbryan.com/)
[https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCtftGy8e0r9DO8ActcyGi4w](https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCtftGy8e0r9DO8ActcyGi4w)
[www.N1O1.com](https://n1o1.com/)
[www.NO2U.com](https://no2u.com/)
[www.nitricoxideinnovations.com](https://www.nitricoxideinnovations.com/)

**Below is my old answer.**

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=325755)
>
> Indeed 2 milliseconds is likely correct.
> But concentrating on it gives wrong impression about what is happening.
> To have this 2 milliseconds NO must first be present and dissolved in the blood and available when those 2 milliseconds are needed to come about.
>
> To check if the supplementation is actually working, I think it would be better to look at different example.
> Say someone with high blood pressure starting to take it.
> After xx while see if his/her blood pressure is any lower (without blood pressure medications).
>
> There is already testing available. It is called Endo-PAT.
> But that test actually check veins if they increase volume under stress.
> So basically, to confirm availability of NO in the blood.
> I think 10% volume increase is the maximum expected, or maybe it is the desirable optimum.
>
>
> ### [Endo-PAT Cardiac Testing Doctor Manhattan NYC](https://newyorkcardiac.com/endopat-test#:~:text=EndoPAT%20testing%20is%20used%20to%20detect%20changes%20in,health%20assessment%20for%20both%20large%20and%20small%20arteries.)
>
> Endo-PAT Cardiac Testing for heart attack & stroke prevention. The test provides the earliest method to clinically detect cardiovascular disease.
>
>  newyorkcardiac.com

### NITRIC OXIDE PRODUCTS – ARE YOU CONFUSED YET? HOW TO KNOW WHAT PRODUCT TO BUY AND WHAT PRODUCTS TO AVOID​

**March 1, 2021**[Blog](https://drnathansbryan.com/category/blog/)
Nitric oxide (NO) products have become very popular over the past 10 years. In fact, if you search nitric oxide products you get over 35 million results in just 1.08 seconds. So how do you know which product may be best for you? This is not easy since in the dietary supplement space, every label says the same things and offers the same claims and benefits statement.

Nitric oxide is gaining awareness in the consumer world because of its potent physiological effects. NO is what regulates blood flow and nutrient and oxygen delivery to every cell in the entire body. I would say that is a pretty important function.

Regulation of blood flow affects every organ system in the body. **It is the loss of regulation of blood flow secondary to loss of nitric oxide production that is responsible for every major disease known.**

For example, if you lose the regulation of blood flow to the sex organs, you develop sexual dysfunction. If you lose regulation of blood flow to the heart, you develop heart disease. If you lose the regulation of blood flow to the brain, you develop vascular dementia, Alzheimers and many other neurological diseases.

Every cell in the body requires certain nutrients and metabolites in order to do its job. It is the job of nitric oxide to open up the blood vessels that serve those individual tissues and cells to make sure each cell gets what it needs to do its job.

### 4 CATEGORIES OF NITRIC OXIDE PRODUCTS​

In normal healthy individuals, our body makes sufficient nitric oxide to get the job done. However, as we age our body loses its ability to product nitric oxide. **This is termed endothelial dysfunction.**

Over time, if not corrected, endothelial dysfunction turns into full blown disease. It is recognized that production of NO is the main function of our **endothelial cells (the cells that line every blood vessel throughout the body)**. This is where nitric oxide based products can provide benefit if they can restore NO production or provide a source of NO.

After more than two decades of research in the NO field, I have learned that nitric oxide products fall into one of four categories.

1.  **Arginine/Citrulline products** – These product first hit the market in the late 1990’s after the Nobel Prize was awarded for the discovery of NO and the realization that L-arginine is the substrate to make NO. The enzyme nitric oxide synthase is what is responsible for converting L-arginine into NO with L-citrulline as a byproduct. L-arginine and L-citrulline are both semi-essential amino acids, meaning that our body makes these amino acids from normal metabolism but we also get both of them from the breakdown of proteins we ingest through out diet. In people that are NO deficient and have endothelial dysfunction, they already have normal levels of L-arginine and L-citrulline. **The availability of these amino acids is not their problem. The problem is that there body has lost the ability to convert L-arginine into NO due to loss of function of the enzyme.** Therefore giving more L-arginine or L-citrulline does nothing to the function of the enzyme. You are just giving the body more of a substrate it cannot convert into NO. Based on the science, Arginine/Citrulline based product have very limited utility in restoring NO production in patients that are NO deficient.
2.  Beet Root based products – Beet based products are everywhere, especially in the sports and endurance world. Beets became popular around 2012 when it was realized many Olympic athletes were drinking beet root juice to enhance their performance. The active ingredient in some beets responsible for the blood pressure lowering effects and enhancement of performance is nitrate. However, **most of the beet products on the market contain no detectable nitrate. I’ve tested all of them in my lab. As a result, they provide absolutely no benefit other than turning your urine and feces a pink color** causing some anxiety in many folks. If a beet root product does not have a standardized amount of nitrate on the label, avoid it. It has no nitric oxide function. It is in fact a “dead beet”. Beet root does not equal nitrate. There are some beet products that actually work but it is not always apparent. Labels are even confusing and misleading. There are superbeets labels that have patents listed and there are superbeet products that do not have patents listed on them which means some superbeet products deliver on nitric oxide and other superbeet products do not deliver on nitric oxide. Pay attention to patents listed on the labels.
3.  **Nitrate based products** – Some companies have become clever and now offer a standardized nitrate amount in the form of capsules or even standardized in food products, including beets. **However, nitrate is inert in humans. Humans are unable to metabolize this molecule.** The human body requires the presence of nitrate reducing bacteria to metabolize nitrate into nitrite and nitric oxide. If you don’t have the right nitrate reducing bacteria, you get no nitric oxide benefit from nitrate. In America, over 200 million people use antiseptic mouthwash on a daily basis. These mouthwashes have been shown to kill the good nitrate reducing bacteria and actually cause an increase in blood pressure. Many more have active oral infections that disrupt the normal oral microbiome and disrupt nitrate reduction to NO. As a result, a nitrate based product may only work in less than a third of the population. Nitrate is an important nutrient, but only if you are able to metabolize it to NO. Most people cannot and that is why they are nitric oxide deficient. Nitrate does not equal nitric oxide.
4.  **Nitrite and Nitric oxide generating products** – People become nitric oxide deficient because they have lost the ability to convert L-arginine into NO and/or they have do not have the ability to utilize nitrate from their diet to make NO due to mouthwash use, antacids, poor diet, etc. So how do you overcome this and restore NO even in this population? You have to do something different. This is where my two decades of research comes in. If your body cannot make NO, then we have to do it for you. The only way to do that is to provide nitric oxide directly. **A second and fundamental function is to restore the nitrate reducing bacteria and restore the function of the enzyme to convert L-arginine into NO. There are only a couple of products that do this and they are protected by my patents and have multiple published clinical trials to prove they work.** These product work in every single person that consumes them.

In my opinion, every single person on this planet should be taking a nitric oxide boosting product since it is the single most important molecule produced in the human body. However, you should not waste your money on products that do not work.

### HOW TO FIND NO PRODUCTS THAT WORK​

When shopping for NO products, do your research and look for the science behind the product and don’t be fooled by creative marketing. L-arginine, beet root and nitrate based products have limited utility.

Characteristics of a good nitric oxide product:

1.  Look for a patent on label or at least some demonstration that it generates NO.
2.  Ask the manufacturer if there are any published clinical studies on the actual product, not an ingredient, but on the actual product.
3.  Is the formulator/inventor an expert in the nitric oxide field? Have they published any scientific papers in the field. There are a lot of talking heads out there for companies that have never published a single scientific paper.

I get emails and calls every single day asking these questions so I thought it would be helpful to provide some clarity.

Companies will continue to try to capitalize on the ever growing nitric oxide field. Educated and informed consumers will drive product demand.

**In full disclosure, I obviously have a financial interest in all of my patents and/or any product that utilizes my patents.** However this is based on over twenty years of basic science and clinical research and dozens of published papers in the field. My objective is to educate the world on the importance of nitric oxide and develop and inform on safe and effective nitric oxide product technology.

Be smart and don’t be fooled by deceptive marketing.

Science matters.

**What is claimed is:
1**. A method of reducing a patient's C-reactive protein level, comprising:
administering to the patient a composition in a form of a lozenge dissolvable in the mouth, the composition comprising:
from about 40 weight parts to about 1000 weight parts of a botanical nitrate source;
from about 20 weight parts to about 500 weight parts of a botanical source of nitrite reduction activity;
from about 20 weight parts to about 500 weight parts L-citrulline; and
from about 4 weight parts to about 100 weight parts of a nitrite salt.

**2**. The method of claim 1, wherein the botanical nitrate source is selected from the group consisting of beet root, artichoke, holy basil, gingko, and mixtures thereof and is present at about 200 weight parts;
the botanical source of nitrite reduction activity is selected from the group consisting of hawthorn berry, Schisandra, green tea, beet root, pine bark, and mixtures thereof and is present at about 100 weight parts;
the L-citrulline is present at about 100 weight parts; and
the nitrite salt is selected from the group consisting of sodium nitrite, potassium nitrite, and mixtures thereof and is present at about 20 weight parts;
and the composition further comprises:
about 1 weight part vitamin B12 in a form selected from the group consisting of methylcobalamin, cyanocobalamin, and mixtures thereof;
about 100 weight parts vitamin C in a form selected from the group consisting of magnesium ascorbate, ascorbic acid, and mixtures thereof; and
from about 50 weight parts to about 1500 weight parts of one or more other ingredients selected from the group consisting of mannitol, xylitol, sorbitol, other sugar alcohols, cellulose, cellulose esters, cellulose ethers, starch, other polysaccharides, oligosaccharides, disaccharides, saccharides, gelatin, polyvinylpyrrolidone, polyethylene glycol, other binders, flavorants, colorants, magnesium stearate, other antiadherent agents, other stearate salts, sweeteners, silica, and other lubricants.

**3**. The method of claim 1, wherein the nitrite salt is selected from the group consisting of sodium nitrite, potassium nitrite, magnesium nitrite, calcium nitrite, and mixtures thereof.

**4**. The method of claim 1, wherein the botanical source of nitrite reduction activity is selected from the group consisting of hawthorn berry, Schisandra, green tea, beet root, pine bark, holy basil, _Gymnema sylvestre_, L9H, ashwagandha root, _salvia_, St. John wort, broccoli, _stevia_, spinach, gingko, kelp, tribulus, eleuthero, epimedium, eucommia, _rhodiola_, green tea, codonopsys, _Panax ginseng, astragalus_, dodder seed, _cordyceps_, berries, tea, beer, grapes, wine, olive oil, chocolate, cocoa, coffee, walnuts, peanuts, borojo, pomegranates, popcorn, yerba mate, and mixtures thereof.

**5**. The method of claim 1, wherein: the botanical nitrate source is selected from the group consisting of beet root, artichoke, holy basil, gingko, and mixtures thereof and is present at about 200 weight parts; the botanical source of nitrite reduction activity is selected from the group consisting of hawthorn berry, Schisandra, green tea, beet root, pine bark, and mixtures thereof and is present at about 100 weight parts; and the nitrite salt is selected from the group consisting of sodium nitrite, potassium nitrite, and mixtures thereof and is present at about 20 weight parts; and the composition further comprises: about 100 weight parts L-citrulline; about 1 weight part vitamin B12 in a form selected from the group consisting of methylcobalamin, cyanocobalamin, and mixtures thereof; and about 100 weight parts vitamin C in a form selected from the group consisting of magnesium ascorbate, ascorbic acid, and mixtures thereof.

**6**. The method of claim 2, wherein the vitamin B12 is in a form selected from the group consisting of methylcobalamin, cyanocobalamin, and mixtures thereof.

**7**. The method of claim 2, wherein the vitamin C is in a form selected from the group consisting of magnesium ascorbate, sodium ascorbate, potassium ascorbate, ascorbic acid, and mixtures thereof.

**8**. The method of claim 1, further comprising from about 20 weight parts to about 1000 weight parts L-arginine.

**9**. The method of claim 1, further comprising from about from about 20 weight parts to about 500 weight parts of a nitrate salt selected from the group consisting of sodium nitrate, potassium nitrate, and mixtures thereof.

**10**. The method of claim 1, wherein the lozenge weighs from about 600 mg to about 2000 mg.

**11**. The method of claim 1, wherein the administering is performed once or twice daily.

My sausages/jerk's recipe:
2 teaspoons of curing salt per 10 pounds of meat.
curing salt has 6.25% of Sodium nitrite.
On the label they say that sodium nitrite can be toxic,
but thankx to Bryan we know that actually it is vital.
The whole recipe has
10tsp=180mg of salt
2 tsp of curing salt
So, at the max I could replace the 10tsp with curing salt.
Before 2tsp now 12 tsp, 12/2=6
Previously according to Bryan I was getting 5% Nitric Oxide from eating cured meat, now 6x more, so 30%.
The 5% is from reading bottom of page #64 on the picture on previous post ($100).
Wonder, wonder, is that a good way to raise NO?

> **What is claimed is:

> administering to the patient a composition in a form of a lozenge dissolvable in the mouth, the composition comprising:

>

The method of claim 1, wherein: the botanical nitrate source is selected from the group consisting of beet root, artichoke, holy basil, gingko, and mixtures thereof and is present at about 200 weight parts; the botanical source of nitrite reduction activity is selected from the group consisting of hawthorn berry, Schisandra, green tea, beet root, pine bark, and mixtures thereof and is present at about 100 weight parts; and the nitrite salt is selected from the group consisting of sodium nitrite, potassium nitrite, and mixtures thereof and is present at about 20 weight parts; and the composition further comprises: about 100 weight parts L-citrulline; about 1 weight part vitamin B12 in a form selected from the group consisting of methylcobalamin, cyanocobalamin, and mixtures thereof; and about 100 weight parts vitamin C in a form selected from the group consisting of magnesium ascorbate, ascorbic acid, and mixtures thereof.

The lozenges have to be dissolved on the tongue, not swallowed whole. The botanical nitrate sources listed in 4 and 5 would normally be ingested as foods or drinks that would be sampled by the tongue and (light emitting) oral microbiome, (which historically wouldn't have been upset by mouthwashes etc. ) They could be got from different foods based on latitude and culture, such as the Mediterranean diet, the whole food plant based diet, William Li's Eat to Beat Disease, probably TCM etc. I haven't read his book but presumably the "dietary strategies" on the cover means he's included those foods.

The dietary sources wouldn't need to immediately produce NO, in fact to compensate for periods of hibernation or hunger, its probably useful some become part of a storage reservoir, and I'd want to know how the minimal quantities in a lozenge compared to the amounts available in normal dietary intake of those foods.

This paper talks about NO in relation to light, including red and NIR, the storage reservoir, but without lozenges or using NO creams for wound healing.

At first run through where I would challenge the paper is on figure 1, which has endogenous NO coming from arginine, but doesn't account for the endogenous frequencies of light in signalling, or uncoupling of mitochondria. (The O2 and arginine is interesting though in relation to hypoxia .....)

### [Light-induced nitric oxide release in the skin beyond UVA and blue light: Red & near-infrared wavelengths](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1089860321000914)

Nitric oxide (NO) is omnipresent in the body and synthesized by 3 isoenzymes (nNOS, eNOS and iNOS), all detected in human skin. NO can be stored in a …

 www.sciencedirect.com

### Dennis Clark — 2023-09-06T19:46:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/man-woman-nitric-oxide.28046/post-326050>

[@JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) [@Sue-UK](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9658/) This thread is overwhelming. Lots to study and know. The challenge is finding the best action steps buried in so much information (kind of like Jack's blogs!). JanSz, I did get those NO test strips and found - nothing useful. Of course, they don't actually test for NO; they test for nitrite. The assumption is that salivary nitrite levels predict gastric NO production once the nitrite is swallowed. Maybe true, maybe not. (My level was between 'low' and 'optimal' on the color scale.) Testing often provides a surrogate for health. Health in this case would jump all the way to vascular conditions, among others. At least as as far as it relates to the main production of NO via eNOS in blood vessels. The oral microbiome, fut health and the gastric microbiome are apparently heavily involved.

Although Nathan Bryan comes across as a one-trick pony - it's a BIG pony! I don't object to his business model, as some here do, since he has such an extensive publication record. Hey - scientists have to make a living, too! (Props from a fellow scientist here.)

One thing coming through this thread is anti-aging. We are all in search of extending our healthspans as best we can. In other words, we want to juke Mother Nature into thinking we're still worthwile beyond our reproductive years. If the official definition of a human generation is correct (i.e., 25 years), staying healthy 'til 50, 75, or 100 is more and more like a game of whack-a-mole.

As for my next action step - since test strips don't really tell me anything useful - is simply to get a lozenge product from Bryan's company and see whether I can make any observable change in my health. Health is the ultimate test of ... well, health. I already have a number of circulation issues I can follow: hands going numb easier than ever, ED that comes and goes, BP high-normal (usually ca. 125/80), ankle edema (although just as likely a lymphatic issue). At least those are some things I can assess at home.

Oh, by the way, reports about organic veggies having lower nitrate content make sense. The most common fertilizers for non-organic veggies provide lots of (ammonium) nitrate. Non-organic veggies should therefore have lots more nitrate.

Just a few thought for now.

Use 4x daily.

### JanSz — 2023-09-06T22:33:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/man-woman-nitric-oxide.28046/post-326057>

This is about skin care.

### [Dr. Nathan Bryan: “Smile every chance to get; Being happy and smiling exudes beauty”](https://medium.com/authority-magazine/dr-nathan-bryan-smile-every-chance-to-get-being-happy-and-smiling-exudes-beauty-186a66bc965a)

Smile every chance to get. Being happy and smiling exudes beauty. People want to be around smiley happy people and people tend to avoid…

 medium.com

**You are an expert about beauty. Can you share 5 ideas that anyone can use “to feel beautiful”? (Please share a story or example for each.)**

1.  Smile every chance to get. Being happy and smiling exudes beauty. **People want to be around smiley happy people** and people tend to avoid those that aren’t happy and don’t smile.
2.  Drink plenty of pure, clean water. We need hydration for our body to work. However, municipal water is toxic with fluoride, chlorine, drug metabolites and toxic chemicals. Drink **2–4 liters** of filtered clean water every day.
3.  **Sweat every day.** Part of the way our body detoxifies itself is through sweating. However, hardly anyone sweats anymore. We need to sweat to rid the toxins from our body and from our skin. I recommend using an infrared sauna. It provides energy and heat, so the body gets the energy it needs and the heat allows us to sweat to eliminate toxins.
4.  **Walk barefoot** on the ground for at least 10 minutes every day. The Earth is a big magnet that gives off electrons as energy. Our body needs energy for the cells to do their job.
5.  Get at least **20 minutes of exercise daily**. We need to keep moving. Exercise helps stimulate nitric oxide production, protects the cardiovascular system, helps us lose weight and it allows us to sweat, all of which are important.

These five things will not only make you “feel” beautiful, but scientific evidence reveals it will make you healthy on the inside and outside.

////////////////////////

**“Do today what others won’t
so you can do tomorrow what others can’t”.**

Totally agree about 'organic' BS. Ditto for 'all-natural'. (Hey, snake venom is all natural. So is strychnine!) Oh, and about my worry about arginine not working - it was based on how arginine drives hGH synthesis, which is limited to folks under the age of about 50. The NOS oxidation of arginine is completely different. No worries!

N101 from Bryan has morphed into a name change to NO2U ([https://n1o1.com/product/nitric-oxide-lozenges-bryan-nitriceuticals/](https://n1o1.com/product/nitric-oxide-lozenges-bryan-nitriceuticals/)). Amazon still lists N101 - and offers $50 bucks off when getting an Amazon credit card, so I'll start there. Looking forward to biohacking with it!

Oh, and regarding polyphenolics, the highest levels acc. to James Duke's Phytochemical Database are found in tea (see Camellia sinensis: [https://phytochem.nal.usda.gov/phytochem/chemicals/show/60621](https://phytochem.nal.usda.gov/phytochem/chemicals/show/60621)), with cacao (Theobroma cacao) coming in second.

### Dennis Clark — 2023-09-08T00:49:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/man-woman-nitric-oxide.28046/post-326115>

**A curiosity:**

I was noodling around looking for less expensive equivalents for NO2U (formerly N101). The closest I found, which is pretty close, is Neo40 ([https://humann.com/pages/get\_neo40-2](https://humann.com/pages/get_neo40-2)). The price for the same number of doses is about the same, a little less for Neo40.

So far so good. The real curiosity came when I found an earlier article co-authored by Bryan, evaluating Neo40: [https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/21530799/](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/21530799/)

And it got even curiouser when I discovered Bryan to be the founder and inventor at the Neo40 company, HumanN. It's listed in his LinkedIn profile as 2009 to the present (not sure if you can access it here: [https://www.linkedin.com/in/drnathansbryan/](https://www.linkedin.com/in/drnathansbryan/)).

Seems like he invented his own current competition for NO2U. And he's still at that earlier company, as far as I can tell I.e., '2009 to present').

Interesting, eh?

BTW, that article is behind a pay wall, so I got it through my old university library account. The pdf file is too big to upload here, so I uploaded in onto Google Drive. If I did it correctly (never a sure thing), you can access it here: [https://drive.google.com/file/d/1UiMzdS4AhmEPIeIhH4uukA53WD5aEdfH/view?usp=drive\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1UiMzdS4AhmEPIeIhH4uukA53WD5aEdfH/view?usp=drive_link)

Let me know if that doesn't work and I'll figure out another strategy.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-09-09T19:30:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/man-woman-nitric-oxide.28046/post-326179>

Keep in mind that certain things put on the skin are absorbed 10x more readily into the system than if ingested. As will almost all things there is a risk reward equation to be considered. If I had a hole in my face the risk/reward is VERY different than having a few wrinkles. Be an informed consumer, read and understand labels/ingredients. Just one of the ingredients of the NO cream:

Phenoxyethanol is found in many natural skin care products but not many understand the dangers of phenoxyethanol. It is increasingly turning up in cosmetic products, many of which are labelled natural or organic. Phenoxyethanol is used as a preservative that extends the shelf life of cosmetic products. It is the popular alternative to parabens.

The side effects of phenoxyethanol have come to public attention in the US when the FDA issued a warning about its use in a cream, called Mommy Bliss, for nursing mothers. The FDA warned that phenoxyethanol can cause shut down of the central nervous system, vomiting and contact dermatitis.

The product’s [Material Safety Data Sheet (MSDS)](https://pubchem.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/compound/2-phenoxyethanol) says that phenoxyethanol is harmful if swallowed, inhaled or absorbed through the skin and that it can cause reproductive damage.

If the mammalian body is supposed to make NO either photoelectrically, and/or _from_ nitrates/nitrate, what does missing the upstream part of the sequence do? Nitrite and nitrates come packaged with other things in nature, just one example being chlorophyll.

Although its probably built into the blueprint for all mammals, for a diurnal mammal with solar panels, to me the ability to make NO photochemically or photoelectrically makes food a secondary route in terms of NO, for example "rainy season" or in a lower quantum yield season that still provides natural sources of the foods supplying the compounds. If the primary photoelectric or photochemical pathway is covered, apart from a secondary pathway for NO, there is value in the other compounds in the whole plant foods that can act as signalling molecules to the microbiome or the mitochondria at the time they are seasonally available. A few added vitamins to the supplement aren't doing the same things.

If humans relied totally on dietary sources, we wouldn't survive seasonal reductions from the photosynthetic food web, or periods of fasting or starvation. Other mammals that get electrons from grounding during hibernation are also not reliant on daily supplies year round to reduce inflammation. As a species I don't think the biochemistry route of ingested nitrites and nitrates alone could support the year round possibilities from having frontal lobes that encourage us to explore and adapt to our environment beyond the capabilities of other mammals. His bit about NO preventing inflammation from a poor diet may be one thing, but even if someone cleans up their diet, will it mean they can reach optimal brain function to do things like exercise free will to make better decisions etc, and the ultimate for me is will using his products prevent me from developing AD, or will it have me focusing financial resources on the wrong things ....?

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-alzheimers-disease-a-photoelectric-disease-too.22868/page-3#post-324464](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-alzheimers-disease-a-photoelectric-disease-too.22868/page-3#post-324464)

_"When we are missing UV and/or IR light for any reason, these photoelectric and photochemical are not made. When full spectrum sunlight is absent in someone who is chronically stressed for any reason, sleep cannot be induced by the adenosine chemical signal from the hypocretin neurons because the incident light signal of blue blocks it. **What else blocks cellular regeneration by circadian de-coupling? Lack of full spectrum solar exposure is the most common reason and most overlooked issue in all of medicine these days.** Proper ocular melatonin cycle requires that these two frequencies (UV/IR) of light be present to stimulate the regeneration processes in the eye during daytime. It also requires ABSENCE of blue 400-465nm at sunset!!!! When these things are off the result always = **INFLAMMATION = too many protons and/or not enough electrons at the mitochondrial cellular level.** If you think about your childhood, when you spent the day at the park of the beach, you might remember how easy it was to fall asleep and get a sunburn. The reason is simple, sunlight induces sleep because the regeneration pathways that use melanopsin need daylight to regenerate. When you did fall asleep, the redness of your skin did not come from the thermal burn, but it was from the increased blood flow due to the release of nitric oxide that acted to bring the arterioles of the dermis layers to the surface. **This is a natural photochemical change induced by sunlight** to allow the skin to absorb the UVA and UVB light at the surface. UVA and UVB light does not penetrate deep. **To absorb the UV light we need the circulatory system to come from the dermis because UV light does not penetrate skin more than a millimeter. The NO engorges and vasodilates the arterioles containing the RBC’s. The RBC’s are filled with hemoglobin and porphyrins that absorb both UV and IR frequencies. Form meets function photoelectrically. "**_

It doesn't mean NO lozenges aren't useful medically, or temporarily. The idea of using creams chronically for cosmetic purposes in a way that blocks oxygen or the skins ability to make NO photochemically or photoelectrically doesn't make a lot of sense, particularly with the added effect of clothing covering areas not being cosmetically targeted. In the Bredeson AD protocols, getting toxins out is an important strategy, so putting chemicals on the skin that have been red flagged for toxicity and that I wouldn't eat might just be adding to the toxic load of an already less optimal energy system. Over time this could potentially make a big difference to the rate of cognitive decline, even if my face is less cosmetically challenged.  I've been hacking oxygenated spring water very cautiously, and have seen benefits to my skin and cognition.

As for the nitric oxide cream, PBM has been used in Europe for years for wound healing and cosmetic uses, often with an oxygen water spray, where the by product is NO. If the sun is out, my own PBM device (which is used in clinics in Europe for wound healing etc) doesn't get used, as its too easy for it to become a lifestyle choice when better (outside) is available to me....:

Of the various phosphodiesterase-5 inhibitors -

-   Sildenafil: 25 mg to 100 mg orally administered 1 hour before intercourse
-   Tadalafil: 5 mg to 20 mg orally administered 1 hour before intercourse
-   Vardenafil: 10 mg to 20 mg orally administered 1 hour before intercourse

Tadalafil has a prolonged half-life, with a low volume of distribution, slow hepatic clearance, and approximately 80% bioavailability in human.
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC6321473/#:~:text=Tadalafil also has a prolonged,approximately 80% bioavailability in human](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC6321473/#:~:text=Tadalafil%20also%20has%20a%20prolonged,approximately%2080%25%20bioavailability%20in%20human).

An _**advanced third generation**_ solid dispersion of TDL comprising polymer in combination with a Self Microemulsifying Composition of TDL (TDL-SMEC-SD) was coated onto rapidly disintegrating inert tablet cores which disintegrated rapidly in water to release SD as a film. TDL-SMEC-SD exhibited rapid in-vitro release and _**bioenahcement**_ with high relative _**bioavailability**_ and improved therapy.
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC7032132/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC7032132/)

The discovery of phosphodiesterase 5 inhibitors (PDE5 inhibitors) for causing the erection of the penis was accidental, noticed as a side effect while being administered to investigate their ability to treat hypertension and angina.
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK549843/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK549843/)

Also note: the side-effect of Tadalafil does not injure the retina.

### JanSz — 2023-09-13T01:27:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/man-woman-nitric-oxide.28046/post-326301>

I asked AI for:
find place where dr. nathan s. bryan talks about glycocalyx
and the answer I got is:
Dr. Nathan S. Bryan is a leading expert in the field of Nitric Oxide. He has had the privilege of working with each of the three Nobel Laureates who discovered this Miracle Molecule1. Dr. Bryan’s research and experience spans over 20 years2. He has written a blog post on his website about nitric oxide products3. However, I could not find any information about glycocalyx on his website or in my search results.

...
what is glycocalyx

The glycocalyx, also known as the pericellular matrix and sometimes cell coat, is a glycoprotein and glycolipid covering that surrounds the cell membranes of bacteria, epithelial cells, and other cells1. It was described in a review article in 19701.

The glycocalyx consists of several carbohydrate moieties of membrane glycolipids and glycoproteins, which serve as backbone molecules for support1. Generally, the carbohydrate portion of the glycolipids found on the surface of plasma membranes helps these molecules contribute to cell–cell recognition, communication, and intercellular adhesion1.

The glycocalyx is a type of identifier that the body uses to distinguish between its own healthy cells and transplanted tissues, diseased cells, or invading organisms1. Included in the glycocalyx are cell-adhesion molecules that enable cells to adhere to each other and guide the movement of cells during embryonic development1.

The glycocalyx plays a major role in regulation of endothelial vascular tissue, including the modulation of red blood cell volume in capillaries1. **It is present throughout a diverse range of microvascular beds (capillaries) and macrovessels (arteries and veins)1**. The glycocalyx also consists of a wide range of enzymes and proteins that regulate leukocyte and thrombocyte adherence, since its principal role in the vasculature is to maintain plasma and vessel-wall homeostasis1.
///////////////////////

### [Glycocalyx - Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Glycocalyx#Disruption_and_disease)

 en.wikipedia.org

[@JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) My interest meets yours

I've not gone through the whole paper, but the illustrations make me think it might be worth a look at.

### **Illustration of the devastating effects of Aluminum on a typical cell related to sulfate inactivation, G-protein signaling, and calmodulin signaling. (a) A healthy cell without Al contamination. eNOS, attached to the membrane at a caveola, produces sulfate, which maintains a healthy glycocalyx with sufficient negative charge. (b) Al binds to the sulfates, eliminating the negative charge, which allows cytokines to penetrate through the glycocalyx, activating G-protein coupled receptor signaling cascades. AlF 4 - disrupts the signal, acting as a phosphate mimic, and Al binds to CaM, inducing eNOS detachment from the membrane. Phosphorylation cascades activate eNOS to produce abundant NO released into the cytoplasm, instead of producing sulfate to enrich the glycocalyx.**​

**AI** is Searching for: nitric oxide menopause vaginal dryness hot flashes

Vaginal dryness is a common symptom of menopause, which is caused by a **decrease in estrogen levels** 321.
Nitric oxide is a molecule that plays a role in the regulation of blood flow and vascular function.
While there is some evidence to suggest that nitric oxide may play a role in vaginal lubrication and blood flow, more research is needed to fully understand its effects on menopausal vaginal dryness 3.

Hot flashes are commonly associated with menopause, which is a natural biological process that marks the end of a woman’s menstrual cycle 32. During menopause, the ovaries produce **less estrogen and progesterone hormones**, which can cause hot flashes and other symptoms such as vaginal dryness, mood changes, and sleep disturbances 1.

### JanSz — 2023-09-14T15:34:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/man-woman-nitric-oxide.28046/post-326387>

**Geroscience** is a field of research that focuses on the intersection of basic aging biology, chronic disease, and health. It aims to understand the genetic, molecular, and cellular mechanisms that make aging a major risk factor and driver of common chronic conditions and diseases of older people

**We do not have bio-markers around true markers of aging.**

Biomarkers of aging are biological parameters that could predict functional capacity at some later age better than chronological age. In other words, they would give the true “biological age”, which may be different from the chronological age.

Some of the main mechanisms identified as potential biomarkers of aging are **DNA methylation**, loss of histones, and histone modification
1\. The epigenetic clock is a promising biomarker of aging and can accurately predict human chronological age 1
2\. Basic blood biochemistry and cell counts can also be used to accurately predict the chronological age 1

[A basic blood biochemistry and cell count test is a routine blood test that measures the levels of various compounds in the blood, including calcium, glucose, sodium, potassium, bicarbonate, chloride, blood urea nitrogen (BUN), and creatinine](https://www.healthline.com/health/blood-tests)
[1](https://www.healthline.com/health/blood-tests). [The test also measures the number of white blood cells, red blood cells, and platelets in the blood](https://www.healthline.com/health/blood-tests) [1](https://www.healthline.com/health/blood-tests)
[2](https://www.webmd.com/a-to-z-guides/complete-blood-count). [The concentration of hemoglobin and hematocrit (the percentage of blood made up of red blood cells) are also measured](https://www.healthline.com/health/blood-tests) [1](https://www.healthline.com/health/blood-tests)
[3](https://www.mayoclinic.org/tests-procedures/complete-blood-count/about/pac-20384919).[This test can help your doctor identify a variety of health conditions, including infections, anemia, high cholesterol, vitamin deficiencies, organ failure, HIV, cancer, diabetes, and more](https://www.healthline.com/health/blood-tests)

Note. Personally have
\==========
Here are some examples of biomarkers of human aging:

-   **Telomere length**: Telomeres are repetitive DNA sequences at the end of chromosomes that shorten with each cell division. [Shorter telomeres have been associated with increased mortality risk and age-related diseases](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biomarkers_of_aging) [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biomarkers_of_aging).
-   **DNA methylation**: DNA methylation is a process by which methyl groups are added to DNA molecules. [Changes in DNA methylation patterns have been associated with aging and age-related diseases](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biomarkers_of_aging) [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biomarkers_of_aging).
-   **Inflammatory markers**: Inflammation is a natural response to injury or infection, but chronic inflammation has been linked to many age-related diseases such as Alzheimer’s disease, cardiovascular disease, and cancer. [Inflammatory markers such as **C-reactive protein (CRP) and interleukin-6 (IL-6)** have been associated with increased mortality risk in older adults](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biomarkers_of_aging) [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biomarkers_of_aging).
-   **Oxidative stress markers**: Oxidative stress occurs when there is an imbalance between free radicals and antioxidants in the body. Oxidative stress has been linked to many age-related diseases such as Alzheimer’s disease, cardiovascular disease, and cancer. [Markers of oxidative stress such as malondialdehyde (MDA) and 8-hydroxy-2’-deoxyguanosine **(8-OHdG)** have been associated with increased mortality risk in older adults](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biomarkers_of_aging)
-   \================

**DUTCHtest measures:**
Oxidative Stress / DNA Damage, measured as 8-Hydroxy-2-deoxyguanosine (8-OHdG) - (Urine)

### JanSz — 2023-09-14T16:21:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/man-woman-nitric-oxide.28046/post-326390>

### [Integrating Autism Spectrum Disorder Pathophysiology: Mitochondria, Vitamin A, CD38, Oxytocin, Serotonin and Melatonergic Alterations in the Placenta and Gut - PubMed](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31682209/)

Such a mitochondria-focussed integrated model of the pathophysiology of ASD has important preventative and treatment implications.

 pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

### Integrating Autism Spectrum Disorder Pathophysiology:​

### Mitochondria, Vitamin A, CD38, Oxytocin, Serotonin and​

### Melatonergic Alterations in the Placenta and Gut​

\---
A **melatonergic** agent (or drug) is a chemical which functions to directly **modulate the melatonin system** in the body or brain.
\---
**Background:** A diverse array of data has been associated with **autism spectrum disorder (ASD)**, reflecting the complexity of its pathophysiology as well as its heterogeneity. Two important hubs have emerged, the placenta/prenatal period and the postnatal gut, with alterations in mitochondria functioning crucial in both.

**Methods:** Factors acting to regulate mitochondria functioning in ASD across development are reviewed in this article.

**Results: Decreased vitamin A**, and its retinoic acid metabolites, lead to a decrease in CD38 and associated changes that underpin a wide array of data on the biological underpinnings of ASD, including decreased oxytocin, with relevance both prenatally and in the gut. Decreased sirtuins, poly-ADP ribose polymerase-driven decreases in nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide (NAD+), hyperserotonemia, decreased monoamine oxidase, alterations in 14-3-3 proteins, microRNA alterations, dysregulated aryl hydrocarbon receptor activity, suboptimal mitochondria functioning, and decreases in the **melatonergic** pathways are intimately linked to this. Many of the above processes may be modulating, or mediated by, alterations in mitochondria functioning. Other bodies of data associated with ASD may also be incorporated within these basic processes, including how ASD risk factors such as **maternal obesity and preeclampsia**, as well as more general prenatal stressors, modulate the **likelihood of offspring ASD**.

///////////////////////////////////////

**My personal look at melatonin.** Please modify it to make it (stil short but correct and on second grade level)**.**

Melatonin is very important, and it is everywhere in the body, lots of it in the brain.

When we are exposed to sunlight our skin gets darker because melatonin went from the inside of the body to the outside, (Melanin).
That means there is temporary deficiency of melatonin inside the body.
That deficiency gives body signal to produce additional melatonin to cover those deficiencies.
Healthy body is able to do it. And stays healthy.
People who have problems with exposure to sunlight get sicker.

**How to help those people?**

///////////////
Melanin and hemoglobin strongly absorb light in the ultraviolet (UV) and visible ranges and they present low absorption in the near-infrared range. Melanin in particular has a photoprotective action because of its light absorption properties

Melatonin is a natural compound that is produced by and found in different organisms including bacteria and eukaryotes
1\. It is a hormone released in the brain at night which controls the sleep-wake cycle in vertebrates 1.
Melatonin is not typically associated with any color, as it is a chemical compound.

### JanSz — 2023-09-14T20:57:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/man-woman-nitric-oxide.28046/post-326407>

Asking AI
does nitric oxide affects mitochondrial heteroplasmy
\--
Searching for: nitric oxide mitochondrial heteroplasmy

Searching for: nitric oxide aging

Searching for: mitochondrial heteroplasmy aging nitric oxide

Generating answers for you…

**Nitric oxide (NO)** plays a crucial role in healthy aging by controlling telomerase, the enzyme that adds telomeres to the ends of DNA, and mitochondria biogenesis
1\. Nitric oxide synthase (NOS) enzymes are responsible for producing nitric oxide in the body. Reduced nitric oxide bioavailability and altered vascular expression and activity of NOS enzymes have been implicated as major players in aging-associated endothelial dysfunction 2.

**Mitochondrial heteroplasmy** is the presence of multiple distinct mitochondrial DNA sequences within an individual
1\. While I couldn’t find any direct evidence that nitric oxide affects mitochondrial heteroplasmy, a study published in Human Molecular Genetics found that mitochondrial heteroplasmy increases significantly with advanced age 1. The study also revealed that changes in heteroplasmy were not uniform with some sites demonstrating a loss of variation (increased homoplasmy) with aging 1.

I hope this helps! Let me know if you have any other questions.

\==================================
AI define heteroplasmy

[Heteroplasmy is the presence of more than one type of organellar genome (mitochondrial DNA or plastid DNA) within a cell or individual](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heteroplasmy) [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heteroplasmy). It is an important factor in considering the severity of mitochondrial diseases. [Because most eukaryotic cells contain many hundreds of mitochondria with hundreds of copies of mitochondrial DNA, it is common for mutations to affect only some mitochondria, leaving most unaffected](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heteroplasmy) [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heteroplasmy).

Although detrimental scenarios are well-studied, **heteroplasmy can also be beneficial.
[For example, centenarians show a higher than average degree of heteroplasmy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heteroplasmy) [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heteroplasmy).** At birth, all copies of mitochondrial DNA are thought to be identical in most humans. [Microheteroplasmy is mutations of up to about 2-5% of mitochondrial genomes, and is present in most adults](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heteroplasmy) [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heteroplasmy). [This refers to hundreds of independent mutations in one organism, with each mutation found in about 1-2% of all mitochondrial genomes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heteroplasmy) [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heteroplasmy). [Very low-level heteroplasmic variance is present in essentially all individuals, even those who are healthy, and is likely to be due to both inherited and somatic single base substitutions](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heteroplasmy) [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heteroplasmy).

I hope this helps! Let me know if you have any other questions.

////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
**Does high heteroplasmy affect aging?**

[Mitochondrial heteroplasmy is the presence of multiple distinct mitochondrial DNA sequences within an individual](https://bmcgenomics.biomedcentral.com/articles/10.1186/s12864-017-4287-0) [1](https://bmcgenomics.biomedcentral.com/articles/10.1186/s12864-017-4287-0). [A study published in Human Molecular Genetics found that **mitochondrial heteroplasmy increases significantly with advanced age**](https://bmcgenomics.biomedcentral.com/articles/10.1186/s12864-017-4287-0) [1](https://bmcgenomics.biomedcentral.com/articles/10.1186/s12864-017-4287-0). [The study also revealed that changes in heteroplasmy were not uniform with some sites demonstrating a loss of variation (increased homoplasmy) with aging](https://bmcgenomics.biomedcentral.com/articles/10.1186/s12864-017-4287-0) [1](https://bmcgenomics.biomedcentral.com/articles/10.1186/s12864-017-4287-0).

[While the relationship between mitochondrial heteroplasmy and aging is not yet fully understood, a study published in BMC Genomics found that **mitochondrial DNA heteroplasmy increased significantly with advanced age**](https://bmcgenomics.biomedcentral.com/articles/10.1186/s12864-017-4287-0) [1](https://bmcgenomics.biomedcentral.com/articles/10.1186/s12864-017-4287-0). [The study also showed that changes in heteroplasmy were not uniform, with some sites demonstrating a loss of variation (increased homoplasmy) with aging](https://bmcgenomics.biomedcentral.com/articles/10.1186/s12864-017-4287-0) [1](https://bmcgenomics.biomedcentral.com/articles/10.1186/s12864-017-4287-0). [Another study published in Human Molecular Genetics found that neutral mitochondrial heteroplasmy increased with age, but did not appear to be associated with any specific disease or phenotype](https://www.bing.com/aclk?ld=e859W_R_-BfOBb5ZsrP7WWfTVUCUw-Sl8vdyN2rRM2D9RYpQtEAEg0hIpXWi4D6RWs8HxX7mC9zzz5VG6bETxcVg61PM5g4wf_vkOIFDcL11iWBuJ7uVmAhLc3-zs7B3SkfDrbBftd2GjhvNr9g54S3w8aiSBwXbltZrPfPNcmjx3aD7rM&&rlid=074e8e2586dd11348098eb26c343cd00) [2](https://academic.oup.com/hmg/article/20/8/1653/811827).

[**Nitric oxide (NO) plays a crucial role in healthy aging by controlling telomerase,** the enzyme that adds telomeres to the ends of DNA, and mitochondria biogenesis](https://www.fightaging.org/archives/2021/03/mitochondrial-dna-heteroplasmy-in-the-aging-heart/) [3](https://www.fightaging.org/archives/2021/03/mitochondrial-dna-heteroplasmy-in-the-aging-heart/). Nitric oxide synthase (NOS) enzymes are responsible for producing nitric oxide in the body. **[Reduced nitric oxide bioavailability and altered vascular expression and activity of NOS enzymes have been implicated as major players in aging-associated endothelial dysfunction](https://bing.com/search?q=heteroplasmy+aging)** [4](https://bing.com/search?q=heteroplasmy+aging).

I hope this helps! Let me know if you have any other questions.

////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////

**Does level of heteroplasmy indicate stage of aging of the body**

While the relationship between mitochondrial heteroplasmy and aging is not yet fully understood, studies suggest that it may be a contributing factor to the aging process 12.
However, it is important to note that **mitochondrial heteroplasmy can also be beneficial.**
For example, centenarians show a higher than average degree of heteroplasmy 1

### JanSz — 2023-09-14T21:08:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/man-woman-nitric-oxide.28046/post-326409>

**Asking AI**
What is the optimal ratio of Linoleic acid and alpha-Linolenic acid in human body
\--
[Alpha-linolenic acid (ALA) and linoleic acid (LA) are essential fatty acids that the human body cannot produce on its own and must obtain from food](https://www.hsph.harvard.edu/nutritionsource/what-should-you-eat/fats-and-cholesterol/types-of-fat/omega-3-fats/) [1](https://www.hsph.harvard.edu/nutritionsource/what-should-you-eat/fats-and-cholesterol/types-of-fat/omega-3-fats/).
[The optimal ratio of LA to ALA is still a topic of debate, but some studies suggest that a ratio of](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fatty_acid_ratio_in_food)
[**1:1 to 4:1 (LA:ALA) may be beneficial for health**](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fatty_acid_ratio_in_food) [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fatty_acid_ratio_in_food)[3](https://www.webmd.com/diet/health-benefits-hemp-seed-oil).

[ALA is found in many seeds and oils, including flaxseed, walnuts, chia, hemp, and many common vegetable oils](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/%CE%91-Linolenic_acid) [4](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/%CE%91-Linolenic_acid). [LA is found in many vegetable oils, including corn oil, soybean oil, and sunflower oil](https://nutritionandmetabolism.biomedcentral.com/articles/10.1186/s12986-018-0330-7) [5](https://nutritionandmetabolism.biomedcentral.com/articles/10.1186/s12986-018-0330-7).

I hope this helps! Let me know if you have any other questions.

### Dr. Marcus Ettinger — 2023-09-15T18:47:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/man-woman-nitric-oxide.28046/post-326441>

First, I'm not a fan of supplemental beta-alanine (BA). It made me feel really weird. BA and carnosine are found in most of the foods I eat, so I'm not worried about a deficiency. As far as its ability to help with intracellular acidosis for working out, I use creatine. Way more bang for the buck. During intense exercise, the breakdown of ATP can lead to the accumulation of protons (H+) and a decrease in muscle pH, contributing to muscle acidosis and fatigue. Creatine's ability to maintain ATP levels allows for continued ATP-driven processes that help buffer some of the excess protons, indirectly contributing to muscle pH stability.

Second, I can only speak for myself. I would not take a pharmaceutical unless I absolutely had to. I can't wrap my head around the idea of taking daily Cialis. Activating or suppressing a chemical reaction in a non-natural manner goes against my philosophy.

One of my gauges is do you (any man) still get "nocturnal penile tumescence" (NPT) or "morning wood"? This is a parasympathetic, NO, testosterone, REM sleep... reaction. If you do, you're golden. If you don't, but you do with the addition of Cialis, and you don't notice any unwanted side effects, go for it.

### Dr. Marcus Ettinger — 2023-09-17T02:17:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/man-woman-nitric-oxide.28046/post-326505>

I had a free moment - When the pH in the stomach rises, indicating that it is becoming less acidic, the HCl-producing parietal cells can respond by producing more HCl. This is often triggered by the presence of food, the anticipation of food, which signals the stomach to prepare for digestion, or the introduction of a buffering agent such as (P.B.).

Other: Gastrin can stimulate parietal cells to produce more HCl when food enters the stomach. (amino acids - phenylalanine, tryptophan, leucine (BCAAs), and glutamine (my favorite).

TLDR
I occasionally took Tums, for heartburn, but it wasn't till I had a physical in 2012 and the Doc found I was bleeding internally that things changed. Had a colonoscopy and endoscopy and they found ulcerative colitis and Barrett's Esophagus.

I had just changed to eating a carnivore diet about a month before the physical. The Doc wanted to put me on PPI's, but after investigating the drug it was clear they shut down the acid pumps in your gut.

I didn't feel that was a good direction to go, so I found information on GERD and such on Chris Kresser's page in which he said, 'the issue with this condition isn't producing too much stomach acid, the issue is you are not producing enough.

Kresser recommended Betaine HCI with a meal until you get a burning sensation and then backing off and taking one less capsule. The first time I tried it - No More Mr. Heartburn.

With this new information from Dr. Ettinger and you referenced. I'm going to try the Potassium Bicarbonate, it's quite likely I'm not getting enough potassium anyhow.

### JanSz — 2023-09-26T00:54:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/man-woman-nitric-oxide.28046/post-326807>

## Association with longevity​

G**enetic variation in FOXO3 has been shown to be associated with [healthspan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Life_expectancy#Healthy_life_expectancy) and [longevity](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Longevity)** in humans.[\[16\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/FOXO3#cite_note-pmid32678081-16) It is found in most [centenarians](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Centenarians) across a variety of ethnic groups around the world.[\[17\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/FOXO3#cite_note-pmid18765803-17)[\[18\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/FOXO3#cite_note-pmid19196970-18)

## Association with intelligence​

In a meta-analysis of 78,308 individuals of European descent, a particular [single nucleotide polymorphism](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Single_nucleotide_polymorphism) (SNP) **(rs2490272**) in an intronic region of FOXO3 and neighboring SNPs in the promoter region, had the strongest associations with intelligence.[\[19\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/FOXO3#cite_note-19)
[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/FOXO3](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/FOXO3)

### JanSz — 2023-12-09T15:09:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/man-woman-nitric-oxide.28046/post-329023>

**vasodilators CO2 and nitric oxide NO**

Vasodilators are substances that cause the blood vessels to relax and widen, which lowers the blood pressure and improves blood flow.
CO2 and NO are two natural vasodilators that have different mechanisms of action.

**CO2 (carbon dioxide)** is a by-product of cellular respiration, which occurs in all living cells. CO2 can diffuse into the blood vessels and bind to hemoglobin, the protein that carries oxygen in red blood cells.
This reduces the amount of oxygen that hemoglobin can bind, which causes the red blood cells to release more oxygen to the tissues.
This increases the oxygen delivery and reduces the oxygen demand of the tissues, which lowers the blood pressure and improves blood flow1.

**NO (nitric oxide)** is a gas molecule that is produced by various enzymes in different parts of the body, such as endothelial cells, smooth muscle cells, and platelets.
NO can diffuse into the smooth muscle cells of blood vessels and activate an enzyme called guanylate cyclase, which increases the production of cyclic guanosine monophosphate (cGMP).
cGMP causes the relaxation of smooth muscle cells, leading to vasodilation2.
NO also has other effects on vascular function, such as suppressing platelet aggregation, inhibiting smooth muscle proliferation, and enhancing angiogenesis3.

Both CO2 and NO are potent vasodilators that can lower blood pressure and improve blood flow in various conditions.
However, they have different effects on different types of vascular beds.
CO2 mainly affects systemic arteries and veins,
while NO mainly affects pulmonary arteries and capillaries1.
Therefore, they have different indications for use in clinical settings.

CO2 is used as a treatment for hypercapnia (high levels of carbon dioxide in the blood), which can cause respiratory acidosis (low pH), coma, or death.
CO2 can also be used as a therapeutic agent for pulmonary hypertension (high blood pressure in the lungs), which can cause shortness of breath, chest pain, or heart failure.
CO2 can be administered by inhalation or injection1.

NO is used as a treatment for pulmonary hypertension (high blood pressure in the lungs), especially persistent pulmonary hypertension of the newborn (PPHN), which is a life-threatening condition that affects some infants after birth.
NO can also be used as a diagnostic tool for pulmonary hypertension during right heart catheterization (a procedure that measures pressure in different chambers of the heart).
NO can be administered by inhalation using a device called an inhaler.

I hope this information helps you understand more about vasodilators CO2 and NO.

Definitely not as fortunate as you. Cialis helps but not enough and NO2U is helping but still, not enough. I hoped the addition of NO2U would make all the difference based on the science, but it hasn't.

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=329023)
>
> **vasodilators CO2 and nitric oxide NO**
>
>
> Vasodilators are substances that cause the blood vessels to relax and widen, which lowers the blood pressure and improves blood flow.
> CO2 and NO are two natural vasodilators that have different mechanisms of action.
>
> **CO2 (carbon dioxide)** is a by-product of cellular respiration, which occurs in all living cells. CO2 can diffuse into the blood vessels and bind to hemoglobin, the protein that carries oxygen in red blood cells.
> This reduces the amount of oxygen that hemoglobin can bind, which causes the red blood cells to release more oxygen to the tissues.
> This increases the oxygen delivery and reduces the oxygen demand of the tissues, which lowers the blood pressure and improves blood flow1.
>
> **NO (nitric oxide)** is a gas molecule that is produced by various enzymes in different parts of the body, such as endothelial cells, smooth muscle cells, and platelets.
> NO can diffuse into the smooth muscle cells of blood vessels and activate an enzyme called guanylate cyclase, which increases the production of cyclic guanosine monophosphate (cGMP).
> cGMP causes the relaxation of smooth muscle cells, leading to vasodilation2.
> NO also has other effects on vascular function, such as suppressing platelet aggregation, inhibiting smooth muscle proliferation, and enhancing angiogenesis3.
>
> Both CO2 and NO are potent vasodilators that can lower blood pressure and improve blood flow in various conditions.
> However, they have different effects on different types of vascular beds.
> CO2 mainly affects systemic arteries and veins,
> while NO mainly affects pulmonary arteries and capillaries1.
> Therefore, they have different indications for use in clinical settings.
>
> CO2 is used as a treatment for hypercapnia (high levels of carbon dioxide in the blood), which can cause respiratory acidosis (low pH), coma, or death.
> CO2 can also be used as a therapeutic agent for pulmonary hypertension (high blood pressure in the lungs), which can cause shortness of breath, chest pain, or heart failure.
> CO2 can be administered by inhalation or injection1.
>
> NO is used as a treatment for pulmonary hypertension (high blood pressure in the lungs), especially persistent pulmonary hypertension of the newborn (PPHN), which is a life-threatening condition that affects some infants after birth.
> NO can also be used as a diagnostic tool for pulmonary hypertension during right heart catheterization (a procedure that measures pressure in different chambers of the heart).
> NO can be administered by inhalation using a device called an inhaler.
>
> I hope this information helps you understand more about vasodilators CO2 and NO.

This insightful quote paints a picture of wisdom born not from an innate desire for grandeur but from a humble journey of self-awareness and growth. A decentralized sage, in this light, is not someone who sets out to dazzle the world with lofty thoughts but rather an individual who, through the weariness of their own folly, discovers a practical clarity. ### NikkiYork — 2023-08-05T23:27:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/visiting-el-salvador-and-belize.28049/post-324751>

A currency is worth as much as what you can buy for it. We might have to pay only 30k now for a Bitcoin, but what you can get for that in ES is independent from that - depending on how “real” their use is.

In parts of Africa the parallel currency is cell phone minutes. Nobody uses it as minutes. But it’s more stable than the countries money. It’s by the way US owned.

BC is about stability.

I went to Ecuador in the 90s. My boyfriend at the time got 200 dollars worth more money at the bank for the same amount I did standing right behind him in line. The ladies liked him, but also the currency was not stable and the exchange rate fluctuated by the minute.

Even if BC isn’t great in ES right now, it’s like a sistered bridge before the old one collapses.

### Jack Kruse — 2023-08-06T15:47:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/visiting-el-salvador-and-belize.28049/post-324759>

No one is complaining in ES now about Bitcoin. They are just waiting for the halving and the Volcano Bitcoin to wind up on the National treasury to change their lives.

### Marko Pollo — 2023-08-06T17:19:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/visiting-el-salvador-and-belize.28049/post-324762>

I'm just trying to figure out how they deal with fluctuations. Is its value pegged to another currency? Do they use another currency for day to day transactions? Are contracts written in BTC or another currency? Prices, do they change with fluctuations in BTC? I have seen in Colombia many times where the money changers just shut their windows when the dollar gets too strong vs COP. Has the price decline effected domestic investment or consumption?

### John Warner — 2023-08-06T23:30:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/visiting-el-salvador-and-belize.28049/post-324772>

How's everyone doin w/ the fluctuation of the dollar, oh sorry I mean the demise of the dollar?
