But. Most people only want a Starbucks coffee in the morning and netflix. Or so Dr. Jack rightfully assessed.

### Marko Pollo — 2023-08-07T01:00:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/visiting-el-salvador-and-belize.28049/post-324783>

> [John Warner said:](/goto/post?id=324772)
>
> How's everyone doin w/ the fluctuation of the dollar, oh sorry I mean the demise of the dollar?

Of all asset classes I've got exposure to: BTC, equities, real estate, $USD, livestock, precious metals or heavy equipment, BTC has taken by far the worse hit. Except for BTC and USD, all have gone up. I'm not attacking BTC, just questioning how it is working out for those using it as currency. Better to be able to look rationally at investments than just being a fanboy parroting stupid shit from other fanboys. BTW, I'd love for BTC to go back up, just not betting my life it will.

### NikkiYork — 2023-08-07T02:18:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/visiting-el-salvador-and-belize.28049/post-324784>

> [Marko Pollo said:](/goto/post?id=324783)
>
> Of all asset classes I've got exposure to: BTC, equities, real estate, $USD, livestock, precious metals or heavy equipment, BTC has taken by far the worse hit. Except for BTC and USD, all have gone up. I'm not attacking BTC, just questioning how it is working out for those using it as currency. Better to be able to look rationally at investments than just being a fanboy parroting stupid shit from other fanboys. BTW, I'd love for BTC to go back up, just not betting my life it will.

Lifestock is a great one. I wish I had some extra bucks, because I have a good opportunity for that also.

Bitcoin taking a hit is very relative to time.

Dr. Jack said up there, that nobody in ES is complaining about BTC. So what more do you need as an answer? It is of course still a bet. Nothing is safe.

We used Bitcoin as currency back in the day. In the US. It was fine. No problems. I never got pizza for it though, but I know people who did. It was more like a donation for a blogger, that sort of thing. Nerdy people fun. We could have should have would have all been rich - and only very few are.

I had mining software on my computer and was going to run it in 2010, but Bitcoin was already complex and it took my laptop computer power a week to mine one coin. I think that was worth $7. So I said eff that, I’m not stupid. Hahaha.

I also had a couple of coins in a wallet that I lost when my computer died. And I don’t even know how many. It was worth peanuts then. That said, he who is diversified will probably do ok, and what more do you need? I also got apple stocks. You win some, you lose some. I thought I lost maybe 50 bucks and that just happens all the time.

Bitcoin then went pretty dark. It was for Silk Road people. Thor was initially not bad. It was for privacy and safety, but that’s when Bitcoin got a bad name.

Then it went up and people got back in with it. And sure enough we lost our coins on Mount Gox. When shall we retrieve? Mind you, a Bitcoin was worth 1000 bucks then. All gone, respectively still in law suits.

I said, don’t touch that again.

I was doing bit torrent seeding at the time instead and I thought it was great for reading all the books. Who knows, maybe reading made me more rich than keeping my coins.

You can’t live life without making mistakes. Without correcting your mistakes, without falling and getting back up again. At least I don’t know any cool people who don’t.

I understand not parroting, but whatever somebody tells you about Bitcoin, doesn’t help you, or does it? And volatility is not for the faint of heart.

Of course I (we all) got back into the game.

I say hodl on. When AI gets so fast that it can clone the ledgers, will it survive? that can never happen - was said one too many times. Of course it can.
We shall see.

# Thread: Mitochondrial uncoupler blowback(e.g. coffee)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondrial-uncoupler-blowback-e-g-coffee.28051/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Warren Canavan — 2023-08-04T12:47:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondrial-uncoupler-blowback-e-g-coffee.28051/post-324680>

Steven Gundry in his book on keto. Talks about how foods with polyphenols act as mitochondrial uncouplers. What this means is the mitochondria stop making ATP to prevent damaging themselves. I think I have put it together why if you take too many, all at once, you just end up feeling like trash: not enough ATP.
This was apparently the problem with a drug called DNP(dintrophenol) which was used as a weight loss drug, it ended up causing cataracts and other problems.

A little is like exercise, its good for you. But you take too much, it hurts you?

Would be interested if any one has an opinion. Are they worth avoiding altogether?

### Marko Pollo — 2023-08-04T20:54:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondrial-uncoupler-blowback-e-g-coffee.28051/post-324697>

I avoid exercise for the sake of exercise at all costs! Sounds like I should do the same with coffee.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondrial-uncoupler-blowback-e-g-coffee.28051/post-329497>

Science gets interesting when you link it to life. Recently on my Patreon blog, I have been linking a story of how deuterium links to life to make a difference in the reality we observe. Deuterium is not a bad thing all the time. In fact, when it comes to receptor biology, drug effect...

### [Checking your browser - reCAPTCHA](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/17223552/)

pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

Yes Polyphenols are very good if taken within the realm of seasonal food intake ...if taken as supplements a big NO

This is due to being a fine balance between ROS and Antioxidant Signaling within the Cell.Nick Lane talks about this extensively in his book that Jack has recommended as one of the books that is a must read "**Power Sex Suicide"**

It becomes very clear once reading Nick lanes book amongst other books he has authored why Jack is dead against supplements and we should be too!!!

The variation of Mitochondrial DNA haplotypes and the ability to try and match Polyphenols is easily overcome by following a seasonal diet for your Zipcode.

# Thread: Achalasia? Ideas please
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/achalasia-ideas-please.28053/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Stefans — 2023-08-04T17:54:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/achalasia-ideas-please.28053/post-324689>

Hi all,

Been unable to swallow solids for 18 months, 30 lbs weight loss, initially diagnosed as GERD but multiple PPI's had no effect, recent gastroscopy showed no evidence of acid damage, biopsies negative for EoE and H. Pylori. I am now having trouble swallowing liquids as well. Ultrasound, Neck CT no signs of tumours/cancer.

Gastro suspects Achalasia which is not very encouraging. Will be getting an esophageal manometry this month to confirm.

43rd latitude, previously heavy tech user. Went on a tech diet, got morning sun and + 1~ hour of afternoon sun for the last three months. No ALAN + blue blockers 2 hours before bed.

D level 50 ng/ml, decent tan.

Diet is blended fish, chicken + seasonal veggies. I am eating lots of ice cream recently as I need to keep weight on and I don't choke on it but now my LDL and Cholesterol levels are rising. Total CHOL: 242 mg/dL HDL: 75 mg/dL LDL 153 mg/dl Triglycerides: 79 mg/dL

Any idea if this is related to melanin?

> initially diagnosed as GERD but multiple PPI's had no effect, recent gastroscopy showed no evidence of acid damage, biopsies negative for EoE and H. Pylori. I am now having trouble swallowing liquids as well. Ultrasound, Neck CT no signs of tumours/cancer.
>
> Gastro suspects Achalasia which is not very encouraging. Will be getting an esophageal manometry this month to confirm.

**Assuming that indeed it is GERD.**
After multitude of decades of GERD and a hell that it comes with
Couple of years ago I got a **COMPLETE relief** using
Potassium Bicarbonate. Got it from Amazon.
One or two flat teaspoons per day:

If it would work for you, make sure that you say thank you to:
[@DrEttinger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/22307/)
He told me about that.

# Thread: Water vapor is a much bigger driver of the greenhouse effect than CO2.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-vapor-is-a-much-bigger-driver-of-the-greenhouse-effect-than-co2.28056/>
**Subforum:** Factor X
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Johan Lindøm — 2023-08-05T20:47:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-vapor-is-a-much-bigger-driver-of-the-greenhouse-effect-than-co2.28056/post-324741>

HUNGA TONGA-HUNGA: 10X MORE GREENHOUSE GAS EMISSION THAN HUMANS: BUT WHERE ARE THE CLIMATE SCIENTISTS? "The largest and highest injection of water into the stratosphere since satellites began taking measurements: 160,900 tons (146,000 metric tons) of additional water vapor had entered the stratosphere since the volcano erupted, reaching a maximum altitude of 33 miles (53 km), which is in the mesosphere, the layer of the atmosphere that extends from the top of the stratosphere to an altitude of 53 miles (85 km)." [https://livescience.com/tonga-eruption-water-vapor](https://t.co/9dMGN0y3YF)

### Johan Lindøm — 2023-08-05T20:47:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-vapor-is-a-much-bigger-driver-of-the-greenhouse-effect-than-co2.28056/post-324742>

### [Tonga Eruption Blasted Unprecedented Amount of Water Into Stratosphere](https://www.nasa.gov/feature/jpl/tonga-eruption-blasted-unprecedented-amount-of-water-into-stratosphere)

tonga20220729

 www.nasa.gov

### Johan Lindøm — 2023-08-05T21:35:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-vapor-is-a-much-bigger-driver-of-the-greenhouse-effect-than-co2.28056/post-324743>

WATER VAPOUR IS THE REAL GREENHOUSE GAS. Water vapour makes up 95% of the greenhouse gases in our atmosphere. Of the sum total of the 3.6% CO2 released into the atmosphere, approximately 0.9% is caused by human activity. Human CO2 makes up less than 1% of the 3.6% of the total greenhouse gases influencing our climate. The real greenhouse gas influencing our climate is water vapour, NOT CO2.

### Johan Lindøm — 2023-08-05T21:46:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-vapor-is-a-much-bigger-driver-of-the-greenhouse-effect-than-co2.28056/post-324746>

### [Record Global Temperatures Driven by Hunga-Tonga Volcanic Water Vapor – Visualized • Watts Up With That?](https://wattsupwiththat.com/2023/08/05/record-global-temperatures-driven-by-hunga-tonga-volcanic-water-vapor-visualized/)

Image above: Global Water Vapor - Source: NASA: https://earthobservatory.nasa.gov/global-maps/MYDAL2\_M\_SKY\_WV Readers may recall that we have reported on the massive amount of water vapor that has been injected into the stratosphere by the 2022 eruption of the Hunga-Tonga volcano. A recent study...

 wattsupwiththat.com

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-08-05T22:46:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-vapor-is-a-much-bigger-driver-of-the-greenhouse-effect-than-co2.28056/post-324749>

Facts^^^

Additionally, no one talks about how increased CO2 in the atmosphere upregulates photosynthesis in plant life to keep the concentration of CO2 under control. Earth is built for handling increased CO2.

I think there are even bigger factors that influence climate changes outside of atmospheric gas concentrations. These are factors outside of human control. Things like fluctuating solar intensity and precession of Earth's axis.

We don't hear about these realities though because the controlling paradigm ultimately can't profit from or regulate these factors.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-05T22:51:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-vapor-is-a-much-bigger-driver-of-the-greenhouse-effect-than-co2.28056/post-324750>

It is my understanding that the sun releases the same amount of energy on to the Earth in one HOUR as all of mankind used in one YEAR. Hard to see how man is making any signifiant difference to our ecosystem.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-11T23:45:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-vapor-is-a-much-bigger-driver-of-the-greenhouse-effect-than-co2.28056/post-334636>

THE REAL REASON FOR CLIMATE CHANGE: The warming from the Sun is cyclical, it's NOT constant. The distance of where you are from the Sun is constantly changing because both the Earth's orbit around the Sun is irregular and the Sun itself wobbles due to the combined gravitational pull of all the planets together. Look at the Schwabe solar cycle of 11 years, the Jose solar cycle of solar Inertial Motion of 179 years, Eddy Solar Cycle of 1000 years, the Bray-Halstatt Cycles of 2300-2500 years, then look into the three Milankovitch Cycles. Short to medium term climate change: Solar Inertial Motion: the combined mass of the planets also moves the position of the Sun through their combined gravitational pull, meaning the Sun moves around following the ever moving barycentre of the Solar system rather than being in a fixed central point in the middle Solar System. That is the key thing to understand: the Sun is moving around, wobbling in spiral like motion as it travels, it is not stationary. Once you understand that all medium term climate change can be explained simply because of the Sun's changing distance from the Earth. None of this has anything to do with humans. None of this has anything to do with CO2. The models of the Solar System you grew up believing as a child were gross over simplifications. They conditioned you to believe that the Solar system has a fixed Sun position with a regular Sun activity with regular orbits, of which the Earth is one. Yet that is not the reality: not only the earth both tilts and wobbles as it orbits, but the orbit is a changing ellipse not a perfect circle, meaning the distance from the Sun is not constant. These are the three Milankovitch cycles. Also other planets have irregular orbits. The combined effect of all these irregular orbits together pulls the Sun off centre of the solar system into the barycentre. A wobbling Sun is the real reason for short to medium term climate change, and an irregular earth orbit, tilt and wobble is the reason for long term climate change. And this is just the beginning of the story of irregularity in the Earth's orbit around the Sun, then there are cycles of Sun activity, making it stronger and weaker according to how close to the 11 year cycle of magnetic poles flip it is, next being in 2024, and how many Sun spots & Solar flares we are exposed to. Then you need to factor volcanic activity, the Hunga-Tonga Hunga underwater volcanic eruption of January 2022 increased the water vapour in the stratosphere by 10%, this in itself will cause considerable warming of the planet in most regions. It's definitely not a simplistic neat black and white story of CO2, a minor greenhouse gas, as 95% of the earth's greenhouse gases are constituted by water vapour instead. Humans have no power to determine either the orbit of the Earth around the Sun or the Sun's internal & external activity, or the water vapour in the atmosphere. Life adapts much more easily to higher temperatures and increases in CO2, particularly plants, vegetation, trees, plankton& phytoplankton, than it does to decreases in CO2. The real danger is a decrease of CO2, and a decrease in temperature, not an increase in either. Once again, we have been deceived by a systematically corrupt scientific funding system linked to oligarchs interests. CO2 was always a control knob for economic prosperity, not climate.

Scientists at the US Department of Energy Oak Ridge National Laboratory (ORNL) have discovered new properties of water that go beyond the known laws of classical physics. [http://journals.aps.org/prl/abstract/10.1103/PhysRevLett.116.167802](https://t.co/FH7kQnCDlz) This discovery in 2016 represented a new fundamental understanding of the behavior of water and the way water utilizes energy. It implies that water molecules in your aquamarine or emerald ring – blue and green varieties of a mineral of a silicate of beryllium and aluminum– are undergoing the same quantum tunneling effect Oak Ridge saw in their experiments. Nature shows us her recipes but she does nto decipher them for us. We need to make sense of what she is capable of. Water confined in small spaces can pass through solid walls. The new tunneling state of water molecules confined in hexagonal ultra-small channels – 5 angstrom across – can happen in minerals and in cells. An angstrom is 1/10-billionth of a meter, and individual atoms are typically about 1 angstrom in diameter. Why is this a big deal? The era of ever increasing information storage density, the physical entity setting the limit to the progress has quickly been reduced to the size of a single atom now for science. The concept of representing a single bit of information by a single atom or molecule has been conceived with the ability to manipulate individual atoms on a surface with the tip of a scanning tunneling microscope. The implications of this ability still has no touched biology. It was believed that achieving information storage densities close to the single-atom limit in real-world devices was far from being feasible, but now this ability in water changes the game. The search for an information carrier which can break the limit set by the finite spacing between single atoms in solid state systems has now been found. This quantum state of water is a pathway of decentralized science operational in cells. During the experiment it was found that when the water is “trapped” in an enclosed area (e.g. Inside aperiodic crystals), the oxygen and hydrogen atoms of water molecules “delocalize” (they separate from each other) and this is when the quantum tunneling mechanism is created. Tunneling is a quantum mechanical phenomenon. In the tunnel effect, particles can move through such barriers that would be impossible according to the laws of classical physics.)

Web 3 ideas like NFTs are only part of building the next generation of the internet, argues the host of the “Hello Metaverse” podcast.

 www.coindesk.com

"Web 3, on the other hand, is a far more specific paradigm that provides clear solutions to specific shortcomings of the Web 2 internet. It is a reaction to the walled-garden ecosystems that platforms like Facebook and YouTube created, which caused people to have their data extracted, privacy breached and ability to control the content they create oppressed. Web 3 subverts that model because it directly addresses the issues of ownership and control."

e.g. Xverse experiences?

Of course want a home that pulls inhabitants to spend more time outdoors. I always look at and consider outside factors first.**

Passive solar design tip that I've heard: "During the heating season, south-facing windows should not be shaded so your home can collect solar energy and store heat"

But as far as letting light in while they're open, having some east and west facing ones also seems like a +.

### NikkiYork — 2023-08-08T16:42:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/window-orientation-for-a-home.28062/post-324832>

I’ll follow this, because I am interested in the same subject: what would the optimal house look like?

I had the idea that any type of retractable roof could be amazing. Also the Hawaiian lanais could inspire.

I once stayed in an old house in Hawai that had no glass at all, I forgot the name of that type of building though, it had something to do with cowboys. It was a building of about 1000 square feet and it was screened in against bugs, no panes.

The overall idea would likely be, to let full spectrum light in as much as possible during the day, while not loosing the shelter qualities needed.

If you are asking about directions, basically you would move with the light. Morning place, midday place, evening place, sleep place. That’s how I would think of it.

I don’t see a home as having a particular face  but I’m weird like that. We put our home “the wrong way” everybody says. It’s perfect. It “faces” backwards towards green and East and not to the street.

# Thread: 5G - Practical Actions
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-practical-actions.28064/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 28
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Trippg — 2023-08-08T19:22:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-practical-actions.28064/post-324835>

I realize the best thing we can do with 5g is to move as far away as possible. But besides that, are there any mitigation techniques that move the needle?

Since 5g is a millimeter wave and a poor penetrator of objects (why they need so many towers), can you shield yourself by placing walls, buildings, trees between you and the tower even if the tower is a few miles away?

### Neurotic Farmer — 2023-08-08T21:55:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-practical-actions.28064/post-324846>

[@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/)

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-08T22:32:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-practical-actions.28064/post-324849>

> [Trippg said:](/goto/post?id=324835)
>
> I realize the best thing we can do with 5g is to move as far away as possible. But besides that, are there any mitigation techniques that move the needle?
>
> Since 5g is a millimeter wave and a poor penetrator of objects (why they need so many towers), can you shield yourself by placing walls, buildings, trees between you and the tower even if the tower is a few miles away?

It is my understanding that a 5G tower (of the high mmWave 20Gz+ type) more than a few hundred feet away is of no concern, it can’t reach you or get thru the walls. 5th Generation technology (5G) using 600Mz (T-Mobile) or 3 - 4 Gz (ATT) can get thru just like 4G LTE signals do. I am not concerned about a tower more that 1/2 mile away talking to my phone. I AM concerned about the phone in my hand talking to the tower 1/2 mile away.

All nnEMF is cumulative so turn off, remove, reduce what you can and keep your total daily load as low as possible. That way the stuff you cannot control in town, down the road will affect you far less. 70%+ of most peoples daily nnEMF exposure is from inside their home. #1 source is the 60Hz AC electric field from the wires in your walls and everything plugged in. Turn of the power to your bedroom at night (and all the other circuits running thru the walls and floor) and get 8 hour a day of little to no nnEMF exposure. During the day unplug EVERYTHING you are not using and stay as far away from the thing you need on. Measure, be informed and make good choices. [Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

Thanks Anthony, sound advice.

### 5G Canary — 2023-08-08T23:49:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-practical-actions.28064/post-324859>

Low population density, ridiculous amount of trees and having neighbors that are willing to remove smart meters. In my opinion... with 5G it is the ground that becomes the bigger issue. I used to walk 4 miles every morning on the same path for 17 years. When they put the fiber optics in the ground for 5G I could hardly walk and all my joints started swelling. I moved to the middle of the street because it naturally felt better. I had no understanding of what 5G was at the time. It was driving my husband crazy that I was walking in the street. I challenged him to test the sidewalk I had been walking and the numbers were off the charts. He was completely shocked with the readings he was getting. We moved pretty quickly after that... with all this new technology coming be prepared to move quickly if needed.

### Trippg — 2023-08-08T23:54:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-practical-actions.28064/post-324860>

Intresting, whats the deal with smart meters? Do they create dirty power, in addition to communicating via RF? Why are they worse than any other IoT device?

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-11T06:13:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-practical-actions.28064/post-324950>

> [Trippg said:](/goto/post?id=324860)
>
> Intresting, whats the deal with smart meters? Do they create dirty power, in addition to communicating via RF? Why are they worse than any other IoT device?

From a purely nnEMF point of view - There are different types of “Smart” meters some “talk” to the Power company thru out the day, others (they claim) only once a day (at night). The main grouping of meters are to all intense and purposes (from an EMF point of view) just a Cell phone attached to the side of your house. TWACS meters use a different system. NO RF, the power company send a VERY low frequency (60Hz signal) down the power line to get the info. they want. There is NO WAY to block/filter out this signal, it is part of the electricity entering your home. There are 8 million+/- TWACS meters mostly in rural areas. ”Smart” meters also have a switch mode power supply in them which create dirty electricity (”DE”). Your TV, lab top, cell phone charger etc. all use switch mode power supplies but they are all different. ALL the “smart” meters in your town are the same and all have the same “DE” signature which also becomes part of the electricity entering your home. If you have 5,000 home in your town/city close to you, you can opt out but there are still 4,999+/- other meters around you. Add to these “smart” meters 5,000 - 10,000+ cell phones and WiFi routers. Could be why Uncle Jack is encouraging us all to move away from crowded areas and go south.

WHAT TO DO? (Before moving south)

Turn off and control the nnEMF you do have control of, the nnEMF originating from inside your home. ALL nnEMF is cumulative, electric fields, magnetic fields as well as the RF. For most people 70%+/- of their daily (negative/harmful) nnEMF exposure is from the 60Hz AC electric fields, from the wires and plugged in appliances in their homes. Create an EMF mitigated sleep sanctuary. You don’t need to spend 10’s of thousands of dollars covering it up . . . JUST TURN IT OFF. Reduce your daily load and be more resilient. The less total daily exposure you have, the less the exposure you do have will affect you . . . Measure your in home EMF (BVK), be knowledgeable, be equipped, be empowered, make informed decisions. [www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

>
> Your husband measured high electric or magnetic fields or both?

[@NikkiYork](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/28233/)

He said both seemed to increase significantly. He said he measured both- just learning the meters at the time- use both a Trifield and an Accoustimeter based on recollection spiking in multiple places. Part of the issue was our neighborhood was getting both Google fiber and experimental HS ATT system. And installation crews were hitting several buried gas lines and power cables at least twice requiring emergency response... lots of chaos. Seemed like a lot of gas line issues that entire year. The big thing he noted at the time was the change- done readings over the year of installation chaos went from negligible to pegging readings. Trucks and crews were running cables everywhere- all under and adjacent to the 4 miles of mirroring sidewalks throughout the development.

Not sure what is going on here because fiber optic cable works on light, (even carrying “5G” data) no electric or magnetic field and no RF until the data it is converted. As Jeromy points out in this article if the convention is done in or close to your home, that can be an issue. There must be something else in the trench because I do not see how it can be the fiber optic cable.

Any help here from a technical person or an engineer?

My husband is an electrical engineer and if it was a single fiber that is correct. But it’s the intermittent boosters, tracing wires and other infrastructure wiring that they seem to install at the same time. Google fiber alone is not the problem... it’s what came with it but they could not answer questions about.

### TANNER — 2023-08-18T18:04:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-practical-actions.28064/post-325289>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=324950)
>
> From a purely nnEMF point of view - There are different types of “Smart” meters some “talk” to the Power company thru out the day, others (they claim) only once a day (at night). The main grouping of meters are to all intense and purposes (from an EMF point of view) just a Cell phone attached to the side of your house. TWACS meters use a different system. NO RF, the power company send a VERY low frequency (60Hz signal) down the power line to get the info. they want. There is NO WAY to block/filter out this signal, it is part of the electricity entering your home. There are 8 million+/- TWACS meters mostly in rural areas. ”Smart” meters also have a switch mode power supply in them which create dirty electricity (”DE”). Your TV, lab top, cell phone charger etc. all use switch mode power supplies but they are all different. ALL the “smart” meters in your town are the same and all have the same “DE” signature which also becomes part of the electricity entering your home. If you have 5,000 home in your town/city close to you, you can opt out but there are still 4,999+/- other meters around you. Add to these “smart” meters 5,000 - 10,000+ cell phones and WiFi routers. Could be why Uncle Jack is encouraging us all to move away from crowded areas and go south.
>
> WHAT TO DO? (Before moving south)
>
> Turn off and control the nnEMF you do have control of, the nnEMF originating from inside your home. ALL nnEMF is cumulative, electric fields, magnetic fields as well as the RF. For most people 70%+/- of their daily (negative/harmful) nnEMF exposure is from the 60Hz AC electric fields, from the wires and plugged in appliances in their homes. Create an EMF mitigated sleep sanctuary. You don’t need to spend 10’s of thousands of dollars covering it up . . . JUST TURN IT OFF. Reduce your daily load and be more resilient. The less total daily exposure you have, the less the exposure you do have will affect you . . . Measure your in home EMF (BVK), be knowledgeable, be equipped, be empowered, make informed decisions. [www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

silly question maybe?
I don't have a smart tv. we still use older ones with the Roku for streaming.
Is my older TV emitting blue light or is this a different type of light?
LCD maybe?
Thank you.

### coho3131 — 2023-08-18T23:01:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-practical-actions.28064/post-325294>

> [TANNER said:](/goto/post?id=325289)
>
> silly question maybe?
> I don't have a smart tv. we still use older ones with the Roku for streaming.
> Is my older TV emitting blue light or is this a different type of light?
> LCD maybe?
> Thank you.

Some of the light will be blue. You can use a spectroscope to measure how much is blue. I'm not an expert. I'm pretty sure someone else will give you a better answer. You can adjust the TV picture to emit less blue. You could also make the jump to watching a fireplace or outdoor fire pit, solving cardboard puzzles, and reading paper books when you are ready.

### TANNER — 2023-08-18T23:05:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-practical-actions.28064/post-325295>

thank you. but a "smart TV" I assume is worse?

### coho3131 — 2023-08-18T23:15:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-practical-actions.28064/post-325296>

> [TANNER said:](/goto/post?id=325295)
>
> thank you. but a "smart TV" I assume is worse?

From a blue light-emitting perspective, I would bet that they are pretty much the same.

The newer TVs are probably even better because you can lower the brightness, contrast, backlight, and color temperature, and still see the picture clearly. Older TVs are not as good at those things.

You can always turn off the WiFi and Bluetooth on the TV and just use it as a monitor.

Again, I bet some of the the more knowledgeable people here will be giving us a more comprehensive answer.

### Marko Pollo — 2023-08-18T23:21:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-practical-actions.28064/post-325297>

# Thread: Children and sports
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/children-and-sports.28066/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### enyaw — 2023-08-08T20:25:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/children-and-sports.28066/post-324838>

How young is too young for a child to play full contact football or soccer with heading allowed? What are the risks? I imagine that repeated sub-concussive blows to the head aren't great for a developing brain. What are potential effects of playing football/soccer even without getting a concussion? What about if a child does get a concussion? Are there long term negative effects or is a young growing brain able to quickly recover?

### Branden G — 2023-08-08T21:18:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/children-and-sports.28066/post-324840>

I will not let my son play contact sports. If your child is interested in football there are alternatives like flag football leagues. There are way too many articles and examples out of there of the effects of taking big hits in football. Look at Aaron Hernandez, Antonio Brown and Tua for example.

My son (5 years old) is enrolled in a beginners parkour class at our local gymnastics facility. They focus on body awareness, spatial awareness and discipline. That’s an option if your child would be interested.

### NikkiYork — 2023-08-08T21:29:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/children-and-sports.28066/post-324841>

Nothing is without risk. BUT

Our son plays baseball. He started with karate for coordination at age 4 and it helped a lot, no full contact though. He is the not sporty artistic type, so it was a ton of work to bring him up into the game. He did soccer, also not an issue as it’s not been physical other than the kids kicking each other.

I won’t/wouldn’t’t put him into football, and I wouldn’t put a girl into ballet either.

I would always say it depends on your kid, body type, interest, personality, character.

Common sense does work.

Also the coaches are a factor. I was a gymnast and our coaches made us do very stupid stuff. I hated my parents for taking me out of it, but they absolutely made the right decision.

### JanSz — 2023-08-08T22:50:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/children-and-sports.28066/post-324851>

>

>

>
> Common sense does work.
>
> Also the coaches are a factor. I was a gymnast and our coaches made us do very stupid stuff. I hated my parents for taking me out of it, but they absolutely made the right decision.

and I wouldn’t put a girl into ballet either.
\===========================

My wife was a professional ballerina.
The swan lake type.

In football the younger they are the less likely they are to get a concussion, especially with helmets, just not enough force (mass x acceleration) to get one. Played all my life and never heard of a concussion until high school.

### John Warner — 2023-08-10T18:28:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/children-and-sports.28066/post-324924>

a 5 year old playing football is for your entertainment no for the kid, get them in nature. I believe young kid sports is more harmful for them and family lifestyle. Has it made any better athletes than there was in the past? Any Nolan Ryan's or Emmit Smith's running around anymore?

### NikkiYork — 2023-08-10T20:12:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/children-and-sports.28066/post-324928>

> [John Warner said:](/goto/post?id=324924)
>
> a 5 year old playing football is for your entertainment no for the kid, get them in nature. I believe young kid sports is more harmful for them and family lifestyle. Has it made any better athletes than there was in the past? Any Nolan Ryan's or Emmit Smith's running around anymore?

Well, probably not football, however, a big chunk of bone density is decided during the growth phase for the rest of your life. At least so I learned.

The osteoplasts (bone building) outnumber the osteoclasts (bone decaying) for as long as a person grows. You want the break down of bone also, because nothing that grows infinitely is helpful, aka cancer.

Any more bone building due to exercise is converted into a tighter internal bone grid. Think of it as more little beams inside the bone. We lose that with age, because there is no longer growth in hight desired or designed by the body. So making use of the fact is not terrible.

Obviously anything in overdose is too much. But sports during growths phase are something I promote. Later too, but I just explained the difference.

### John Warner — 2023-08-10T20:28:30+0300

>
> Any more bone building due to exercise is converted into a tighter internal bone grid. Think of it as more little beams inside the bone. We lose that with age, because there is no longer growth in hight desired or designed by the body. So making use of the fact is not terrible.
>
> Obviously anything in overdose is too much. But sports during growths phase are something I promote. Later too, but I just explained the difference.

I did say get them in nature, meaning running, jumping ,climbing, crawling, etc... Movement is key to me.

>

My sister was into gymnastics from a young age. National “B” Team in England and she had all kinds of crazy back problems when she got older. Soccer, bad for the knees. Most things in moderation are good and beneficial, excess rarely is.

### enyaw — 2023-08-28T15:35:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/children-and-sports.28066/post-325637>

My son is 10 and going into 5th grade. He's played flag football for the last few years but they don't offer it for 5/6th grades and older. A lot of his friends are playing full contact football this year because there isn't a flag option. We decided not to let him participate. He seems fine with it. Unfortunately, we dropped the ball on getting him into something else this fall. Oh well. Once I get the rugby league going, we won't have this issue. (It'll still be flag rugby at this age.)

### John Warner — 2023-08-28T16:40:13+0300

### maria@modernhuman — 2024-09-21T16:30:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/children-and-sports.28066/post-339794>

Tennis + golf. Outside. No sunscreen. No sunglasses.

### JeremyG — 2024-09-21T22:09:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/children-and-sports.28066/post-339821>

Risk vs reward. I don't see any upside to playing either sport. I'd focus on racquet sports, as they develop hand/eye coordination, response time, and lateral movement.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-09-24T18:26:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/children-and-sports.28066/post-339961>

Kids main sport should be grounding with skin exposed.

How do you create higher agency children from low dopamine germlines from parents?

Many ways, but here is an easy one.

Today's lesson is in a thread format on transgenerational epigenetics. If you enjoy it share it.

The difference between "should and will" is a massive chasm for the low agency thinker. Every person in life painfully experiences the chasm between their inner vision and its ultimate expression in reality. The chasm is never completely bridged. We all have the conviction, perhaps illusory, that we have much more to say than appears in our actions when we take control of our beliefs to make better choices. Here is how those choices can be improved daily.

# Thread: Are our thought patterns apart of or separate from light, water, and magnetism?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-our-thought-patterns-apart-of-or-separate-from-light-water-and-magnetism.28068/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 32
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TaylorF — 2023-08-08T22:53:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-our-thought-patterns-apart-of-or-separate-from-light-water-and-magnetism.28068/post-324853>

I have been dealing with a chronic illness for about 8 years now and my life goes through a vicious cycle:
1) When I am teaching, for years in NYC now online in a remote location, I am happiest and feel best mentally, even though I have more nnEMF in my life and I don't necessarily eat well, get enough sun, or drink enough water (I do make it a point to get morning light, and midday sun everyday),
2) When I am drinking mountain valley water and taking care of my diet, and getting plenty of sun my health is good, but my mental health struggle with loneliness and agoraphobia over the summer (out of work) becomes terrible.

I seem to go through this in and out of the school year. When I have light, water, magnetism over the summers, everything feels good except my mental state. When I am teaching, I gain weight, there's more stress, less sunlight, more nnEMF but I am happier. What gives?

I just started CBT to work through the anxiety and agoraphobia, so maybe there is more to the picture than just sunlight, good clean water low in deuterium, and avoidance of nnEMF. Maybe past traumas don't allow us to heal, even in the best possible environment?

### Marko Pollo — 2023-08-08T23:23:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-our-thought-patterns-apart-of-or-separate-from-light-water-and-magnetism.28068/post-324854>

While I agree water light magnetism is important, so is having a challenging life every day. I require some physical activities every day so that I am tired and ready for bed. We are meant to be struggling to survive, just like every wild animal in history. Could you try teaching outdoors, working as a camp counselor or something of the sort?

### Neurotic Farmer — 2023-08-08T23:37:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-our-thought-patterns-apart-of-or-separate-from-light-water-and-magnetism.28068/post-324856>

> 1) When I am teaching, for years in NYC now online in a remote location, I am happiest and feel best mentally, even though I have more nnEMF in my life and I don't necessarily eat well, get enough sun, or drink enough water (I do make it a point to get morning light, and midday sun everyday),
> 2) When I am drinking mountain valley water and taking care of my diet, and getting plenty of sun my health is good, but my mental health struggle with loneliness and agoraphobia over the summer (out of work) becomes terrible.
>
> I seem to go through this in and out of the school year. When I have light, water, magnetism over the summers, everything feels good except my mental state. When I am teaching, I gain weight, there's more stress, less sunlight, more nnEMF but I am happier. What gives?
>
> I just started CBT to work through the anxiety and agoraphobia, so maybe there is more to the picture than just sunlight, good clean water low in deuterium, and avoidance of nnEMF. Maybe past traumas don't allow us to heal, even in the best possible environment?

Is it possible that teaching provides a distraction to the mind and that is why you "feel happier"? And in the summer you don't have that distraction, instead sort of feels like you're reinforcing the illness because you're doing all of these things to help you heal?

You miss the physical connection with your students... it’s the way nature intended. **We are meant to experience the body and the mind as a unitary phenomenon an not as separate entities. So in order to be optimal we need to stay firmly rooted and grounded in our body. Just like a tree and how they connect.** If you’re living mostly optimal and have a good redox I would continue to go visit and connect with your school and students. Maybe even more often. Those connections are equally important to you and your students. Most optimal would be to become a homeschool teacher... it’s decentralized and you control the environment.

### NikkiYork — 2023-08-11T05:35:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-our-thought-patterns-apart-of-or-separate-from-light-water-and-magnetism.28068/post-324948>

> [Joseph LeBlanc said:](/goto/post?id=324943)
>
> Becoming a homeschool teacher would be my dream. I wouldn't even know where to begin to make that a reality though. It would need to somehow pay the bills.

You can do it!

Homeschooling your own child? That’s really not hard, I did it for 1.5 years during Covid and up until probably 7th grade it shouldn’t not be too hard. After that depends on you, hahaha.

Homeschooling others? That is a job. You could look into it. I’ve home schooled with Blueridge academy and they have teachers there that accompany home schooling. Somebody who collects the tests and such.

From my experience home schooling takes up way less time than regular school and if you can work with your child around, you can still work.

I worked and homeschooled, but my husband works from home. My son still says I am his favorite teacher. It depends on your child of course. Some children might not be as easy.

I also put him in a forrest school 2 days a week that was paid for from unschooling. For social contacts and to be outside in nature. You get money for things like that from your state, because you don’t occupy a spot in public school.

I don’t know how many children you have. More than one certainly is more difficult, but usually the home schooling/unschooling community offers get-togethers a or groups your kid can join.

I started meeting up with other parents to understand how this works. There are Facebook groups and many helpful Moms and Dads.

If your class grows to big you’ll be able to hire more teachers... decentralize for the win!

### Saichi — 2023-08-14T20:42:13+0300

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=325032)
>
> “Filtered“ meaning what exactly? Theory is theory, facts are facts. A 60Hz fielded is a 60Hz field. Measuring equipment (meters) would show what is actually going on. My partner worked in a Faraday Room and most of the people in there got sick.

What sort of work and electronics were in your partner's faraday? Was the circuit correctly wired as in no hot and neutrals reversed? And was the faraday shielding itself grounded? Everyone has 60Hz AC including Jack except real off grid people. I filter with these units: [https://saticshield.com/super-duty-power-perfect---emi-meter/](https://saticshield.com/super-duty-power-perfect---emi-meter/)

I measured a 90% reduction in DE using Alpha Labs EMI meter (over 95% with Satic Shield's EMI meter but that's surely because it has a smaller frequency range) and 70% reduction in electric and magnetic fields with Alpha Labs 3-axis gauss meter and Gigahertz Solutions ME 3951A with the above installed.

Do you not shield your living spaces? If I remember correctly you posted you live in a small town and in my experience without personal wireless devices and no 'close' cell masts average background RF is around 40 to 100 µw/m2 which both ime and building biology knowledge isn't sufficient for health much less optimal health.

I can't use my phone for more than a minute without feeling symptoms/ill, same would apply to wifi/bluetooth which I never use.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-14T23:30:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-our-thought-patterns-apart-of-or-separate-from-light-water-and-magnetism.28068/post-325071>

> What sort of work and electronics were in your partner's faraday?

My partners Faraday experience was in a military facility. That said we are aware of many people (including one well known bio hacker) who professionally put in a Faraday Room only to get more sick and end up taking it out.

> Everyone has 60Hz AC including Jack except real off grid people.

You are right most people do have 60Hz AC fields, even “off grid” people unless they chose to go all DC - OR - people who have MC cable and grounded everything - or - people who turn off the circuits especially at night. Kill the circuit, No 60Hz AC therefore no “DE” or magnetic or RF (on those circuits). Sure, “Everyone” has 60Hz AC but some have MUCH more than others.

The whole point of a Faraday Room, from an EMF mitigation point of view, is to eliminate ALL nnEMF including 60Hz AC. If you introduce 60Hz AC into a Faraday Room you will be exacerbating the electric field problem and exposure.

> I measured a 90% reduction in DE using Alpha Labs EMI meter (over 95% with Satic Shield's EMI meter but that's surely because it has a smaller frequency range)

Filtering the “DE” only takes care of a VERY small part of the problem. ”DE” is nothing more than an artifact/harmonic of the 60Hz AC. Some people are particularly sensitive to these harmonic frequencies and a “DE” filter can reduce them BUT all these filters leave the 60Hz exactly as it was and it is the 60Hz AC (50Hz Europe etc) that does much of the harm. The Alpha Labs, Satic Shield, Greenwave and Stetzer EMI meters all measure a narrow frequency range. The best, most scientific way is with an oscilloscope. Using an oscilloscope our whole house “DE” PQFilter got better results in real world, independent tests than the Satic. Link to results here: [https://liveemfsafe.com/measure-1](https://liveemfsafe.com/measure-1)

> and 70% reduction in electric and magnetic fields with Alpha Labs 3-axis gauss meter and Gigahertz Solutions ME 3951A with the above installed.

It is physically impossible for ANY “DE” filter (by itself) to reduce electric and/or magnetic fields by 70%. Physics is physics. If you got a 70% reduction in the electric and/or magnetic fields it is for a different reason. Something else was done/changed.

> Do you not shield your living spaces? If I remember correctly you posted you live in a small town and in my experience without personal wireless devices and no 'close' cell masts average background RF is around 40 to 100 µw/m2 which both ime and building biology knowledge isn't sufficient for health much less optimal health.

I have never published the RF reading I have at my place so I do not know where you got those numbers. The Building Biology guide lines for “no concern” RF is based on data gathered and seen health responses to which many other factors contributed. This does not mean everyone will have a health response or even be affected above this level, it certainly does not mean a reading below this level is required for health. Recommended maybe but not necessarily required. You are sensitive to things I am not . . . but I may be sensitive to things you are not.

> I can't use my phone for more than a minute without feeling symptoms/ill, same would apply to wifi/bluetooth which I never use.

You are obviously very sensitive, I know this can make life difficult, we have helped hundreds of EHS people over the years. That said every person’s symptoms and sensitivities are different and require unique solutions. What you need, have found works for you, may not work or be required by others. My set up works for me and my family and I KNOW what my readings are.

What I do know, from years of experience working with hundreds of EHS clients is that people who turn off the 60hz AC at night and create a nnEMF mitigated sleep sanctuary, become less sensitive to, and less affected by ALL nnEMF during the day. They get their lives back.

### coho3131 — 2023-08-15T01:50:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-our-thought-patterns-apart-of-or-separate-from-light-water-and-magnetism.28068/post-325076>

I still know less about EMF compared to most people here, but it always made sense to me that a Faraday room could have unplanned toxicity, because the Faraday room does not let any EMF (nnEMF or natural EMF) escape. Our human bodies make EMF, so in a Faraday room, I was thinking we might actually be able to "microwave" ourselves, especially when we are close to the walls.

Is this accurate, or would the dose be too low to cause toxicity? If someone has EHS, might it be even more concerning?

Also, is the EMF-blocking clothing probably toxic in the same way, because it blocks our natural EMF from leaving our body, and also deflects it back through our bodies? I'm thinking that those EMF-blocking wallets in someone's pants pocket or purse might be a risk factor for genital and pelvic cancers.

\[ Unfortunately, I think many "treatments and cures" end up having more unintended side effects than benefits. \]

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-15T02:48:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-our-thought-patterns-apart-of-or-separate-from-light-water-and-magnetism.28068/post-325078>

> [coho3131 said:](/goto/post?id=325076)
>
> I still know less about EMF compared to most people here, but it always made sense to me that a Faraday room could have unplanned toxicity, because the Faraday room does not let any EMF (nnEMF or natural EMF) escape. Our human bodies make EMF, so in a Faraday room, I was thinking we might actually be able to "microwave" ourselves, especially when we are close to the walls.
>
> Is this accurate, or would the dose be too low to cause toxicity? If someone has EHS, might it be even more concerning?
>
> Also, is the EMF-blocking clothing probably toxic in the same way, because it blocks our natural EMF from leaving our body, and also deflects it back through our bodies? I'm thinking that those EMF-blocking wallets in someone's pants pocket or purse might be a risk factor for genital and pelvic cancers.
>
> \[ Unfortunately, I think many "treatments and cures" end up having more unintended side effects than benefits. \]

Hi Mike, I really can’t speak to the effect of a Faraday room might have on natural (in body) produced EMF, my gut is that would not be an issue. The whole point of a Faraday room is to either keep all nnEMF out or in and a well designed one will do both. This is why you can’t have anything inside that produces any EMF because it is reflected back and bounces around. Think of turning on a light in a mirrored room. Bring a cell phone into a Faraday room and it will switch up to maximum power looking for a connection and that RF is just mirrored back. Also, anything that blocks RF attracts electric fields and the electric fields need to be dealt with at the same time. Many people forget this part and is where grounding comes in. A Faraday room could also cause the rooms next to it to have higher RF.

I DO think a well designed Faraday room can work for a very sensitive person IF they have the understanding and discipline to use it correctly.

An RF blocking hat is also an electric field antenna, unless grounded. If the RF signal comes from the direction of the opening (looking at a cell phone) the hat could reflect the signal back into the head making things worse. A cell phone case should block the signal from getting to you while leaving the back open to keep the signal as low as possible. If you are looking at the screen with the blocking material behind the phone you are reflecting the signal towards you making exposure worse.

> \[ Unfortunately, I think many "treatments and cures" end up having more unintended side effects than benefits. \]

Agreed.

Kill the circuit, No 60Hz AC therefore no “DE” or magnetic or RF (on those circuits). Sure, “Everyone” has 60Hz AC but some have MUCH more than others.

> The whole point of a Faraday Room, from an EMF mitigation point of view, is to eliminate ALL nnEMF including 60Hz AC. If you introduce 60Hz AC into a Faraday Room you will be exacerbating the electric field problem and exposure.

Anyone who doesn't live in a very rural area needs shielding to achieve anything remotely close to optimal health/environment regardless if one can feel moderate levels of RF. And in cities RF levels are usually extreme >1000 µw/m2 and well above it. And in small towns RF levels are usually moderate to high.

> It is physically impossible for ANY “DE” filter (by itself) to reduce electric and/or magnetic fields by 70%. Physics is physics. If you got a 70% reduction in the electric and/or magnetic fields it is for a different reason. Something else was done/changed.

Why I posted "filter" and not "DE filter" specifically. Before Satic updated their website they advertised their included E and M filtering more. I don't know how their filter works but I'm just happy it does. They never stated how much it does but I was pleasantly surprised, very surprised actually, to measure the degree it does.

> I have never published the RF reading I have at my place so I do not know where you got those numbers. The Building Biology guide lines for “no concern” RF is based on data gathered and seen health responses to which many other factors contributed. This does not mean everyone will have a health response or even be affected above this level, it certainly does not mean a reading below this level is required for health. Recommended maybe but not necessarily required. You are sensitive to things I am not . . . but I may be sensitive to things you are not.

I never posted I knew what your background levels were. I've owned Safe and Sound Pro since it was first put on the market by SLT, now own three of them, and have been measuring levels of nearly everywhere I go for years. I have more knowledge than I ever wanted about RF levels.

> You are obviously very sensitive, I know this can make life difficult, we have helped hundreds of EHS people over the years. That said every person’s symptoms and sensitivities are different and require unique solutions. What you need, have found works for you, may not work or be required by others. My set up works for me and my family and I KNOW what my readings are.

My sensitivity isn't what makes life difficult, the failure of consciousness coupled with technology that has put us all in such an unconscionably toxic environment does. Just like you I never 'felt' wifi when I was still using it on my laptop with my router shielded with multiple layers of 120dB foil. The analogy Firstenberg makes to RF is fish in sea trying to describe water. I chronicled my own health revelations here years ago I was totally ignorant of beforehand.

> What I do know, from years of experience working with hundreds of EHS clients is that people who turn off the 60hz AC at night and create a nnEMF mitigated sleep sanctuary, become less sensitive to, and less affected by ALL nnEMF during the day. They get their lives back.

My experience along with others mitigating nnEMF to building biology standards become more sensitive not less in actual ability to feel it in real time, which I don't consider an unwelcome burden.

Turn one on and do a slow motion video with your phone camera. If there is flicker you will see it. This is not scientific because there can still be flicker you don’t see with this method but it weeds out the really bad ones. Have you tried candles and a mirror?

### Wouterbekkers — 2023-08-11T23:13:50+0300

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-12T00:57:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/indoor-light.28069/post-324971>

Most (older) LED lights flicker VERY badly. Newer ones not so much but do need to be tested. When I have to be on the computer I use color correction on my screen and a red light behind it to help counteract any blue, especially in the evening. You can get 660nm LED grow lights which is the frequency used in many Red Light Therapy Lights. There are different schools of thought on this (as there is on everything) but I figure a little counter balance is probably better than non. For reading after sunset (and avoiding legos on the floor) candles are the recommendation. Candles give off NIR & IR with the visible light.

In regards to your chart, remember sunlight changes with time of day, time of year, latitude, altitude and weather and there has NEVER been sunlight after sunset.

# Thread: Sunhunter's journal to happiness
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunhunters-journal-to-happiness.28072/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### SunHunter ☀️ — 2023-08-12T15:54:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunhunters-journal-to-happiness.28072/post-324980>

Dear black swans,

Let me tell you my story. I am female, 50 years and live in Germany.

### **How I found Dr. Kruse**​

After eight years of being a fairly happy carnivore, I became increasingly dissatisfied with my health. My weight kept creeping up and then was higher than it had ever been. The other illnesses also began to come back. Then hot flashes came along. So I started looking for explanations on the Internet again. I came across Sarah Kleiner and her leptin reset course. There she also mentioned Jack Kruse.

### **Mother /Grandmother History**​

My grandmother died at 95. She was in pretty good health. Her problem issues were heart, pancreas and rheumatism. Too low blood pressure and heart weakness. She always complained of a dry mouth. She was active and had a bright and curious mind until high age and she was full of thankfulness.

My great-grandmother died of parotid glands cancer.

My mother is 83 and also in pretty good shape. She has osteoporosis, rheumatism, high blood pressure. Maybe also heart weakness, but undiagnosed cause she stays away from the doctors. She ist still interested in life and lives on her own, doing gardening.
In her twenties/thirties she worked in a pharma chemical lab with lots of toxic substances. She stopped shortly before I was born. That’s maybe where my autism comes from?

### **Prior to discovering Dr. Kruse**​

I got my first mobile phone in 2000 when I was 28 and since then I have been in blue light and EMFs 24/7. I put aside all warnings because they seemed esoteric bullsh\*t.

Throughout my early years I never ate crap or fast food. My parents were really invested into vegetarian real food. We ate heaps of self made goat cheese, butter, eggs and vegetables from our garden. With 32 years I discovered low carb and sticked with that until I was 42.

#### Carnivore​

Then I tried carnivore and had immense health wins with that (Lost 17kg, more energy, improved focus and mood, no acne, no dandruff any more, less wrinkles), so I stayed 90% carnivore until this spring.

After 2-3 years into carnivore, my weight slowly kept creeping up and also some of my former health issues returned. In 2020 I got Thyreoditis de Quervain which is thought to be a rare autoimmune disease of the thyroid. I thought to be quite healthy, never catching a cold, not taking any meds, low blood pressure, low blood sugar, but I had to admit that it was not optimal. I don’t have a lot of lab data because I almost never went to the doc. When I got hot flashes last summer I started researching the internet to find explanations and a cure.

I first started with a very high fat and very strict carnivore diet, eating 120-150g fat/day. That got me rid of the hot flashes, but I gained 7kg during 4 months in autumn and this extreme diet also led to heart palpitations and leg cramps. I added magnesium and potassium which helped a bit.

#### Iodine protocol​

Then I tried the Iodine protocol with 2-8 drops of Lugol’s daily. I think that was a huge mistake. I did this 5 months during winter and spring, until I came across Dr. Kruse mentioning that Lugol’s is not good. I had a lot of bromoderma (kind of acne caused by bromine detox) no matter how slow I went with increasing the iodine. I also gained another 7 kg during that time. I stopped Lugol’s in May but I still have this bromoderma with new breakouts almost daily.

### **Working the Protocols starting Nov 2022**​

Parallel to the high fat and the iodine protocol, I tried to follow the leptin reset protocol from Sarah Kleiner (I think she got most of it from Dr. Kruse) with eliminating nnEMFs, reducing blue light, seeing the sunrise, being more outside, eating a big breakfast and early dinner.

Plus CT in the lake with temperatures between 3 and 12 degrees C (37 - 55 F) twice a week for 2-7 minutes.

I also started to read the blogs and listening to Dr. Kruse podcasts. It was hard to hear about the benefits of sunlight being locked in a German winter! In my despair I bought a red light panel and a UV lamp.

Since spring I am hunting the sun. Whenever there is a 15min sunny slot between the clouds I jump outside and throw off my clothes. That’s why I choose my name, Sunhunter .

I am pretty tanned and outside most of the time. I also still swim in the lake, but of course the water is warmer now in the summer.

So while doing the iodine protocol, I also started the leptin rx during winter. And while my energy and mood improved A LOT, I still kept gaining weight. Why is that? Is it because of the Lugol’s?

My weight has stayed stable at 80kgs since spring. At least I am not gaining any more. But how can I start losing? No matter what I do, the weight is stubborn.

I have an EMF meter and measured and checked my environment thoroughly. We still have the WIFI on during day, but I use ethernet cables 98% of the time and I stay far away from the WIFI router. I live on the edge of a small town with a population of 20k. We also shut down the circuits in the bedrooms during night since a couple of weeks. My sleep has gotten better, and also my HRV has increased.

I added some veggies and carbs back into my diet, because it’s summer time. Most of my food is regional and seasonal, with some exceptions like lemons, coffee, occasional almonds.

I threw out the microwave and things like xylitol.

### **Labs** May 2023​

CRP 0.1
TSH 2.92 (maybe still from the Thyroiditis de Quervain)
Glucose 107 (It’s always high in the morning and then drops below 85 during the whole day, even after my keto meals)
Triglycerides 59.7
HDL 50
LDL 243 (Maybe from vitamin d supplements? I stopped them in March, 2 months before the labs)
Vitamin D 44.8
hbA1c 29.7
haemoglobin-A1C 4.87%

### **Books read so far**​

Power, Sex, Suicide - Nick Lane
Fourth phase of water - Dr. Gerald Pollack
Cells, gels and the engines of life - Dr. Gerald Pollackcomp
Health & Light - John N.Ott
Epi Paleo Rx - Dr Jack Kruse
The Body Electric - Robert O Becker MD

### **Health Wins**​

I have always been a night owl, but after some days of seeing the sunrise I was a completely different person. Jumping out of the bed with energy and great mood! Never need any breaks any more.

My memory improved. Still not at the same level as Dr. Kruse

My focus, concentration, ability to get in the flow and just do the work improved a lot.

I am a much happier person, simply enjoying life most of the time.

My myopia gets better whenever I am in the sun for a couple of days, but is back again after some rainy days where I am mostly inside.

I am less cold (compared to before and compared to other people), but still cold adaptation is a long way to go for me I think.

My hot flashes came back in spring but they have mostly vanished again.

I am menopausal but don’t suffer from all the typical things.

### Biggest Questions​

1.  What is causing the bromoderma /acne and how can I stop it?
2.  Why can’t I lose weight? It seems I am missing a huge thing.

Thanks for reading!

Hi Sunhunter

In terms of the acne/bromoderma link, I'd first be looking at body water ... Where do you think the bromine is coming from or came from? How was it diagnosed as bromoderma acne? Its a halogen, as is fluoride, (fluoroderma?) which also displaces iodine, and iodine is needed for the hydrogen bonding networks to make exclusion zone water in the body, affecting redox, and its redox before detox. So auditing of your current intake of the halogens bromine, chlorine and fluoride could be a first step. So tap water, fluoride toothpastes and mouthwashes etc. Aluminium is another one - on its own its bad enough, but as a tag team with fluoride its worse, particularly if the blood brain barrier is weakened by nnEMF. Fluoride is implicated in thyroid problems and (your Mum link) osteoporosis. Aluminium reflects light, slowing down reactions and possibly interfering with the ability to make vit D in the skin. Aluminium and fluoride are both implicated in symptoms associated with autism.

The skin is also an elimination route for aluminium, along with the urine and the gut. It is better to be in the skin (which sloughs off, or can be removed via sweat) than the brain. Silica is the way that the body eliminates aluminium, and that's through silica rich plant foods or silica rich water, which if you were full on carnivore for several years, might have slowly led to accumulation in the body's tissues of even modest amounts. Even normal tissue or bone turnover can release it back into the circulation. In terms of the lugols, if the iodine is causing a dumping of halogens into the system, I believe in theory it could come out of one place and finish up somewhere else (a secondary pathway) if the primary detox pathway for a toxin isn't working well, which is a good thing if it protects the brain or vital organs.

This is an overview of silica - there's a small section on acne. The thing with aluminium is that silica in the diet or water takes out aluminium, so people think of it as silica takes out aluminium, but then I look at it as in the absence of silica from food or water, exposure to aluminium will take out silica, affecting collagen, including that of the skin, added to the reduction of silica thought to be part of aging. In terms of removing systemic aluminium, (for autism and AD for example) supplements of things like horsetail are much less effective than silica rich mineral water. The UK has no sources of natural silica rich water, so I use imported water for regular detoxing because of my risk of AD (mother and maternal grandmother). In Germany there are several waters that are good sources. I can give you the list if you like.

### [Health Benefits of Silica](https://www.healthaid.co.uk/blogs/news/health-benefits-silica#:~:text=Acne,and%20rebuilding%20the%20connective%20tissues.)

Health Benefits of Silica, bamboo extract, silica for hair loss, silica for skin.

 www.healthaid.co.uk

_“What we have found in clinical trials, involving both healthy individuals and individuals with disease, is that drinking around a litre of silicon-rich mineral water every day can speed up the removal of toxic aluminium from the body via the kidneys and ultimately urine. In fact, our studies showed that individuals experienced significant reductions in their body burden of aluminium, including falls of up to 70% in one case, over a 12-week period.”_

### Sue-UK — 2023-08-13T17:59:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunhunters-journal-to-happiness.28072/post-325004>

> [SunHunter ☀️ said:](/goto/post?id=324993)
>
> Yes, please give me the list

Sorry this is a bit long, but having mentioned it, and to avoid unintentionally sending you down what might be the wrong rabbit hole for your context, I’ve tried to explain it in terms of mine.

[https://pubs.rsc.org/en/content/articlepdf/2013/em/c3em00374d](https://pubs.rsc.org/en/content/articlepdf/2013/em/c3em00374d)

Experiments done by the author of the paper to reduce body load of Al used a silicon rich water with 32 mg/L and he went on to use one at 55 mg/L. I’ve also used one with 93 mg/L. They are all imported as we have no natural sources, but Germany has several brands. The silica content on bottles is shown as X mg/L but for systemic use the key number is the amount of orthosilicic acid, (OSA), which isn’t usually shown on the bottle. But the 32mg/L I use has 50, so in terms of cost it's working out if it's cheaper to drink half a litre of a water with an OSA of 100, or drink double the amount of the 50, and a choice might also include what else is in a particular brand, and what daily minimum I’m aiming for. How much water someone uses might also depend on their Al body burden, whether they can increase dietary intake of silicon, (and some foods supply more OSA than others per 100g), what reduction in exposure they can do, and what exposure they aren’t able to avoid at this moment in time. (For example not a good idea to drop prescribed medications without medical supervision, then there’s air pollution etc). Anyway, here's the list of German waters (source Silica Water the secret of healthy blue zone longevity in the aluminium age, by Dennis N Crouse, PhD).
The first figure is the silica in mg/L, the OSA figure is in brackets.

Vulkani Heilwasser 83.5 (133.6)
St Linus Heilvasser 58.9 (92.2)
Dunaris 54.9 (87.8)
Hirschquelle 53.9 (86.2)
Konig Otto Sprudel 50 (80)
Wildsber Quelle 40.9 (65.4)
Gerolsteiner 40.2 (64.3)
Teinacher 40 (64)
Tonisteiner 35.4 (56.6)
Wittenseer Quelle 33.4 (53.4)
Eifel Stil 33.3 (53.3).

Although the paper above gives an overview of some of the potential exposures to aluminium, and elimination routes, in terms of the elimination via faeces, what it misses that I think is connected to Jack’s work is that even if it goes down through the gut without going into circulation, it has the potential to interfere with the light signalling of the microbiome and in the skin its reflectance properties can interfere with absorbing light, particularly UV. My concern in terms of being a carnivore who might not be pooping every day is that I will have a microbiome/gut effect for longer than if I did, but either way any ingested or excreted in the bile could be absorbed or reabsorbed on the way down.

A carnivore such as a lion in the wild will eat the internal organs and intestines of the herbivore it eats, so will be getting a source of fermenting plant fibre and silicon, as well as the bacteria from the gut of their prey, whilst also getting more microbial diversity from their environment. The modern carnivore diet is more like a lion in a zoo and in the wild lions are not exposed to anything like the levels of aluminium that humans expose themselves to, often unknowingly.

The so called blue zone areas found in specific areas of Costa Rica, Japan, Greece, Sardinia, and in Loma Linda California, despite different diets, all share having natural silicon rich water drinking water, used to water their crops, and their traditional diets included latitude based plant foods rich in silicon. The UK has no natural sources of silicon rich mineral water, which is possibly why oats became a year round staple, along with barley, wholegrain wheat/rye bread, potatoes and/or other roots (with skins) and beer … I see it as we became the human equivalent of the larder hoarding squirrel, adding in what could be dug up or scavenged or hunted in winter as we went along, together with stored fermented foods and drinks. More microbiome diversity, less aluminium exposure and more sources of silicon.

With less historical exposure to Al, and by keeping aluminium out, I think in the past countries with definite seasons were better able to make use of the sun when it was available, and survive seasonal cold far more naturally than we do now. Although in earlier times we would have had periods with no food, and burned body fat for days, the same thing now might dump fat stored toxins into the circulation, going from a relatively safe storage site to the brain or other major organ, so water fasting is not a risk I’d take at the moment. Another reason the carnivore, high fat diets or going beyond IF to extended fasting isn’t for me is that although chronic high levels of blood sugar and insulin are bad, chronically low blood sugar and insulin would be a risk for my n=, because I’ve read that insulin degrading enzymes also degrade amyloid plaque, and my mother and maternal grandmother had dementia. Whatever benefits are seen initially to insulin sensitivity from these diets, once insulin sensitivity is restored, chronically if nothing stimulates insulin, there would be no signal from lowering blood sugar to stimulate insulin degrading hormone. So the epi paleo with seasonal carbs suits me better in terms of year round DHA, moderate IF, adding seasonal carbs in summer, but in winter I adjust winter epi paleo to suit my n=1 context.

The other reason I have for regularly using the water is because of what nnemf potentially does to a cell or system contaminated with aluminium, in terms of heat damage as well as interfering with light signalling. If the aluminium is ingested and the blood brain barrier is intact, the body may dump it in the skin, collagen cartilage or bone etc (diseases of aging like osteoporosis, vascular system problems etc), so the brain may have relative protection if anything that affects the BBB is avoided, such as a head injury. But nnemf weakens the BBB ….
