### ChristineH — 2023-08-26T20:29:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunhunters-journal-to-happiness.28072/post-325602>

> [SunHunter ☀️ said:](/goto/post?id=324980)
>
> Dear black swans,
>
> Let me tell you my story. I am female, 50 years and live in Germany.
>
>
>
> ### **How I found Dr. Kruse**​
>
> After eight years of being a fairly happy carnivore, I became increasingly dissatisfied with my health. My weight kept creeping up and then was higher than it had ever been. The other illnesses also began to come back. Then hot flashes came along. So I started looking for explanations on the Internet again. I came across Sarah Kleiner and her leptin reset course. There she also mentioned Jack Kruse.
>
>
>
> ### **Mother /Grandmother History**​
>
> My grandmother died at 95. She was in pretty good health. Her problem issues were heart, pancreas and rheumatism. Too low blood pressure and heart weakness. She always complained of a dry mouth. She was active and had a bright and curious mind until high age and she was full of thankfulness.
>
> My great-grandmother died of parotid glands cancer.
>
> My mother is 83 and also in pretty good shape. She has osteoporosis, rheumatism, high blood pressure. Maybe also heart weakness, but undiagnosed cause she stays away from the doctors. She ist still interested in life and lives on her own, doing gardening.
> In her twenties/thirties she worked in a pharma chemical lab with lots of toxic substances. She stopped shortly before I was born. That’s maybe where my autism comes from?
>
>
> ### **Prior to discovering Dr. Kruse**​
>
> I got my first mobile phone in 2000 when I was 28 and since then I have been in blue light and EMFs 24/7. I put aside all warnings because they seemed esoteric bullsh\*t.
>
> Throughout my early years I never ate crap or fast food. My parents were really invested into vegetarian real food. We ate heaps of self made goat cheese, butter, eggs and vegetables from our garden. With 32 years I discovered low carb and sticked with that until I was 42.
>
>
> #### Carnivore​
>
> Then I tried carnivore and had immense health wins with that (Lost 17kg, more energy, improved focus and mood, no acne, no dandruff any more, less wrinkles), so I stayed 90% carnivore until this spring.
>
> After 2-3 years into carnivore, my weight slowly kept creeping up and also some of my former health issues returned. In 2020 I got Thyreoditis de Quervain which is thought to be a rare autoimmune disease of the thyroid. I thought to be quite healthy, never catching a cold, not taking any meds, low blood pressure, low blood sugar, but I had to admit that it was not optimal. I don’t have a lot of lab data because I almost never went to the doc. When I got hot flashes last summer I started researching the internet to find explanations and a cure.
>
> I first started with a very high fat and very strict carnivore diet, eating 120-150g fat/day. That got me rid of the hot flashes, but I gained 7kg during 4 months in autumn and this extreme diet also led to heart palpitations and leg cramps. I added magnesium and potassium which helped a bit.
>
>
> #### Iodine protocol​
>
> Then I tried the Iodine protocol with 2-8 drops of Lugol’s daily. I think that was a huge mistake. I did this 5 months during winter and spring, until I came across Dr. Kruse mentioning that Lugol’s is not good. I had a lot of bromoderma (kind of acne caused by bromine detox) no matter how slow I went with increasing the iodine. I also gained another 7 kg during that time. I stopped Lugol’s in May but I still have this bromoderma with new breakouts almost daily.
>
>
>
> ### **Working the Protocols starting Nov 2022**​
>
> Parallel to the high fat and the iodine protocol, I tried to follow the leptin reset protocol from Sarah Kleiner (I think she got most of it from Dr. Kruse) with eliminating nnEMFs, reducing blue light, seeing the sunrise, being more outside, eating a big breakfast and early dinner.
>
> Plus CT in the lake with temperatures between 3 and 12 degrees C (37 - 55 F) twice a week for 2-7 minutes.
>
> I also started to read the blogs and listening to Dr. Kruse podcasts. It was hard to hear about the benefits of sunlight being locked in a German winter! In my despair I bought a red light panel and a UV lamp.
>
> Since spring I am hunting the sun. Whenever there is a 15min sunny slot between the clouds I jump outside and throw off my clothes. That’s why I choose my name, Sunhunter .
>
> I am pretty tanned and outside most of the time. I also still swim in the lake, but of course the water is warmer now in the summer.
>
> So while doing the iodine protocol, I also started the leptin rx during winter. And while my energy and mood improved A LOT, I still kept gaining weight. Why is that? Is it because of the Lugol’s?
>
> My weight has stayed stable at 80kgs since spring. At least I am not gaining any more. But how can I start losing? No matter what I do, the weight is stubborn.
>
> I have an EMF meter and measured and checked my environment thoroughly. We still have the WIFI on during day, but I use ethernet cables 98% of the time and I stay far away from the WIFI router. I live on the edge of a small town with a population of 20k. We also shut down the circuits in the bedrooms during night since a couple of weeks. My sleep has gotten better, and also my HRV has increased.
>
> I added some veggies and carbs back into my diet, because it’s summer time. Most of my food is regional and seasonal, with some exceptions like lemons, coffee, occasional almonds.
>
> I threw out the microwave and things like xylitol.
>
>
>
> ### **Labs** May 2023​
>
> CRP 0.1
> TSH 2.92 (maybe still from the Thyroiditis de Quervain)
> Glucose 107 (It’s always high in the morning and then drops below 85 during the whole day, even after my keto meals)
> Triglycerides 59.7
> HDL 50
> LDL 243 (Maybe from vitamin d supplements? I stopped them in March, 2 months before the labs)
> Vitamin D 44.8
> hbA1c 29.7
> haemoglobin-A1C 4.87%
>
>
>
> ### **Books read so far**​
>
> Power, Sex, Suicide - Nick Lane
> Fourth phase of water - Dr. Gerald Pollack
> Cells, gels and the engines of life - Dr. Gerald Pollackcomp
> Health & Light - John N.Ott
> Epi Paleo Rx - Dr Jack Kruse
> The Body Electric - Robert O Becker MD
>
>
>
> ### **Health Wins**​
>
> I have always been a night owl, but after some days of seeing the sunrise I was a completely different person. Jumping out of the bed with energy and great mood! Never need any breaks any more.
>
> My memory improved. Still not at the same level as Dr. Kruse
>
> My focus, concentration, ability to get in the flow and just do the work improved a lot.
>
> I am a much happier person, simply enjoying life most of the time.
>
> My myopia gets better whenever I am in the sun for a couple of days, but is back again after some rainy days where I am mostly inside.
>
> I am less cold (compared to before and compared to other people), but still cold adaptation is a long way to go for me I think.
>
> My hot flashes came back in spring but they have mostly vanished again.
>
> I am menopausal but don’t suffer from all the typical things.
>
>
>
> ### Biggest Questions​
>
> 1.  What is causing the bromoderma /acne and how can I stop it?
> 2.  Why can’t I lose weight? It seems I am missing a huge thing.
>
>
>
> Thanks for reading!

Verstehe. Wie ich verstehe, bekommt man auch die Strahlungen wenn es bewölkt ist. Also möglichst viel Zeit draußen und in der Natur verbringen. Und viel Fisch essen, was auch Jod vom Natur her hat. Kaffee nur wenn man da ist wo die Bohnen wachsen, am Equator.

# Thread: Collection of good places for grounding
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/collection-of-good-places-for-grounding.28073/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### NikkiYork — 2023-08-12T18:01:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/collection-of-good-places-for-grounding.28073/post-324983>

I’m trying g to figure out where to ground best and I thought, if we collect this together, it could help everybody.

1) on the beach, best in wet sand, if it’s ok with hands and feet touching the ground

2) in New York in Central Park on one of the bedrock stones that reaches all the way into the ground - not sure, if there was a specific one

3) on earth/ground soil that seems to be far away from the grid.

And I have a question: I have a steel and aluminum deck that is free standing. It is basically an industrial mezzanine. Four steel poles anchored into the ground with concrete. Not attached to the house. The deck flooring is an aluminum pan that was filled with concrete.

When I water the plants and I’m standing barefoot in a puddle on our deck, it feels pretty good. It should be grounding well, am I correct?

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-12T22:49:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/collection-of-good-places-for-grounding.28073/post-324991>

Measure and you will know. This is one thing a body voltage meter is also good for. Since you are grounded when using a BV meter if you touch something that is grounded your BV drops (to zero sometimes) because you are double grounded. The field you are in is exactly the same but the induced E-field goes to the second ground not the meter. If your deck is a good ground this will happen. IF the deck is not grounded but picking up electric fields, the BV reading will go up as you get closer and jump up when you touch it. Most refrigerators, dish washing machines, tumble dryers are grounded and your BV reading drops when you touch them. If you stand on the deck WITH SHOES ON whatever BV reading you get is a good indication of the 60Hz AC field that the deck is picking up all the time. Even if the deck is grounded, being metal and close to the house there is a good chance it is carrying a 60Hz AC field. IF SO, grounding on the deck would like grounding to the house ground. Grounding/Earthing in Nature, by definition, is being away from “not-nature” (any source of nnEMF electric fields).

# Thread: Better back to school laptop option
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-back-to-school-laptop-option.28075/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### DLO — 2023-08-13T17:06:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-back-to-school-laptop-option.28075/post-324999>

I like the direction this is going new safer certifications.

Lenovo Xiaoxin 12.7-inch learning tablet is here. Unlike the scores of Android tablets dotting the marketplace, the new Lenovo Xiaoxin is special in that it features a 12.7-inch ‘paper-like’ LCD display that causes the least eye fatigue. Lenovo said this has been achieved thanks to the incorporation of the nano-etching AG process and diffuse reflection technology that has imparted a paper-like feel to the display while still being in color.

As [GizmoChina](https://www.gizmochina.com/2023/08/10/lenono-xiaoxin-learning-tablet-for-2499-yuan/) reported, the hardware-level low blue light feature which can effectively reduce blue light emission to the minimum goes a long way in making it safe and easy for the eyes. Lenovo is claiming this has helped the tablet achieve the first RÜV LCD paper display certification. Apart from this, the tablet has also received the Rheinland global eye protection 3.0 certification, Rheinland flicker-free certification, Rheinland growth care certification, and National Eye Engineering Center eye protection certification five-stage authoritative eye protection certification.

Apart from this, the Lenovo Xiaoxin also lets parents exercise appropriate control over the tablet’s Wi-Fi and Bluetooth connectivity options. All of this makes the tablet ideal for use by the student community who can focus on consuming content pertaining to their education even for long hours without getting worried about the eyes getting fatigued in the process. With the right parental controls applied, the students will have the minimum distraction, letting them focus more on their studies instead.

### Lenovo Xiaoxin learning tablet with 12.7-inch ‘paper-like’ display now on pre-order​

August 11, 2023 By [Sovan Mandal](https://goodereader.com/blog/author/sovy) [4 Comments](https://goodereader.com/blog/tablet-slates/lenovo-xiaoxin-learnign-tablet-with-12-7-inch-paper-like-display-now-on-pre-order#disqus_thread)

[](https://assets.goodereader.com/blog/uploads/images/2023/08/11104942/lenovo-xiaoxin-learning-tablet-with-paper-like-display.jpg)
Lenovo Xiaoxin 12.7-inch learning tablet is here. Unlike the scores of Android tablets dotting the marketplace, the new Lenovo Xiaoxin is special in that it features a 12.7-inch ‘paper-like’ LCD display that causes the least eye fatigue. Lenovo said this has been achieved thanks to the incorporation of the nano-etching AG process and diffuse reflection technology that has imparted a paper-like feel to the display while still being in color.
As [GizmoChina](https://www.gizmochina.com/2023/08/10/lenono-xiaoxin-learning-tablet-for-2499-yuan/) reported, the hardware-level low blue light feature which can effectively reduce blue light emission to the minimum goes a long way in making it safe and easy for the eyes. Lenovo is claiming this has helped the tablet achieve the first RÜV LCD paper display certification. Apart from this, the tablet has also received the Rheinland global eye protection 3.0 certification, Rheinland flicker-free certification, Rheinland growth care certification, and National Eye Engineering Center eye protection certification five-stage authoritative eye protection certification.
Apart from this, the Lenovo Xiaoxin also lets parents exercise appropriate control over the tablet’s Wi-Fi and Bluetooth connectivity options. All of this makes the tablet ideal for use by the student community who can focus on consuming content pertaining to their education even for long hours without getting worried about the eyes getting fatigued in the process. With the right parental controls applied, the students will have the minimum distraction, letting them focus more on their studies instead.
Coming to the specs, the Lenovo Xiaoxin comes with a 2.6GHz octa-core processor that is coupled to 8 GB of memory and 128 GB of storage. You can further add another 1 TB of storage via microSD cards as per your needs. A large 10200mAh promises ample run time, with Lenovo claiming around 8 hours of video playback time with a single full charge of the battery.
The 12.7-inch LCD display has a 16:10 aspect ratio and 2944 x 1840 pixels resolution. It comes with a USB Type-C port that is also used for charging the device. There is an audio port available too. On-board sensors include GPS navigation along with a compass, gyroscope, and a light sensor.
The tablet is currently on pre-order in China where it is priced 2499 Yuan, which comes to around $346. Lenovo said early buyers will be provided with 50,000 Yuan worth of learning resources along with a protective case, a tablet stand, and AI learning tools.

### lsu9546 — 2023-08-13T22:50:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-vitamin-d.28076/post-325023>

Got a question and would like some input. Have a friend who is tan, spends lots of time in sun(sunrise, day, and sunset), works a job that they are outside a lot. Has a good sollar callous and doesn't need sunscreen and never uses it. Doesn't wear sunglasses at all. Eat animal based diet high in fat and eats seasonally for veggies and fruits. Lives in Louisiana so not a terrible latitude and lives in a small town with about 2000 people. Checked house already and emf is not bad. Gets about at least 1.5 hours per day with just shorts on in sunlight during UVB. On weekends has a pool and spends sometimes 6 hours in midday sun. They are physically active and not obese. Limits ALAN and wears blue blockers after sunset. Had labs done and still have low vitamin D of 17ng/ml. Could there be some genetic defect in producing vitamin D or something else could be causing this?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-vitamin-d.28076/post-325077>

> [lsu9546 said:](/goto/post?id=325023)
>
> Got a question and would like some input. Have a friend who is tan, spends lots of time in sun(sunrise, day, and sunset), works a job that they are outside a lot. Has a good sollar callous and doesn't need sunscreen and never uses it. Doesn't wear sunglasses at all. Eat animal based diet high in fat and eats seasonally for veggies and fruits. Lives in Louisiana so not a terrible latitude and lives in a small town with about 2000 people. Checked house already and emf is not bad. Gets about at least 1.5 hours per day with just shorts on in sunlight during UVB. On weekends has a pool and spends sometimes 6 hours in midday sun. They are physically active and not obese. Limits ALAN and wears blue blockers after sunset. Had labs done and still have low vitamin D of 17ng/ml. Could there be some genetic defect in producing vitamin D or something else could be causing this?

I would consider that -
1) this may be a lab measurement error
2) 1,25(OH)2D3 was ordered instead of 25-OH 25(OH)D3

[https://asbmr.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1359/jbmr.070716](https://asbmr.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1359/jbmr.070716)

### [Vitamin D: Metabolism](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2879391/)

The biologically active metabolite of vitamin D, 1,25(OH)\[2\] D\[3\] , affects mineral homeostasis and has numerous other diverse physiological functions including effects on growth of cancer cells and protection against certain immune disorders. This chapter ...

 www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

### [Don’t routinely measure 1,25-dihydroxyvitamin D unless the patient has hypercalcemia or decreased kidney function.](https://www.aafp.org/pubs/afp/collections/choosing-wisely/140.html)

Many practitioners become confused when ordering a vitamin D test. Because 1,25-dihydroxyvitamin D is the active form of vitamin D, many practitioners think that measuring 1,25-dihydroxyvitamin D is an accurate means to estimate vitamin D stores and test for vitamin D deficiency, which is...

 www.aafp.org

### JanSz — 2023-09-04T23:20:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-does-red-light-therapy-and-methylene-blue-fit-in-light-cycle.28077/post-325928>

[@Elkim](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/29210/)

### [Biohacking Your Way to Wellness - Small Biohacks That Make a Big Difference](https://drnathansbryan.com/biohacking-your-way-to-wellness/)

Discover the role of nitric oxide in biohacking and how we can optimize our own biology to combat disease and stay healthy.

 drnathansbryan.com

[](https://drnathansbryan.com/)

### DISPELLING CONFUSION REGARDING METHYLENE BLUE​

Let me dispel some confusion regarding a popular biohack called **methylene blue.** We have used methylene blue in the research lab for decades **to inhibit an enzyme called soluble guanylyl cyclase (sGC)**. sGC is a primary target for nitric oxide that leads to an increase in a second messenger called cGMP.

**This is the messenger that allows for smooth muscle relaxation and dilation of blood vessels.
This is also the target of drugs like Viagra for erectile dysfunction.

Inhibiting nitric oxide based signaling with methylene blue is not a good idea, especially long term or on a daily basis.** Obviously, these effects **are dose dependent** and micro-dosing with methylene blue may not have these effects but the mechanism of action is clear.

Anything that inhibits nitric oxide based signaling will lead to reduced blood flow, increased inflammation, oxidative stress, immune dysfunction and the early onset and progression of chronic disease.

Low dose methylene blue may work by hormesis. Hormesis is defined as a phenomenon in which a harmful substance gives stimulating and beneficial effects to living organisms when the quantity of the harmful substance is small.

The concept of hormesis is to periodically dose and this is not designed to be done daily or else the body does not have a chance to respond and upregulate dependent pathways. So, take caution in the use of methylene blue and don’t overdo it.
**I avoid it all together.**

### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-07T18:52:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-does-red-light-therapy-and-methylene-blue-fit-in-light-cycle.28077/post-332285>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-practice-long-distance-running-affects-my-health.28083/post-325108>

Hy, I'm new here (this is my first post!)
I start reading Dr. Jack's work and start with the blog (all the blogs about leptin and his book) and of course most of the recent podcasts because some of the old ones I'm not able to found.

I always practice sport all my life and I love to run (long, very long distances!) I'm trying to integrate all I'm learning from Dr. Jack's posts and see if I can handle the practice of running with good health.

My question is there any post that you recommend reading here or on Patreon (I'm also a member there!) where he talks about sports endurance?

Thank you very much.

### JanSz — 2023-08-15T20:41:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-practice-long-distance-running-affects-my-health.28083/post-325111>

Speaking of endurance.
When we are healthy endurance comes easier.
One would like to know that he/she started going downhill with health about 10 years before actually getting sick? And that this is a time to start thinking about the situation.

There is a metric that may help in assessing this.

Think about many miles of your arteries, heart, veins. Cardiovascular system.
That system is not rigid, it is not set in stone.
When healthy that system's volume is variable, it volume depends on amount of stress any particular part of it is under at the time.
Person whose cardiovascular system is rigid is a sick person.
They have, memory problems, Alzheimer's, diabetes, Parkinson, arterial sclerosis, heart attacks, etc.

There is a test that evaluates playability of vascular system.
Supposedly, the cardiovascular system begins losing playability at least ten years before visible onset of actual disease.

The known factor promoting playability is NO, Nitric Oxide.
NO when released is a gas.
The test actually evaluates how veins react to stress.
So, it indirectly tells if there is enough or there is a deficiency of NO.

You may feel 100% healthy, and actually be 100% healthy at the time of the test
but
if you get warning 10 years ahead, you will have a time to do something about it.

EndoPath test

### coho3131 — 2023-08-15T23:47:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-practice-long-distance-running-affects-my-health.28083/post-325119>

> [Gomes1983 said:](/goto/post?id=325108)
>
> Hy, I'm new here (this is my first post!)
> I start reading Dr. Jack's work and start with the blog (all the blogs about leptin and his book) and of course most of the recent podcasts because some of the old ones I'm not able to found.
>
> I always practice sport all my life and I love to run (long, very long distances!) I'm trying to integrate all I'm learning from Dr. Jack's posts and see if I can handle the practice of running with good health.
>
> My question is there any post that you recommend reading here or on Patreon (I'm also a member there!) where he talks about sports endurance?
>
> Thank you very much.

There is a good amount of data on the cardiac risks involved in endurance sports. It's not natural for humans to go at high intensity for long periods of time. You probably know the story, but the first human to ever run a marathon might have actually died shortly after finishing the effort.

I love running fast and far, but I realize it is not great for my heart, and I am sacrificing my cardiac lifespan in exchange for a mental health benefit. I used to run 45-55 miles a week, including at least one long run a week of 10-24 miles, but after reviewing the data, I decided to cut back on distance running. I am one of the people that call themselves a "recovering endurance addict".

I added **Erwan Le Corre** types of physical activity, walking, hiking, sprinting, strength training (kettlebells and dumbbells, and not benches or bars), swimming, biking, gardening, yard work, cross country skiing, soccer, and cold exposures to diversify the physical risks also.

I only exercise outdoors unless there is a severe storm. I always go outside with only shorts, and no shirts, socks, or hats unless it is dangerously cold or I need physical protection for a body part, my face, or my eyes \[ the lawnmower does like to throw stones, and thick brush can poke eyes and rip skin.  \]. I will wear a hat and gloves before I put on a shirt if it is very cold.

If you are up for a new physical challenge, you might like to try cold water swimming through the winter. Please do not try this if you have a risk factor that would make this dangerous for you. I would suggest getting used to the cold water gradually also by starting in late summer and going at least every other day as the weather gets colder. I made it all the way to 39 degrees F (that is as cold as the liquid phase gets) last year, but only for about 20 seconds! I use neoprene socks, gloves, and a hood when it gets pretty cold. When it gets very cold, I keep my head above the water. I was still swimming for short periods of time while the water temperature was in the high-40s F, but after that I just waded for short periods of time, and sometimes in intervals. There is nothing like the feeling that stays with you for a while after getting out of really cold water. Be careful of vertigo also, so get out of the water slowly and hang on to the dock or sit on the ground for a while until you know how your body will respond. I swim in a small water-filled quarry that is near our property, and I tie one end of a rope to a dock and the other end of the rope around my waist so I can pull myself out. I also always go with at least one other person that stays on shore and could pull me in also if needed for safety. It's fun!

### Dr. Marcus Ettinger — 2023-08-18T00:40:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-practice-long-distance-running-affects-my-health.28083/post-325249>

I use this as my gauge for running only and put in around two 10Ks per week. I also have my sweet spot for walking, HIIT, and strength training. My goal is to find the sweet-spot in benefit - not too little, and not too much.

**2012 Observational data from following 52,000 men and women over 15 years. Highlights:**

\- Overall, running was associated with a 19% lower risk of all-cause mortality when compared to those that did not run.

\- There is a U-shaped curve relationship to pace, distance, and frequency. No running can be the same as running too much.

\- Individuals who ran six and seven miles per hour had a significant 21% and 27% lower risk of all-cause mortality, whereas those who ran eight or more miles per hour had a nonsignificant 7% lower risk of all-cause mortality.

\- Running up to 20 miles per week was associated with a significantly reduced risk of death, with the largest benefit observed in those who ran between 10 to 15 miles weekly

\- For those who ran 20 to 25 miles and more than 25 miles per week, however, there was a nonsignificant 10% and 5% reduction in all-cause mortality. My thesis: 20+ miles per week is no longer a hormetic stress and now dramatically increases oxidative stress. The mitochondria can’t produce the needed SOD and Glutathione… to keep up with demand, due to exhaustion of precursors. No increased benefit in all-cause mortality is the result, and based on the below data, it may even be quite harmful.

\- Similarly, the mortality benefit was observed in individuals who ran two to five days per week, but the benefit began turning in the other direction when subjects ran six or seven days per week. At six and seven days, the reduction in all-cause mortality was not statistically significant.

Lee DC, Pate RR, Lavie CJ, et al. Running and all-cause mortality risk--is more better? American College of Sports Medicine 2012 Annual Meeting; June 2, 2012; San Francisco, CA.

Additional:

\- Regular and intense endurance exercise such as these events might lead to myocardial fibrosis, particularly in the atria, interventricular septum, and right ventricle, which in turn can lead to atrial or ventricular arrhythmias. Endurance exercise may also be associated with coronary artery calcification, diastolic dysfunction, or arterial stiffening.

O'Keefe JH, Patil HR, Lavie CJ, et al. Potential adverse cardiovascular effects from excessive endurance exercise. _Mayo Clin Proc_ 2012; 87:587-595.

### coho3131 — 2023-08-18T01:08:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-practice-long-distance-running-affects-my-health.28083/post-325253>

> [Dr. Marcus Ettinger said:](/goto/post?id=325249)
>
> I use this as my gauge for running only and put in around two 10Ks per week. I also have my sweet spot for walking, HIIT, and strength training. My goal is to find the sweet-spot in benefit - not too little, and not too much.
>
> **2012 Observational data from following 52,000 men and women over 15 years. Highlights:**
>
> \- Overall, running was associated with a 19% lower risk of all-cause mortality when compared to those that did not run.
>
> \- There is a U-shaped curve relationship to pace, distance, and frequency. No running can be the same as running too much.
>
> \- Individuals who ran six and seven miles per hour had a significant 21% and 27% lower risk of all-cause mortality, whereas those who ran eight or more miles per hour had a nonsignificant 7% lower risk of all-cause mortality.
>
> \- Running up to 20 miles per week was associated with a significantly reduced risk of death, with the largest benefit observed in those who ran between 10 to 15 miles weekly
>
> \- For those who ran 20 to 25 miles and more than 25 miles per week, however, there was a nonsignificant 10% and 5% reduction in all-cause mortality. My thesis: 20+ miles per week is no longer a hormetic stress and now dramatically increases oxidative stress. The mitochondria can’t produce the needed SOD and Glutathione… to keep up with demand, due to exhaustion of precursors. No increased benefit in all-cause mortality is the result, and based on the below data, it may even be quite harmful.
>
> \- Similarly, the mortality benefit was observed in individuals who ran two to five days per week, but the benefit began turning in the other direction when subjects ran six or seven days per week. At six and seven days, the reduction in all-cause mortality was not statistically significant.
>
> Lee DC, Pate RR, Lavie CJ, et al. Running and all-cause mortality risk--is more better? American College of Sports Medicine 2012 Annual Meeting; June 2, 2012; San Francisco, CA.
>
> Additional:
>
> \- Regular and intense endurance exercise such as these events might lead to myocardial fibrosis, particularly in the atria, interventricular septum, and right ventricle, which in turn can lead to atrial or ventricular arrhythmias. Endurance exercise may also be associated with coronary artery calcification, diastolic dysfunction, or arterial stiffening.
>
> O'Keefe JH, Patil HR, Lavie CJ, et al. Potential adverse cardiovascular effects from excessive endurance exercise. _Mayo Clin Proc_ 2012; 87:587-595.

This is some of the data I was thinking about when I mentioned the cardiac risks involved in endurance sports.

Jack's comment - - > "If you have low heteroplasmy no. If you have higher levels, yes."

Also, the above data is not in light-optimized people, and there are no light controls either.

A lot of those "runners" were also probably eating the SAD, eating too many grams of carbohydrates, were not CT-adapted, and were not optimal fat metabolizers.

I'm now thinking that significant endurance activity might not be as harmful, or it might not be harmful at all, or it could be helpful, in people that have optimized mitochondria.

What do you think about these points?

### Dr. Marcus Ettinger — 2023-08-18T02:15:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-practice-long-distance-running-affects-my-health.28083/post-325258>

Just using logic here and personal observation. They are running outside. At an 8-minute-per-mile pace, you can run 7 miles in just under an hour and 14 miles in just under 2 hours. I bet the vast majority are doing this outside, all year long. I doubt light adaption is an issue here. Runners, on average get more sun than most other people. I also doubt that the majority of runners are following a (SAD) diet comparable to a non-runner. Maybe more carbs, but burning through them better than a sedentary person. You don't often see fat distance runners. Even mitochondriacs, on average, are not doing CT regularly. I'm a surfer, so I do it often. If I wasn't I doubt I'd spend 3,000 for a CT set-up. Lastly, running and especially weight-training optimizes mitochondria. Just my opinion here, light is one of many factors for an optimized life. Light with vigorous movement, for humans, goes hand-in-hand.

### coho3131 — 2023-08-18T02:22:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-practice-long-distance-running-affects-my-health.28083/post-325259>

> [Dr. Marcus Ettinger said:](/goto/post?id=325258)
>
> Just using logic here and personal observation. They are running outside. At an 8-minute-per-mile pace, you can run 7 miles in just under an hour and 14 miles in just under 2 hours. I bet the vast majority are doing this outside, all year long. I doubt light adaption is an issue here. Runners, on average get more sun than most other people. I also doubt that the majority of runners are following a (SAD) diet comparable to a non-runner. Maybe more carbs, but burning through them better than a sedentary person. You don't often see fat distance runners. Even mitochondriacs, on average, are not doing CT regularly. I'm a surfer, so I do it often. If I wasn't I doubt I'd spend 3,000 for a CT set-up. Lastly, running and especially weight-training optimizes mitochondria. Just my opinion here, light is one of many factors for an optimized life. Light with vigorous movement, for humans, goes hand-in-hand.

Thank you!

Photobiomodulation = LLLT = red & infrared light/laser therapy = it can help neurodegenerative diseases = Alzheimer’s disease, Parkinson’s disease, FTD, Huntington’s disease. LONG COVID etc How? PBM increases ATP production. PBM increases improves mitochondrial thermodynamics without the need of food or exercise. Photobiomodulation treatment has been shown to improve cell survival, decreases apoptosis, improve autophagic efficiency, alleviates oxidative stress, improve calcium ion homeostasis in mtDNA, suppresses inflammation, and rescues/stimulates mitochondrial function from heteroplasmy or low NAD+ states. IT is a better choice than any NAD+ stimulating drug. Natural PBM is how you should start your morning. THE SUN.

Portuguese dehydrated cod is not a jerky. It's heavily salted for preservation and can sit in cardboard at room temp. It has to soaked in water and the water pitched a few times. Cod has an amazing ability to reconstitute to a fleshy texture.

I'm Portuguese decent and eat alot of cod. Where I live there's market that has a big room of dehydrated cod from around the world

I bought frozen bacalao in Panama and then rehydrated it over a few days in the fridge with several water changes,. I probably needed to do a little bit more because it was still pretty salty when I eventually fried it. But it was delicious.. I havent ever felt so happy when eating cod like that before. And it was only 5-6 dollars for a reasonable sized piece. [@DLO](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19314/) I bought it in the Rey at Coronado.

# Thread: Questions coming up daily upon waking..today was Environment, EMF Filters
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-coming-up-daily-upon-waking-today-was-environment-emf-filters.28089/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Martha Ray — 2023-08-17T05:10:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-coming-up-daily-upon-waking-today-was-environment-emf-filters.28089/post-325167>

<table style="width: 100%"><tbody><tr><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

I wake up almost every morning with questions tons of them so many I cannot write them all down.
I have my families spiritual and physical health on my mind day and night and I do not have a local face to face support system to brainstorm with. My husband who is 85 does not buy into a lot of what I've come to believe however he does read blogs and articles I print out for him and he does try to accommodate me with a number of things RO water, cold showers, lighting etc. I have not found a good functional integrative forward thinking practitioner to work with who can take our medicare + insurance.

**Two things today.

1)** **If,** (as in a 1st class condition) **we never get well in the environment we get sick in,** **does that always mean moving south?
We live in Central Virginia at 38° N / 78° W.**

We both show English as the highest percent in our _23 and Me_ ancestry report.

Husband maternal Haplogroup is **T2b** and he grew up in a rural town in the _Shenandoah Valley of_ Virginia '38-'79, then in Houston to '79 to 1998 and '**98 to present 2023 in Central Virginia.**
My Maternal Haplogroup **H1a3** and I grew up inside the loop of Houston, Texas from '52 to 1998, then'98-present 2023 in Central Virginia.
Since living here in central Virginia in the same home 25 years we both became mildly hypothyroid and I developed cancer in 2009 and my husband developed Afib in 2013. One of the prior owners, a husband & wife, of our current property female had Alzheimer's and husband had cancer . I know there are many, many factors but my husband wants to cut our living expenses and I want a healthier environment so we have been looking to move slightly west and slightly south to less densely populated towns.
The Towers and Antenna's _**62** towers and **428** antennas within a 3.0 mile radius_ concern me and many of them are in clear site of our home.
Our home has been evaluated by an EMF expert and EMF is high. The worse being from dirty electricity measured high both inside and around our property.

I believe it is true that messing with the eyes is one fast way to mess with one's health. In my husbands case he had **cataract surgery** of his right eye done on 12/3/2013. (He had use of **steroid antibiotic eye drops** in relation to that surgery.). Afib began 7 days after that a 2nd surgery was done and the left eye was operated on on 01 14 2014, and we were both stressed by the loss of our little dog on Jan 28, 2014 and we bought a used 2010 hybrid Ford Fusion on New Years Eve 2013.

I suspected causal factors with his Afib: **Surgical stress,** those **antibiotic eye drops -UV/blue light blocking lens filters** resulting retinol signaling malfunction of bodily circadian rhythms And I have been wondering, what are the long time effects of loss of Vitamin D, melanopsin, melatonin from the **100% of blue filtered in both of his cataract lens** ever since then. I have encouraged my husband to get as much sunlight on his skin as possible. Meds and procedures (3 ablations and 9 cardio versions) had not been the cure we had hoped for.
Challenged by our abilities and our means at 70 and 85 years of age, plus what we want and what we can afford to move. My husband has stated before that a move may kill him and I always tell him, that, that is not an option! But I contemplate all the research and logistical factors. We may need to build a home but new building materials come with other sets of problems. Plus I have trouble even walking and am somewhat handicap with fragile skin, weak muscles, two bad knees and one bad shoulder. I've never fully recovered from 2009 cancer treatments. Might be best to remediate the best we can where we are...I love our home and after 2 years of searching have not yet found anything I liked better. We made 3 offers on properties but I was just as happy when 2 of our offers were not accepted, happy to not have to move. Decisions have to be made in 1-2 days or the homes sell. I can barely get a viewing lined up before then. The last 3rd offer we made by the Grace of God our Realtor got us an inspector to do a spot check for quick unofficial standard home inspection and crawl space had extremly fungus (mold). We were happy to retract our bid the evening of the same day we made it. I hate to be pressured plus hate mold and hate crawl space foundations----what were we thinking? The home was beautiful, not too old or too new, had a good price, great yard and a great community and location. Painful and Thankful at the same time!
**2)** **EMF Wise** I want to remediate our home NOW but hard to invest in that if we are trying to move. I want either / or a **full house filter ($2,600.)** or **Kill Switch ($800. w/two** [**Stetzer**](https://www.stetzerelectric.com/) **or** [**GreenWave**](https://greenwavefilters.com/) **filters** -one for each of our 2 panels- about $30. dollars a piece).
Do any of your know about [**The STETZERiZER® Home Kit**](https://www.stetzerelectric.com/product/stetzerizer-home-kit/) **? It is also $800.00.

EMF around us ... might also be related to the earth's grid where we are and I have no idea how to check that.

And I have wanted a** [**_Magnetico Sleep Pad_**](https://magneticosleep.com/) _for a long time_...but not sure if our under ground electric sources, inside wiring, or if being on a 'concrete' slab that does not get a ground if _**Magnetico Sleep Pad**_ will help or hurt us. First of all we can not afford it but if I can finance it I still need to choose between the 3 strengths, hire some body builders to install it and figure out if I need a King size or a Queen size. Can't decide to get King size or buy a whole new Queen size bed since we may move and room dimensions for a King Size bed is 13 X 13 minimum. Older homes within our budget do not always come with that much space. The weight of the Magnetico the cost of moving it to a new home (if we ever find one) and the cost of new pillows and linens would be a lot for us....however all could work out financially if we get what we hope for when we sell our home. We have a buyer for our home but what we will want for it depends on what we can find to buy.
It might be healthier if we were but we do not want the chance to be homeless ... by selling our home first.
With God all things are possible, if it is His will he will provide the help we need.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-17T05:43:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-coming-up-daily-upon-waking-today-was-environment-emf-filters.28089/post-325168>

>
> I wake up almost every morning with questions tons of them so many I cannot write them all down.
> I have my families spiritual and physical health on my mind day and night and I do not have a local face to face support system to brainstorm with. My husband who is 85 does not buy into a lot of what I've come to believe however he does read blogs and articles I print out for him and he does try to accommodate me with a number of things RO water, cold showers, lighting etc. I have not found a good functional integrative forward thinking practitioner to work with who can take our medicare + insurance.
>
> **Two things today.
>
> 1)** **If,** (as in a 1st class condition) **we never get well in the environment we get sick in,** **does that always mean moving south?
> We live in Central Virginia at 38° N / 78° W.**
>
> We both show English as the highest percent in our _23 and Me_ ancestry report.
>
> Husband maternal Haplogroup is **T2b** and he grew up in a rural town in the _Shenandoah Valley of_ Virginia '38-'79, then in Houston to '79 to 1998 and '**98 to present 2023 in Central Virginia.**
> My Maternal Haplogroup **H1a3** and I grew up inside the loop of Houston, Texas from '52 to 1998, then'98-present 2023 in Central Virginia.
> Since living here in central Virginia in the same home 25 years we both became mildly hypothyroid and I developed cancer in 2009 and my husband developed Afib in 2013. One of the prior owners, a husband & wife, of our current property female had Alzheimer's and husband had cancer . I know there are many, many factors but my husband wants to cut our living expenses and I want a healthier environment so we have been looking to move slightly west and slightly south to less densely populated towns.
> The Towers and Antenna's _**62** towers and **428** antennas within a 3.0 mile radius_ concern me and many of them are in clear site of our home.
> Our home has been evaluated by an EMF expert and EMF is high. The worse being from dirty electricity measured high both inside and around our property.
>
> I believe it is true that messing with the eyes is one fast way to mess with one's health. In my husbands case he had **cataract surgery** of his right eye done on 12/3/2013. (He had use of **steroid antibiotic eye drops** in relation to that surgery.). Afib began 7 days after that a 2nd surgery was done and the left eye was operated on on 01 14 2014, and we were both stressed by the loss of our little dog on Jan 28, 2014 and we bought a used 2010 hybrid Ford Fusion on New Years Eve 2013.
>
> I suspected causal factors with his Afib: **Surgical stress,** those **antibiotic eye drops -UV/blue light blocking lens filters** resulting retinol signaling malfunction of bodily circadian rhythms And I have been wondering, what are the long time effects of loss of Vitamin D, melanopsin, melatonin from the **100% of blue filtered in both of his cataract lens** ever since then. I have encouraged my husband to get as much sunlight on his skin as possible. Meds and procedures (3 ablations and 9 cardio versions) had not been the cure we had hoped for.
> Challenged by our abilities and our means at 70 and 85 years of age, plus what we want and what we can afford to move. My husband has stated before that a move may kill him and I always tell him, that, that is not an option! But I contemplate all the research and logistical factors. We may need to build a home but new building materials come with other sets of problems. Plus I have trouble even walking and am somewhat handicap with fragile skin, weak muscles, two bad knees and one bad shoulder. I've never fully recovered from 2009 cancer treatments. Might be best to remediate the best we can where we are...I love our home and after 2 years of searching have not yet found anything I liked better. We made 3 offers on properties but I was just as happy when 2 of our offers were not accepted, happy to not have to move. Decisions have to be made in 1-2 days or the homes sell. I can barely get a viewing lined up before then. The last 3rd offer we made by the Grace of God our Realtor got us an inspector to do a spot check for quick unofficial standard home inspection and crawl space had extremly fungus (mold). We were happy to retract our bid the evening of the same day we made it. I hate to be pressured plus hate mold and hate crawl space foundations----what were we thinking? The home was beautiful, not too old or too new, had a good price, great yard and a great community and location. Painful and Thankful at the same time!
> **2)** **EMF Wise** I want to remediate our home NOW but hard to invest in that if we are trying to move. I want either / or a **full house filter ($2,600.)** or **Kill Switch ($800. w/two** [**Stetzer**](https://www.stetzerelectric.com/) **or** [**GreenWave**](https://greenwavefilters.com/) **filters** -one for each of our 2 panels- about $30. dollars a piece).
> Do any of your know about [**The STETZERiZER® Home Kit**](https://www.stetzerelectric.com/product/stetzerizer-home-kit/) **? It is also $800.00.
>
> EMF around us ... might also be related to the earth's grid where we are and I have no idea how to check that.
>
> And I have wanted a** [**_Magnetico Sleep Pad_**](https://magneticosleep.com/) _for a long time_...but not sure if our under ground electric sources, inside wiring, or if being on a 'concrete' slab that does not get a ground if _**Magnetico Sleep Pad**_ will help or hurt us. First of all we can not afford it but if I can finance it I still need to choose between the 3 strengths, hire some body builders to install it and figure out if I need a King size or a Queen size. Can't decide to get King size or buy a whole new Queen size bed since we may move and room dimensions for a King Size bed is 13 X 13 minimum. Older homes within our budget do not always come with that much space. The weight of the Magnetico the cost of moving it to a new home (if we ever find one) and the cost of new pillows and linens would be a lot for us....however all could work out financially if we get what we hope for when we sell our home. We have a buyer for our home but what we will want for it depends on what we can find to buy.
> It might be healthier if we were but we do not want the chance to be homeless ... by selling our home first.
> With God all things are possible, if it is His will he will provide the help we need.

> I have not found a good functional integrative forward thinking practitioner to work with who can take our medicare + insurance.

Consider finding GP General Practitioner who
takes your insurance
He should give you what you are asking for as far as tests, prescriptions

you figure out what you want
\========================================
Do not sell your home, minimally fix it.
Mold. Get a single craftsman and lots of Clorox.
Do not use big companies or specially any mold specialists.
To fight mold long term, dehumidifiers (more than just one), lots of air and sunlight.

Get very large party tent and porta potty.
Spend most time under the tent or just outside.

# Thread: Black light good or bad?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/black-light-good-or-bad.28090/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area

### Elizabeth Leonard — 2023-08-17T16:19:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/black-light-good-or-bad.28090/post-325186>

Hello, still new to all this, so maybe there is a basic knowledge that I don't have that would help answer the question.

I got a free black light from my sister last night, and aside from the fact that it is fun to use, I am wondering what the effects of it are... good? bad? (assuming the position that any light is sub par to real light from sun)

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-08-17T22:46:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/black-light-good-or-bad.28090/post-325240>

Any artificial light is sub par to sunlight, yes. Also, artificial UV is better than no UV.

But, if you are getting plenty of sun in the middle portion of the day, then a black light is not necessary.

### Elizabeth Leonard — 2023-08-18T15:44:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/black-light-good-or-bad.28090/post-325274>

Recently on the video Jack have shown and discussed light that he travels with.
He was presenting 30W version of that light.
When I found it (Amazon), I found that they make also 60W version.
That is what I got. (June 16, 2023)

### Dragonfly — 2023-12-13T17:40:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/black-light-good-or-bad.28090/post-329182>

Does anyone know what the caveats are? I recently got a fairly inexpensive 385–400 nm LED strip and it’s neat, but I do wonder whether it’s safe to use it indoors all day long and stare directly at the diodes at times.
Thank you!

### JanSz — 2023-12-13T18:08:16+0200

> [Dragonfly said:](/goto/post?id=329182)

> Thank you!

When initially Jack was talking about this light,
the major reason was that it contained 380 nm

### Dragonfly — 2023-12-13T19:40:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/black-light-good-or-bad.28090/post-329194>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=329183)
>
> When initially Jack was talking about this light,
> the major reason was that it contained 380 nm
>
>

I see, in terms of benefit or mitigation of harm? Conceivably, it could either be the case that artificial UV-A light can be quite dangerous since it’s not within the full spectrum or, as is probably the case with IR, it’s alright at low enough intensities since you’re made to be out in the sun all day and that’s some heavy radiation. That’s what I’m trying to assess.

### Dragonfly — 2023-12-24T12:13:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/black-light-good-or-bad.28090/post-329449>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=329183)
>
> When initially Jack was talking about this light,
> the major reason was that it contained 380 nm
>
>

Also, did he mention how it was positioned? I’m concerned about the direct vs. indirect eye exposure aspect—does it matter?

### Anthony Smith — 2023-12-24T18:23:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/black-light-good-or-bad.28090/post-329455>

The goal is to get as much direct sun as possible, no glass/window and no sun glasses. 42% of the sun is NIR and 5%+/- UV (depends on location and time of day etc) so IF using artificial light duplicate the sun, never use UV without NIR. (NIR by itself will not harm).

### calebthomas — 2023-12-24T20:58:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/black-light-good-or-bad.28090/post-329458>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=329455)
>
> The goal is to get as much direct sun as possible, no glass/window and no sun glasses. 42% of the sun is NIR and 5%+/- UV (depends on location and time of day etc) so IF using artificial light duplicate the sun, never use UV without NIR. (NIR by itself will not harm).

Im sure this is a silly question, i'm a new member, but if NIR makes up 42% and UV 5%, what makes up the rest?

### Anthony Smith — 2023-12-24T21:01:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/black-light-good-or-bad.28090/post-329459>

> [calebthomas said:](/goto/post?id=329458)
>
> Im sure this is a silly question, i'm a new member, but if NIR makes up 42% and UV 5%, what makes up the rest?

Visible light 400nm - 700nm

Blanked trezor is a nightmare. I would only go through Trezor themselves. On the bottom of the site they have a chatbot linked.

### [Trezor hardware wallet recovery seed issues](https://trezor.io/support/a/trezor-recovery-issues)

In cryptocurrencies, a recovery seed (sometimes referred to simply as "seed") is a list of words in a specific order which store all the information needed to recover a wallet.

 trezor.io

> Do you guys still by BTC or are you waiting for drop in 2024? Maybe I got this wrong? just want to make sure. Thx

I am 83.5 yo. Live NJ USA. Have my GBTC in Shwab on IRA account.
**I keep all of it as HODL.
With assumption that within 5 to 7 years it will go up.
I do not work the last 26 years and like to keep 5 to 7 years' worth of my expenses in cash.**
The IRA accounts are arranged in such a way, that the money in it were not previously taxed.
After the IRA owner reaches 70.5 yo, he must transfer certain amount of its value out and pay taxes on that amount.
On the end of the Dec 31 account is evaluated for that amount.
That amount of money must be transferred by the end of the next year.
I do not want to wait until the end of the next year because BTC (or anything I ever owned) fluctuates widły.
So, usually in the last five trading days of December I will sell approximate amount and keep it as cash, double check it on January 2 to see if I have enough cash to make the required transfer.
/
Other that above, all else is HODL.
..........................
As of now each of my $$ that bought GBTC on 12/7/2020 is worth 1.662
had I got BTC it would be worth 2.397
Previously I was on SPY which now is worth 1.263 (I am surprised by that; it was supposed to be a whole wheelbarrow of money)

There is a person who bought his GBTC month after me (1/4/2021) he is still waiting for recovery. His one $ is worth today 0.993

### Gomes1983 — 2023-08-17T22:27:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tsh-low-with-high-cholesterol-should-be-a-worry.28092/post-325238>

Hey everyone...
I would like to have some feedback from some blood tests I made today. I start my Leptin Reset 2 weeks ago...
I will share the numbers that I'm worried about...

Total Cholesterol . 327mg/dl
Cholesterol not HDL 228 mmg/dL
Triglicerydes - 64mg/dL
HDL - 99 mm/dL
us CRP - 0,030 mg/dl
Vit D 47ug/L
TSH - 0,680mUI/L

I know that's a lot of things missing in this picture, but in your opinion what should be next steps? I'm not doing sports for now (I will wait until the end of my leptin reset) and I'm seeing all the sunrises and exposed to sun every morning and afternoon.

Thank you

# Thread: Laptop emf mitigation
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-emf-mitigation.28094/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Evee — 2023-08-19T03:56:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-emf-mitigation.28094/post-325303>

So I am heading to Mexico in the fall but I have to work while I’m there, so I’m buying a laptop and I just went to the Mac store and the electrical fields coming off of MacBooks are HORRIBLE (tested with a Trifield)

My thought is to buy a really long usb wired mouse and keyboard and sit like 5 ￼ feet back from it.

Also, I’m planning on working with the laptop unplugged as I’m assuming that the laptop being plugged in at the store is was part of why the electrical fields were so high, but does anyone have a MacBook and know HOW much unplugging it removes the EMF?

But aside from working on it unplugged and using USB keyboard and mouse so I can sit far back - Is there anything else that I am missing?

I normally work on an iMac w a grounded plug which tests pretty well as long my face isn’t right up in it

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-19T07:58:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-emf-mitigation.28094/post-325313>

I would guess a MacBook would be the same as an iPad. Picture says a thousand words. The 463mV was not from the iPad but another power cord close by. Unplugged, running on battery there is no 60Hz AC field so just don’t use it while it is charging. You still have RF/WiFi/Bluetooth to consider. Hard wire your internet connection, turn off WiFi and Bluetooth.

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### Evee — 2023-08-19T23:14:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-emf-mitigation.28094/post-325346>

Thank you!!!!! I got my hands on an old laptop today and watched the v/m go from 304 to zero on a Trifield! Crazy! I do turn off bluetooth and wifi and I'm going to use a wired usb mouse and keyboard because I noticed the laptop keyboard has too much magnetic field even when unplugged. Crazy and kinda scary what I didn't know just a month ago.

Thank you for your answer!

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-19T23:25:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-emf-mitigation.28094/post-325347>

> [Evee said:](/goto/post?id=325346)
>
> Thank you!!!!! I got my hands on an old laptop today and watched the v/m go from 304 to zero on a Trifield! Crazy! I do turn off bluetooth and wifi and I'm going to use a wired usb mouse and keyboard because I noticed the laptop keyboard has too much magnetic field even when unplugged. Crazy and kinda scary what I didn't know just a month ago.
>
> Thank you for your answer!

The TriField measures the ambient electric field not what is being induced thru your body which in what the body voltage meter is doing. You will notice you get a very different reading on the TriField when you hold it VS putting in down. 304V/m on a TriField is horrendous! That said zero is always a good reading, but I am alway suspicious when I see zero. Was the lap top plugged in/charging or running on battery?

### Evee — 2023-08-19T23:40:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-emf-mitigation.28094/post-325349>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=325347)
>
> The TriField measures the ambient electric field not what is being induced thru your body which in what the body voltage meter is doing. You will notice you get a very different reading on the TriField when you hold it VS putting in down. 304V/m on a TriField is horrendous! That said zero is always a good reading, but I am alway suspicious when I see zero. Was the lap top plugged in/charging or running on battery?

Thank you, good to know. 304 was plugged in. zero unplugged. Magnetic fields stayed around 3 on the keyboard even unplugged though, but dropped to ambient levels (.2) on a wired usb keyboard 2 feet away. So seems that even unplugged, better to wire in a keyboard and mouse and sit back from a laptop. (essentially use it for it's portability, but sit in relation to it like a desktop computer)

# Thread: My son's journey to health
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-sons-journey-to-health.28099/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### clunaluna — 2023-08-19T19:32:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-sons-journey-to-health.28099/post-325329>

Hello everyone,

I'm a new member and I'm here to help and support my son in his healing journey.

My son is 9 years old, suffered a metabolic stroke (hypoglycemia) at birth and went on to develop infantile spasms at 7 months and 14 months of age. He was successfully treated with ACTH shots both times. Along with these, came nocturnal episodes of partial seizures and nystagmus. The seizures stopped for a few years.

Right before the pandemic in 2019 he was diagnosed with cyclical neutropenia, and ever since then he's been on a cycle of illness. At first every month, and ever since he got Covid last year, every 2 weeks (and now he also gets grand mal seizures, which is new to him). We live in Queens, NY and after spending some time back home in the DR last year and noticing an improvement in his health, we have decided to move (hopefully before this fall starts).

I'm wondering if anyone here has had success treating either neutropenia or seizures. Feel free to point me to a blog post if so. I'm trying to read as much as possible.

Thank you in advance!

### Saichi — 2023-08-19T19:51:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-sons-journey-to-health.28099/post-325331>

Have you mitigated nnEMF in your home?

### clunaluna — 2023-08-19T20:00:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-sons-journey-to-health.28099/post-325333>

No, we're currently packing to move out to a new city within a few weeks. But I know it must be really bad (Queens, NY there must be thousands of antennas nearby)
I'm reading about how to mitigate nnEMF from my laptop etc once we're in our new space.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-19T20:52:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-sons-journey-to-health.28099/post-325340>

> [clunaluna said:](/goto/post?id=325333)
>
> No, we're currently packing to move out to a new city within a few weeks. But I know it must be really bad (Queens, NY there must be thousands of antennas nearby)
> I'm reading about how to mitigate nnEMF from my laptop etc once we're in our new space.

Before you move just turn off as many breakers as you can, especially for the bedrooms at night. Every little bit helps. Lots of nnEMF mitigation info here: [www.LiveEMFSafe.com](http://www.LiveEMFSafe.com)

### clunaluna — 2023-08-19T22:14:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-sons-journey-to-health.28099/post-325344>

Thank you so very much! Will do and look through the website as well.

### Sue-UK — 2023-08-20T11:13:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-sons-journey-to-health.28099/post-325353>

Apart from cleaning up the nnemf environment it might be worth checking out aluminum exposure. I have a book (Silica water the secret of healthy blue zone longevity in the aluminum age by Dennis D Crouse) and there’s a section on seizures, giving quite a few references to research carried out that shows seizures can be induced by aluminum, as well as several anecdotal reports from parents seeing reductions in seizures from using silica rich mineral water. If you think of what happens if you put aluminum foil in a microwave, it may be worth considering if aluminum is a factor in the improvements seen when you spent time in a lower nnemf environment and where exposure from things like air pollution were probably reduced too. Although it's in childhood vaccines as an adjuvant, there are also many other exposure routes, from meds, foods, cooking methods (cooking food in aluminum foil being one of the worst). Some exposure routes are worse than others, for example IIRC there’s no blood brain barrier at the olfactory bulb, and in any event nnEMF weakens the BBB.

Chris Exley is the UK scientist who has researched aluminum for years (I have his book, and read a lot of his research) but I’ve picked this report of a case study which notes him in the references, because the case involved seizures.
[https://www.researchgate.net/public...\_with\_silica-rich\_mineral\_water\_A\_case\_report](https://www.researchgate.net/publication/334676388_Childhood_neuro-motor_disorders_treated_with_silica-rich_mineral_water_A_case_report)

Although they did an audit of potential exposures, I would suspect this to be way underestimated, and doesn’t account for the child’s environment for exposure or in terms of nnEMF.

### clunaluna — 2023-08-20T20:55:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-sons-journey-to-health.28099/post-325359>

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=325353)
>
> Apart from cleaning up the nnemf environment it might be worth checking out aluminum exposure. I have a book (Silica water the secret of healthy blue zone longevity in the aluminum age by Dennis D Crouse) and there’s a section on seizures, giving quite a few references to research carried out that shows seizures can be induced by aluminum, as well as several anecdotal reports from parents seeing reductions in seizures from using silica rich mineral water. If you think of what happens if you put aluminum foil in a microwave, it may be worth considering if aluminum is a factor in the improvements seen when you spent time in a lower nnemf environment and where exposure from things like air pollution were probably reduced too. Although it's in childhood vaccines as an adjuvant, there are also many other exposure routes, from meds, foods, cooking methods (cooking food in aluminum foil being one of the worst). Some exposure routes are worse than others, for example IIRC there’s no blood brain barrier at the olfactory bulb, and in any event nnEMF weakens the BBB.
>
> Chris Exley is the UK scientist who has researched aluminum for years (I have his book, and read a lot of his research) but I’ve picked this report of a case study which notes him in the references, because the case involved seizures.
> [https://www.researchgate.net/public...\_with\_silica-rich\_mineral\_water\_A\_case\_report](https://www.researchgate.net/publication/334676388_Childhood_neuro-motor_disorders_treated_with_silica-rich_mineral_water_A_case_report)
>
> Although they did an audit of potential exposures, I would suspect this to be way underestimated, and doesn’t account for the child’s environment for exposure or in terms of nnEMF.

Thank you so much, I'm definitely looking into this study as well as the book you recommended. Last year I started clearing out cookware that was deemed toxic as well as the aluminum foil in cooking which I did use for a while. Also stopped using the microwave a few years ago. Thanks again for sharing, I definitely have tons to learn. Happy Sunday!

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-20T23:31:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-sons-journey-to-health.28099/post-325363>

> [clunaluna said:](/goto/post?id=325359)
>
> Thank you so much, I'm definitely looking into this study as well as the book you recommended. Last year I started clearing out cookware that was deemed toxic as well as the aluminum foil in cooking which I did use for a while. Also stopped using the microwave a few years ago. Thanks again for sharing, I definitely have tons to learn. Happy Sunday!

Many people do not realize microwave ovens, WiFi and Bluetooth ALL use the 2.4 gigahertz frequency. Only difference is the power level.

### JanSz — 2023-08-21T00:39:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-sons-journey-to-health.28099/post-325370>

### [Pediatric Feeding Disorders Program](https://www.kennedykrieger.org/patient-care/centers-and-programs/feeding-disorders-program)

The Pediatric Feeding Disorders Program treats the full spectrum of feeding disorders and works with children with behavior-related feeding issues. Services include an outpatient program, a day program and an inpatient program.

 www.kennedykrieger.org

# Thread: Need Advice - Underweight, Epi-Paleo/Carnivore
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-advice-underweight-epi-paleo-carnivore.28100/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jcoyote — 2023-08-19T19:55:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-advice-underweight-epi-paleo-carnivore.28100/post-325332>

Hi there,

I am struggling with eating.

I spoke a little about this in my journal but could use some serious support. I am very confused as to how to proceed.

I want to be strong like a Sherpa, be able to engage in physical activities like climbing, hiking, swimming and running. Feel powerful and dynamic. I am 107lb at 5’8” currently and feel constantly tired.

I focus my diet around:

Salmon
ground grass fed beef
Lamb roasts grass fed
Raw butter and sour cream
Pastured egg yolks

I eat within 30 minutes of rising 50g of protein at least and vary the above foods for my remainder of the day. In keeping with the leptin reset I have avoided any snacking. I shoot for three meals but sometimes I am not hungry from 8am when I finish breakfast until 2pm then I am sometimes ravenous other times not but am uncertain whether to eat a lot or a little as I should try to get another meal in.

To add to this I am uncertain whether to add intentional stressor such as resistance training to cut back on other forms of activity, or how to go about getting enough fuel in.

Whether I should eat until satiety, passed it, when I’m hungry or at set times.

I know I don’t feel great if I eat past 7pm. The earlier I finish my first meal to 6 the better I feel. But if I eat too early I am often unable to to sleep throughout the night.

Adding in seasonal carbs help with circadian yolking but they are electron poor. How should I navigate this? And what if any are to be included in what portions?

I am located in Vancouver, British Columbia Canada and planning currently to find a way south.

Any advice on how to gain weight, increase electrons in my body for cognitive functioning, improved decision making and improving body compsition/strength/functionality would be extremely helpful. Thank you.

### OptimalSkr — 2023-08-19T20:38:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-advice-underweight-epi-paleo-carnivore.28100/post-325337>

Hey there - I know for me cuting out the carbs caused serious weight loss. I also felt disgusting on carnivore. I went back to a balanced approach.

Are you eating plants and veggies? I really don't think these cause harm as some people say.

I think you should try adding sweet potatoes and even clean white rice. Even if they are "electron poor" you have to try what works for you. Hope this help

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-19T21:34:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-advice-underweight-epi-paleo-carnivore.28100/post-325341>

> [jcoyote said:](/goto/post?id=325332)
>
> Hi there,
>
> I am struggling with eating.
>
> I spoke a little about this in my journal but could use some serious support. I am very confused as to how to proceed.
>
> I want to be strong like a Sherpa, be able to engage in physical activities like climbing, hiking, swimming and running. Feel powerful and dynamic. I am 107lb at 5’8” currently and feel constantly tired.
>
> I focus my diet around:
>
> Salmon
> ground grass fed beef
> Lamb roasts grass fed
> Raw butter and sour cream
> Pastured egg yolks
>
> I eat within 30 minutes of rising 50g of protein at least and vary the above foods for my remainder of the day. In keeping with the leptin reset I have avoided any snacking. I shoot for three meals but sometimes I am not hungry from 8am when I finish breakfast until 2pm then I am sometimes ravenous other times not but am uncertain whether to eat a lot or a little as I should try to get another meal in.
>
> To add to this I am uncertain whether to add intentional stressor such as resistance training to cut back on other forms of activity, or how to go about getting enough fuel in.
>
> Whether I should eat until satiety, passed it, when I’m hungry or at set times.
>
> I know I don’t feel great if I eat past 7pm. The earlier I finish my first meal to 6 the better I feel. But if I eat too early I am often unable to to sleep throughout the night.
>
> Adding in seasonal carbs help with circadian yolking but they are electron poor. How should I navigate this? And what if any are to be included in what portions?
>
> I am located in Vancouver, British Columbia Canada and planning currently to find a way south.
>
> Any advice on how to gain weight, increase electrons in my body for cognitive functioning, improved decision making and improving body compsition/strength/functionality would be extremely helpful. Thank you.

Just listened to a podcast. This guy had been vegan for several years, had some health issues, started eating meat, then went Palio then went full Carnivore. Took a while for this transition but it worked for him. I know some very healthy Vegans and Palio’s and Carnivores, I also know people in each of these groups who are having health problems. “Eat for your body type” is often mentioned by people who make changes and find their nutritional groove.

Think of the thousands/millions of things your body does quite well every day, because you are not paying attention and getting in the way. It’s like it actually knows how to take care of itself. Try this - get quiet/meditate/pray and ask your body what it wants. Speak to your appetite and tell it to “desire” what your body needs. Try to fast for a few days, at least 3 days if you can, water only, kind of a reset to break habits, then see what comes up.

If you eat something and it doesn’t taste good or feel right don’t eat it. When I did this my tastes and eating habits dramatically changed.

Note: I did a 40 day fast (with some liquid food) but I know people who have done 10 days or even 3 days and got good results. I broke my fast at a wedding and had raw oysters, they were AMAZING - very NOT recommended. Breaking a long fasting, more than a week/10 days needs to be done carefully.

### coho3131 — 2023-08-19T23:59:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-advice-underweight-epi-paleo-carnivore.28100/post-325350>

> [jcoyote said:](/goto/post?id=325332)
>
> Hi there,
>
> I am struggling with eating.
>
> I spoke a little about this in my journal but could use some serious support. I am very confused as to how to proceed.
>
> I want to be strong like a Sherpa, be able to engage in physical activities like climbing, hiking, swimming and running. Feel powerful and dynamic. I am 107lb at 5’8” currently and feel constantly tired.
>

> Pastured egg yolks

I shoot for three meals but sometimes I am not hungry from 8am when I finish breakfast until 2pm then I am sometimes ravenous other times not but am uncertain whether to eat a lot or a little as I should try to get another meal in.
>
> To add to this I am uncertain whether to add intentional stressor such as resistance training to cut back on other forms of activity, or how to go about getting enough fuel in.
>

>
> Adding in seasonal carbs help with circadian yolking but they are electron poor. How should I navigate this? And what if any are to be included in what portions?
>
> I am located in Vancouver, British Columbia Canada and planning currently to find a way south.
>
> Any advice on how to gain weight, increase electrons in my body for cognitive functioning, improved decision making and improving body compsition/strength/functionality would be extremely helpful. Thank you.

If I were you, I would try local and seasonal sources of carbohydrates first, and eat them in the same meal with a good amount of fat.
These carbohydrates might also work well with 10-20 grams of protein right after physical activity.
Local and seasonal fruits, "vegetables with seeds", and root vegetables would be at the top of my list.
I would also consider small amounts of local honey, which would probably be considered seasonal while there are flowers growing.
White rice might be a third option, but I would avoid industrial-produced rice.
Since you are not overweight, quality pastured dairy from a local farm might also be a decent option if you get more desperate, but there are significant metabolic risks to consider, and many people will do much better without dairy. Butter or ghee might be the best starting point.

I would also consider eating your meals and snacks outside in the sunlight.

### jcoyote — 2023-08-20T23:51:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-advice-underweight-epi-paleo-carnivore.28100/post-325364>

> [OptimalSkr said:](/goto/post?id=325337)

> Hey there - I know for me cuting out the carbs caused serious weight loss. I also felt disgusting on carnivore. I went back to a balanced approach.
>
> Are you eating plants and veggies? I really don't think these cause harm as some people say.
>
> I think you should try adding sweet potatoes and even clean white rice. Even if they are "electron poor" you have to try what works for you. Hope this help

I haven't been eating any plants. From my understanding they contain toxins and the nutrients they contain can be obtained more easily from animals products. Perhaps I am incorrect on this. I dont wish to fall victim to dogma.

I am quite nervous to add carbohydrates back in. I dont want to do anything to make my condition worse or my redox.

I really do appreciate your advice though.

I read EMF 4 and it spoke about why athletes feel they need carbohydrates. I wondered if it is possible using carbs as a short term supply of energy to get me back to a stronger baseline is possible even if it is like taking out a loan as they are electron poor. Im not certain. Do you know much about the cytochrome 1 ans 2 pathways and how they interact with seasonality?
