Try to fast for a few days, at least 3 days if you can, water only, kind of a reset to break habits, then see what comes up.
>
> If you eat something and it doesn’t taste good or feel right don’t eat it. When I did this my tastes and eating habits dramatically changed.
>
> Note: I did a 40 day fast (with some liquid food) but I know people who have done 10 days or even 3 days and got good results. I broke my fast at a wedding and had raw oysters, they were AMAZING - very NOT recommended. Breaking a long fasting, more than a week/10 days needs to be done carefully.

Hi Anthony,

Thank you for your response.

Do you think there is danger in me not eating for several days if I am underweight already? What should I do on those days? very little activity?

What does your diet look like for you now?

### jcoyote — 2023-08-21T00:00:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-advice-underweight-epi-paleo-carnivore.28100/post-325366>

> [coho3131 said:](/goto/post?id=325350)
>
> If I were you, I would try local and seasonal sources of carbohydrates first, and eat them in the same meal with a good amount of fat.
> These carbohydrates might also work well with 10-20 grams of protein right after physical activity.
> Local and seasonal fruits, "vegetables with seeds", and root vegetables would be at the top of my list.
> I would also consider small amounts of local honey, which would probably be considered seasonal while there are flowers growing.
> White rice might be a third option, but I would avoid industrial-produced rice.
> Since you are not overweight, quality pastured dairy from a local farm might also be a decent option if you get more desperate, but there are significant metabolic risks to consider, and many people will do much better without dairy. Butter or ghee might be the best starting point.
>
> I would also consider eating your meals and snacks outside in the sunlight.

Hi Mike,

I appreciated your insight.

So intentionally adding in carbohydrates on top of thre meat and fat I am already eating would be your suggestion? An example of this would be to add carrots with butter to the salmon and raw sour cream I am already having in a meal? How much carbohydrates should I aim for?

What are your thoughts on oats, and potatoes?

Is/should "gaining weight" be a priority? I am underweight but is there ever benefit to putting weight on just to put weight on? I don't want to put weight on and compromise cellular health or trash my redox!! That is my fear!

How would you consider eating the honey and at what time of day? I don't crave sugar much at all so this could be a challenge.

I eat all of my meals outside/grounded!

I then let my body do what it was designed to do. I have never, nor will I ever focus on minutia or what is out of my control. I can control going out into natural sunlight. I can't control what my body does with it. Here is my version of an Epictetus quote, "The only things we can control are our character and choice. Why then do we spend the majority of our life worrying about all the things outside of our control."

What are some of your daily practices and non negotiables for living based on nature? How do you remain driven but in synch, open to the inevitablity of life while having goals and how did you discern those goals for yourself?

### JanSz — 2023-08-21T00:40:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-advice-underweight-epi-paleo-carnivore.28100/post-325371>

### [Pediatric Feeding Disorders Program](https://www.kennedykrieger.org/patient-care/centers-and-programs/feeding-disorders-program)

The Pediatric Feeding Disorders Program treats the full spectrum of feeding disorders and works with children with behavior-related feeding issues. Services include an outpatient program, a day program and an inpatient program.

 www.kennedykrieger.org

### coho3131 — 2023-08-21T01:25:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-advice-underweight-epi-paleo-carnivore.28100/post-325373>

> [jcoyote said:](/goto/post?id=325366)
>
> Hi Mike,
>
> I appreciated your insight.
>
> So intentionally adding in carbohydrates on top of thre meat and fat I am already eating would be your suggestion? An example of this would be to add carrots with butter to the salmon and raw sour cream I am already having in a meal? How much carbohydrates should I aim for?

Yes, adding foods with carbohydrates. Carrots have a good amount of sugar, so you can experiment with that, or you could experiment with something that has mostly starch. I would consider starting by slowly adding carbohydrate by maybe 5 grams every other day. There won't be a perfect way to do this, and each person would be different.

> [jcoyote said:](/goto/post?id=325366)
>
> What are your thoughts on oats, and potatoes?

You will have to experiment on yourself. Oats and potatoes in season would be fine choices for experimenting. Potatoes are nightshades, and increase inflammation, so you would want to consider that. Some people think that if your ancestors came from potato-eating regions, then you might tolerate them better.

> [jcoyote said:](/goto/post?id=325366)
>
> Is/should "gaining weight" be a priority? I am underweight but is there ever benefit to putting weight on just to put weight on? I don't want to put weight on and compromise cellular health or trash my redox!! That is my fear!

I think you will have to decide how much you want to weigh based on how you want to feel, look, and perform. There is probably a J-shaped curve when it comes to total body weight and general health. If it was me, I would be trying to add muscle slowly to gain weight. I would also want to have a healthy amount of subcutaneous adipose, but not extra abdominal or visceral adipose.

> [jcoyote said:](/goto/post?id=325366)
>
> How would you consider eating the honey and at what time of day?

Honey might be one of your last choices if the other whole foods are not getting you where you want to be. The timing of the carbohydrate-rich foods will depend upon your goals, and how you want to balance longevity versus current performance.

> [jcoyote said:](/goto/post?id=325366)
>
> I don't crave sugar much at all so this could be a challenge.
>
> I eat all of my meals outside/grounded!

Very nice!

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-21T02:04:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-advice-underweight-epi-paleo-carnivore.28100/post-325374>

> [jcoyote said:](/goto/post?id=325365)
>
> Hi Anthony,
>
> Thank you for your response.
>
> Do you think there is danger in me not eating for several days if I am underweight already? What should I do on those days? very little activity?
>
> What does your diet look like for you now?

It is my understanding that eating regular meals every day is a very modern thing and that the body benefits from periodic random rest (fasting) periods. I can’t imagine 3 days will do any harm.

What should you do during that time -
whatever you feel like. Don’t be surprised if you feel tired, depending on what you have been eating/drinking recently the body often goes into detox during the first few days of a fast (which is why many people don’t like doing them). I do recommend some movement even if you are tired, this is good for blood and lymphatic flow and elimination. I would recommend drinking lots of lemon water to flush things out. This said, you may have an energy boost, some people do.

When I did my long fast I had been drinking coffee and did not have the best diet. The first few days were brutal but then I had the most amazing energy boost and mental clarity I have ever experienced. I only intended to fast for about 10 days but I felt so good I just kept going. Trust me I was way underweight by the time I finished and I lived a normal life and worked the whole time. The wedding I broke my fast at - I was the photographer.

Get out of your head and listen to your body, when it comes to what it needs it is much smarted than your mind.

### jcoyote — 2023-08-30T05:35:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-advice-underweight-epi-paleo-carnivore.28100/post-325738>

> [Dr. Marcus Ettinger said:](/goto/post?id=325372)
>
> Response to both questions - I have tried every type of diet other than veganism. I have fasted and Intermittent Fasted. I just found what works for me - nutritional ketosis. I do eat a touch of carbs, mainly to support my microbiome, not because I need them.
>
> I surf, weight train, cross-country run, IR sauna, body surf, go to the beach, desert, and mountains as much as possible, I study daily, I write, I teach, I socialize, I do not worry or focus on what I can't control, I don't watch the news or take to heart what others say. I listen to them and research for myself. Even Jack. I cross-check a lot of the intricate details of what he says and I can't find a shred of backing evidence in any piece of literature. A recent example: "DNA only codes for semiconductive proteins." DNA codes for all proteins, semiconductive (photosynthesis and in the cell membrane) and non-semiconductive (3D structure and sequence of amino acids). So, DNA codes for all proteins.
>
> Don't make things complicated. Eat, and live your life as best you can, based on evolutionary biological principles, and nature will do the rest. Your mind (intellect) is the issue, not your body - unless you have a disease or illness I don't know about.

Hi Dr. Ettinger,

Thank you for your response.

I have read some of your posts on other peoples questions. Specifically the one about medical school advice. I was wondering where you would guide someone who is uncertain what the want in life and how to get it. I often feel I need a quantum mentor or someone to bounce things off of to make the steps and gains I need to make. I guess realistically I want my ear to stop fluttering, I want to be able to move and feel strong again to hike, swim, climb, and play music. I want to be over my fear of sound and have a greater purpose in life. I am uncertain how to achieve these things. And how much diet is really impacting them at all. Or how much my thoughts can be changed. Whether through prayer or meditation, through stressors like cold or resistance training (both things I am struggling to have the capacity for at the moment).

Jack spoke about the light story and how our thoughts are really all tied up in our light choices. I have been the best I have been in five years being out in the sun all day for the summer however in Canada rain is coming and the cold, and the sun is slowly decreasing its stay. I wonder abouthe power of the mind in my location and with my specific situation. I read about stoics, spiritual teachers, philosophers and those who have insane power of will like Navy seals who overcome extroardinary percieved limitations. I wonder if that s possible in my case in my environement or like Jack says I really do need to move and slowly over time imporving my redox will allow for the resources to reinvent myself. I do have authentic determination and a desperate hunger for truth. I want to try my very best I just need to know what to aim for.

My health journey seems to slowly be confusing itself with a spiritual one or existential one. I am maybe lost in the weeds, I am not sure. There is so much information, its hard to make a plan.

I emjoy your responses and wisdom. I feel the things you say resonate with me.

An affirmation to be written by your hand and read to yourself 100x/day - I'm okay even if there are loud noises and I can have the purpose I choose for life, and I can have all the ups and downs that may happen along the way. Read Bruce Lipton's, "Biology of Belief". Live in the Sun while being tortured. Does the sun dictate your biology or does the torture?

Jack spoke about the light story and how our thoughts are really all tied up in our light choices. Thoughts are not tied to light unless you believe they are. Do you believe they are? Example: Everyone in Vancouver BC is fucked-up because of low-light compared to below 30 degrees latitude? Not by a long shot. You choose to be a victim based on your story or you choose to conquer because of your story. Light does not factor into that. I like Jack because he pushes people's buttons. So do I. Light-Water-Magnetism are layers and there are more. Control what you can and accept what you can't. ### cL34rC0mm — 2023-09-02T02:42:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-advice-underweight-epi-paleo-carnivore.28100/post-325854>

As a long time carnivore I recommend increasing your fatty meat intake and also ADD some saturated fat (stearic fat) on top of that fatty meat intake. Make sure to see the sunrise each day, grounded barefoot on damp grass, soil or beach sand, NO sunglasses, NO sunscreen and few if any clothes. Make sure to get maximum AM sunlight exposure (sunrise>10am), even cloudy days allows IR-A+UV-A and 20-30% UV through to your skin. Always eat your big meal of fatty meat+additional stearic fat(saturated) within 60 minutes of rising in the morning. The nutrient content and fat will help you create more electron transfer and produce more cell water versus using carbs.

Since you are not in ideal latitude for optimal health, I would consider making some short trips (2-3wks) to either Mexico or El Salvador to increase your base redox until you have your plan solidified to move south.

Cheers!

RESEARCHERS ACHIEVE MIND CONTROL USING ONLY LIGHT

With [results published](http://dx.doi.org/10.1016/j.neuron.2021.11.003) in the scientific journal Neuron, the system consists of a single light source that emits a specific wavelength of blue light. This light allows the team to turn on or off the release of these neurotransmitters, resulting in the ability to manipulate emotion and behavior at a chemical level. And, the research team notes, depending on the particular area of the brain being targeted, very specific emotions and even behaviors can be induced or inhibited, depending on the desired result.

[https://thedebrief.org/researchers-achieve-mind-control-using-only-light/](https://thedebrief.org/researchers-achieve-mind-control-using-only-light/)

[https://interestingengineering.com/...ough-light-the-emerging-world-of-optogenetics](https://interestingengineering.com/health/mind-control-through-light-the-emerging-world-of-optogenetics)

Controlling brain circuits with light...
To pinpoint the precise neural circuits behind motivation, the scientists used a technique that enables them to control neural activity through light. This technique, known as optogenetics, utilizes a light-sensitive ion channel originally found in algae called channelrhodopsin.

[https://sitn.hms.harvard.edu/flash/... light,found in algae called channelrhodopsin](https://sitn.hms.harvard.edu/flash/2013/issue134a/#:~:text=Controlling%20brain%20circuits%20with%20light,found%20in%20algae%20called%20channelrhodopsin).

> That is not the point. Let me put it this way. Can you live in the sun and eat nothing but white rice for the rest of your life? No. You will die. If your arm was in a cast, pinned to your side, for a year and you were in the sun the whole time, after a year when the cast came off you may never regain its function again due to permanent atrophy of muscle and nerve. Can you mentally and physically do well, thrive, being beaten and humiliated by a spouse daily, while seeing sunrises, sunsets, and laying out daily with a vitamin D level of 100? Not a chance. If you drink a six-pack of Coke a day and eat Twinkies while laying out for hours a day, is that going to prevent high triglycerides, diabetes, and heart disease? No. The sun is great, but it's not going to protect you from Fentanyl, heroin, statins, Plavix, arsenic, lead, osteoporosis or sarcopenia if you don't move, etc...

### RitaC — 2023-08-23T02:44:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-dc.28104/post-325429>

Hello everyone,

I am truly thrilled to join this community. I have Dr. Kruse’s “Epi-Paleo Rx” book and have avidly listened to many of his recent and older podcasts. In fact, I’ve gone through the Rubin-Kruse-Huberman podcasts three times each! My learning journey has been transformative, and I’m eager to delve into the workshops and Q&A sessions.

While I plan to read the blogs, I must admit that reading on the screen strains my eyes. I’m on a mission to correct my myopia and, by following Uncle Jack’s advice, have already reduced it by 2 diopters. My objective is to continue my learning here, apply the recommendations personally, and share the insights with my clients.

Today, I came across a picture of the new clinic in El Salvador on Facebook. The sight of it made me instantly register as a member. I am not only keen on visiting but am also genuinely interested in contributing to the new project on medical decentralization. Being fluent in Spanish might come in handy!

Thank you for welcoming me into this community.

Rita

# Thread: Following the Sun
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/following-the-sun.28106/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### The.Naked.Doctor — 2023-08-23T06:24:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/following-the-sun.28106/post-325447>

Hello! Im Vanessa...

Im originally from the Bahamas ( pirate ancestors from England many generations ago - in case you're wondering why im white ;P ), and have been a sun worshipper my whole life (ha originally perhaps stemming from trauma as I was teased incessantly for being "albino" as the only white kid).

Luckily at age 14 when I had acne a very clever dermatologist recommended I get "just short of peeling" sunburn/red every weekend as a treatment. Ive been completely addicted to the sun since. I went to university in Florida and then to naturopathic med school in Toronto, then back to Nassau to practice.

I currently live in New Zealand in what is a pretty optimal environment - very rural, black sand volcanic beaches, waterfalls, and cold water in the bush in my backyard, however..... from about may-august the UV drops below 3/4, and im struggling!

My parents live in Southern Chile/Patagonia (after moving from the Bahamas in 2014 due to fears of being too close to the US and wanting to have a self-sustained lifestyle - grow all own food, have access to own water etc). My father's (retired MD) health, has been terrible since moving to Chile and he desperately misses surfing and the ocean, so they are looking to relocate to costa rica (we had a condo there when we were in the Bahamas so they are very familiar/comfortable with CR).

We are thinking to spend oct-may in NZ and then the rest of the year in CR/Central America (yes eye on El Salvador!!!).. but im wondering about the effect of spending half the year in the southern hemisphere and half in the north and the impact/stress on my biology,...

What are your thoughts on this? I love the idea of constantly being in UV above 8/9 and having endless summer!
Also love to connect with Central Americans or other Kiwis's!

Thanks!

### Jack Kruse — 2023-08-23T15:06:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/following-the-sun.28106/post-325464>

Time to move to El Salvador if you really want this lifestyle and health.

# Thread: Clarification needed on morning light
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clarification-needed-on-morning-light.28107/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TMellody — 2023-08-23T16:50:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clarification-needed-on-morning-light.28107/post-325468>

Hi there, I am hoping a veteran mitochondriac can help clarify something for me.

I live in Florida 500 feet from a river. The bugs are merciless. So I can’t stay out at sunrise for too long or I will get eaten alive. So what I’m currently doing is watching sunrise totally clothed while grounded for about 15 minutes until I can’t take it anymore. Then I get up and go for a 5 mile walk (bugs are better when I walk) from about 715-9am with a sports bra/short shorts in full Sun (have an area around a lake with no trees). I am in the Sun when UVa shows up around 815. Then I generally go into work around 10am for a few hours. I’m back at home around 2-3pm and lay out nude for about an hour. Often more. I try to get a minimum of 12,000 IUs of D a day. Just depends on the day but always at least an hour unless it’s storming.

My question: is the afternoon light good enough for my naked body or am I missing out by not being naked at dawn with balanced IRa/UVa light? And if so, does anyone have a solution for bugs for someone who lives in FL near a river? Or a better way to do my morning routine?

Thanks in advance, yall. ☺️ Cheers!!!!

### JanSz — 2023-08-23T17:05:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clarification-needed-on-morning-light.28107/post-325470>

What counts more are results.
Use sunlight to achieve these two (testable) results.
If you have to, fight bugs with fragrance oils. Put them on you clothing not on your skin.

Cortisol chart
Vitamin D levels.

### [DUTCH Plus® - DUTCH Test](https://dutchtest.com/product/dutch-plus/)

The DUTCH Plus® takes hormone testing to a new level. In addition to sex hormones and their metabolites, the DUTCH Complete™ looks at the overall diurnal pattern of free cortisol, and the total and distribution of cortisol metabolites. The DUTCH Plus® adds the Cortisol Awakening Response (CAR)...

 dutchtest.com

### [DUTCH Plus® (Female) - DUTCH Test](https://dutchtest.com/resource/dutch-plus-female-sample-report/)

 dutchtest.com

You want your cortisol to be somewhere between max and min.
Note that this test is different (more inquisitive) than say ZRT tests (that are also good at least for a first approximation).

>

What I have bought in advance of going to El Salvador are these battery operated UV kill lamps attuned to bugs favourite band of UV light I am planning to use these to see if it does something. and that it redirects the insects away from my internal uv light to the kill lamp.

The other way is to buy yourself a mosquito net tent and zip yourself in They retail on Amazon They compress down into something that fits into a suitcase. I was almost inclined to dump mine but I just have to angle it correctly in the suitcase. I also need to open it and fix the rip on it with some duct tape. but by and large I felt very safe in it from insects because I left the windows open in the first place I used it and the mesh on the windows was not perfect. Even I would get attacked by insects whilst watching the sunrise. I didnt think about moving it though.

The blurb says this. I actually had mine with me in Panama and in El Salvador but it was a bit of a tricky customer to repack and I decided to get bitten rather than utilise it at every move How ever If youre in one location you can just hang it up after use and then not have to move it . I saw another one when I was in El Salvador in Atami in a house used by our tribe.

"this pop-up mosquito net is not only suitable for use in bed, but also can be used as a tent in the yard in which you can enjoy the sun during the day, and enjoy the stars and moon at night. Or use it as an outdoor tent when camping in spring and autumn." The mesh is all over it.

Im not sure if this will help anyone but if you dont want a fan it'll do.

Do you know if your house/apt has wiring errors and net currents? If so you'll need a certified EMRS consultant, none located in Louisiana but a few in Texas and one in Georgia:

### [Electromagnetic Radiation Specialists™ - Building Biology Institute](https://buildingbiologyinstitute.org/find-an-expert/certified-consultants/electromagnetic-radiation-specialists/)

Electromagnetic Radiation Specialists complete a rigorous training regimen that includes hands-on training in advanced electromagnetic radiation and natural healthy building subjects but is not BBI-certified to address indoor air quality issues unless IAQ-certified by an authorized third party...

 buildingbiologyinstitute.org

Shielding for RF if you want to achieve 'no harm' levels is very time, labor, and money intensive something I have more experience doing than I ever wanted but if you live close to a macro cell tower moving would be the best solution as you'd be getting blasted even if you were to shield when you step outside your home. Same goes for high voltage power lines if close proximity for E and M fields.

I just realized the four-way and two-way lights switches I have for my foyer and dining lights are run with just two conductor wires no third traveler. I'm not going to have the walls ripped open to fix it I'm just keeping those breakers off and using sockets for lamps which are luckily on separate balanced circuits. Sad that most homes in the country have wiring errors and people are getting blasted hard completely clueless since their circuits still function.

Everyone of this forum should own the Safe and Sound Pro II at the very minimum. RF levels have been seriously cranked up in many places since the FEMA test this month.

I've owned Anthony's remote kill switch for years and consider it essential.

**My world is minimally invasive spine and musculoskeletal medicine.**
\---------------------------------------
One of the best of my experiences with doctors was working with DO.

A doctor of osteopathy (DO) is a fully trained and licensed physician.
They employ holistic practices for preventative health and healing known as **osteopathic medicine.**
DOs aim to improve people’s overall health and wellness by treating the whole person.
This includes osteopathic manipulative medicine,
which involves stretching, massaging, and moving the musculoskeletal system.
\==========
Dr, John Crysler (may he RIP) basically saved my life.
I was working also with another DO.
...............

lumbar disc disease.

### JanSz — 2023-10-27T16:54:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newly-graduated-interventional-spine-and-rehabilitation-physician-seeking-decentralized-training-and-network-opportunities.28109/post-327584>

Our bodies are made of bones and joints.
They are expecting certain loads.
When we are at rest, we have certain (stationary) weight.
When we move the load on bones and joints is dynamic. **impact force**
Just walking exerts dynamic load on our joints.
One hundred pounds of stationary weight becomes 600 pounds of dynamic.
Just when walking, more when running.
So, if person whose bodies expects to carry 150# actually has 200 or 300 pounds
multiply that six time (make sure that you do not lose your hairs seeing result).

Actually, there is a better way to visualize this situation.
Run into rigid wall and hit it with your body. (Do that only in imaginary way, not real).
But our joints see all that in their daily life all the time when we are moving.
..
**What is good weight and what is not.**

### Anthony Smith — 2023-10-28T05:18:16+0300

The Building Biologists I have asked have not noticed an increase in RF levels. I do not know of anything in Atlanta. I get calls from time to time from people who believe they are being “targeted” because they are having symptoms all of a sudden. It is my belief that there is a threshold for EMF in the same way as for many other things and when you cross the line everything changes. Heat is an example, people can be outside and enjoy the heat for years but if they ever get heat stroke they have to be very careful thereafter. It could be a new tower close by OR is it possible your friend reached their limit? Have them turn off as much EMF in their home especially at night - WiFi, bedroom circuits etc for a week and see what happens.

### dr.g — 2023-11-09T18:50:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newly-graduated-interventional-spine-and-rehabilitation-physician-seeking-decentralized-training-and-network-opportunities.28109/post-328083>

> [Dr Rohen Kapur said:](/goto/post?id=327575)
>
> Retired ex psychiatrist here with lumbar disc disease. Got ideas.
>
> Get in touch ( [[email redacted]](mailto:[email redacted]))
>
> Full title Dr Rohen Kapur graduated 1994 from Dundee University

The pathophysiology of Lumbar DDD is still debated and its exact cause is not known. The disc has no blood supply and it traditionally has been taught that it receives its nutrients via passive diffusion from the endplates of the vertebral bodies. There are mitochondria within the disc. Overall, since the loss of water within the proteoglycan network is what leads to disc dehydration, desiccation and ultimately height loss and structural loss of integrity, maintaining hydration within the disc seems a reasonable place to start. Since the mitochondria make water, I have a feeling that loss of functional mitochondria leads to loss of hydration within the disc. As far as treatments are concerned, IR light can penetrate to the disc and there are some places that can offer light treatments for the spine. Unfortunately where I practice, this is mainly done by chiropractors, and I not sure of the efficacy or the dose. Studies are limited. Don't think it would hurt however.

From an interventional perspective, there are disc treatments ranging from platelet rich plasma injections, bone or fat derived stem cell concentrate injections (BMAC), and allograft nucleus pulpous injections (company Via disc) that can be done. The evidence is mixed. However, there is recent data showing the mesenchymal stem cells (MSCs) can release exosomes, and also mitochondria where they are placed, which mechanistically would lead me to believe that bone marrow derived stem cell injections into the disc would help increase energy to the disc at least temporarily. It is unknown how long the stem cells stay within the disc and how long they are active for.

There are also treatments of methylene blue into the disc which have shown good results in asian studies, but this is not mainstream or conventional treatment. Would work to increase metabolism at the mitochondrial level.

Viadisc is a new product in phase 3 trials which have good results and rehydrate the discs.

There are also non regenerative treatments for disc/endplate disease call intercept which a basivertebral nerve ablation, which effectively eliminates the pain signal from the nerve that innervates the vertebral endplates, but functionally will cause osteoporosis at that level given enough time. Treatment is palliative in nature and done for intractable pain.

Happy to answer any other questions. Lumbar DDD is a massive problem for humans and the the research on how it actually functions is just getting started. A decentralized view is required. There is also something going on in the endplates either from energy failure at the bone and or from lack of proper blood supply. Could be light and or inflammation related. There are weird studies showing certain antibiotics also can delay or reverse DDD in some cases.

Still looking for a unified theory of disc disease from a decentralized perspective if anyone else wants to chime in.

### dr.g — 2023-11-09T19:12:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newly-graduated-interventional-spine-and-rehabilitation-physician-seeking-decentralized-training-and-network-opportunities.28109/post-328089>

> [NikkiYork said:](/goto/post?id=326734)
>
> What’s your “specialty” or field of interest, or alternative approach, however you want to put it?

Primary Specialty is Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation. Fellowship training is in Interventional Spine.

My personal field of interest is "regenerative medicine" or orthobiologics as it pertains to the spine mainly, but also to the peripheral joints, shoulder, knees, tendons, bones etc. Concept of taking blood or bone marrow and concentrating the cells to reinject into places that are low energy diseased tissue to help regeneration. Becker's work on regeneration is some of the bedrock for these treatments. However I have not been able to find much data on treating bone marrow derived mesenchymal stem cells with weak electric currents pre injection to help their regenerative potential which I think needs to be explored. Working to build a regenerative practice is my long term goal. Of course these treatment's are not covered by insurance and most people cannot afford cash so it's a business model that is tenuous to build in a centralized framework.

I also have an interest in neuromodulation, peripherally and centrally. Providing electrical energy to the cells to help with neuropathic conditions has a lot of potential and the field is exploding.

My approach now is to learn how to view the diseases I have been trained to treat from a decentralized view so that I can offer full patient centered approaches that are whole health. I want to get a light bed that is programmable so that my patients also have access to alternative therapies. In Seattle there is very low light in the winter and TONS of mood and metabolic disruption as a result. As a specialist, I generally focus on the issue at hand (mainly back pain) but the actual person's overall health can get lost in the weeds. Primary Care does a horrible job actually treating underlying chronic co-morbidities.

If you are anyone you know has a decentralized hypothesis for chronic musculoskeletal conditions and degenerative spine conditions, I'd love to connect.

>
> When it would be a good time to visit him in the hospital?
> ///////////

**Do you always need a shunt after aneurysm surgery?**
No, a shunt is not always necessary after successful aneurysm surgery.
[The need for a shunt is typically determined by the presence and severity of hydrocephalus, a condition where there is an accumulation of cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) in the brai](https://www.hydroassoc.org/predicting-the-need-for-a-shunt-after-a-brain-bleed/)n.

[After an aneurysm surgery, patients often develop hydrocephalus and require placement of an external ventricular drain (EVD) to temporarily drain CSF and decrease intracranial pressure](https://www.hydroassoc.org/predicting-the-need-for-a-shunt-after-a-brain-bleed/)[1](https://www.hydroassoc.org/predicting-the-need-for-a-shunt-after-a-brain-bleed/). [Once the period of risk for vasospasm (narrowing of blood vessels leading to reduced blood flow to the brain) has ended, the EVD is clamped to stop external drainage of CSF](https://www.hydroassoc.org/predicting-the-need-for-a-shunt-after-a-brain-bleed/).

[Doctors then determine if the hydrocephalus has improved and if the patient can tolerate removal of the drain](https://www.hydroassoc.org/predicting-the-need-for-a-shunt-after-a-brain-bleed/)[1](https://www.hydroassoc.org/predicting-the-need-for-a-shunt-after-a-brain-bleed/). [If the hydrocephalus is not improved or gets worse after the EVD has been clamped, the patient will require placement of a permanent ventriculoperitoneal (VP) shunt](https://www.hydroassoc.org/predicting-the-need-for-a-shunt-after-a-brain-bleed/).

[In some cases, a patient passes the clamp trial, the EVD is removed, and no VP shunt is placed, but later, the patient suffers from delayed cognitive and motor recovery](https://www.hydroassoc.org/predicting-the-need-for-a-shunt-after-a-brain-bleed/). [If the doctors believe that hydrocephalus is causing the delay in recovery, they may decide that a VP shunt is required](https://www.hydroassoc.org/predicting-the-need-for-a-shunt-after-a-brain-bleed/).

[However, it’s important to note that not all patients who undergo aneurysm surgery will develop hydrocephalus or require a shunt](https://www.hydroassoc.org/predicting-the-need-for-a-shunt-after-a-brain-bleed/).
[The decision to place a shunt is made on a case-by-case basis, taking into account the patient’s individual circumstances and the judgment of the medical team](https://www.hydroassoc.org/predicting-the-need-for-a-shunt-after-a-brain-bleed/).

\======================================

**Life with a cerebrospinal fluid (csf) shunt after aneurism surgery**
[After an aneurysm surgery, a cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) shunt may be placed to divert CSF from the brain, usually to the abdominal cavity](https://www.bmj.com/content/355/bmj.i5209).
[The shunt should place no or few restrictions on driving, travel, sexual intercourse, sports, and pregnancy once stable after the procedure](https://www.bmj.com/content/355/bmj.i5209). However, there are some concerns that patients may have about their shunt.
[For example, UK guidance states that patients must not drive for six months after an operation involving the ventricular end of the shunt](https://www.bmj.com/content/355/bmj.i5209).

\========================================

**The timeline for determining the need for a shunt** after aneurysm surgery can vary based on individual circumstances. However, here is a general timeline:

-   [**Immediately after surgery**: Patients often develop hydrocephalus and require placement of an external ventricular drain (EVD) to temporarily drain cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) and decrease intracranial pressure](https://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/brain-aneurysm/diagnosis-treatment/drc-20361595).
-   [**After the period of risk for vasospasm has ended**: The EVD is clamped to stop external drainage of CSF](https://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/brain-aneurysm/diagnosis-treatment/drc-20361595). [This is called a clamp trial](https://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/brain-aneurysm/diagnosis-treatment/drc-20361595).
-   [**During the clamp trial**: Doctors determine if the hydrocephalus has improved and if the patient can tolerate removal of the drain](https://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/brain-aneurysm/diagnosis-treatment/drc-20361595).
-   [**If hydrocephalus is not improved or gets worse after the EVD has been clamped**: The patient will require placement of a permanent ventriculoperitoneal (VP) shunt](https://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/brain-aneurysm/diagnosis-treatment/drc-20361595).
-   **If a patient passes the clamp trial**: The EVD is removed, and no VP shunt is placed. [However, if the patient suffers from delayed cognitive and motor recovery, doctors may decide that a VP shunt is required](https://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/brain-aneurysm/diagnosis-treatment/drc-20361595).

It’s important to note that these timelines can vary based on the individual patient’s condition and recovery.
[Regular follow-ups with the surgeon’s office are recommended, **typically within 2 weeks** of being discharged from the hospital](https://lisafoundation.org/what-to-expect/).
[If a CSF shunt was placed after aneurysm surgery, **regular follow-ups** are needed to ensure it functions well](https://lisafoundation.org/what-to-expect/).

////////////

# Thread: Acupuncture made my chronic fatigue 100X worse

**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions

### matthewkeegan2001 — 2023-08-23T22:43:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/acupuncture-made-my-chronic-fatigue-100x-worse.28111/post-325496>

I have and have had severe chronic fatigue for several years. When I was 16 and been ill for one year I got acupuncture. I went for up to 15 sessions despite it making me feel worse. It caused extreme tingling and pain all over my body, I could barely stand up or walk, it destroyed ever part of me. I went to a healing)reiki style acupuncturist shortly after and that immediately undid some of the damage by the previous acupuncture. It's several years on and I'm still severely ill due to many other things happening since then. But what did the acupuncture do to make me feel so much worse?

### jackmck — 2023-08-30T22:52:38+0300

I've searched on this topic a little. I'm sorry to say I couldn't find anything conclusive online indicating if one brand was better than the other.

In the end, I bought a new MacBook Air laptop, disabled Bluetooth and Wi-Fi, plugged in ethernet, installed Iris... and added an external keyboard and mouse so my body was as far away from the thing as possible. And I don't use it while it's charging.

>
> In the end, I bought a new MacBook Air laptop, disabled Bluetooth and Wi-Fi, plugged in ethernet, installed Iris... and added an external keyboard and mouse so my body was as far away from the thing as possible. And I don't use it while it's charging.

Using any laptop, tablet or phone unplugged (not charging) should mean NO 60Hz AC electric field, On battery you should only have a DC field which should not be of any concern even if you have the thing on your lap. Unless, the ethernet cord is picking up an electric field, which they can. Do you have an electric field meter, preferably a body voltage meter to test for this? If there is an electric field you can get an ethernet ground plug/adapter to fix this.

### jackmck — 2023-09-04T18:24:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-laptop.28116/post-325920>

> [Natalie A said:](/goto/post?id=325911)
>
> Hey there, What keyboard and mouse did you end up working with a MacBook? I have one as well. Thanks!

Hey Natalie, I decided on a corded mechanical keyboard that has no additional LEDs. I read that mechanical keyboards are low EMF - the connection is made only when a key is pressed. Luckily mechanical keyboards are quite popular with gamers. Mine is called a Ducky One. For my mouse, I was not able to be so choosy. I bought a Logitech corded mouse from a shop in Santa Fe, NM. It does the job well. From reading online, I get the feeling that most mouse options (that are corded) will have relatively low EMF. My guesswork so far (without finding any reviews that include EMF levels) has been - the fewer features the better.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-09-04T21:00:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-laptop.28116/post-325922>

Jackmck & Natalie A - with a laptop not plugged in/charging, what EMF are you getting from any non Bluetooth keyboard and/or mouse?

### jackmck — 2023-09-12T19:18:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-laptop.28116/post-326286>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=325922)
>
> Jackmck & Natalie A - with a laptop not plugged in/charging, what EMF are you getting from any non Bluetooth keyboard and/or mouse?

Hey Anthony, sorry for my late reply - my ethernet cable was giving off a large amount of EMF without me realising it. So, I ordered a grounding cable for it... and I'm back online! Wow, the difference with the grounding cable plugged in is great. The laptop was at somewhere around 250V/m and now it's down to 5V/m. Thanks so much for suggesting this. I was frying myself without knowing.

So, to your question, with the laptop not plugged in/charging, the ethernet cable and grounding cable attached, using the TriField EMF meter model TF2:

On the 2023 MacBook Air 13"keyboard surface I get: 0.7mG on Standard MAG (fluctuates between 0.4 to 2.1 with use), 1V/m on Standard ELEC (fluctuates between 0 to 1 with use), 3.5mG on Weighted MAG (fluctuates between 3.4 to 8.3 with use), 7V/m on Weighted ELEC (fluctuates between 6 to 8 with use) and the RF is mostly at 0.021mW/m2 (and fluctuates between 0.006 to 0.064 with use).

From the Ducky One Corded Mechanical Keyboard I get: 0.1mG on Standard MAG, 0V/m on Standard ELEC, 0.2mG on Weighted MAG, 1V/m on Weighted ELEC and the RF is at 0.000mW/m2. These numbers have slight fluctuations with use also.

From the Logitech Corded Mouse I get: 0.1mG on Standard MAG, 0V/m on Standard ELEC, 0.1mG on Weighted MAG, 0V/m on Weighted ELEC and the RF is at 0.000mW/m2. I didn't test the mouse under use. Little tricky to balance the TriField on top.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-09-12T20:32:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-laptop.28116/post-326287>

Are these readings with you holding/touching the meter or not? If not touching how close are you to the meter each time? With the electric field reading, if you are in an electric field you will get a higher reading holding the meter than when not. When not holding the meter as you get closer to it you will see the reading go up (more often than not). This is why I prefer the body voltage method because it tells you what your body is actually exposed to, touching not touching the computer, mouse etc. Put the TF2 meter next to the MacBook, stand as far away as possible but still see the reading, what e-field reading do you get with and without the ethernet plugged in (when not charging)? When not charging any mag reading is DC not AC so don’t even worry about it.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-01-15T02:37:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-laptop.28116/post-330242>

Defender Shield is a good product IF you are using a laptop on your lap. It will stop the RF going down into your lap (Very important if pregnant or wanting to have children any time soon). That said RF shields are mirrors and reflect RF back so there is a possibility as Mike says for it to actually increase the RF heading towards you. Solution - hard wire (ethernet) the computer and turn off WiFi and Bluetooth (RF) and do not use it while charging (electric fields).

I thought it was only for vaccinated people but as it turns out it can affect ANYONE who has been exposed to the covid-19 virus.

### JanSz — 2023-08-26T18:08:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cardiac-problems-after-covid-19-exposure.28117/post-325599>

The fasting insulin level you want to strive for is about 2 or 3 microU per mL (μU/mL). If it’s 5 or above, you clearly need to lower your insulin level to reduce your risk of high blood pressure and other cardiovascular health hazards. Keep in mind that the so-called "normal" fasting insulin level is anywhere from 5 to 25 μU/mL. Please do not make the mistake of thinking that this "normal" insulin range equates to optimal.

# Thread: Has anyone tested Ocushield to determine if their cell phone and laptop screen protectors really block out blue-light?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/has-anyone-tested-ocushield-to-determine-if-their-cell-phone-and-laptop-screen-protectors-really-block-out-blue-light.28118/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ana D — 2023-08-27T16:30:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/has-anyone-tested-ocushield-to-determine-if-their-cell-phone-and-laptop-screen-protectors-really-block-out-blue-light.28118/post-325614>

Hello I'm new and am wondering if someone has already done the homework to determine how to best reduce blue-light from cell phones and laptops. I already installed IrisTech on my personal home computer but am not permitted to install anything on my work laptop so am looking for an external liner/protector for the laptop. Thanks.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-27T19:49:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/has-anyone-tested-ocushield-to-determine-if-their-cell-phone-and-laptop-screen-protectors-really-block-out-blue-light.28118/post-325622>

I can’t speak to Ocushield but what I do is - having adjusted the color balance in setting to be as warm “not blue” as possible, also have as much natural light around me as I can (may not be an option for you at work) AND have a small (rechargeable) battery powered RED LED light next to the screen to add back in the red light. Not perfect but a step in the right direction when screen time is unavoidable.

# Thread: how about dehumidifier water as DDW

**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 6

### sunman — 2023-08-27T18:39:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-about-dehumidifier-water-as-ddw.28119/post-325621>

I've thought about using the water from my dehumidifier and running it through a RO system to make it drinkable; i suppose this may end up being lower deuterium content water as well, maybe; your thoughts are appreciated; thanks.

### Marko Pollo — 2023-08-27T20:20:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-about-dehumidifier-water-as-ddw.28119/post-325623>

Might want to test for metals from the evaporator: possible aluminum, lead etc.

### Neurotic Farmer — 2023-08-28T14:32:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-about-dehumidifier-water-as-ddw.28119/post-325634>

> [EDI said:](/goto/post?id=325621)
>
> I've thought about using the water from my dehumidifier and running it through a RO system to make it drinkable; i suppose this may end up being lower deuterium content water as well, maybe; your thoughts are appreciated; thanks.

This is an interesting idea!!

### JanSz — 2023-08-28T21:05:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-about-dehumidifier-water-as-ddw.28119/post-325653>

> [EDI said:](/goto/post?id=325621)
>
> I've thought about using the water from my dehumidifier and running it through a RO system to make it drinkable; i suppose this may end up being lower deuterium content water as well, maybe; your thoughts are appreciated; thanks.

Mechanical problems with that idea.
Water from dehumidifier is at atmospheric pressure.
You would need pump to get it to something like 50-80 psi.
Then you would lose about half of that water to get the RO water.

I have AC and dehumidifier in basement.
Same kind of water comes also from AC.

I just buy RO water at Costco.
I tested that RO water
and
water from my kitchen sink. See below the results.
Each line with my tests there cost me $250.

Actually, if you want just right deuterium level in your drinking water ...
I am 5'8" and 150#, drink daily 2.5 to 3 liters of water
4 or 5 half liter bottles of RO water from Costco
and one half liter of DDW-25
this if the least expensive that I know of:
If you live closer to them you can get 5 gallons container even cheaper.

### coho3131 — 2023-08-28T20:34:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heteroplasmy-alcohol.28122/post-325649>

### [Study of mitochondrial DNA deletion in alcoholics - PubMed](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/10803772/)

An acquired mutation of Mt-DNA, at least in the encoding ATPase region, may result from alcohol drinking and may be reversed by stopping drinking.

 pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

# Thread: Thyroid removed, how to work around it?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-removed-how-to-work-around-it.28124/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rod MidlifeMetabolicHealth — 2023-08-29T11:27:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-removed-how-to-work-around-it.28124/post-325686>

Hi all, I'm fairly new at quantum biology, but from my numerous leaps down the rabbit hole, I've come to understand how important the thyroid is in all this. However, what happens if your thyroid has been removed? Case: Man, aged 45, had thyroid cancer in his 20s resulting in his thyroid being removed and since then on thyroid replacement therapy.

How does missing the thyroid gland impact how all the quantum biology works? And, more importantly, how can he mitigate his lack of a thyroid?

### JanSz — 2023-08-29T14:47:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-removed-how-to-work-around-it.28124/post-325689>

Probably it would be better if Jack addressed the issue.
From what come to my mind:

thyroid replacement is better done with NTH, Natural Thyroid Hormone.
dose someplace between 3 and 5 grains
To figure out the dose:
check body temperature in the morning, when waken up, still in the bed
aim at 36.6C=97.88F
check pulse
FreeT3 in the mid laboratory range

Thyroid hormones when made in the body need iodine and selenium.
Those items are used in the body not only to make thyroid hormones but have other functions.
So even without thyroid gland it is good idea to watch them over.

Do not recall now, but it is somewhere in the posts, try to search for it.
Jack mentioned importance of
sonic hedgehog
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. The sonic hedgehog (Shh) pathway plays important roles in tumorigenesis, tumor growth, drug resistance, and metastasis. We and others have reported earlier that this pathway is highly activated in thyroid cancer. However, its role in thyroid cancer stem cell (CSC) self-renewal and tumor...

 www.mdpi.com

### JanSz — 2023-08-29T15:19:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-removed-how-to-work-around-it.28124/post-325691>

iodine and selenium
I supplement selenium.
I supplement iodine using Lugo's
Lugol's contain iodine and potassium iodide.
There are reasons why KI (potassium iodide) is need.
That is, supplementing with just iodine is insufficient.

To check for iodine status, it is best to do 24 hr urine test.
One should pee out as much iodine as he/she took.
If it is less meaning body is missing on iodine.

Selenium and other I check using
spectracell.com
see my latest known below. I am 83.25 yo

# Thread: Eggs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eggs.28125/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 31
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### healthy2015 — 2023-08-29T13:23:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eggs.28125/post-325687>

We have a friend who has noticed when they eat farm raised eggs it messes up their sleep and when they don’t eat eggs things return to normal again- any reason why this could be? Thank you

### Neurotic Farmer — 2023-08-29T13:34:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eggs.28125/post-325688>

Can you provide more context? When are they eating the eggs? Are they eating egg whites only, or the whole egg? Are the chickens subsisting on their natural diet, or are they supplemented feed? If supplemented, what does that feed consist of? Can you elaborate on "messes up their sleep"?

### healthy2015 — 2023-08-29T15:30:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eggs.28125/post-325692>

They buy supermarket eggs so not overly sure what the diet is but most likely grains. Regarding sleep nothing else has changed for them apart from eating 3 eggs a day which results in waking up 2/3/4 in the morning, they mitigate emf, they blue block and also see sunrise and sunset

Thanks

### coho3131 — 2023-08-29T16:42:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eggs.28125/post-325700>

Industrial Egg Layer Feed components include many by-products of various food industries like Wheat Middlings, Corn Distillers Dried Grains with Solubles, Soybean Meal, Processed Grain By-Products, Grain Products, Plant Protein Products, Fish Meal, Canola Meal, Dehydrated Alfalfa Meal, and others. There would probably also be various other components like sources of minerals, amino acids, and preservatives.

[@Marko Pollo](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/21456/) probably knows more details.

### coho3131 — 2023-08-29T16:49:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eggs.28125/post-325701>

Small family farms would be more likely to feed their egg laying hens things like locally grown oats, corn, and wheat, and table scraps, and the hens would also have their run of the property for the pasture, grasses, clover, insects, worms, and small animals.

### Neurotic Farmer — 2023-08-29T17:06:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eggs.28125/post-325703>

> [healthy2015 said:](/goto/post?id=325692)
>
> They buy supermarket eggs so not overly sure what the diet is but most likely grains. Regarding sleep nothing else has changed for them apart from eating 3 eggs a day which results in waking up 2/3/4 in the morning, they mitigate emf, they blue block and also see sunrise and sunset
>
> Thanks

You mention "they". So is this happening to more than one member of that household? Is it only when "they" eat 3 eggs? What about if they eat 1? Does it change? Where are "they" located? Is it natural for chickens in their area to be laying eggs right now? Who knows where the eggs are coming from when purchased from the supermarket. Perhaps there is a circadian mismatch that could be contributing to this early morning wakefulness.

### Marko Pollo — 2023-08-29T17:51:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eggs.28125/post-325705>

I've got no idea why they can't sleep. In my experience, even with free range birds on a small farm, a fair percentage of nutritional need comes from a feed bag. I do everything I can to feed all my animals from our local environment like scraps, bugs, grasses and fruit, but I still need to give them feed. I am planting more fruit trees for this. We are getting 50 more meat birds chicks tomorrow to harvest in late fall.

### Neurotic Farmer — 2023-08-29T17:59:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eggs.28125/post-325707>

> [Marko Pollo said:](/goto/post?id=325705)
>
> I've got no idea why they can't sleep. In my experience, even with free range birds on a small farm, a fair percentage of nutritional need comes from a feed bag. I do everything I can to feed all my animals from our local environment like scraps, bugs, grasses and fruit, but I still need to give them feed. I am planting more fruit trees for this. We are getting 50 more meat birds chicks tomorrow to harvest in late fall.

Yup, agree. We supplemented ours with no soy, no corn, non-GMO feed. Pricier, but worth it in our opinion.

### coho3131 — 2023-08-29T18:43:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eggs.28125/post-325711>

While of course local grain would be optimal, how much more money is it for no-soy feed that is certified organic versus non-GMO? (In my opinion, non-GMO is probably more important than less pesticide residue, but both would be nice.)

### coho3131 — 2023-08-29T18:46:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eggs.28125/post-325712>

> [Marko Pollo said:](/goto/post?id=325705)
>
> I've got no idea why they can't sleep. In my experience, even with free range birds on a small farm, a fair percentage of nutritional need comes from a feed bag. I do everything I can to feed all my animals from our local environment like scraps, bugs, grasses and fruit, but I still need to give them feed. I am planting more fruit trees for this. We are getting 50 more meat birds chicks tomorrow to harvest in late fall.

Could you plant a section of pasture with clover (and other legumes) to help with giving them less feed?

Some of our local family farms are thinking of raising worms for this also.

### Neurotic Farmer — 2023-08-29T19:36:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eggs.28125/post-325715>

### Neurotic Farmer — 2023-08-29T19:40:38+0300

> Could you plant a section of pasture with clover (and other legumes) to help with giving them less feed?
>
> Some of our local family farms are thinking of raising worms for this also.

Not sure where [@Marko Pollo](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/21456/) is, but when we had our chickens last year here in Texas, it was during a devastating drought (which is happening again right now). We couldn't grow anything if we wanted to where we were rotating them. In fact, we would have to move them twice as far some days just to avoid the gaping cracks in the ground. We fermented their feed to help stretch it further and give them more water.

### Marko Pollo — 2023-08-30T00:06:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eggs.28125/post-325731>

I have heard that you can't starve a profit out of a cow. Probably the same with chickens. I supplement with crumble, but back off if there are double yolkers. The other day I was going to weedwhack around the corral but for some reason just started pulling the weeds by hand. About an hour later I uncovered a nest with a hen sitting perfectly still on her eggs. Hopefully we get some chicks. Ducks are better foragers than chickens
and delicious also. GMO is not organic, so if it is labeled organic its non GMO. If you are going to get some chicks, you may want to get them now, so they'll be ready for winter and mature enough to start laying in the spring. I recommend getting extras as you will probably lose some and its nice to have an abundance of eggs.

Cows, goats, sheep's have double stomachs they are able to process that stuff.
And they do not have to be organic or grass fed to do it.
But I think (looking for proof) that eggs should also be acceptable (from pov of LA).
\---------------

In the last month I am preoccupied with Nitric Oxide (NO).
Two things that stuck in my mind.
Vegetables these days are **poor** in delivering N3O.
That goes **double for organic** vegetables.
Above was tested and verified.

Looks like humanity is screwing itself.
Too much LA
Not enough NO
Not even talking about sunlight.

It looks like all neolithic diseases can be derived from the two above.
Starting with high blood pressure ending with Alzheimer's and multitude of other in between.

Catholic nun from Africa
first announced that she cooperates hand in hand with Muslims
and is begging for food.

Large bottle of Coca Cola
and
loaf of Wonder bread

### [Egg-spectations: are Eggs Seasonal?](https://www.oldtime.farm/blogs/blog-1/are-eggs-seasonal)

Today, we expect eggs to be available on grocery store shelves year round and expect hens to be egg laying machines who produce almost an egg a day throughout the year. But ... Is that "normal" for hens? Is that what really happens on local farms? Are our expectations in line with "normal"...

 www.oldtime.farm

# Thread: The Power of Grounding and Expedited Healing
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-power-of-grounding-and-expedited-healing.28129/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### IanKites — 2023-08-30T06:50:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-power-of-grounding-and-expedited-healing.28129/post-325740>

Hello everyone, thanks to Dr JK, At 53 years of age I'm a massive advocate of grounding. January 1st 2022 I was flying through the air kitesurfing at approximately 30 ft up off the water and to this day, I'm still not sure what went wrong but it all came unstuck and i fell out of the air backwards hitting the water and breaking a rib. The following 4 days was spent in a zerogravity recliner attempting to breath and sleep. MY phone rang on the 5th day and a courier announced a package had arrived. It was a grounding kit from the USA I had actually forgotten that I had ordered. It plugged into the ground of the powerpoint and had 2 leads to clip onto the ECG style sticky patches they stick onto patients for checking heart rate.. I quickly read some of the book provided and stuck a patch onto the base of either foot just next to the left and right balls off my feet and connected the grounding wires from my wall power socket ground. My wife returned home 2 hours later and asked how I was and I explained there had been no change still bad pain when I moved, at which point I stood up to go to the restroom and as I held my breath waiting for the intensity of pain to hit, the pain came in but at least 50% less. THAT'S IN JUST 2 HOURS of GROUNDING! So I slept for the 1st time in almost a week since the accident that night for the whole night with the patches on either foot. 3 Weeks later the rib had healed! Everything I was reading was saying I will be out for a minimum of 6 weeks and some further time after that I can resume activity. I WAS BACK ON THE WATER KITESURFING IN 3 WEEKS.
2 days after getting back on the water my harness broke while I was in the air again at a similar height and fell into the water on my back and broke the rib above the 1st healed rib. So this time I stayed at the resort and had the owners of the resort hit a 5 ft piece of steel reinforcing into the ground by my room window as they only had a 2 pin wall socket (Vietnam) and used the same patches connected to the steel in the ground. The same amazing results with a massive reduction in pain.
Grounding is phenomenal. We sleep grounded now with a sheet on our bed underneath the main sheet. Our Sleep has been transformed especially after the circadian disruption of the Covid shots and we dream good dreams every night. Incredible.

### JanSz — 2023-08-30T16:13:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-power-of-grounding-and-expedited-healing.28129/post-325749>

Make sure that you have a plenty of Nitric Oxide.

Nitric Oxide (NO) is dissolved in our blood (if you have it).
When any part of our body is under stress, cardiovascular system there causes release of NO.
NO is a gas. When as gas it last there a small fraction of a second.
Walls of our arteries or veins feeling NO, expand, under heavy stress up to a 10% of volume.
That causes higher blood delivery to affected area.
\----
Lack or insufficient NO causes that cardiovascular system have a constant volume.
That is the reason for many diseases, but immediately for insufficient energy in places that needs that.
...

### healthy2015 — 2023-08-30T10:48:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cookware.28130/post-325743>

Is Stainless steel or Porcelain okay to use in the over?

### JanSz — 2023-08-30T15:59:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cookware.28130/post-325748>

> [healthy2015 said:](/goto/post?id=325743)
>
> Is Stainless steel or Porcelain okay to use in the over?

Watch out for any handles that may burn.

Porcelan
look up their bottoms
they often have it written down.

About stainless steel anything.
Watch out if you are using Chlorine Dioxide water.
It corrodes stainless steel.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### coho3131 — 2023-08-30T19:31:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cookware.28130/post-325761>

> [healthy2015 said:](/goto/post?id=325743)
>
> Is Stainless steel or Porcelain okay to use in the over?

Acidic substances will cause stainless steel to leach nickel and chromium.
Many porcelains, ceramics, and similar materials could contain lead, arsenic, or other heavy metals, and might be coated in glazes that might contain lead, cadmium, or other heavy metals.
We use enamel-coated cast iron pots and pans from France for cooking, which are not perfectly free of toxins, but might be the closest you can get. You could also use lead-free tempered glass baking dishes in the oven.
You can buy testing materials for lead and cadmium, but I do not know if they are accurate or reliable.

### coho3131 — 2023-08-30T19:32:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cookware.28130/post-325762>

### [Stainless Steel Leaches Nickel and Chromium into Foods During Cooking](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4284091/)

Toxicological studies show that oral doses of nickel and chromium can cause cutaneous adverse reactions such as dermatitis. Additional dietary sources, such as leaching from stainless steel cookware during food preparation, are not well characterized. ...

 www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

Jumping back here as I've had a thought. The perioral dermatitis started up around the time I started working full-time. In other words, when I started spending 8 hours a day looking at a blue light computer screen whilst indoors. Perhaps the isolated blue light is damaging the skin on the face?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/perioral-dermatitis.28136/post-329219>

Instead of paying for inferior, exogenous nitric oxide with your money, you can pay for superior, endogenous nitric oxide with your time in the sun this way.

Order one of Jacks new computers. Until it arrives can you work outside or by an open window? Adjust screen color, add a red light behind the screen to counter the blue.

# Thread: Which Musicians dont die earlier? Which music doesnt kill you?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-musicians-dont-die-earlier-which-music-doesnt-kill-you.28137/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Butters — 2023-09-02T13:32:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-musicians-dont-die-earlier-which-music-doesnt-kill-you.28137/post-325862>

So I found I want to make more music and get better at guitar. But when you research you quickly find
that most if not all popular musicians die earlier A LOT like 25 years earlier than normal population.

The question to make the right decision is: Is it the choice of the instrument (like electric guitar) and the EMF of the Instrument environment or is it
the choice of the light environment, where and when you play? Or is it both equally?

What if you were playing in a band, but you would do concerts only in daylight, practise in daylight hours etc. but you would play still electric guitar?
What if you were playing an acoustic instrument, but you would play in evening and nighttime hrs?

You have no real control because todays electric musicians all play in shitty light and at shitty times. But I found older Bluegrass guitarists seem to lived longer, like Doc Watson. You have Roger Waters and David Gilmore who get to 80 and same for the Stones.
I found this list and there are so few guitar players:

[https://www.ihopeidiebeforeigetold.com/oldest-living-rock-stars.php](https://www.ihopeidiebeforeigetold.com/oldest-living-rock-stars.php)

Both my wife (now 70+) and my son (36) are professional musicians. Wife Jazz, son Rock/Blues. I didn’t think it is the music that kills you or the EMF or even the time of the concert so much as what you consume in “the green room” before and at the party after . . .

Kris Kristofferson was a party hard musician and ran with the wild crowd. He was doing heroin and dating Jannis Joplin when she died. I believe that was part of his wake-up call and Jonny Cash helped him get clean. Kris is still alive 87, Jonny died at 71.

### Dennis Clark — 2023-09-07T21:54:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-musicians-dont-die-earlier-which-music-doesnt-kill-you.28137/post-326105>

What happened to Jimmy Buffet? The question occurred to me when I saw that he died of a 'rare form of skin cancer'. His website obit said it was Merkel Cell Skin Cancer (a carcinoma). It is, indeed, exceedingly rare ([https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Merkel-cell\_carcinoma](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Merkel-cell_carcinoma)). Although Jimmy's reputation of being a beach bum in Margaritaville was widespread, he was still a musician who must have played 'in shitty light and at shitty times' a lot. Maybe he didn't really get out in the sun enough AND he compounded it with artificial (high blue?) lighting and nnEMFs due to his profession. Just a thought.

# Thread: WiFi Router Guard Cover
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-router-guard-cover.28139/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### coho3131 — 2023-09-03T18:14:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-router-guard-cover.28139/post-325896>

We prefer wired connections to our computers, but we use our WiFi Router for defined periods for certain things.

Do the guard covers work to reduce our nnEMF exposure? If they are affective, do you have any product suggestions?

Thank you.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-09-03T21:43:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-router-guard-cover.28139/post-325900>

If you think of your WiFi router as a light, you just need something metallic between you and it, the back side can be open, think lamp shade. You want to be in the shadow. Your WiFi router is putting out 2.4 GHz (same as your microwave oven and all Bluetooth devices) a metallic screen that looks like the door on your microwave oven will help block most (not all) of the WiFi. A piece of metal mosquito screen, a metal mesh paper tray (the smaller the holes the better). Put a box over it lined in aluminum foil. You still want to be as far away from it as possible.

### coho3131 — 2023-09-04T01:19:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-router-guard-cover.28139/post-325904>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=325900)
>
> If you think of your WiFi router as a light, you just need something metallic between you and it, the back side can be open, think lamp shade. You want to be in the shadow. Your WiFi router is putting out 2.4 GHz (same as your microwave oven and all Bluetooth devices) a metallic screen that looks like the door on your microwave oven will help block most (not all) of the WiFi. A piece of metal mosquito screen, a metal mesh paper tray (the smaller the holes the better). Put a box over it lined in aluminum foil. You still want to be as far away from it as possible.

Thank you!
So metal mesh really works?
If I put a metal shield on one side, will it bounce off and create twice the waves directed in the other direction?

### Anthony Smith — 2023-09-04T02:47:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-router-guard-cover.28139/post-325905>

> [coho3131 said:](/goto/post?id=325904)
>
> Thank you!
> So metal mesh really works?
> If I put a metal shield on one side, will it bounce off and create twice the waves directed in the other direction?

It is my understanding that is so. It acts as a mirror. The screen on the front of a microwave oven is called a Tesla screen, it blocks the microwaves from coming out (most of them).

### coho3131 — 2023-09-04T03:23:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-router-guard-cover.28139/post-325906>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=325905)
>
> It is my understanding that is so. It acts as a mirror. The screen on the front of a microwave oven is called a Tesla screen, it blocks the microwaves from coming out (most of them).

Thank you!

# Thread: Mystery of the milksick
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mystery-of-the-milksick.28141/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JanSz — 2023-09-04T18:02:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mystery-of-the-milksick.28141/post-325918>

My girl, my Guardian Angel, since she arrived makes my life bearable after my Dear Wife died in 2017, six years ago.
She has insanely green thumb. She grows numerous plants and flowers on our small piece of properties.
Most of the stuff she is buying but some she digs out from places.
Now we are studying next of her digs.
This is going to be a Southern Catalpa.
The problem is that it currently grows near White snakeroot (Ageratina altissima).
White snakeroot is a hell of the story.

### [Ageratina altissima - Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ageratina_altissima)

 en.wikipedia.org

Cow, sheep eats it, person drinks milk or eat cheese and he she is done.

### [Milk sickness - Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Milk_sickness)

 en.wikipedia.org

White snakeroot contains the toxin tremetol; when the plants are consumed by cattle, the meat and milk become contaminated with the toxin. When milk or meat containing the toxin is consumed, the poison is passed on to humans.
If consumed in large enough quantities, it can cause tremetol poisoning in humans.

Milk sickness was suspected as a disease in the early 19th century as migrants moved into the Midwest; they first settled in areas bordering the [Ohio River](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ohio_River) and its tributaries, which were their main transportation routes. They often grazed their cattle in frontier areas where white snakeroot grows; it is a member of the daisy family.

Nancy Hanks Lincoln, who died in 1818, was the mother of Abraham Lincoln.

\=============================

### coho3131 — 2023-09-05T03:14:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mystery-of-the-milksick.28141/post-325954>

We have one of those in our garden also. It is said to be pretty invasive, but ours has been kept in check by just trimming. I keep it because it is one of the last wildflowers to bloom during the fall in our area, and it provides food to beneficial insects during that time of year.

### coho3131 — 2023-09-05T03:17:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mystery-of-the-milksick.28141/post-325955>

We don't have a cow, sheep, or goats to milk. I'm not even sure milk is that great for you in a nnEMF environment, unless you don't have access to other food for enough calories.
