# Thread: British Court Rules That Competent & Conscious Patient Can Be Denied Life-Sustaining Treatment Against Her Will
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/british-court-rules-that-competent-conscious-patient-can-be-denied-life-sustaining-treatment-against-her-will.28143/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Huck — 2023-09-05T02:36:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/british-court-rules-that-competent-conscious-patient-can-be-denied-life-sustaining-treatment-against-her-will.28143/post-325951>

I just wondered what everyone thought about this, both medically and ethically.

> **British doctors are seeking to take a 19-year-old critically ill female patient off the intensive care despite her objections and those of her parents.
>
> Unlike most such cases, the woman known only as “ST” is conscious and communicative.**
>
> Yet, the doctors argue that she is not being realistic about her chances of survival from a rare disorder.
>
> **Now a British court has agreed and ordered that she can be placed on end-of-life care against her will.**
>
> ST is suffering from a rare genetic mitochondrial disease that is progressively degenerative. The case has [similarities to that of Charlie Gard](https://www.foxnews.com/health/parents-of-brain-damaged-baby-lose-fight-to-keep-him-on-life-support), an infant who was removed from life support at the insistence of doctors despite objections from the parents. The Gard family was seeking to take Charlie to the United States for experimental treatment.
>
> ST has been in the ICU for the past year, requiring a ventilator and a feeding tube. She also requires regular dialysis due to chronic kidney damage from her disease.

[more](https://www.zerohedge.com/medical/british-court-rules-competent-conscious-patient-can-be-denied-life-sustaining-treatment)

### Jack Kruse — 2023-09-05T14:40:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/british-court-rules-that-competent-conscious-patient-can-be-denied-life-sustaining-treatment-against-her-will.28143/post-325973>

Given what they just did with Covid why are you surprised? Medical freedom is nonexistent in any of the 196 countries on Earth.

### JanSz — 2023-09-05T22:05:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/british-court-rules-that-competent-conscious-patient-can-be-denied-life-sustaining-treatment-against-her-will.28143/post-325993>

> [Huck said:](/goto/post?id=325951)
>
> I just wondered what everyone thought about this, both medically and ethically.
>
>
>
> [more](https://www.zerohedge.com/medical/british-court-rules-competent-conscious-patient-can-be-denied-life-sustaining-treatment)

May not be exactly but similar what was the situation my Dear Wife was in in 2017.
She started being sick couple days before 4th of July.
Got sick.
Went into hospital was intensively treated to a point.
At that point doctor called family conference.
He told all of us that he reached a point that he does not have anything more to offer.
He also thinks that she will not live longer than 9 months.
He will sign a paper to that effect.
From that point she has to leave hospital.
We have a choice to take her or he will transfer her to some facility.
At that point all I knew is that I want to take her home.
I have not realized the rest (which was not so bad (in retrospect).
We both were retired, both on Medicare insurances.
Apparently, there is another part of Medicare Insurance to which my wife was transferred.
That part (100% paid by insurance) cares for people in that situation.
She got a special bed and air machine.
Daily nursing care
Every few days registered nurse visit
Medications were arrived previously ordered by nurse.
Religious assistance of her choice.
\--------
My wife was fully conscious, able to talk, just not able to move any muscle.
You could have her solve differential equations or multiply matrixes.
So her mind was fully functional.
She asked why they told her that she is going to die sun.
So, I started to argue with nurse about some of the stuff that she was telling me to give her.
Eventually nurse told me the deal.
She was not given any medications with the intention of curing her.
She was given stuff that made her feel better and most important to ease her otherwise terrible pain.
Fentanyl, morphine, lidocaine ointment.
If we had any idea of attempting to cure her, no problem, just sign piece of paper and they will go away.
"Normal" Medicare will come back, and they will pay only for what they think is proper.
There is more.

Overall, I think that my wife got a good deal from her Medicare coverage.
I am not broken financially and able to continue on living.
I am praying every day for her. She was really unusually good wife.

Heart breaking situation, but that is why insurances exist.

///////////////

### The.Naked.Doctor — 2023-09-06T00:53:56+0300

Clinically I use PWA (pulse wave analysis) via the [sygmocor device](https://atcormedical.com/technology/sphygmocor-xcel/) to check for arterial calcification. I really love this as its functionally related to cardiovascular biological age and obviously is much less cost and radiation than a coronary CT

### JanSz — 2023-09-06T01:23:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-the-ladies.28144/post-326005>

> [The.Naked.Doctor said:](/goto/post?id=326003)

I really love this as its functionally related to cardiovascular biological age and obviously is much less cost and radiation than a coronary CT

Coronary CT was at one time recommended by Jack.
By now it is relatively common.
Easy to get.
Just take $100 and show up in the place.
Better call them and make appointment.
No doctor's script required.
..........
An Agatston score is a measure of the amount of calcium in the coronary arteries, which indicates the extent of plaque buildup and the risk of heart disease and stroke12345. It is calculated based on a low-dose CT scan of the heart14. The score ranges from 0 to higher numbers, with higher scores indicating greater plaque deposits and higher risk of coronary events2

I had it done twice.
The first score was 2.
The second score was 1.
clean as a whistle at 83yo
\=============================

### The.Naked.Doctor — 2023-09-06T01:53:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-the-ladies.28144/post-326011>

Yes thank you, im very familiar with calcium cores and order them frequently.
Unlike in (im assuming the US?) in NZ its about 3500K out of pocket to have a CT score done.
While they are an important assessment of risk, I do find that clinical devices (that use PWA) are excellent for assessing central BP and arterial calcification as well as functional risk.
We can retest this as much as possible in about 5 minutes, without exposing patients to additional radiation, and can see how things like grounding/Omgea 3's/PBM etc etc. change blood pressure dynamics and pulse wave (the pressure wave reflected back to the heart indicates how stiff arteries are) measures...
Some people may have a high Ca score but low risk, while others with less arterial Ca build-up (but say poor compensatory mechanisms, lower inflammation etc ) may be at higher risk..
That's why I combine PWA assessment with imaging in my practice.
Whats the deal by the way? It seems quite a random rant to get onto in regards to my post re a yoga pose..... ?
Have a wonderful day and thank you from brining this up!

how stiff arteries are)

////////////////////////////////////////////////////
how stiff arteries are)

How stiff arteries are that really depends on the Nitric Oxide level.
With low NO level you get all kind of cardiovascular problems, not only stiffness.

# Thread: "Smart" Meter Removal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meter-removal.28146/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 35
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### coho3131 — 2023-09-06T02:42:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meter-removal.28146/post-326019>

I wrote an email to the gas company customer service to have the smart meter removed and they said it can not be removed. What is the next thing I can try? Is there a certain type of letter I can use? Thanks.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-09-06T03:42:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meter-removal.28146/post-326025>

> [coho3131 said:](/goto/post?id=326019)
>
> I wrote an email to the gas company customer service to have the smart meter removed and they said it can not be removed. What is the next thing I can try? Is there a certain type of letter I can use? Thanks.

While it is there put a metallic shield NOT in front of it but between it and your house. If you can’t get between it and the house (most electric “smart” meters) put the shield on the inside wall of your home where the meter is on the outside. Meter shield covers on the outside protect your Neighbor and push the EMF into you home!

### coho3131 — 2023-09-06T03:49:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meter-removal.28146/post-326026>

> While it is there put a metallic shield NOT in front of it but between it and your house. If you can’t get between it and the house (most electric “smart” meters) put the shield on the inside wall of your home where the meter is on the outside. Meter shield covers on the outside protect your Neighbor and push the EMF into you home!

Thank you! A piece of sheet metal would be fine, right?

> [coho3131 said:](/goto/post?id=326026)

> Thank you! A piece of sheet metal would be fine, right?

Yup. Measure before and after if you can so you will know.
In this photo there are 2 “Smart” meters on the outside wall. I had a reading of 2.004 mW/m2 before and 0.0012 mW/m2 after putting up aluminum foil (which I grounded).

### Anthony Smith — 2023-09-07T01:02:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meter-removal.28146/post-326066>

You can spend a lot of money on meters. I started with a TriField TF2 and the Cornet in the photo. Both do a reasonable job for a non professional and measure E, M & RF. I think a Body Voltage Meter (BVK) is the best and most accurate way to measure electric fields [www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse). Just for magnetic fields the Alpha Labs UHS2 is a good one and for RF the TF2 and Cornet are reasonable but I recently read some good about the GQ EMF-390 which is also a “tri-field” meter and measures up to 8 GHz . Have fun.

### coho3131 — 2023-09-07T01:20:10+0300

I think a Body Voltage Meter (BVK) is the best and most accurate way to measure electric fields [www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse). Just for magnetic fields the Alpha Labs UHS2 is a good one and for RF the TF2 and Cornet are reasonable but I recently read some good about the GQ EMF-390 which is also a “tri-field” meter and measures up to 8 GHz . Have fun.

Wow, no use in the cover then! So the smart meter is on the outside of a wall of my bedroom. Can I put something inside to help? There are random surges. Also, I borrowed a cornet meter and there’s a huge difference between their acceptable ranges and building biologists per a YouTube I saw. Can you comment on this? With the building biologists adjusted Cornet scale in the settings, is yellow ok or just green? Thanks! Your input is very helpful!

### Anthony Smith — 2025-01-21T21:33:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meter-removal.28146/post-345531>

To shield from a “smart” meter and/or a WiFi router - the shield should be between it and you.
For “smart” meters figure out exactly where it is and put the shield on the inside wall.
A shields/cover over the meter is great for your neighbors but may cause the meter to increase its strength and therefore your exposure on the inside.

As far as the reading you get on ANY RF meter . . . The lower the better . . . Green is better than yellow etc.

### MikeR — 2025-01-22T00:15:08+0200

>
> As far as the reading you get on ANY RF meter . . . The lower the better . . . Green is better than yellow etc.

How far above/below and to the right/left of the meter should the shield extend?

### Mat.I — 2025-01-22T00:23:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meter-removal.28146/post-345547>

Not that this helps you, however may help others in Australia - I think it was from 2017 that new houses required smart meters and the energy authorities rolled out upgrades to existing houses (in a very sneaky way). There is a rule that has been passed to allow consumers to request that there energy provider disable the communication link and revert to a non-communicating smart meter. This will address the RF issues.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-01-22T00:56:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meter-removal.28146/post-345549>

> [MikeR said:](/goto/post?id=345542)
>
> How far above/below and to the right/left of the meter should the shield extend?

I tell people a 3 - 4 ft square. Get a big poster or piece of art And put aluminum foil or metal mosquito window mesh behind it.

Measure and you will know.

### Tripvan — 2025-01-22T07:07:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meter-removal.28146/post-345565>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=345547)
>
> Not that this helps you, however may help others in Australia - I think it was from 2017 that new houses required smart meters and the energy authorities rolled out upgrades to existing houses (in a very sneaky way). There is a rule that has been passed to allow consumers to request that there energy provider disable the communication link and revert to a non-communicating smart meter. This will address the RF issues.

In Queensland, last week I was able to opt out again, but received the following email:

> **We are not upgrading your electricity meter
> **
> You have opted out of upgrading your electricity meter
>
> We are writing to let you know that we have actioned your request to not take part in our smart meter upgrade service.
>
> At Alinta Energy, we strive to better our services and provide additional benefits and improved functionality to our customers. However, we understand that everyone has different preferences, and we respect your choice to decline the upgrade at this time.
>
> **However the rules are changing**
>
> The Australian Energy Market Commission (AEMC) is introducing new rules for energy retailers to fast track the roll out of smart meters to their customers, so that every household and business has a smart meter. Once these rules come into effect, we need to let you know the option to opt out will be removed which means you?ll eventually be put back in the pipeline to receive an upgrade.
>
> In addition to the above, if at any time your current meter is deemed faulty by your network or you choose to have work performed to your meter (for example the installation of solar); a smart meter will need to be installed in line with energy regulations, to which an opt-out is unavailable.

It appears AEMC has announced a smart meter rollout acceleration period from 1 December 2025 to 31 May 2031

[https://www.aemc.gov.au/rule-changes/accelerating-smart-meter-deployment](https://www.aemc.gov.au/rule-changes/accelerating-smart-meter-deployment)

[https://www.aemc.gov.au/sites/default/files/2024-11/Final%20rule%C2%A0determination%C2%A0%20271124%20%28For%20publication%29.pdf](https://www.aemc.gov.au/sites/default/files/2024-11/Final%20rule%C2%A0determination%C2%A0%20271124%20%28For%20publication%29.pdf)

The somewhat better news is they're still going to offer Type 4A meters, which are the "smart meter" without communications, where a meter reader will still come out and read them:

> The final retail rule removes this provision in the NERR, which means customers on standard retail
> contracts will not be able to opt out of a new meter deployment.
> Consistent with current arrangements, meters with remote access capabilities disabled (type 4A)
> are available to customers who refuse a smart meter where these capabilities are enabled (type 4).

>

>
>  privacy.org.au
>
> (more info about Type 4A)

I'm in NSW however the AEMC rules apply here also.. When I checked in last (off the top of my head)
\- new installations have the ability to select a 4A (no remote connection) meter in lieu of a 4 meter (with a remote connection
\- existing that already have Type 4's and you wish to deactivate - you can request this deactivation via the energy provider (likely around $250) , however make sure the actual comms module gets removed (which powers the cellular modem). FYI - Some energy providers had the option of upgrading the modems only to existing 2G/3G meters due to the shut down of the 2G/3G networks to accept 4G/LTE etc..

Obviously the underlying issue is the use of the internal antenna within the steel meter box enclosures creates very low signal strength therefore power density increases (it increases your exposure based on the sum of power density x time) . A absolute fall back to get around this (in the case of not being able to disable) would be to run a remote external antenna (which there is already factory provision for on the modem PCB). This will result in no RF being directed through the house (only if correctly setup) and will result in an overall decrease in power density. Noting (I think) the AEMC stipulate 5min intervals for data dumps...

I will check in with someone that I helped through the process a few months ago to see if they lodged the request with their energy provider and what the outcome was..

N.B. any unauthorised alterations or tampers to smart meters are monitored and raise notification alarms at the monitoring service.. Even the 4A have a log that can be downloaded onsite only (not remote)..

### Anthony Smith — 2025-04-04T08:35:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meter-removal.28146/post-350058>

The only thing that makes a “smart” meter “smart” is a built in cell phone.

I talk to people all day calling me on a cell phone, inches from their head, worried about their “smart” meter, 50+ feet away. What can they do about it? Put a piece of in foil on the inside wall where the “smart” meter is, to block the signal coming into the house, especially sleep areas, get off your cell phone and be outside in the sun and live your life.

Now if you are worried about the invasion of privacy and the electric company selling your electrical usage information to the highest bidder, that’s another story.

PS - if you put a “Faraday” cages over the meter outside and not the back of it, it just pushes the signal INTO your home. It also has to power up to make it’s connection.

# Thread: grounding swimming in fresh water vs. 1 or 2 parts of body touching earth
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-swimming-in-fresh-water-vs-1-or-2-parts-of-body-touching-earth.28147/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Martha Stett — 2023-09-06T18:22:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-swimming-in-fresh-water-vs-1-or-2-parts-of-body-touching-earth.28147/post-326048>

I heard in a Q&A with a QB expert that we absorb no more electrons from swimming in fresh water than if we were to just ground with bare feet on grass or with one finger on a tree. This strikes me as illogical, as the contact surface area is of course so much greater. Thanks for clarifying.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-09-06T18:47:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-swimming-in-fresh-water-vs-1-or-2-parts-of-body-touching-earth.28147/post-326049>

> [Martha Stett said:](/goto/post?id=326048)
>
> I heard in a Q&A with a QB expert that we absorb no more electrons from swimming in fresh water than if we were to just ground with bare feet on grass or with one finger on a tree. This strikes me as illogical, as the contact surface area is of course so much greater. Thanks for clarifying.

When using a body voltage meter, which measures the amount of electric field being induced into the body going to ground thru a ground wire, any contact to ground gets the same result, the whole hand or one finger. Grounding pads/sheets work the same way, one tiny wire makes the ground connection.

### Marko Pollo — 2023-09-07T00:48:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-swimming-in-fresh-water-vs-1-or-2-parts-of-body-touching-earth.28147/post-326063>

I think we are supposed to be constantly adsorbing energy from the sun and them sending electrons to ground through the soles of our feet or any other part of our bodies, however we also make contact with and ground through wet grasses and other plants. And as we are getting rid of electrons, the plants, and microbes in the soil accept our charge and convey it to ground, probably to their benefit. We are only talking about millivolts, so we don't need a large connection, just one that is recurring often. I usually am working in wet grass with wet boots and pant legs and feel I am always grounded.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recommended-books-to-read.28149/post-326154>

Someone was kind enough to send me a list of books he got that Jack recommends: (its most likely not complete. please add to it if you know of some that isnt mentioned here)

Gerald pollack - the 4th phase of water

Roeland van wijk - light in shaping life

[Johnjoe McFadden](https://www.amazon.com/Johnjoe-McFadden/e/B001HMKD0M?ref=sr_ntt_srch_lnk_1&qid=1690774037&sr=1-1) and [Jim Al-Khalili](https://www.amazon.com/Jim-Al-Khalili/e/B001HD1W88?ref=sr_ntt_srch_lnk_1&qid=1690774037&sr=1-1) - life at the edge

Nick lane - power sex and suicide

Robert o becker - the body electric and others

Robert becker’s friend, andrew marino - going somewhere

F. Hollwich - the influence of ocular light perception on metabolism in man and in animal

Gilbert Ling - In search of the physical basis of life

Samuel Milhan - dirty electricity

Lyn McLean - the force

Becker & Marino - electromagnetism and life

Becker - cross currents

John Ott - Health and Light

### GavinH — 2023-09-10T10:20:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recommended-books-to-read.28149/post-326193>

Mae-Wan Ho - The Rainbow and the Worm
Nick Lane - The Vital Question

# Thread: I've tested positive for Kruse19
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ive-tested-positive-for-kruse19.28150/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### tomstaplez — 2023-09-08T01:56:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ive-tested-positive-for-kruse19.28150/post-326116>

Kruse early symptoms include-

Not sure what to believe in anymore.
Anger turned disappointment for practically all government institutions.
Realizing most doctors are salesmen.
Blank stares from friends and family talking about 3 legged stools.
Every single problem you have now seems easily solvable in time, for free.
Holstered emf meter.
Opening noncustodial wallets.
Spending more time in Canada Post Office than ever before-(bullbitcoin).
Looking at stranger's complexion, footwear and hair creepily.
Downloading learn-to-speak Spanish apps hell bent on moving to El Salvador.
Ear pain due to wife yelling at my dirty feet.
Bridge pain from wearing glasses first time ever in my life.
Uncertainty determining Jack's face or a Jack quote tattoo.
Still not having a clue about water but local fats and proteins have better water.
Still not having a clue about magnetism but nnEMF is bad.

Long term Kruse symptoms include-
Will advise...

Weak baseline data-
39 male.
6foot, 183lbs.
44.1 N.
2 Fitzpatrick scale, w ability to get dark brown.
Farmer/carpenter/butcher.
Fit, athletic, tad bit 'skinny fat'.
Rarely grounded.
Whole foods diet, heavy on pork. Almost zero seafood.
Black, fast growing, thick, slowly greying hockey hair.
Intermittent Dandruff with bouts of gigantic flakes.
Intermittent AWFUL armpit odor (plz help).
Minor face-skin dryness in the past.
Minor tinnitus randomly.
Small weak teeth, wisdoms remain.
Used to be good for one migraine a year.
Minor bright light sensitivity.
SLIGHT lower back pain.
Sharp cognition.
Ears, eyes and dexterity great.
Depression? What is that?
Zero meds.
Pure blood.
Drink a tad.
Cannabis prob too much.
Never sunglassed ever.
Sunrise rarely.
Sunset bit more than rarely.
Deep sleeper.
Devils itch survivor x4
Childhood video games to the max.
Screen time pretty heavy.
nnEMF likely bad, more so in the past.
Always detested fiat and government.

European potato famine ancestory, both parents as well.
Mom- pale, indoors forever, hates sun, 50yrs ungrounded, limited nnEMF, cancer survivor, low meds.
Grandma- pale, died with Parkinsons.
Grandpa- pale redhead, heart disease.
Dad- "healthy", CR dialed in, plenty of sun on face and arms, zero meds, never grounded, poor teeth, near sighted, near deaf, limited nnEMF.
Grandparents- heart disease.

### tomstaplez — 2023-09-14T03:54:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ive-tested-positive-for-kruse19.28150/post-326383>

Sep-13, 2023
I think I have most of my ducks in order now.
nnEMF has been identified, and or eliminated. Wasn't as bad as I suspected.
Gotten into a decent routine, I've seen most sunrises barefoot, since early Kruse symptoms began. Briefly tho, 5-30min, I need a good book or something.
Try to 'go dark' earlier at night now, I've gotten a cheap pair of blueblockers I'm wearing at night. Blue light definitely gets thru, im assuming they block just the high blue peaks.
It feels like winter is approaching fast here- "the best time to start is now".
Luckily diet, and freezers are already packed full of fat and protein (we raise hens and hogs).
44 N untreated well water has to be above average right?
One thing I have to do research on is cold vs hot therapy.... If cold is exceptionally beneficial in lower light environments, is hot exposure exceptionally bad?!?! Because watching sunrise in a hottub would be something i could easily adapt to. Can you ground the hottub?!?

Wife and I are going portaging for 4 days next week. Grounded 4 days straight, lakes will be chilli, sunrises and sunsets 100% hungover or not. Zero nnEMF. When we get back, I'll try to tighten up my home routine. Thx bye.

### Jack Kruse — 2023-09-14T20:03:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ive-tested-positive-for-kruse19.28150/post-326401>

Would you need more sun, not less, because your mitochondrial efficiency is dropping via heteroplasmy, which means less matrix water is created and mtDNA dynamics make less melatonin? Is the same true for red light / NIR? ANSWER: You need more sun not less because your mitochondrial efficiency is dropping via heteroplasmy = less matrix water created and less melatonin made by mtDNA dynamics.

^^^^^^

### tomstaplez — 2023-09-14T23:50:23+0300

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=326401)
>
> ^^^^^^

Jack touches on many things in that one ^ of course, but most likely-
'just come to El Salvador and experiment. See for yourself, or be a dumb dumb and constantly battle cultural norms and your climate'

In fact, we are going to vacation there next month. Check out the ark.

Hopefully Jack hires my nurse wife .

### tomstaplez — 2024-07-02T16:19:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ive-tested-positive-for-kruse19.28150/post-336433>

~10 months post infection update.

Changes I've made....
Much more skin in the game, I'm the darkest I've been in years, my friends think I'm one of the millions of new Indian immigrants.
Watching most sunrises with my dogs, usually grounded.
Renovated our small house, our bed has very minimal nnEMF now, and is grounded direct to soil.
Blue blocking eyes and skin post sunset very consistently.
Mitt full of eggs and usually fatty meat most mornings.
Any excuse to do anything outside I now take, built an outdoor kitchen for instance.
Very conscious of nnEMF, unplugging everything when not in use, including power tools, tv's, Bluetooth ect.
Built a wood fired sauna, went from a hottub guy to sauna lover. Using it less in this hot weather, but I believe it has adapted me to working in the summer weather.
Raw milk maple syrup icecream is my cheat treat.
PBM every other day or so.
For the past couple weeks I've been collecting unfiltered rainwater, freezing about 20% and drinking the remains. Deuterium depletion I believe?! And I have a hunch this will have one of the biggest impacts for me. Can't confirm yet.
Custom made all leather footwear on route.

Things I need to improve....
Booze, weed. In no way am I an addict. But I like my social time with wife and friends, and our culture is yolked to those things.
More CT. I don't even find it difficult, just a lot of prep work.
More seafood but dayum $$$$$, sardines got old real fast.
Religious on the sunrises.
Seasonal and local food.
Stretching/flexibility.
I've almost given up spreading this word, a few loves ones are coming around but maaaaaan doctrination is fuckin real. Coldvid has chinked their armor tho.
Buying more Bitcoin. (Lost my non-kyc purchasing ability, that hurts).

Effects....
Devil's itch PTSD has been conquered. That was a large hindrance for me.
My iddy bitty man boobs are stubborn bastards, im nearing a six pack with man boobs wt heck?
Minor face skin issues I had are all but gone.
Tinnitus and dandruff still come and go but less severe and often.
Stank ass garlic-onion-poison armpit smell is still present, but not constant.
No change to my good sleeping, still good energy, eyes, ears, dexterity all mint. Overall still feel good and sharp. Routinely outperform dudes a decade younger than me.

Future plans?
Contemplating building an off-grid earth berm house in a cellphone dead-zone on our farm. And renting out our current house. Easily controllable nnEMF, always grounded, all natural building materials, loads of natural light ect.
Moving to the equator unfortunately won't happen for a bit atleast. We loved our time in El Salvador, and I understand it would be a wise move for longevity.

Overall, I don't think I've found the culprit to my tiny little problems. I'm very blessed that I continue to feel 'healthy'.

Keep up the good work Jack n co. You guys and gals are tough sob.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Melanin Replacement
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melanin-replacement.28151/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kathy Spears — 2023-09-08T02:34:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melanin-replacement.28151/post-326118>

I understand how important melanin is and that it is in places other than skin (such as the brain). What I am unsure of is if we are able to replace it. When Dr Jack talks about vitiligo patients pulling their melanin in I immediately wonder why they can't replace it with sun exposure rather than having to steal it from the skin. I'm sure the biggest issue is patients are being told to stay out of the sun. However, I have many clients with hypo pigmented skin from injuries such as burns who tan regularly and their white spots don't seem to ever repigment. How did Dr Jack get that woman's vitiligo repaired? Can we increase our melanin just by being in the sun? Sorry if this is very basic but I can't understand the details until I get the foundation.

### Jack Kruse — 2023-09-08T03:53:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melanin-replacement.28151/post-326120>

Can you make melanin if your skin isnt capable? Don't you need proper time stamping by circadian biology for it to operate? Is POMC operational pretranslationally or post?

### Kathy Spears — 2023-09-08T04:18:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melanin-replacement.28151/post-326121>

So, assuming your circadian biology is good, your skin should be able to make melanin? Getting a tan doesn't mean you are making melanin, correct? You are just bringing it (what you already have) to the surface in response to sun exposure. Making new melanin requires the body to be in a very healthy state. Is this correct? Is POMC operational pre translationally? That's my guess. I'm sorry but I'm really trying to understand this.

### Jack Kruse — 2023-09-08T06:34:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melanin-replacement.28151/post-326123>

No POMC isn't without UV light. That is the story of this series

### Jack Kruse — 2023-09-11T04:53:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melanin-replacement.28151/post-326229>

Why did I have a ubiquitnation series before a mitochondrial series or quantum engineering series on Patreon?

In mammals, a master clock localized in the suprachiasmatic nucleus of the hypothalamus synchronizes cellular clocks in other central nervous and peripheral tissues with each other and with external time. At the molecular level, these clocks are based on an interregulatory network of clock genes, including the 3 Period genes (_Per1–3_), that control CRs by rhythmic orchestration of 5–10% of the cellular transcriptome in a post translational tissue-specific manner. CR time stamp genes after they are translated and cause diseases.

**Transcription-translation feedback loop** (**TTFL**) is a cellular model for explaining [circadian rhythms](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Circadian_rhythm) in behavior and [physiology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Physiology). Widely conserved across species, the TTFL is auto-regulatory, in which transcription of clock genes is regulated by their own protein products. Rev-erba is one and so is NF-kappa beta.

### **Circadian clock protein Rev-erbα regulates neuroinflammation.**​

### Rev-erbα, a nuclear receptor and circadian clock component, is a mediator of microglial activation and neuroinflammation. It has been observed that time-of-day oscillation in microglial immunoreactivity and in the hippocampus, is disrupted in Rev-erbα-/-mammals. Rev-erbα deletion caused spontaneous microglial activation in the hippocampus and increased expression of proinflammatory transcripts, as well as secondary astrogliosis. Transcriptomic analysis of hippocampus from Rev-erbα-/- mammals revealed a predominant inflammatory phenotype and suggested dysregulated NF-κB signaling. Primary Rev-erbα-/-microglia exhibited proinflammatory phenotypes and increased basal NF-κB activation. Chromatin immunoprecipitation revealed that Rev-erbα physically interacts with the promoter regions of several NF-κB-related genes in primary microglia.​

**Nuclear factor kappa-light-chain-enhancer of activated B cells** (**NF-κB**) is a protein complex that controls [transcription](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Transcription_\(genetics\)) of [DNA](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/DNA) by way of mtDNA signaling (by the mPTP), [cytokine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cytokine) production and cell survival. NF-κB is found in almost all animal cell types and is involved in cellular responses to stimuli such as light stress, [cytokines](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cytokine), [free radicals](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Free_radical), [heavy metals](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heavy_metals), nnEMF [irradiation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ultraviolet_irradiation), oxidized [LDL](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/LDL), and bacterial or viral [antigens](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Antigen). NF-κB plays a key role in regulating the immune response to infection. Incorrect regulation of NF-κB has been linked to cancer, inflammatory and [autoimmune diseases](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Autoimmune_diseases), [septic shock](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Septic_shock), [viral infection](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Viral_infection), and improper immune development. NF-κB has also been implicated in processes of [synaptic plasticity](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Synaptic_plasticity) and memory.

Light controls all clock genes.

The Bmal party is kicked off in the morning by SUNLIGHT, and acts to increase PER by night (among many, many other things). As the day progresses, REV-ERB the Repressor slowly shuts down Bmal, so that peak PER occurs in the evening and doesn’t carry over until the next morning. GSK3b activates REV-ERB the Repressor.

# Thread: Aarons Optimal Journal Beginning
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aarons-optimal-journal-beginning.28152/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 65
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gcazz — 2023-09-08T12:20:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aarons-optimal-journal-beginning.28152/post-326124>

Hi all. I recently came across Dr Kruse through the Huberman/Rubin podcast after another influencer shared the details.
Upon listening I was amazed as many others are. I've listened to a lot of different people over the years and some that are considered fringe but never heard anything like what Dr Kruse speaks of.
I listened to this podcast probably 5 times over. I have a delivery business and use a lot of this time to listen to podcasts or webinars.

A little about me. I'm 41 years old and live on the Gold Coast of Australia. Have worked hard most of my life lots of different roles. But usually 2 jobs at a time and not sleeping much for years at a time. I also have always had sleeping problems from a child I remember complaining about not being able to get to sleep while my siblings just seemed to drop off once they hit the bed.
I never used to consider circadian biology important and thought I could will myself through anything.
When I was 39 I had a pretty crappy reaction to the second vac shot. Horrendous fevers and shaking and urine looked like cola colour. I also had what felt like blood pressure issues whenever I trained. I've done martial arts my entire life and I felt like I was going to pass out even do some basic training.
Just before my 40th birthday I tore my bicep tendon which has now healed quite well.
I've had brain fog for a long time now and I struggle to think of when it started. When I was younger I had close to a photographic memory and I felt like my brain had another gear. For the last 5 or 6 years it's felt like the gear has gone. I'm not sure how to explain it any better than that sorry.
It's kind of, when something gets difficult my brain just says no. Like it won't try to think any harder.
I've also had psoriasis for about 4 or 5 years now. Particularly bad inside my ears and my beard.

Now I'm very new to this. I also like to be a little skeptical of new things or ideas. However what Dr Jack is suggesting to start with is simple and easy why not try.
So sat barefoot in the garden for a couple weeks and got an earthing sheet. First 2 nights of sleep were dramatically better.
After approx a month of grounding I noticed I no longer got faint when in the gym. Dramatic difference here.
After listening to some more podcasts and webinars while I was using bluetooth earphones every day to listen to them.
I bought cable earphones a week ago today. Haven't used them in a week now and i've got close to sunrise sun every morning. Fully clothed and then a following 10 minutes naked in front of red light panel. I can say that in 3 days my psoriasis is 99.9% gone. Absolutely incredible. Its been so bad I have to shake my shirt off and wipe it clean every time I move. For that to have changed in 3 days or less is mind blowing to me.
So I have now ordered some red light blocking glasses and plan to continue to add things as they make more and more sense to me.

I have some people I love dearly that have some different conditions so the more I learn and can pass on the better.
I've always loved transformation stories in the gym but I don't think there's any better than hearing some of your stories here from sick to well literally changing your lives. I love reading them all. Thank you look forward to speaking with lots of new people.

### JanSz — 2023-09-08T21:33:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aarons-optimal-journal-beginning.28152/post-326146>

I suggest 3 items.

1\. follow Jack Kruse advice
2\. after your 30th birthday get familiar with Nitric Oxide story. Better described as fight for optimal blood supply to any place that blood should go. [https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/man-woman-nitric-oxide.28046/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/man-woman-nitric-oxide.28046/)
3\. Linoleic Acid (omega-6). We all are drowning in it, that is not only humans but primarily the animals that humans eat.

If you figure out how to lessen this #3 problem, please do not forget telling me about.

/////////////////

Glad the grounding is working for you. Grounding/Earthing makes you part of the circuit, outside (away from any 60Hz AC field) in Natures DC field, is always good. You mention an Earthing sheet, IF you are grounding inside try turning off the breakers - the 60Hz (50Hz) AC field in your home at night AND ground to the outside NOT the house ground - for even better results.

### John Warner — 2023-09-08T21:55:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aarons-optimal-journal-beginning.28152/post-326150>

> [Gcazz said:](/goto/post?id=326124)
>
> Hi all. I recently came across Dr Kruse through the Huberman/Rubin podcast after another influencer shared the details.
> Upon listening I was amazed as many others are. I've listened to a lot of different people over the years and some that are considered fringe but never heard anything like what Dr Kruse speaks of.
> I listened to this podcast probably 5 times over. I have a delivery business and use a lot of this time to listen to podcasts or webinars.
>
> A little about me. I'm 41 years old and live on the Gold Coast of Australia. Have worked hard most of my life lots of different roles. But usually 2 jobs at a time and not sleeping much for years at a time. I also have always had sleeping problems from a child I remember complaining about not being able to get to sleep while my siblings just seemed to drop off once they hit the bed.
> I never used to consider circadian biology important and thought I could will myself through anything.
> When I was 39 I had a pretty crappy reaction to the second vac shot. Horrendous fevers and shaking and urine looked like cola colour. I also had what felt like blood pressure issues whenever I trained. I've done martial arts my entire life and I felt like I was going to pass out even do some basic training.
> Just before my 40th birthday I tore my bicep tendon which has now healed quite well.
> I've had brain fog for a long time now and I struggle to think of when it started. When I was younger I had close to a photographic memory and I felt like my brain had another gear. For the last 5 or 6 years it's felt like the gear has gone. I'm not sure how to explain it any better than that sorry.
> It's kind of, when something gets difficult my brain just says no. Like it won't try to think any harder.
> I've also had psoriasis for about 4 or 5 years now. Particularly bad inside my ears and my beard.
>
> Now I'm very new to this. I also like to be a little skeptical of new things or ideas. However what Dr Jack is suggesting to start with is simple and easy why not try.
> So sat barefoot in the garden for a couple weeks and got an earthing sheet. First 2 nights of sleep were dramatically better.
> After approx a month of grounding I noticed I no longer got faint when in the gym. Dramatic difference here.
> After listening to some more podcasts and webinars while I was using bluetooth earphones every day to listen to them.
> I bought cable earphones a week ago today. Haven't used them in a week now and i've got close to sunrise sun every morning. Fully clothed and then a following 10 minutes naked in front of red light panel. I can say that in 3 days my psoriasis is 99.9% gone. Absolutely incredible. Its been so bad I have to shake my shirt off and wipe it clean every time I move. For that to have changed in 3 days or less is mind blowing to me.
> So I have now ordered some red light blocking glasses and plan to continue to add things as they make more and more sense to me.
>
> I have some people I love dearly that have some different conditions so the more I learn and can pass on the better.
> I've always loved transformation stories in the gym but I don't think there's any better than hearing some of your stories here from sick to well literally changing your lives. I love reading them all. Thank you look forward to speaking with lots of new people.

Keep goin, it's like drinking from a fire hose sometimes but its worth it.

### [DUTCH Plus® - DUTCH Test](https://dutchtest.com/product/dutch-plus/)

The DUTCH Plus® takes hormone testing to a new level. In addition to sex hormones and their metabolites, the DUTCH Complete™ looks at the overall diurnal pattern of free cortisol, and the total and distribution of cortisol metabolites. The DUTCH Plus® adds the Cortisol Awakening Response (CAR)...

 dutchtest.com

\===============================================
how to increase dopamine levels
Dopamine is a neurotransmitter that plays a key role in regulating mood, motivation, and pleasure1. There are several ways to increase dopamine levels naturally. Here are some of them:

**Exercise**: Exercise is one of the best ways to increase dopamine levels naturally. It can help increase the number of dopamine receptors in your brain, which can lead to increased dopamine activity1.
**Get enough sleep:** Getting enough good quality sleep is important for maintaining healthy dopamine levels1.
**Meditation**: Meditation can help increase dopamine levels by reducing stress and anxiety1.
**Eat foods rich in tyrosine:** Tyrosine is an amino acid that is used to make dopamine in your brain. Foods that are high in tyrosine include almonds, bananas, avocados, eggs, beans, fish, and chicken1.
**Consume probiotics:** Certain species of bacteria in your gut can produce dopamine which may impact mood and behavior2.

If possible, stay away from high raises.
Aim at 15-20% body fat.

### Gcazz — 2023-09-25T11:51:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aarons-optimal-journal-beginning.28152/post-326794>

Random thoughts of the day along with my current understanding or misunderstanding
Writing this for my own benefit in the future hoping to look back and find where I am at this point in time and correct my understandings over time.

Eating produce from where you live? Because the sunlight close to you is responsible for creating that food? The light creates the food instead of who knows what.

What are photos? Are they considered blue light? Photos stored light?
Are old people closer to wild? They like to get up early and go to bed early. Is this because they are unhealthier in general and the body resorts back to more primal needs as it gets sicker?
Light charges your mitochondrial water. Wether thats bad light or good light. Limiting fake light and charging with real light is important.
Current changes made to date.
Morning sunlight as much as possible.
Blue light blockers when with tech or in artificial settings.
As much seafood as possible. Have discovered I have a love of scallops.
Ordering BVK with E pen in the next day or so to measure my environment for a base level.

Something I noticed on the weekend that was interesting to me. Went to an axe throwing place to catch up with some high school friends.
During the second game I felt like I had some hand eye coordination back and like I was in the zone a little. Haven't had this feeling since I used to play basketball many years ago. Felt really dialed in.
Not sure where exactly this came from but was very noticeable to be. I used to at one point feel very coordinated and almost like I could pick up any sport. But it's been a long long time!!!
Thank you to everyone that messages or comments with support, It truly is appreciated.

Gcazz said: I felt like I had some hand eye coordination back and like I was in the zone a little.
Not sure where exactly this came from but was very noticeable to be.
\======
Recently Jack discussed that in the reference to football players.
\===========================================
Gcazz said: As much seafood as possible. Have discovered I have a love of scallops.
\======
Start looking at seafood as a source of most of your fat.
There are more reasons than one, which suggest eating seafood, and I mean wild seafood.
The reason is LA, Linoleic acid, omega-6.
Currently the whole world's food is DROWNING in LA.
LA comes from seed oils.
Everything that humans eat, is fed with EXTREME amounts of seed oils.
Usually, we know about the common processed foods, and stay away from it.
But when you look at the pig or chicken or fish you would never think that you are really looking at the seed oil fat.
To really appreciate your own status, get (simple) test.
Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum
You want LA/ALA to be (1 - 4)
And make sure that you stay away from more sophisticated tests, cheating you is already in progress.
My plan
no nuts, except macadamia
whole avocado only (Avocado oil is already cheated).
Similarly most olive oils are cheated, but whole olives should be ok.
butter or ghee, some coconut oil
practically no chicken or pork, but I am thinking that possibly chicken soup when frozen after making it, remove all fat and possibly it should be ok to eat.
Bacon, fully fried, so no fat is left, should possibly be ok too.
Beef, goat, they are also fed seed oils but they have 4 stomachs, they are able to remove almost all LA, so their fat should be acceptable.

Vegetables, they are our source of N3O which eventually should become NO, Nitric Acid.
Today's vegetables, especially organic type, are poor source of N3O.
Nitric Acid is a major content in our blood.
It regulates our whole cardiovascular system, from heart, arteries to the smallest veins.
It adjusts continuously diameter of veins, heart or arteries. Eventually it keeps their insides clean and not obstructed. Other than cardiovascular system, NO takes care of numerous other health issues.

To assess your status, you should get
Coronary Calcium Score, Agatston Score
When we are first born, clean system will give score 0, zero.
Most people have that score in hundreds, those closer to heart attacks has it in thousands.
My last score was 1. Previous 2.
Nitric Oxide status is really worth investigation.
/////////////
Aim at 10-20% body fat. But get there attempting to not loose muscle mass.
Aim at fasting insulin under 5, 2-3 better.
When in that area, body fat and fat or carbs that you eat are treated differently than in fat peoples.
\======

### JanSz — 2023-09-25T20:15:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aarons-optimal-journal-beginning.28152/post-326803>

Dense, small-particle LDL cholesterol in your body is a risk factor for heart disease risk, while large, fluffy LDL particles constitute a lower risk, **but here’s the kicker: Eggs convert small LDL particles to large particles

eggs increase the HDL or good cholesterol levels in your body**.
///////////////
But if Your Agatston score is substantial, (Coronary Calcium Score)..
Agatston score is a semi-automated tool to calculate a score based on the extent of coronary artery calcification detected.
Make sure that you have plenty of Nitric Oxide.
It cleans arterial plaque (among many other good things it does).
**//

Things I’ve noticed the last couple weeks.
I now no longer need melatonin to get to sleep.
Honestly mind blowing to me only used it once in the last three weeks.
I’ve had sleep problems most of my life.
Could stare at the ceiling for hours before getting to sleep.
Never fall asleep doing things in my entire life.
This weekend just gone I fell asleep while getting a massage.
Can not believe it happened.

Lots more grounding and sun daily too. I have the benefit of being able to work from home very grateful for.

Stay away from exogenous Melatonin, Jack is not a fan it thins the retina by ruining photoreceptor regeneration.

### [Impact of oral melatonin on the electroretinogram cone response - PubMed](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/19922677/)

Oral melatonin appears to reach the eye through the circulation. When it is administered at a time of day when it is not usually present, melatonin appears to reduce input to retinal cones. We believe that the impact of melatonin on retinal function should be taken into consideration when used...

 pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

### Gcazz — 2023-10-22T02:59:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aarons-optimal-journal-beginning.28152/post-327467>

This week due to some sick family members (who work for us) my daily morning routine didn't happen. Definitely noticed the difference within the week.
Mental attitude probably the first thing noticed changing.
Positives of the week had more time to go through material.
Now have 4th phase of water in audio version that I'm part way through thanks to Spotify now carrying audio books. Also have life at the edge lined up for after.

Been quite slack in actually reading the blogs. But have a couple hours sitting in the sun this morning while my partner does the Raw Challenge here on the gold coast.
Stressful week ahead with my father having bypass surgery Tuesday next week.
Also really enjoyed the Kruse for Dummies course.
Planning on finishing the required blogs for that course today and listening again tomorrow.
Hopefully get to have my morning routine back this week.
My partner and I now sleep with our windows and curtains open allowing that morning light to wake us every day.

Current understanding post Kruse for Dummies.
Is coming along still lots and lots to read and understand however some things are starting to make sense.

### Gcazz — 2023-11-08T08:16:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aarons-optimal-journal-beginning.28152/post-328020>

Observation this week.

Have started noticing that nobody even considers their environment as a reason for illness.

It's literally all just food. Even ppl that don't like artificial food struggle to grasp that out whole environments today are artificial.

Get the water inside right then charge it with the right source ☀️

Lots more understanding required on shrinking of proteins.

Getting the water right means forming the ez on the outside which is like a crystal and enhances the sunlights effect.

Homework for myself. Understand what polarisation actually is and what causes it.

Occasionally test my sleep with the oura ring. I know it's not popular here and I don't use all the time but my hrv has been steadily increasing even with only getting 6.5 hours sleep a night the last month. Mainly due to sleeping with our curtains open and letting the sun light wake us up.

Getting to sleep a little earlier has been difficult to adjust to.

I've been devouring a lot of content and actually finished a couple of blogs. I laugh every time I hear someone ask Dr Kruse a question and he responds with. It's simple. Then answers with what used to sound like a different language.

Some of the basics are truly beginning to make sense finally I'm def not ready for any pop quizzes yet though

### Anthony Smith — 2023-11-08T18:31:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aarons-optimal-journal-beginning.28152/post-328036>

I have read that some of the great literature of our past, great ideas and inventions came in the middle of the night “between the sleeps” as it was called. If you accept that waking for a short time is natural, enjoy the peace and any inspiration of the moment, then getting back to sleep is natural also.

### Gcazz — 2023-11-09T04:24:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aarons-optimal-journal-beginning.28152/post-328064>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=328029)
>
> Getting to sleep a little earlier has been difficult to adjust to.
> \------------------------------------------------------------------
> Sleep is a very big part of our life.
>
> There is a video on this topic. Look it up.
> We are meant to have a two parts of our night sleep.
> Supposedly it is ok to have a light activity in the middle of the night.
> Cuddling with sleeping partner maybe part of that.
> Not really sure if sex maybe a part of it.
> For me, it makes me wide awaken.
> Not sure how good it is from pov of maintaining restful sleep.
> OTOH, after (good) sex, sleep comes easily.
> But that may often be difficult, the need to satisfy two peoples at the same time.
>
> The way it mostly goes these days, I think, is that sleep is rather continuous one part of time.
>
> Personally, I could use (simple) explanations of what kind of **ideal sleeping pattern** we should be aiming for.
> ...........................
> Personally, I have given up on ideals I may have.
> Try not to even touch my partner at night.
> Sex, wait for nice middle of the day, no alcohol to blur that experience.
> /

I think i'm quite similar personally don't like my sleep to be broken up. However i've only had good sleep habits for a short time. So pretty open to other ideas. I think sex would make me awake too once anything breaks up my sleep in the morning for example my partners alarm. I'm up and sleep is over for me.

### Gcazz — 2023-11-14T09:43:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aarons-optimal-journal-beginning.28152/post-328269>

Dr Kruse mentioned not knowing how much I wanted to understand about it all.
The true answer is since I found this information it feels almost like it's more important than anything and everything else!!! I have an obsessive nature with very few things in my life, BUT those things I am very obsessed with. This has become one of them. Due to the nature of my lifestyle right now. (Trading time for money) I have a little more time available to listen to information over reading. I know this is not ideal but it is what it is for now.
Part way through the audio book "Life on the Edge" which I am loving!!! Especially after the Kruse for Dummies course. Not only is it piecing a lot of things together but it's really an enjoyable to listen to reading. This has given new meaning already to the term "entanglement" I head Dr Kruse use regularly.
I've just ordered the Body Voltage Kit and pen on the weekend just gone and looking forward to implementing that and testing out my house.
This last week i've felt a lot more engaged and present. I can hold a conversation during my zoom meetings where previously I would quite often have a deer in headlights reaction. I haven't added much more into my routine just yet. (I can be a slow adopter) I am regularly grounding we quite often have a little medicinal mj on the grass of an evening. Not too much but that's an enjoyable evening practice I look forward to with my partner while we download for the day.
I quite often have canned mackeral at the moment, not ideal but being consistent more important to me at the moment than ideal is.
I have the luxury of at 41 years old having made some reasonable life decisions these last 3-4 years thanks in large part to the best partner ever, which has led to me being able to work from home. I'm making sure I get a decent amount of sun most days (Still needs some work).

I'm also very excited to have some ideas for my home gym for the future. Some more strongman type equipment will def be involved around our big shed once one of our developments plays out finally.
Also once that finally happens we will take a small portion and give ourselves a little holiday.

### reubendiamond — 2023-11-14T19:05:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aarons-optimal-journal-beginning.28152/post-328277>

> [Gcazz said:](/goto/post?id=326124)
>
> Hi all. I recently came across Dr Kruse through the Huberman/Rubin podcast after another influencer shared the details.
> Upon listening I was amazed as many others are. I've listened to a lot of different people over the years and some that are considered fringe but never heard anything like what Dr Kruse speaks of.
> I listened to this podcast probably 5 times over. I have a delivery business and use a lot of this time to listen to podcasts or webinars.
>
> A little about me. I'm 41 years old and live on the Gold Coast of Australia. Have worked hard most of my life lots of different roles. But usually 2 jobs at a time and not sleeping much for years at a time. I also have always had sleeping problems from a child I remember complaining about not being able to get to sleep while my siblings just seemed to drop off once they hit the bed.
> I never used to consider circadian biology important and thought I could will myself through anything.
> When I was 39 I had a pretty crappy reaction to the second vac shot. Horrendous fevers and shaking and urine looked like cola colour. I also had what felt like blood pressure issues whenever I trained. I've done martial arts my entire life and I felt like I was going to pass out even do some basic training.
> Just before my 40th birthday I tore my bicep tendon which has now healed quite well.
> I've had brain fog for a long time now and I struggle to think of when it started. When I was younger I had close to a photographic memory and I felt like my brain had another gear. For the last 5 or 6 years it's felt like the gear has gone. I'm not sure how to explain it any better than that sorry.
> It's kind of, when something gets difficult my brain just says no. Like it won't try to think any harder.
> I've also had psoriasis for about 4 or 5 years now. Particularly bad inside my ears and my beard.
>
> Now I'm very new to this. I also like to be a little skeptical of new things or ideas. However what Dr Jack is suggesting to start with is simple and easy why not try.
> So sat barefoot in the garden for a couple weeks and got an earthing sheet. First 2 nights of sleep were dramatically better.
> After approx a month of grounding I noticed I no longer got faint when in the gym. Dramatic difference here.
> After listening to some more podcasts and webinars while I was using bluetooth earphones every day to listen to them.
> I bought cable earphones a week ago today. Haven't used them in a week now and i've got close to sunrise sun every morning. Fully clothed and then a following 10 minutes naked in front of red light panel. I can say that in 3 days my psoriasis is 99.9% gone. Absolutely incredible. Its been so bad I have to shake my shirt off and wipe it clean every time I move. For that to have changed in 3 days or less is mind blowing to me.
> So I have now ordered some red light blocking glasses and plan to continue to add things as they make more and more sense to me.
>
> I have some people I love dearly that have some different conditions so the more I learn and can pass on the better.
> I've always loved transformation stories in the gym but I don't think there's any better than hearing some of your stories here from sick to well literally changing your lives. I love reading them all. Thank you look forward to speaking with lots of new people.

That's an awesome story man, good for you! May I ask what red light panels you got? I have seen some online that are astronomically expensive so was wondering if you found some that are worthwhile?

### Gcazz — 2023-11-15T01:27:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aarons-optimal-journal-beginning.28152/post-328284>

> [reubendiamond said:](/goto/post?id=328277)
>
> That's an awesome story man, good for you! May I ask what red light panels you got? I have seen some online that are astronomically expensive so was wondering if you found some that are worthwhile?

Thanks mate.
Yeah of course you can.
I ended up getting an infraredi I think it's called the pro max.
Mainly because it had a 20% discount at the time plus free shipping. And I got the door hook because it's cheaper and can manipulate the height easier.
I kind of treated it like a gateway drug to the sun
Now I use it as a supplement mainly for the areas that don't get enough light.
If I was to buy again I might look for one that's smaller and can stay on longer in the background now I'm getting more sunlight.
Hope that helped a little.

Better sleep = better deterium depletion.

Have recently finished reading Light and Health by John Ott. Incredible read!!! However it's very frustrating thinking about the arrogance of humans. We always seem to think we know it all or act like we do and when a new idea is brought in it's treated as stupid if you don't have any studies to back it up. Knowing this book alone has been out there for so long is insane to me.
I'm trying to pass this book on to people I care about as an intro to thinking about light.
I also went camping this past weekend for 4 days and had the absolute best time ever. We were just outside of Murwillumbah northern NSW in a calderra and we slept amazingly. The first night we ended up sleeping in till 11am which is unheard of for us especially in a tent. Everyone was shocked but the whole time I was wondering about the magnetic energy in the area.
Finally starting to get through a couple of blogs as well. I have a decent printer now and have printed quite a bit out so I can take time out in the middle of every day to get some sun time and a short read which is my time during the work day.
Have also massively upped my seafood which has made huge differences to my recall and conversational skills. I recently did my first ever podcast which I would never have said yes to a few months ago.
I'm also working my way through all the Q and A's currently around August 2016.
I regularly think about what Dr Kruse must have gone through over the years. I mean the tide is starting to swing now but looking back through your old stuff you've not changed in a long long time and I can only imagine how many different times it would've been so so much easier for you to keep all of this to yourself and not open yourself up to all the drama legal and otherwise.
I'm glad you now seem to have some good people around you making amazing things happen.
Speaking of the tide turning while camping I met a psychologist that doesn't work in the field she was trained in. Instead she is more of a holistic health practitioner. I overheard her talking to some of the group we were camping with (about 20 ppl for a bday party) informing them that the eye was a type of clock and I got so excited. I haven't met anyone in person to discuss any of this with yet. I thought she must know Dr Kruse but turns out she didn't of course I mentioned she should check it out. But I was excited to randomly stumble across someone on a similar path.

### Gcazz — 2024-08-28T11:51:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aarons-optimal-journal-beginning.28152/post-338997>

Just getting back from our wedding moon in Tasmania and had a fantastic time and a bunch of different experiences.

For the most part even though the air is super fresh everywhere the winter sun is dismal everywhere which I was expecting. I wasn't expecting the recommendations not to wear sunscreen though. I haven't heard that before. It's hard to not notice how pale most people are.
The other thing that stood out to us is how much Tas loves sweets! Chocolate shops are everywhere. Also bakeries are packed every morning. Reasonably hard to find meat and veg type meals anywhere. Also the arts are very popular there with a lot of creativity in a lot of the places.

Most places here I felt like crap, Stanley was noticeably the only place in TAS I felt good and the grass was so soft and nice to lie in.

We did multiple animal tours as well as cave tours and all the tour guides are very clear and “knowledgeable” on how our light affects the animals. Red light is used everywhere and they constantly mention how red light doesn’t affect the animals yet still believe that light has no effect on us humans.

Same in the caves. Mentioning the light and how it's bad for the cave animals. Being in the cave with the lights off was an interesting experience and fascinating to think about parts of nature that have been around for a long time and how they might move slow but there is no stopping it.

I’ve also been thinking about oxygen a little bit lately I have purchased quite a few books on the Dr Kruse reading list and slowly making my way through them. Oxygen is one of them.

Currently on beckers book and reading Marino’s book with my partner as an early evening thing we do together.
Have read a lot of blogs and books on our holiday and I’m ready to go in 100% now no time for half measures any more.

Also so many black swans everywhere in Tassie all around the oceans hanging out which was pretty cool.

We recently purchased a decent ink bottle printer and I've printed out a novel load of blogs and am finally smashing through them now.

Seafood was incredible in Tas and also stumbled upon an incredible cheese company on that is the only company in Aus that's allowed to make cheese from raw milk insanely delicious!

Some changes have made since returning home.
Recently bought some lights from chroma. mini portal and the d light. The d light hasn't been used much it's more of a supplement.
The mini portal is now used as the sun goes down and looking at purchasing a few other lights I discovered after the 2018 vermont video (which I finally got, Well Well worth it!!! Slides and explanations were very clear).
The goal is to sleep earlier and have no light however not quite there yet. We have been reading of an afternoon early evening instead of watching tv and enjoying spending real time together reading. I have the luxury of working from home most of the time so have been spending several hours outside every day.

No idea how knowing all this stuff for so long uncle Jack hasn't gone crazy. Hearing all these things makes me so angry but in a way that's almost indescribable.

Sometimes we're too close to something to see it's beauty.

Perception of time in the body. Parkinsons - moving at the wrong speed due to internal clocks being wrong.

How to find your primal sense may be my current favorite blog so far. And I'm going to pass it to a family member who is going through some mental health issues atm hopefully he can take it in. My wife and I believe it's perfect timing for him.

On a personal note i've been focusing on being kind and not being nice. Something i've found difficult previously always trying to help people even if they are on the titanic.

# Thread: WATER AND WBG SEMICONDUCTION BASICS in the living state.

### Jack Kruse — 2023-09-08T17:37:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-and-wbg-semiconduction-basics-in-the-living-state.28153/post-326132>

When K+ is low inside energy transduction goes down. When a lifeform dies 100% of water and K+ leaves the cell. When K+ leaves the cell so does light that is also stored at the electronic and vibrational level. This destroys the cell as a dissipative structure. When water is made in the mitochondria and leaches out redox power drops, energy drops, light is lost and information transfer and biochemical fidelity drop.

The change in K+ in cell water alters the hydrogen bonding network inside of a cell. How that bond angle change alters what is possible in a cell.

When you get extensive changes in the pattern of amino acids and lipids, it indicates that something has happened with the energy metabolism in the colony of mitochondria.

When K+ and water leak out, salt links to muscle proteins, actin, and myosin, because no energy is present with water's superconducting cables. (This explains how rigor mortis manifests). Mg2+ links up to salt linkages between the proteins to create rigidity. So Mg2+ is stuck in those bridges while K+ leaks out as redox power drops. Shows you why Mg2+ is not a major cation in energy transfers because it needs polarized water to create electrolytic flow inside of the cell. It is also why Mg2+ cannot do anything helpful without the power of other parts of the quantum mechanics of action.

Magnesium's most important role is as a divalent cation is to make ATP in as a co-factor.

### ND Hauf — 2023-09-09T22:43:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/contact-lens.28160/post-326186>

I don’t wear glasses or contacts but what I hear repeatedly on the forum is…….

The eye is an extension of the brain and a contact lens doesn’t allow for oxygen to flow.

The contact lens alters/doesn’t allow full spectrum light through the retinal pathway.

Contacts might dick with the shape of your eye…not too sure about that one I might be making it up but believe I heard it somewhere.

Full spectrum light can enter around outside of glasses if necessary to wear.

Shitty eyesight needs first 30 minutes of sun everyday no excuses. Yes it gets through clouds.

LASIK appears to suck hard on Gov. Monkey Pox balls…..might have an option crystal lens replacement or no replacement. I’m probably butchering this a little. Basically messing with your cones is no bueno….

The backcountry in your photo is one of my happy places when the light is optimal.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-11-10T21:41:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/contact-lens.28160/post-328142>

> [nevagator said:](/goto/post?id=328140)
>
> What’s NPC?

## [NPC](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NPC_\(meme\))​

Political Internet meme
The NPC, derived from non-player character, is a far-right Internet meme that represents people who do not think for themselves or do not make their own decisions; those who lack introspection or intrapersonal communication. It is often used to describe those who do not share conservative views—particularly liberals. The NPC meme, which graphically is based on the Wojak meme, was created in July 2016 by an anonymous author and first published on the imageboard 4chan, where the idea and inspiration behind the meme were introduced.

### JanSz — 2023-11-10T22:46:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/contact-lens.28160/post-328144>

The term “NPC” is an acronym for “Non-Player Character”. [It is used in the context of video games to refer to any character that is not controlled by a player or an AI](https://www.businessinsider.com/guides/tech/npc-meaning).
[These characters can play important roles in the game or simply populate the game’s world](https://www.businessinsider.com/guides/tech/npc-meaning).

The term “NPC” has also been used to create an internet meme known as the “NPC Wojak”. [This meme represents people who do not think for themselves or do not make their own decisions, symbolizing those who lack introspection or intrapersonal communication](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NPC_%28meme%29)[2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NPC_%28meme%29). [The meme, which originated from the imageboard 4chan, gained widespread attention during the presidency of Donald Trump](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NPC_%28meme%29).
