I am 56 born with very poor eyesight +20 contact lens script, glasses used to be very heavy & like milk bottle bottomed lenses.
I have worn high level gas permeable non UV protected lenses for 40 years. I eat primal, lift weights & see sunlight here in Australia a hell of a lot.

Def not NPC, I am probably more awake than most of you on here, you hear Dr Jack talk about deep state & military industrial complex, I have been looking down the deep dark hole since 1984, so have worked around the food system, medical system, education system, corporate system & government systems as best I can since then, the dark truth would put 98% of you in a psych ward.

My bloods & DNA methylation tests are solid. I am in superb physical condition, never get a cold or flu, never in hospital, not on an pharma dealer drugs or pills. & many people tell me I look more like 45 not 56 years.
Never jabbed with anything other than TB once as a kid.
So I am doing well despite the contacts on my eyes, if anything I am now increasing the sunlight I get all over me, not just sunrise every morning.
Currently I live in the southern half of Australia, Melbourne (lockdown city in 2021 CONVID) never ever wore a mask or complied to the Masonic & 13 bloodlines who captured this country. I am moving to Gold Coast, more sun & if the digital ID & CBDC looks like it will hit, watch out Jack, I will end up cashed up on our doorstep in El Sal.

We need Australians connecting & pushing the message out to fight or we are f\*cked, mind you a part of me feels Jack tells Australians we are screwed, because he really wants the sleepy sheep to wake the hell up.

### Robb Peterson — 2024-10-16T02:04:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/contact-lens.28160/post-341070>

I've been happily wearing Bosch & Lomb BioTrue OneDay lenses for years. Fantastic for computer work because I wear the OneDay Presbyopia lens in one eye for both near and far reading, and the regular OneDay lens in the other eye for distance. This is called Modified MonoVision and works great for many people. No more straining neck up and down with progressive lens glasses. Yet, they have UV filtering. I will look for another brand so that I can let in beneficial sun spectrums. Nothing adverse to report about lack of oxygen getting to my eye. Women often can't wear contacts comfortably post menopause because of dryness. LASIK is good if you're young, my eye doctor told me that people in their 50s+ should not do it because they will need readers eventually. If you do LASIK you may experience refraction stars when driving at night and looking at headlights and have dry eyes. That is my wife's experience with LASIK. Hope this helps.

### Pete Collins — 2024-10-16T06:19:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/contact-lens.28160/post-341077>

Thanks for the insights Robb, me I never have or will consider LASIK. I recall Dr Jack telling a story of a young woman suiciding post LASIK, she fell into a depression, I recall the original event years ago, I seem to recall a major factor in her depression, was because the treatment damaged the lenses in her eyes & she saw the world only as if she were looking through a window full of cracks & scratches.

### Steve Z — 2024-10-16T06:59:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/contact-lens.28160/post-341079>

> [Woodstock said:](/goto/post?id=341051)
>
> I have been really confused about the contacts issue. glad I found your post. I was thinking do I need LASIK then? I really don’t want someone cutting my eyes up. Intuition at least is right on that! A big NO! I am just struggling with my morning dog walks and wanting to see other dogs coming on the path so I need to be able to see! I guess glasses are the better option? And take them off as much as possible as the sun comes up? Eventually moving to a beach where the sun comes up on the ocean side and sphinx is obviously the ideal. Someday

Dr Kruse is not a fan of LASIK. They do not know where neuropsin (UV-A photorecptor) is located in the eye and could destroy that, causing collateral effects, including suicide. I had LASIK over 20 years ago, long before I realized that type of danger was associated with it.

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC7431709/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC7431709/)

### Jack Kruse — 2025-07-21T17:19:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/contact-lens.28160/post-356738>

New blog out on LASIK: [https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-62-130349379](https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-62-130349379)

Why is putting an excimer laser in the hands of a refractive eye surgeon like giving a serial killer a universal hotel room key?

Over 3.8 billion years, sunrise’s role in TCA/urea activation evolved with light, temperature and oxygen variations to optimize energy and repair, with thanatotranscriptomic genes adapting to these variables and these biological cycles. LASIK demoloshes this stiochiometry in nanoseconds.

Daytime TCA activity stores fat reserves, reducing sleep’s metabolic burden and enhancing repair (e.g., mitophagy).
Circadian Sync: Sunrise aligns melatonin suppression with TCA/urea cycle activation, ensuring deep sleep occurs at the end of a day that kills you slowly. It works this way so your colonies of mitochondria can recover every night to give you more time on Earth. If this fails you can die rapidly in ways you cannot fathom.

This blog tells you how the thanatotranscriptomic genes evolved and what their stiochiometry is. These genes were critical in evolving wakefulness from sleep. Enhancing AM light exposure (e.g., UV-mimicking therapies) amplifies TCA/urea benefits, complementing DDW production and ideal oxygen utilization to create precision in UPE output to extend life day after day. Temperature control is critically important to this thanatotranscriptomic method. This evidence is still seen in melatonin action today. It is maximally active in the brain after a 3-4 degree drop in temperature of the CSF. LASIK destroys this relationship.

### Base_Strength — 2025-07-28T04:11:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/contact-lens.28160/post-357058>

> [Woodstock said:](/goto/post?id=341051)

> I have been really confused about the contacts issue. glad I found your post. I was thinking do I need LASIK then? I really don’t want someone cutting my eyes up. Intuition at least is right on that! A big NO! I am just struggling with my morning dog walks and wanting to see other dogs coming on the path so I need to be able to see! I guess glasses are the better option? And take them off as much as possible as the sun comes up? Eventually moving to a beach where the sun comes up on the ocean side and sphinx is obviously the ideal. Someday!

> [Woodstock said:](/goto/post?id=341051)
>
> I have been really confused about the contacts issue. glad I found your post. I was thinking do I need LASIK then? I really don’t want someone cutting my eyes up. Intuition at least is right on that! A big NO! I am just struggling with my morning dog walks and wanting to see other dogs coming on the path so I need to be able to see! I guess glasses are the better option? And take them off as much as possible as the sun comes up? Eventually moving to a beach where the sun comes up on the ocean side and sphinx is obviously the ideal. Someday!

That's what I do. I switched from contacts to glasses and made sure they didn't coat them with UV protection. On my morning walks I spend first 10 to 15 minutes with no glasses and then take them off to admire the beauty. You still need to see!! I still wear contacts ever now and then and found some that don't have UV coating on them.

# Thread: Excercise = better mitochondria?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excercise-better-mitochondria.28161/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jeremiasbtome — 2023-09-10T18:59:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excercise-better-mitochondria.28161/post-326194>

Is it true that fitness Training will allow the mitos to become more resilient and adaptive and keep your body optimal?

### coho3131 — 2023-09-10T20:15:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excercise-better-mitochondria.28161/post-326200>

I think it depends on the person. There is not an answer to this question that will always be correct, because there are too many variables to consider.

### Dr. Marcus Ettinger — 2023-09-10T20:27:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excercise-better-mitochondria.28161/post-326202>

I have researched exercise extensively, and besides sunlight, nothing comes close to turning good things on and turning bad things off. This is what I have compiled so far. You will see that many of the below compounds are mitochondria-oriented.

-   ******What genes or compounds are upregulated or downregulated via exercise?******
    -   Exercise has been shown to upregulate multiple genes and compounds in the human body. Some of the genes and compounds that have been shown to be upregulated with exercise include:
        -   \[\[BDNF\]\] (Brain-derived neurotrophic factor)
        -   \[\[IL-6\]\] (Interleukin 6) - This inflammatory cytokine is downregulated by exercise.
        -   Lymphoid-derived \[\[IL-17\]\] - This inflammatory cytokine is downregulated by exercise.
        -   \[\[IL-10\]\] - This anti-inflammatory cytokine is upregulated by exercise.
        -   \[\[VEGF\]\] (Vascular Endothelial Growth Factor)
        -   NOS3 (Nitric oxide synthase 3) \[\[Nitric Oxide\]\]
        -   \[\[PGC-1α\]\] (Peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor γ coactivator 1-α)
        -   \[\[GLUT4\]\] (Glucose transporter 4)
        -   \[\[IGF-1\]\] (Insulin-like growth factor 1)
        -   HSP72 and HSP70 \[\[Heat Shock Protein\]\]
        -   \[\[SIRT1\]\] (Sirtuin 1) helps to regulate \[\[FOXO1\]\] and PGC-1a via CR and exercise.
        -   \[\[FOXO3a\]\] - which is a transcription factor that is involved in various cellular processes, including stress response, DNA repair, and apoptosis.
        -   \[\[AMPK\]\] - AMPK signaling pathway, which is involved in cellular energy homeostasis and stress response.
        -   \[\[Nrf2\]\] (nuclear factor erythroid 2-related factor 2) - which is a transcription factor that regulates the expression of genes involved in antioxidant defense and detoxification.
        -   \[\[SOD\]\] (Superoxide dismutase)
            -   [https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/j.1440-1681.1997.tb01196.x](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/j.1440-1681.1997.tb01196.x)
            -   [https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/0003986188906236?via=ihub](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/0003986188906236?via%3Dihub)
        -   \[\[GPx\]\] (Glutathione peroxidase)
            -   [https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/0003986188906236?via=ihub](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/0003986188906236?via%3Dihub)
        -   \[\[NAD+\]\] Nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide (Co-Enzyme)
        -   \[\[Nucleotide Excision Repair (NER) Pathway\]\]
        -   \[\[8-Oxoguanine (8-OG)\]\] - This biomarker of \[\[Oxidative Stress\]\] is downregulated by exercise.
        -   \[\[MOTS-c\]\] - a small mitochondrial-encoded peptide is upregulated by exercise.
        -   \[\[Humanin\]\] - This antiaging, mitochondria-derived peptides is upregulated by exercise.
        -   \[\[Histone Acetyltransferases (HATs)\]\] - Muscle regeneration and hypertrophy.
        -   \[\[GPLD1\]\] - GPLD1 can prevent or repair damage to the blood-brain barrier and improve cognitive function lost during aging.
        -   \[\[Sulfotransferase\]\] - Sulfation
        -   \[\[Vitamin D Receptor\]\] (VDR)
        -   \[\[Transforming growth factor-beta\]\] (TGF-β) - collagen support
        -   \[\[TP53 Gene\]\] - \[\[Cancer Suppression\]\] and \[\[Mitochondrial Biogenesis\]\]
        -   \[\[PTEN Gene\]\] - \[\[Cancer Suppression\]\]
        -   \[\[Sgk1 Enzyme\]\] - ion transport, cell survival, and inflammation
        -   Recombinant growth differentiating factor 15 \[\[GDF15\]\] - suppressing appetite, blocks the slowing of metabolism that occurs during dieting.
        -   \[\[Human Growth Hormone\]\] (see study below)
        -   Heme oxygenase-1 \[\[HO-1\]\]
    -   **Can exercise downregulate inflammatory cytokines?**
    -   Yes, exercise can have an impact on cytokine levels and help downregulate certain cytokines associated with inflammation. Regular exercise has been shown to exert anti-inflammatory effects and modulate the immune system. While the exact mechanisms are still being studied, here's how exercise can influence cytokine levels:
        -   Decreased Pro-inflammatory Cytokines: Exercise has been associated with a reduction in pro-inflammatory cytokines, such as TNF-α, IL-6, and IL-1. These cytokines are often elevated in chronic inflammatory conditions. Exercise-induced improvements in insulin sensitivity and reductions in adipose tissue contribute to the downregulation of pro-inflammatory cytokines.
        -   Increased Anti-inflammatory Cytokines: Exercise can stimulate the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines, such as IL-10. IL-10 helps regulate the immune response and has anti-inflammatory properties. Regular exercise can promote a balanced cytokine profile by increasing the production of these anti-inflammatory cytokines.
        -   Modulation of Cytokine Signaling: Exercise can influence cytokine signaling pathways and receptor expression, leading to alterations in cytokine responses. This modulation can contribute to a more controlled and balanced immune response.
    -   Exercise activates the cardiomyocyte dSir2/\[\[FOXO1\]\]/SOD and dSir2/FoxO/bmm pathways to delay cardiac aging. In Sestrins, Sesn1 is mainly expressed in muscle, where it is involved in the metabolic response to exercise and is associated with TORC2-Akt activity and \[\[PGC1α\]\]. In addition, skeletal muscle secretes myokines (ImpL2) that inhibit insulin signaling and promote mitochondrial autophagy. Exercise through apoLpp to regulate abnormal lipid metabolism, it also activates Nmnat/NAD+/dSir2 to resist lipotoxicity.

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

She also has high fasting insulin, and about 40# of body weight to lose. Losing her hairs tremendously.
\--------------------------------------------------------
The way we normally look at that,
other than sunlight and good sleep
she should
exercise and eat lots of fat and minimize carbohydrates.
Especially bad would be wheat and outright sugar.

\================================================
Now, I came across another opinion.
And the opinion is that the health problems people have is because of the way we eat fat.
We often eat about 30% fat and getting into problems.
Eating keto solves many of those problems.
But it looks like, many health problems could also be avoided if we would eat only 10% or less of fat.

Since I have learned that our farmed animals, birds, fish are source of extreme amounts of Omega-6 Linoleic acid, and therefore very bad, and that food called organic food is also really nothing to be happy about,
therefore, it maybe a good idea to investigate different magic option.
Carbosis.
More about it here:

,

[https://deniseminger.com/2015/10/06/in-defense-of-low-fat-a-call-for-some-evolution-of-thought-part-1/#swank](https://deniseminger.com/2015/10/06/in-defense-of-low-fat-a-call-for-some-evolution-of-thought-part-1/#swank)

### Dr. Marcus Ettinger — 2023-09-11T02:18:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excercise-better-mitochondria.28161/post-326216>

​

These are just some of the questions on my lifestyle questionnaire. I would need more information than the data you provided. That said, I have done close to 75 Dutch tests, maybe more, and I have never had that Oxstress marker come back elevated. I was always shocked to see it come back that way. Her's may be wrong or there is something really off with her that I would not ignore and get a deeper set of labs to find out what else is going on. Again, with the data from the questionnaire, it would give me clues of where to look and a direction to follow for further review.​

**Lifestyle Questionnaire**​

What is your career/job? \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_
What is your passion/favorite hobby and do you do it regularly?\_\_\_\_\_\_
What time do you get up in the morning? \_\_\_\_\_\_
What time do you go to bed at night? \_\_\_\_\_\_
Do you use electronics before bed? \_\_\_\_\_\_ Do you use a blue light filter/app? \_\_\_\_\_\_
On a stress-scale from 1-10 (10 most stressful) What is your stress level with everything combined (money, finance, boss, loved ones, friends, your personal health...)? \_\_\_\_\_\_
Do you work in an office or work outside? \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_
Do you use fluoride toothpaste and/or mouth rinse? \_\_\_\_\_\_
Have you ever had fluoride treatments/sealants on your teeth? \_\_\_\_\_\_
What type of water do you drink at home ( tap, bottled spring water, reverse osmosis, distilled)? Circle the answer(s) that apply
Do you eat seafood? \_\_\_\_\_\_ Circle the answer(s) that apply (shrimp, oysters, mussels, clams, scallops, salmon \[wild or farmed\], sardines).
On average, how many meals per month do you eat seafood? \_\_\_\_\_\_
How many meals do you cook at home each week? \_\_\_\_\_\_
Do you cook fresh food or prepared food (ex. boxed, canned, pre-packaged)?
Do you drink milk? \_\_\_\_\_\_ How often? \_\_\_\_\_\_
How many meals each week do you eat out? \_\_\_\_\_\_
Do you lift weights? \_\_\_\_\_\_
Do you jog or run? \_\_\_\_\_\_
Do you make a routine effort to go barefoot outside? \_\_\_\_\_\_
Do you make a routine effort to expose yourself to the sun? \_\_\_\_\_\_

# Thread: Plantar fasciitis from grounding?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plantar-fasciitis-from-grounding.28162/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 9

### NikkiYork — 2023-09-10T19:10:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plantar-fasciitis-from-grounding.28162/post-326195>

2 weeks ago I got a pea sized bump inside my foot under the sole where the arch part is. Couldn’t walk for three days, but it was burning man, so I toughened it out and couldn’t check the internet for diagnose.

Thought it might be a splinter, but no point of entry. Maybe a planter’s wart? Anyway, it went away. A friend said gout?

Anyways, it went away pretty much by itself.

Today I am waking up and the upper part right under the ball of my foot is swollen and hurts a lot, same type of pain, same foot, the left, different area, no bump, more dispersed, but hard-ish to the touch.

So it’s not a wart, not a splinter, since the first nodule went away, it’s not plantar fibroma. I am self diagnosing: plantar fasciitis.

I had a bout of that after carrying my kid on the left about 7 years ago and I have been doing stretching excercise since - every morning, because that stuff is really annoying to have.

What has changed? I have been actively grounding, and wearing my moccasins more. I love moccasins since I had my first pair as a child, so against all advices, I always come back to them.  you know, when you love something

I recently walked a lot on sand/beach, barefoot, and, like I said, without real sole, just leather under my feet (I’m taking out the moccasin insole).

People told me, “you are not a Native American, stop wearing these shoes, get an orthopedic insole”. I tried that too, but it doesn’t feel right to me. I have leather flip flops by vionic that are really arched. I don’t know, if that’s the right thing to do?

Anyways, the pain is really annoying. Like you want to walk on your knees.

Any suggestions or opinions on the matter? Thank you.

P.s.: I am in the sun as much as my life allows, something like 6-8 hours a day. I can practically not up that exposure.

I go to bed at 9 and sleep 8 hours.

We unplug all bedroom electricity over night. (And no, the bed situation is not ideal yet, I sleep better on a crappy air mat in a tent than on our mattress).

### JanSz — 2023-09-10T19:32:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plantar-fasciitis-from-grounding.28162/post-326196>

After you lay for couple hours in the bed, say sleeping,
then you plan to get up,
turn, still sitting on the bed,
**but feet not touching the floor.**
Now tell us what happens when you put your body weight on your feet.
If you got sharp pain that mostly went away some seconds later, and now it is dull itch,
you must have broken something that was broken before you went to bed
and healed somewhat overnight.
That was superficial healing that you interrupted when you put weight on your feet.
If you have
what I described
you have broken or greatly interrupted, some tendon.

...

### NikkiYork — 2023-09-10T21:33:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plantar-fasciitis-from-grounding.28162/post-326207>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=326197)
>
> Hi Nikki, with stuff like this (weird body things) my wife and I find our 850nm NIR red light takes care of it most the time. If it were me I would rest my foot on the light 2x day for 10-15 mins.

What a great idea, thanks Anthony.
I have a portable truelight that can do

Yellow (580) + red (660 and 630) + NIR (850), optional pulsing

Or no yellow added. Would you do with or without yellow? With or without pulsing?

I’ll try that immediately. Thank you.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-09-10T23:47:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plantar-fasciitis-from-grounding.28162/post-326209>

> [NikkiYork said:](/goto/post?id=326207)
>
> What a great idea, thanks Anthony.
> I have a portable truelight that can do
>
> Yellow (580) + red (660 and 630) + NIR (850), optional pulsing
>
> Or no yellow added. Would you do with or without yellow? With or without pulsing?
>
> I’ll try that immediately. Thank you.

It is my understanding red 660 & 630 are for surface skin issues very little penetration (1-3mm) but 850nm goes the deepest (2-3 inches). I have not used yellow or pulsing so I can’t say. Your issue seems to be deep so I would start with 850 THEN 850 pulse and see which gets the best results. Then add 660 and 630 try again, then yellow, they may add something. Again my understanding optimal results 2x/day for 10-15 mins each. Let me know.

### JanSz — 2023-09-11T01:09:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plantar-fasciitis-from-grounding.28162/post-326212>

> [NikkiYork said:](/goto/post?id=326205)
>
> That’s great info, Jan. Getting out of bed most certainly is the toughest time of the day for me, but I’ve been doing better and better with it, so I don’t think it’s micro injury in this case. I’ll look out for it on other occasions though.

I was not really talking about how tough it is getting of the bed.
I was trying to figure out if you feel breaking something in the morning that somewhat healed over night.

If that is actually happening to you
there is an idea of preventing that from happening.
You would have to position your foot for overnight sleeping.
Position your foot in stretched position, such as if you were standing.
So, in the morning you would not have to go thru this breaking event.
That would give your foot chance to heal.
**You would have to sleep that way for 2-3 weeks.**
After that 2-3 weeks would come time to face the exam.
Sllep without that brace, step on the foot, see if it hurts.
If it does, start again but do it longer.

There are all kind of braces you can get to position your foot for the night time in bed.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-09-11T03:58:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-defense-of-low-fat.28163/post-326220>

Denise Minger in the video JanSz posted had some fairly conclusive data that showed good, long term health benefits with people, even diabetics on a high carb low (<10%) fat diets. I was very surprised. One important fact is that back in the day low fat was below 10%, today low fat is below 30%. She showed high carb with 30%+ fat had very poor result but when fat was below 10% very good results.

### Sue-UK — 2023-09-12T14:38:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-defense-of-low-fat.28163/post-326276>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=326208)
>
> [View attachment 25169](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/25169/)

I haven't tested for ApoE4, because not being ApoE4 doesn't mean no risk of dementia, and is less protective for me because of my maternal mitochondrial inheritance (mother and grandmother had dementia), an inheritance I've passed on to my children.

A chronic 65 per cent fat diet and going into ketosis at night giving chronically low insulin levels may miss the benefits of the insulin degrading enzyme. If insulin is chronically high it doesn't do its secondary role of degrading amyloid, but my concern is that if insulin is chronically low it wouldn't be triggered, so again wouldn't degrade amyloid. Ketones are uncouplers, and I don't think we are meant to be permanently uncoupled. Historically, at my latitude in spring as light levels start increasing meat would be lean and with available greens and roots a less than 10 per cent fat would be dictated by the environment. But that's an inland mammal. It would be easy to go over 10 per cent fat with seafood, so the < 10 per cent fat benefit doesn't include that nuance. Both Dale Bredesen (The End of Alzheimers) and William Li (Eat to beat disease and Eat to beat your diet) includes seafood, and seafood and olive oil features in the Mediterranean diet which was trialled on firefighters on shift work. That and the blue zone centenarian studies show that even the dietary "swamp" zone is nuanced by the types of fat, not just the amount. The Dale Bredesen book doesn't involve light water and magnetism etc, doesn't exclude saturated fat completely, is full of testing and supplementing info, but the biggest take I got from it was about the insulin degrading hormone. In line with the firefighter study, he recommends a 10 hour eating window for ApoE4 carriers. He gets results with patients using his protocols, but its whether his protocols and supplements have to be a permanent lifestyle. The let down was him saying that the aluminum theory of AD has never been substantiated, when I think there are enough studies that implicate it as a causal factor, and one that with what Jack has said about aluminum's UV reflecting properties (vitamin D? ROS? Endogenous UV signalling?) shouldn't be ignored, particularly as its easy to do an audit of current and historical exposure and get it out of the body.

### JanSz — 2023-09-12T17:25:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-defense-of-low-fat.28163/post-326284>

> [Anne V said:](/goto/post?id=326275)
>
> What carbs are you eating in winter/spring at 50N lat and above?

Anne
You had a heart surgery. You must be very low in Nitric Oxide.
I think that should be your priority.
This is really about endothelial surfaces anywhere in cardiovascular system.
When one has sufficient Nitric Oxide and do not see expected results,
there may be another problem.
glycocalyx (signaling structure)
It sits on top of all endothelial surfaces.
It may have a problem with telling Nitric Oxide (dissolved in the blood) to go into gas phase and stretch out the veins or arteries to allow optimized blood flow.
I am not there yet.

But right away one can say that everywhere that plaque deposits are already formed on endothelial surfaces, the Nitric Oxide is not going to be able to listen to any glycocalix requests.

I am looking for ways to wrap plugged in electrical cords in some shielding material, to lower the EMF they produce. I haven't found an existing thread about it. [@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/) and [@ShaneR](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26002/) , do you have any recommendations? Thank you!

### JanSz — 2023-09-11T19:36:51+0300

### [MC Cable | WireAndCableYourWay.com](https://www.wireandcableyourway.com/mc-cable)

 www.wireandcableyourway.com

### Anthony Smith — 2023-09-11T22:18:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electrical-cord-shielding.28165/post-326256>

> [[email redacted] said:](/goto/post?id=326230)
>
> I am looking for ways to wrap plugged in electrical cords in some shielding material, to lower the EMF they produce. I haven't found an existing thread about it. [@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/) and [@ShaneR](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26002/) , do you have any recommendations? Thank you!

You can buy shielded electrical cords or make your own, as people here have stated. You can buy sleeves to put over regular cords. Metal Clad (MC) cable works be being grounded. The electrical field follows the path of least resistance to ground, that’s how MC cable works. Measure to know that whatever you do/try has worked. You can also put up a grounded metallic shield behind your desk with all the cords behind it and have the same results. You can also turn off power in your bedroom at night and not worry about it. If you don’t have a way to measure get a BVK here: [www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

### ShaneR — 2023-09-11T23:33:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electrical-cord-shielding.28165/post-326259>

Good stuff so far. I'll add the following:
1\. Always verify before and after with a meter capable of measuring AC electric fields (my preferred way), or body voltage. It is very easy to create a ground loop and make the problem worse.
2\. If it is a device power cord like a monitor or computer, with the standardized female end, you can swap [**one of these**](https://youtu.be/qrJpi60bIes?si=vyuNtnAFDAKAyrKt) in and reduce the E-field.
3\. [**This**](https://www.electrahealth.com/safe-wrap--electric-field-emf-guard-for-cords-and-cables-by-electrahealth-research-institute.html?aff=16) is newer on the market but is less bulky than MC cable, fitting over the existing cable.
4\. Don't forget to verify with a meter!!!

Moreover, "it appears" these shots will be the first licensed product vials to hit the shelves. The FDA told these companies in JUNE to make a shot, and they did in less than 60 days. However, despite 361,000,000 potential contraindications between the two drugs and 19,000 other licensed drugs, the FDA actually licensed them for commercial marketing, which is why they will not let them outside of the PREP Act's absolute immunity.

NURSES, all seasonal influenza shots are also under the PREP Act for this year, which means you are not required to take the shot under a mandate. However, the HHS Sec has brought ALL child vaccines under the PREP Act to provide absolute immunity for all damages, bringing into question the vaccine injury fund and how it might not be available to new victims. HHS ALSO is providing PREP Act immunity for ALL seasonal influenza "vaccines," which means that no one can be required to participate, and I am releasing a video explaining the legal reason why.

AMERICA - EVERY child vaccine administered this year is under absolute immunity, and every seasonal influenza vaccine is under absolute immunity. Additionally, many masks, testing articles, and protective equipment are still under EUA and the PREP Act.

**Here is what is covered:** Any antiviral, any drug, any biologic, any diagnostic, any other device, any respiratory protective device, or any vaccine manufactured, used, designed, developed, modified, licensed, or procured: To diagnose, mitigate, prevent, treat, or cure COVID–19, or the transmission of SARS–CoV–2 or a virus mutating therefrom; or to limit the harm that COVID–19, or the transmission of SARS–CoV–2 or a virus mutating therefrom, might otherwise cause; (b) a product manufactured, used, designed, developed, modified, licensed, or procured to diagnose, mitigate, prevent, treat, or cure a serious or life-threatening disease or condition caused by a product described in paragraph (a) above; (c) a product or technology intended to enhance the use or effect of a product described in paragraph (a) or (b) above; or (d) any device used in the administration of any such product, and all components and constituent materials of any such product.

**WAIT, you're not ready for this:** HHS Sec extended the disease categories under the PREP Act, which could be 100% of all diseases and medical treatments. "The category of disease, health condition, or threat for which I recommend the administration or use of the Covered Countermeasures is not only COVID–19 caused by SARS-–CoV– 2, or a virus mutating therefrom, but also other diseases, health conditions, or threats that may have been caused by COVID–19, SARS–CoV–2, or a virus mutating therefrom, including the threat of increased burden on the healthcare system due to seasonal influenza infections occurring at the same time as COVID–19 infections, which will lead to an increase in the rate of infectious diseases."

In other words, if you go to the hospital due to an auto accident and the hospital uses a respirator that is under the PREP Act in a manner causing you significant harm, the hospital can claim PREP Act immunity because they were under an "increased burden" to attend to all the other patients in the hospital for COVID-19 even though you did not go to the hospital for COVID-19 treatment. This means going to any hospital is one of the most dangerous things one can do because of this law.

The PREP Act is the most unconstitutional piece of legislation ever to come out of Congress. Don't believe me? Congress wrote into the PREP Act that the judicial branch of government can not issue a writ of mandamus nor review the legality of the HHS Sec's actions. In other words, the legislative branch provided one person with god-like authority that they said that no person in America can touch including the President or the Supreme Court. An authority overriding the U.S. Constitution, state's sovereignty, and our Due Process rights. No person can require any other person to participate in a "covered countermeasure" under PREP Act immunity and I intend to get that ruling from nations courts around the world. Hopefully, this can make the Supreme Court Act shut down the criminals in Congress who are using medical tyranny to grab power over the people.

This is why medical freedom laws are mandatory in the new world order being built by the criminals in Washington DC. The fact that they are creating immunity from the judicial branch tells you all you need to know. Your duty is below.

### Jack Kruse — 2023-09-13T17:55:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-2023-assault-from-the-government-linked-to-medical-tyranny.28168/post-326345>

There is one solution. Medical freedom laws separate medicine & state.

YOU HAVE NO IDEA WHAT IS COMING. Benjamin Rush did. Section 215 of the Patriot Act breaks the Constitutional 4th Amendment and that amendment is the key to the Deep State's real plan of overturning the key parts of the Constitution that gives the shadow government complete control of the USA below your level to see it. It is also why they fought so vehemently against the three SCOTUS nominees under Trump. Both of them defend the 4th Amendment in a big way. This should explain to you why domestic politics has gotten so nasty. The inclusion of medical records was highlighted by a reporter, Joy Buchanan from the Daily Press in Newport News, Virginia, who was alerted to a clause in one medical center's brochure for patients on privacy. The brochure stated that the center “may disclose medical information about you to authorized federal officials so they may without limitation (i) provide protection to the President, other authorized persons or foreign heads of state or conduct special investigations, or (ii) conduct lawful intelligence, counter-intelligence, or other national security activities authorized by law.”

Ms. Buchanan reported the story in the Daily Press on 29 December (sect A: 1). Subsequent investigations have shown that virtually all medical centers have incorporated the same or similar language in their brochures. Under the Health Insurance Portability and Accounting Act, patients must be told that their records are private and about any exceptions to that rule. The statements are often a single paragraph in documents several pages long, which are given to patients before they see a doctor. Michael Williams, associate professor of neurology and neurosurgery at Johns Hopkins University, Baltimore, said most people in the United States don't seem to know about Section 215. “I only found out about it in June 2005 at a meeting of the American Medical Association. If patients knew about this, I think they would be bothered—or I hope they would be,” he said. “If our records can be seized with no safeguards then we're all in trouble.”

You still don't understand what COVID was really about. The Prep Act of 2023 is another domino effect of the Patriot Act.

### Jack Kruse — 2023-09-13T18:02:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-2023-assault-from-the-government-linked-to-medical-tyranny.28168/post-326347>

Public Readiness and Emergency Preparedness (PREP) Act

The PREP Act was enacted on December 30, 2005, as Public Law 109– 148, Division C, § 2. It amended the Public Health Service (PHS) Act, adding section 319F–3, which addresses liability immunity, and section 319F–4, which creates a compensation program. These sections are codified at 42 U.S.C. 247d–6d and 42 U.S.C.

The Supreme Court has held that the use of force by police and correctional officers violates the Fourth Amendment when it is “excessive.” Police and correctional officers receive qualified immunity if it isn't clearly established that their use of force was excessive.

The PREP Act Declaration amendments preempt requirements that would result in a qualified person being unable to prescribe, dispense, or administer vaccines as authorized by the state or U.S. territory. Licensing laws that are less restrictive than those in the Declaration amendments are not preempted.

What are the exceptions to the PREP Act?

The sole exception to PREP Act immunity is for death or serious physical injury caused by “willful misconduct.” However, individuals who die or suffer serious injuries directly caused by the administration of covered countermeasures may be eligible to receive compensation through the Countermeasures Injury Compensation ...

The declaration provides immunity from liability (except for willful misconduct) for claims: of loss caused, arising out of, relating to, or resulting from the administration or use of countermeasures to diseases, threats, and conditions.

Waiving Immunity

In rare cases, a former witness can waive immunity after receiving it. This allows the prosecution to bring charges against them based on their testimony. The waiver can be explicit, which means that the witness signs a written waiver.

The declaration provides immunity from liability (except for willful misconduct) for claims of loss caused by, arising out of, relating to, or resulting from the administration or use of covered countermeasures to diseases, threats, and conditions identified in the declaration.

For countermeasures falling under Section VII(a)—those relating to federal contracts or other federal agreements—PREP Act immunity generally extends through December 31, 2024. The same rule applies to Section VII(d) and VII(e) countermeasures—the two new categories added in the Eleventh Amendment.

[https://www.phe.gov/emergency/events/COVID19/COVIDVaccinators/Pages/PREP-Act-Immunity-from-Liability-for-COVID-19-Vaccinators.aspx#:~:text=The%20declaration%20provides%20immunity%20from,conditions%20identified%20in%20the%20declaration.](https://www.phe.gov/emergency/events/COVID19/COVIDVaccinators/Pages/PREP-Act-Immunity-from-Liability-for-COVID-19-Vaccinators.aspx#:~:text=The%20declaration%20provides%20immunity%20from,conditions%20identified%20in%20the%20declaration.)

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/intracutaneous-methylene-blue-for-severe-pain-in-shingles.28169/post-326318>

Is this worth a try for severe shingles pain (post herpetic neuralgia) that is resistant to gabapentin and tramadol?

### [\[Efficacy of intracutaneous methylene blue injection for moderate to severe acute thoracic herpes zoster pain and prevention of postherpetic neuralgia in elderly patients\] - PubMed](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27777201/)

Intradermal injection of methylene blue can effectively shorten the disease course, reduce the pain intensity and prevent the development of postherpetic neuralgia in elderly patients with herpes zoster.

 pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

Eggs
even chicken or turkey should be acceptable if you meticulously get rid of fat and use only muscles and bones.

Overall, human body has two (good) magic areas as far as food content.
Less that 10% of fat in food
More than 65% fat in food
but when we eat in between we develop problems. Hello high fasting insulin.
Most diets are 30% fat, and that causes problems.

### Marko Pollo — 2023-09-16T23:35:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-does-it-take-for-vitamin-d-levels-to-go-up-naturally.28177/post-326500>

I was in Ecuador in 2019 with emf meters in hand, doing ct pretty often, wearing shorts and a tankini shirt. UVI 15-17 was normal at high altitude. Got a test after 3 months and it was up but still low. When covid happened we stayed in Colombia above 10000ft. Again UVI of 15-17. We were locked down and I couldn't really leave the property. I watched every sunrise in my tankini shorts and spent almost every minute outside gardening and sunning for 3 months Watched the sun set from my bed through a screen. I don't have the numbers, but it went up into a good range.
When I first started this I got a great tan but then developed big white spots. They all went away after maybe 6 months of sunning.

If it was me, I would just focus on making as many healthy choices as possible.
If you are doing everything that you possibly can, I am not sure what good knowing your level would do anyway.
If it's high, you might lose motivation to keep improving, and if it is low, you might get angry, frustrated, stressed, anxious, or depressed.
I think higher stress levels and mental health symptoms are devastating to our health.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-does-it-take-for-vitamin-d-levels-to-go-up-naturally.28177/post-326516>

> [coho3131 said:](/goto/post?id=326506)
>
> I think worrying about vitamin D levels decreases your redox.
> If it was me, I would just focus on making as many healthy choices as possible.
> If you are doing everything that you possibly can, I am not sure what good knowing your level would do anyway.
> If it's high, you might lose motivation to keep improving, and if it is low, you might get angry, frustrated, stressed, anxious, or depressed.
> I think higher stress levels and mental health symptoms are devastating to our health.

I’m a machine when it comes to motivation and I’m not worried. It’s probably the hun in me, because if I set my mind to something, I don’t stop. And the whole light affair only makes sense.

I’ve been making “healthy choices” for years, but I don’t think they were healthy, if that makes sense. I’ve listened to experts and even paid them money, but I didn’t trust any of it, because all the advice seemed random. I just didn’t have any better idea, so I might as well try things.

Dr. Kruse is finally somebody who makes sense and even if not everything turns out to be right, the level of coherency is great. The logic is there, so I’m not worried at all.

What good does knowing a level do? The answer is: numbers.  measuring stuff is cool in itself. Comparing numbers and documenting, it’s interesting.

I have about 200 orchids and I just watch them, that’s all I do. I learn so much from these aliens, it’s awesome. There is no importance in it though.

Numbers are a form of observation. Like I measure my son. I’m not worried that he’s not growing. I still track it.

If I had infinite amounts of money, I would measure and test everything and track and document it. (Though I mostly skip documenting disease, because I don’t need the memory). I would hire people to do it.

I have also started conducting experiments, mostly with, for and by myself, from early on, like 8 or so. I do things like that, because it’s… interesting.

And because I radically don’t believe anything that I haven’t experienced myself. I can work with assumptions to not seem too weird, but I’m well aware that’s what I do, assuming.

Thank you for your empathy, I think I understand what you mean.

Hi Nikki, just another number here. Before discovering Dr Jack, i supplemented daily 2k UI and got up levels from 30ng to 40ng. Let's say one year not completely regularly but more or less.

I have tried to "overdose" around 10-15k UI per day for 6 months, but miniscule to no changes.

All those 18 months i have been moderately outside, but not done any specific effort.

For the last 6 months i have been proactively seeking to be outside and as naked as possible without supplementation at all. Not measured changes yet. All in the same environment. Will do a test in 2-3 weeks out of curiousity to see if there are any changes. Got a nice good looking tan now.

Not the data point you asked for, but it is a data point at least .

I’ve tried to figure out the VanAllenbelt
situation.

From what I got, there are two “natural”? Ones and then third ones pop up here and there man-made and vanish.

I believe that above LA is more complex than that. And the term van Allen belt would not hit it. It probably doesn’t have a name. Possibly this, whatever it is, will surround the whole planet soon. I sure hope not. Alas, it’s been done twice I guess.

And I would go further than VanAllen belt-like, because we are dealing with a multitude of phenomena that are combined in that region, that don’t have names.

If we observe the sky, we have new types of clouds, new types of what is called marine layers that are not marine layers, new types of rainbow type occurrences, as halos as fractured rainbows, as really funky light spectacles… we no longer have thunder and lightning like we used to…

We have all the rockets starting from here, we have the radio waves, we have 5g, radioactive whatever it is, we have laser emitting diodes galore, we have rapidly changing environmental factors of many more facets, example non-permeable ground coverage, large burned surfaces. Soil that does not harbor microorganisms…

So where is that butterfly when you need it?

Did you know that I live in a place that for 8 years now, the Department of water and electricity of Los Angeles has not been able to provide first world standard electricity to?

You didn’t know that, right? I cannot bake anything in our oven at night, no electricity. I cannot have a washing machine. My house runs on 15 amp which is less than an Rv in an rv park. Those get 50amp.

We also have almost no cell phone reception here and 4 years ago spectrum had to lay a cable so that we could get internet.

I can’t say I mind it  I think the little we have is still too much. I also am quite aware why I am living here and not anywhere else. I have moved 26 times by my own volition as an adult. I’m not scared of moving

I’ve had plans and visions all my live and it’s worked out very well so far. I am aware that I live in a dirt hole, it’s calculated - and not with hubris, which really is hard to convey via online.

### JanSz — 2023-09-19T00:48:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-does-it-take-for-vitamin-d-levels-to-go-up-naturally.28177/post-326577>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=326572)
>
> A comment was made above about not trusting centralized testing. I was **supplementing VIT D** during winter 5000 sometimes 10,000/day and had blood work done. My D was 140+! A question to all the testers here, how long should one stop supplementing anything before getting tested for the results to be realistic?

anytime you take a vitamin D supplement, you also need to be mindful of taking extra vitamin K2 and magnesium.

The K2 is needed to prevent arterial calcification **while magnesium is required for vitamin D conversion.** If you take large doses of vitamin D, you may inadvertently deplete magnesium, as it’s required in the conversion of vitamin D into its active form.

Research26 has shown you need 146% more vitamin D to achieve a blood level of 40 ng/ml (100 nmol/L) if you do not take supplemental magnesium, compared to taking your vitamin D with at least 400 mg of magnesium per day.

[@NikkiYork](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/28233/)
**What is your magnesium status?**

I changed my diet and spent as much time outside as I could (seasonal dependent). This is all before knowing about Dr Kruse. I ate alot of shellfish, home made curries (shout to Chef Jet Tila), and dairy (raw, local milk, cheeses, Greek God full fat yogurt with local blueberries, Skyr, Redhill or Redwood Farm Goat Kefir, local Portuguese sausages that have the lungs and such) as well as beef and some organ meats. Not much in the veggie department unless a neighbor gifted stuff from their garden). I was not sun bathing. I was walking early am sunrise with the dog and sunsets in the evening. My face was red/tan (did not peel), most of me pale. The rest of my skin exposure was season dependent. Additionally I was supplementing magnesium and L-theanine at night and two tsp of Black Seed Oil a day (I use it swish my mouth and gargle after teeth brushing)

Vit D.25-OH, Total (ng/ml): 24 -> 64
Triglycerides (mg/dl): 164 -> 91 (Below 100 indicates weight loss from previous test, I lost 29 lbs)
Cholesterol, Tot (mg/dl): 242 -> 194
HDL (mg/dl): 47 -> 41
LDL (calc) (mg/dl): 162 -> 136

I imagine if I took the test again in Dec, Vit D number would be lower. I have to bump it up as high as possible before the fall/winter season. Which reminds I have to go buy a dozen oysters

**Magnetic Thinning:** A strong DC magnetic field helps maintain the **Exclusion Zone (EZ)** in the water lattice even in the dark. It keeps the metabolic water "thin" and "fluid," preventing the 1:96 deuterium ratio from seizing the engine while you sleep.

**4\. Haplotype "Compensation"**

**Yellow/Blue Haplotypes (Northern):** The pad augments your **UCP (Uncoupling Protein)** light show, providing more "torque" for the Woodward Effect to clear the "tritium shrapnel."

**The Decentralized Mechanic's Protocol for the Pad:**

# Thread: Invisalign vs metal braces?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/invisalign-vs-metal-braces.28179/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### airplaneman — 2023-09-15T23:30:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/invisalign-vs-metal-braces.28179/post-326454>

What are the thoughts on these options for orthodontics? Other threads have mentioned metal braces come the side effects of jump conducting nnemfs and other stuff in QE48, this sounds especially bad in a major city. Is there anything that can be done to mitigate this? Alternatively, invisalign might have some plastics toxicity or something else I am not aware of? I'm also interested in alternatives to the alternatives if anyone has info or experience. Thanks for your inputs!

### coho3131 — 2023-09-15T23:48:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/invisalign-vs-metal-braces.28179/post-326456>

I would consider avoiding braces unless the benefits outweigh the risks for you as an individual. Having perfectly straight teeth is not required for good health. Orthodontists make a lot of money on braces, so they are interested in everyone having them. You might be able to consider just having a retainer made instead of full braces. With just a retainer, you could wear it whenever you wanted, and in locations where nnEMF is lower. Finding an orthodontist that would only do a retainer might be challenging; I am guessing that many of the younger doctors might not want you as a patient, but an old school orthodontist who has more wisdom might be perfect for you. Good luck!

### JanSz — 2023-09-16T00:53:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/invisalign-vs-metal-braces.28179/post-326459>

There are bateria in the mouth that dentist insists you get rid off.
Do not listen to that "advice".
It is good for dentist not for you.

In a recent podcast jack you said you should not do intense excercise if you have a mitochondrial disease.

With me having tourettes/ ocd and myopia would you say its fine for me doing bodyweight exercise/ weights and sprints?

### Winona — 2023-09-17T01:03:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dont-excercise-when-you-have-a-disease.28181/post-326503>

Just my opinion, my daughter also has tourettes and possibly mito disfunction not disease, she doesn’t weights/low impact exercise and it makes her feel amazing but she feels awful after any high intensity cardio. I think exercise is really important for people with tourettes and the comorbidities, especially for your mental health.

### John Warner — 2023-09-17T18:21:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dont-excercise-when-you-have-a-disease.28181/post-326527>

One should move their body in nature, the stress level depends on the person, one should be evaluated or have an awareness of their body where they can evaluate their outcomes. Movnat starts on the ground with basics, one can build on the success at their own pace. No need for weights or a gym, body weight is plenty.

### Ryan Carter — 2023-09-17T21:32:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dont-excercise-when-you-have-a-disease.28181/post-326531>

I believe stimulating mito biogenesis in poor fragile mitochondria / with impaired free radical signaling is a recipe for issues down the line, but also the signals to the brain and the demands to adapt with everything else.

I am sure bodyweight calisthenics (grounding basically with nature) does no harm and adds value. [@Skip Platt](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/21979/) is impressive here.

My background is from 55kg to 97 kg with, being leaner and more vital than ever - it's easy to do when you get Dr. Kruse's principles and dial in the environment. It only clicked when leaving the UK and being in Central America for the last 3 years. The results are outstanding. I eat maybe 1500-2000 calories if I am bothered to count - not even hungry that much. That's the grounding/light/darkness effect here.

All plants proliferate here in Nicaragua.
Do you think humans replicate this? Yes. A friend joined me from the UK for 3 months and put on 5 kilograms of muscle outside / grounded/trained with me / fuelled on ceviche + plantains + eggs.

Sort out the foundations, then worry about the cherry on the cake.

### JanSz — 2023-10-20T18:23:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1-1-to-4-1-la-ala-may-be-beneficial-for-health.28188/post-327447>

Some of the most damaging components in our modern diet are processed vegetable oils, as they contain excessive amounts of oxidized omega-6 linoleic acid, a polyunsaturated fat (PUFA).
The biological damage they cause is even worse than that caused by refined sugar and high fructose corn syrup.

The additional problem is that the farmed animals, birds and fish are fed with insane amounts of seed oils.

It would be of service to our friends to spread that news wildly.

**Linoleic Acid is screwing so many things in our life.**
At this time, it should be safe to assume that 50 to 75% of our problems are due to it.
The other major is lack of **Nitric Oxide**, creating another horrifying thing to our cardiovascular system.
Even worst, LA's half-life is two years.
It takes dedication and very long time to get rid of it, especially when, most of us has an 50x excess or more.
Even if you are persistent and lucky, on the end of first year expect to still have 25x excess, 12.5 on the end of second year.
So, do not even think of not having enough of it. Especially that whatever you eat, contains LA.

While LA is considered to be an essential fatty acid and support health when consumed in modest amounts,
an excessive intake of LA leads to the formation of oxidized linoleic acid metabolites (OXLAMs).
They impair mitochondrial functioning.

\==============================

### JanSz — 2023-10-21T04:13:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1-1-to-4-1-la-ala-may-be-beneficial-for-health.28188/post-327455>

Eating a high-fat diet, when the fats are primarily LA from processed vegetable and seed oils, is far worse than eating a chronic high-carb diet. The type of fat is of crucial importance, as it impacts your mitochondrial, cellular and metabolic functioning.

Fats like LA can persist in your cell membranes for months to years, continuing to wreak havoc with your metabolism the entire time, while the sugar is quickly metabolized.

Aside from that, this also helps explain why processed foods are so fattening. It's not just that they're loaded with added sugars. They're also loaded with LA that breaks your metabolic machinery and promotes fat accumulation and inflammation.

• CDC (Centers for Disease Control and Prevention)

### Jack Kruse — 2023-09-21T15:59:09+0300

**The COVID-19 vaccines were the largest human experiment ever done in the history of mankind.
People who took the vaccines for the first time took a genetic injection of foreign genetic material that produced in their body for an uncontrolled duration \[of\] time and quantity, the Wuhan Spike protein, the protein on the surface of the virus that causes so much damage in the human body**
Many of you still have the Spike protein in your cells and your tissues. Every study that’s looked at this issue has actually identified this central issue in the cause of damage. It mimics a foreign body reaction. The human body does not seem to have enzymes that can break down this protein like it could any other natural protein and have us get rid of it.

**It’s because this protein is not natural because the mRNA was engineered in China by Dazek and paid for by Fauci and the industrial military complex program that was transfered from Fort Deitrich to Fauci's control at NAIAD.
Never forget It was engineered in a Chinese biosecurity lab using blueprints that came from U.S. researchers fully funded and supported by the National Institutes of Health and the National Allergy Immunology branch run by Dr. Anthony Fauci**

The vaccines have left about **15% of those who’ve taken them with some form of a medical problem**. In clinical medicine this is a huge number that should have governments calling for recalls and bans. Instead the vaccine machine inside of HHS is pushing more boosters for Americans.

About a third of people who took the vaccine; they’ve had no side effects whatsoever, and they appear to be exhibiting no side effects emerging. So, if one took the shot and they’re perfectly fine, didn’t even have a sore arm, they’re extremely unlikely now to develop a problem.

But it’s people who developed an initial reaction and were sick for a few days or weeks afterwards — I really worry about them because we are seeing the late development of myocarditis and cardiac arrest **even two years after the vaccine**. Blood clots developing **even two years after the vaccine**. In fact, another episode of COVID could provoke these.

**_How to deal with the spike protein?_**

Improve your solar redox first to upregulate immune function via melanin & alpha MSH to clear the protein disassembled proteins. Then proceded to the detox step.

The three natural substances, **nattokinase, bromelain, and curcumin are the key part of the clearance protocol for the spike protein.**

1\. [https://www.jpands.org/vol28no3/mccullough.pdf](https://www.jpands.org/vol28no3/mccullough.pdf)

This was the very first [detoxification protocol](https://www.jpands.org/vol28no3/mccullough.pdf) published in the U.S. medical literature. It can be found in the Journal of American Physicians and Surgeons.

Despite a billion-dollar investment in long COVID research by the Biden administration, not a single dollar of funding has been directed toward researching vaccine injuries. So, it won’t be the government but dissenting doctors who will be leading the way in discovering optimal treatments and management for vaccine injuries.

# Thread: Any Dog Health Experts Here??
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-dog-health-experts-here.28194/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Neurotic Farmer — 2023-09-21T20:24:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-dog-health-experts-here.28194/post-326662>

Hypocholesterolemia in my puppy-daughter. Not sure how old she is as we adopted her 5 years ago. We assume about 10 now. She's a shih-tzu.
Historically has eaten a raw beef/organ diet; however, recently she started uncontrollably itching. Read that the fat from animals can be bothersome for some dogs (not sure why it would just show up now), so we switched her about a month ago to a predominantly fish diet with white fish, salmon, green mussels, and homemade fish stock. Itching is probably 85% better.
She has lost over 1lb in the process. She's just under 9lbs now which feels really small. Did some bloodwork because we weren't expecting that and it, overall, looks good; however, her cholesterol came back "a little low".
That seems worrisome to me, for a number of reasons, but especially because she's been eating plenty of cholesterol since we adopted her. Could this be a poor light environment? nnEMF exposure?
Any thoughts would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance,
Courtney

### Anthony Smith — 2023-09-21T22:04:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-dog-health-experts-here.28194/post-326664>

I have a little experience with animals having had horses, donkeys, goats, chickens, rabbits, cats, and lots of dogs, also my wife was a groomer for a while. We found lamb was best with the dogs with issues (didn’t try fish). As far as nnEMF being involved, test where the dog hangs out/sleeps and see what readings you get. I have cleaned up a few homes and the pets responded as well as the people. Dogs sleep area should be as clean as yours, 10mV - 100mV if possible with a body voltage meter.

I do not know about Old-Goats.
I had terrible back pains, since 2-3 years before I came to USA in 1971.
So. I have always been looking for this type of solutions.
Was almost ready for spine surgery when I learned about chiropractors.
No more back pains (crossed fingers), knock on wood.
Later, I had to move and find another chiropractor.
Different story.
To make it short, use only chiropractors who use hands only (no machines of any kind).
Years latter
my wife is dying in great pains.
Fentanyl
Morphine
Lidocaine

Tested by my dying wife:
After Fentanyl, Morphine and something else
she is still in pain.

Doctors as usual always are trying to help.
So, I got prescription for **5%** Lidocaine.
In Walgreens, Pharmacist (my neighbor) tells me:
That will cost you $350 and waiting two weeks.
But there is 4% Lidocaine and almost free.
So you can get it in pharmacy, no need for script.
Or Amazon.

Make sure to use rubber gloves applying it.
Do not use any patches that contain lidocaine, they are falling off.

Moreover, these ideas suggest that antisense RNA transcription inhibits sense expression of DNA using light and dark by mediating chromatin modifications and premature transcription termination.
