### Anthony Smith — 2023-10-01T22:32:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cell-tower-4g-5g-education.28205/post-326953>

Each Telephone company has its own 5G frequency range it uses. The higher the frequency the faster the data but the less distance it will travel and penetrate thru walls etc. This is why they are expanding the tower network. You may have AT&T which for 5G uses “C-band” (3-4 GHz) but the towers around you also carry T-Mobile, Sprint, Verizon etc. AND all there towers also carry 4G which goes thru “everything”. This means your 4G signal is no longer coming from 1/2 or 1 mile away but from a few hundred feet. The good side of that is the power can be much lower and your phone can operate at much lower power. The further the away the tower is the more power (stronger signal) your phone uses to make the connection. So there are pros and cons to all this. Is it better to make an internet connection for several mins with 4G or a few seconds with 5G?

Is there a safe distance? Cell tower signals 3G/4G can travel for miles and as I said above the further away the tower the harder your phone 12” from your head has to work. 4G technology tower sends out a broad signal in a cone shape, your phone send out a signal 360º. 5G send out a board signal also but when it makes to connection to a 5G device it sends a focused beam at the device. If you do not have a 5G enabled device and no one close to you does then you will not get 5G “beamed” even if there is a 5G tower BUT if you live in a populated area there are lots of 5G devices all around you all the time.

According to [www.AntennaSearch.com](http://www.AntennaSearch.com) from our office in a town of 85K+/- there are _64 towers and 272 antennas within a 3.0 mile radius._ The closest is 1/2 mile away. If there are 40K people on our side of town how many cell phones are being used at any given time - 1K? 10K? How many “smart” meters, how many “smart” cars? How many WiFi signals? From my home - _6 towers and 12 antennas within a 3.0 mile radius and the closest is 1+ mile away._

Individual cell towers in big cities are bigger (more powerful) also:
Miami, FL - _370 towers and 1752 antennas within a 3.0 mile radius - how many cell phones?
Portland, OR - _251 towers and 2146 antennas within a 3.0 mile radius
Dallas, TX - _266 towers and 2564 antennas within a 3.0 mile radius___
Is there a safe distance? I would not want to live within 1200 feet of a (small) cell tower and 1/2 mile would be better - in a low density area. Even living in a small town if you have 50, 100, 500 homes within 1/2 mile of you, how many 4G/5G cell phone signals are there? There is a reason Jack tells us to move away from populated areas. I hope this helps you make an informed decision.

### Niki Stanton — 2023-10-02T00:34:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cell-tower-4g-5g-education.28205/post-326957>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=326953)
>
> Each Telephone company has its own 5G frequency range it uses. The higher the frequency the faster the data but the less distance it will travel and penetrate thru walls etc. This is why they are expanding the tower network. You may have AT&T which for 5G uses “C-band” (3-4 GHz) but the towers around you also carry T-Mobile, Sprint, Verizon etc. AND all there towers also carry 4G which goes thru “everything”. This means your 4G signal is no longer coming from 1/2 or 1 mile away but from a few hundred feet. The good side of that is the power can be much lower and your phone can operate at much lower power. The further the away the tower is the more power (stronger signal) your phone uses to make the connection. So there are pros and cons to all this. Is it better to make an internet connection for several mins with 4G or a few seconds with 5G?
>
> Is there a safe distance? Cell tower signals 3G/4G can travel for miles and as I said above the further away the tower the harder your phone 12” from your head has to work. 4G technology tower sends out a broad signal in a cone shape, your phone send out a signal 360º. 5G send out a board signal also but when it makes to connection to a 5G device it sends a focused beam at the device. If you do not have a 5G enabled device and no one close to you does then you will not get 5G “beamed” even if there is a 5G tower BUT if you live in a populated area there are lots of 5G devices all around you all the time.
>
> According to [www.AntennaSearch.com](http://www.AntennaSearch.com) from our office in a town of 85K+/- there are _64 towers and 272 antennas within a 3.0 mile radius._ The closest is 1/2 mile away. If there are 40K people on our side of town how many cell phones are being used at any given time - 1K? 10K? How many “smart” meters, how many “smart” cars? How many WiFi signals? From my home - _6 towers and 12 antennas within a 3.0 mile radius and the closest is 1+ mile away._
>
> Individual cell towers in big cities are bigger (more powerful) also:
> Miami, FL - _370 towers and 1752 antennas within a 3.0 mile radius - how many cell phones?
> Portland, OR - _251 towers and 2146 antennas within a 3.0 mile radius
> Dallas, TX - _266 towers and 2564 antennas within a 3.0 mile radius___
> Is there a safe distance? I would not want to live within 1200 feet of a (small) cell tower and 1/2 mile would be better - in a low density area. Even living in a small town if you have 50, 100, 500 homes within 1/2 mile of you, how many 4G/5G cell phone signals are there? There is a reason Jack tells us to move away from populated areas. I hope this helps you make an informed decision.

\- If you are living in a rural area, but around that zone are some urbanized areas with a lot of towers, how "safe" are you really? (think a dot in a circle, or a dot in a triangle and the dot being your rural area)

### Daulatwant — 2025-02-17T22:58:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cell-tower-4g-5g-education.28205/post-346909>

Maybe take a reading in a hole several feet underground and compare it to above ground?

### Anthony Smith — 2025-02-18T00:13:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cell-tower-4g-5g-education.28205/post-346915>

> [michellejorge said:](/goto/post?id=346905)
>
> I found a lead on for a building biologist I'll reach out to him but any thoughts on the relatively safe reading per my Cornet despite the crazy 700 plus antenna and towers described above? Am I missing something? Thanks

Sometimes it works out that way. It depends on where the towers are pointing. You may be in a dead (quiet) zone between coverage areas. Take the reading at different times of the day. Readings can be high in a business area during the day but quiet at night OR in a residential area quite during the day and high once everyone gets home and turns on all the devices In the evening.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-09-26T06:03:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-indoor-generation.28206/post-326820>

I expect this is the background to this video . . . A person online claimed that men think about the Roman Empire 2 - 3 times a day. A woman thought that that was crazy and could not possibly be true so she asked a bunch of guys “How many time a day do you think about the Roman Empire” every guy she asked said 2 - 3 times a day. She was shocked and posted it online . . .

America is the new Rome, socially and financially . . . Many guys see the parallel between America and Rome. All that is stated in the video PLUS add to that the decadence of Rome, our money has gone: our savings (Soc. Sec.), military, local government and private pensions, mutual funds and insurance etc.

The US debt is $33 Trillion
BUT - of that “only” $7.4+/- trillion is owed to foreign countries:
#1 - Japan $1.2T, #2 - China $870B, #3 - UK $680B, then lesser amounts to many other countries. BUT . . . The US is owed $9.4T by other countries, #1 - UK $1.24T, #2 - Cayman Islands $1.21T, #3 - Japan $820B, #4 - Canada $705B . . . so that is not the problem. (End 2022 numbers)

The problem is the rest of the $25.6 Trillion debt is owed to . . . us - ourselves:

National debt held by Government total $12.26T - Fed $5.5T & other Governments accounts (Soc Sec - $2.71T, Military Retirement - $1.36T etc.)
Nation debt held by Private - Mutual Funds - $2.84T, Depository institutions - $1.81T, State & Local governments - $1.55T, Pension Funds - $1.15T, Insurance companies - $368B etc.

Why are “they” trying to kill us off, especially all the old people with “viruses” and jabs? Because “they” have borrowed ALL our money/saving/pensions etc. and can’t pay it back. Add to that it is estimated the (known) derivative debt is 10x the worth of ALL global assets.

Definitely time to build an Ark.

# Thread: Does or How does Light style influence the  Randle cycle?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-or-how-does-light-style-influence-the-randle-cycle.28208/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Martha Ray — 2023-09-26T18:53:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-or-how-does-light-style-influence-the-randle-cycle.28208/post-326824>

I have been wondering how eating outdoors gets signals to help the body best digest or handle the food I eat under outdoor light. The _**glucose fatty-acid cycle**_ must be involved. That led me to The Randle Cycle debates.

Am I right, to avoid eating sugars and protein together, or to avoid eating carbs and fat together....??? I am currently eating keto low carb and mostly animal foods but have issues with (carb, sugar and decaf coffee) cravings and constipation.

Reminds me of the 60's and 70's [_**Natural Hygiene Movement**_](https://www.healthscience.org/about/natural-hygiene-movement/) that believed in 'proper' food combining thinking that taught not to eat dairy or starches with meat (also biblical I think) and if fruit is eaten it is to be eaten alone. Reasoning was that the body produces different enzymes to break down different foods and the reasoning with the fruit was that it fermented if it is layered on top of slower more dense slow digesting foods it would ferment since it digests quickly. Well I generally ate that way since the 70's and it eliminated my indigestion / acid reflux that I used to suffer with as a teenager.

Also in 2005 I did a [_**Seven Day Adventist**_](https://hartlandwellness.com/program/) 11 day stint at one of their retreats and ate a pod of garlic with a huge fruit smorgasbord in the very early morning and a moderate plant food early lunch, a charcoal / grape juice smoothie in the early afternoon and no food the rest of the day only for dinner a little herbal tea. So a good time of 'intermittent' fasting. Walking and working out in a very warm therapy pool (I did not know and they must not have known either the dangers of fluoride and pool chemicals). However I felt great lost 10 lbs but did have strong cravings for animal foods and when I went back to eating non vegan shortly after I returned home I had the worst indigestion I have ever had in my life, before or since. When I called to ask the retreat folks they explained that my body had stopping making the **enzymes** to break down animal foods. I did tax my body because I choose to break my vegan experiment with a spicy lamb and veggie Kabob. Not wise at all. The whole 'retreat' and going back to the SAD diet took me backward from any benefit and shortly after I gained the weight back, developed hypothyroidism and in 2009 I was Dx with cancer.

Just reminiscing...

Are there absolutes for all humans?
Or are we all so uniquely different from each other due to our perceptions our hodgepodge of what we believe to be true.

### JanSz — 2023-09-26T19:16:19+0300

**one must have body fat under 20%**
....
Eventually, secondarily, that comes with very low fasting insulin of no more than 5, but 1-3 would be better.
.....
When one set his/her mind to lose some body fat it should be done in a way that preserves muscle mass.
\=================

Fatty acids should be discussed widely.
Most important is Linoleic Acid, LA, Omega 6.

Situation of LA is beyond disaster.
LA is everywhere, it is hard to figure what to eat, because LA is everywhere in high amounts.
In the meantime, medical establishment is emphasizing that sufficient amount of LA is critical to our survival.

This is like worrying about sufficient hydration first when helping drowning person.

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1-1-to-4-1-la-ala-may-be-beneficial-for-health.28188/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1-1-to-4-1-la-ala-may-be-beneficial-for-health.28188/)

### NikkiYork — 2023-09-26T19:43:57+0300

I now eat like a great big white shark as much as I can

And I put on probably 10 pounds since, crossed the line to overweight. And I will deal with that later. Because the reason that I am overweight is low-ish redox. I am working on my melanin.

I’ve done probably all diets in the world. They don’t do shit in the long run. Of course you can lose weight with a lot of “tricks” and you can build a ton of muscle also, but it’s not sustainable, if the root cause of having a certain body stays the same.

I would ask myself: what is my goal? (Could be weight loss, could be health, could be weight gain, or getting as old as possible, finding a partner, not getting cancer, etc.)

Depending on what it is, you can pick a strategy - as best s you know.

Of course there are absolutes for all humans (define absolute, example, if you swim, you get wet, that’s a good absolute, and yes, you can come at me, with, but “what if”), and of course not one person is the same as another, not one environment is the same as another, myriads of variables make for your unique situation.

I eat with my family outdoors as often as possible and I thoroughly enjoy it  I’ll do that until a good reason not to presents itself.

What is low vitamin d?
In a Krusean world low vitamin d is everything under 60ng/ml.

In centralized medicine you are “fine” between 20-40ng/ml.

That’s one point to consider.

Second point: there is tanning and tanning.
One person gets dark brown, the other a slight beige. There is a correlation of vitamin d levels.

A black person can however still be black and be vitamin d deficient. They will burn in the sun.

Low redox is also a gradual thing.
I have low redox in my opinion. Somebody else might have way lower redox and think they are fine.

I hope that answers it a bit.

Extract...

"When calcium is effluxed for any reason, (non native EMF #1 cause today), cells immediately switch from making sulfate in surface blood vessels to nitrate synthesis. So sulfate levels drop everywhere. This directly affects Vitamin D 3 and cholesterol function, as you will soon see. **Neither can be sulfated as they normally would be, even when the sun is shining brightly.** This is why dermatologist believe the sun is bad when it is not. ### [Down on Vitamin D? It could be the cause of chronic inflammation](https://www.unisa.edu.au/media-centre/Releases/2022/down-on-vitamin-d-it-could-be-the-cause-of-chronic-inflammation/)

### JanSz — 2023-09-29T15:37:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tanned-with-low-vitamin-d.28210/post-326903>

Let's attempt to save first the 85 - 90% of people with problems.
Problems with diabetes, inflammation and in-ability to raise vit D when exposed to sunlight.
**Lose weight.**
Look at wild animals in wilderness.
None of them are fat.
Low body fat will remove many problems.
Do not look at your body weight. People feel defensive when discussing their weight, plus, it is much less accurate indicator.
**Look at your body fat.
Keep it under 20%**
While going for lower body fat do it slowly, do not loose muscle mass.

\===========================

### [Down on Vitamin D? It could be the cause of chronic inflammation](https://www.unisa.edu.au/media-centre/Releases/2022/down-on-vitamin-d-it-could-be-the-cause-of-chronic-inflammation/)

World-first genetic research from the University of South Australia shows a direct link between low levels of vitamin D and high levels of inflammation.

 www.unisa.edu.au

**How about large body fat, is it not the #1 source of chronic inflammation.
Who they did the research on? Fat or skinny people?**

### JanSz — 2023-09-29T20:21:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tanned-with-low-vitamin-d.28210/post-326911>

Unfortunately, vitamin D deficiency is common in U.S. populations,11 and those with the lowest levels have a higher risk of cardiovascular disease.12 The kidneys have an important role in making vitamin D useful within the body as they convert it from supplements or from the sun into an active form.13 **However, those suffering with chronic kidney disease also experience low vitamin D levels, not from lack of absorption or sun exposure, but from lack of activation within the kidneys.**

In a study published in the American Journal of Kidney Diseases, researchers found low vitamin D levels may be able to predict early kidney disease. Those who were deficient were twice as likely to develop albuminuria, type of protein in the urine, over a period of five years. This condition is an early indication of kidney damage.

[https://www.healio.com/news/nephrology/20180226/low-vitamin-d-levels-linked-to-early-signs-of-kidn](https://www.healio.com/news/nephrology/20180226/low-vitamin-d-levels-linked-to-early-signs-of-kidn)

### [Albuminuria: Albumin in the Urine - NIDDK](https://www.niddk.nih.gov/health-information/kidney-disease/chronic-kidney-disease-ckd/tests-diagnosis/albuminuria-albumin-urine)

Defines albuminuria and discusses who is at risk. Reviews the signs of albuminuria and provides information about testing procedures and treatment options.

 www.niddk.nih.gov

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cannot-convert-white-spots.28211/post-326898>

I have several white spots that will not produce melanin, and are irritated in sunlight, primarily on my upper chest and upper back.

I’m at a decent spot with my solar callus - I can lay in mid day sun in the summer in Ohio (UV 14) for an hour in one position and not burn (coming from someone of Finnish and German descent)

Every time these pure white spots, which are originally flat, become pink and swollen. Each are about 1-2 mm in size. Irritation lasts for 1-2 days after.

I’ve read many blogs and scoured much of the forums for an answer but none seem applicable.

I was convinced they were deuterium being extracted onto the skin with cholesterol, but have been consistently exfoliating for months with no changes.

They make maximizing my time in the sun very difficult.

Any thoughts? I would buy 5 lbs of oysters for anyone who can reverse this lol

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cannot-convert-white-spots.28211/post-326905>

Kendall,
I had 2 bouts of white spots on my upper back and shoulders after embracing the sunshine that both resolved themselves without any intervention other than more sunshine. Haven't had any for over three years. Are you staying in the sun all morning until the UV shows up? The morning spectrums may build the callous on your white spots better than just coming out for an hour at mid day. We are designed for all day sunshine. I am also of Northern European ancestry as are all the dark brown Germans that pack the beaches in Spain. Good luck

**Today PSA on the flu since it is the season coming:** Most influenza outbreaks disproportionately kill juvenile, elderly or already weakened patients; in contrast, the 1918 pandemic killed predominantly previously healthy young adults. Modern research, using virus’ taken from the bodies of frozen victims has concluded that the virus kills through a cytokine storm (overreaction of the body’s immune system). The strong immune reactions of young adults ravaged the body, whereas the weaker immune systems of children and middle-aged adults resulted in fewer deaths among those groups. These cytokine storms are mediated via NF kappa beta and IL-6 levels in the brain and body. You learned about them in my early leptin blogs.

During the solar cycle 14 of 1918, a tremendous change in the Sun’s spot activity dramatically altered the Sun’s magnetic field, the solar wind, and the Earth’s magnetic field simultaneously. This solar activity was able to moderate the Earth's surface chemistry for UV light. UV light causes virus growth in seawater as I laid out in Brain Gut 2 blog. What was found when 1918 epidemiologic data was mixed with data NASA scientists found about the event later on, was that as the sun's power decreased because less activity was seen, those in the coolest climate sustained the lowest death rates from influenza.

Cooler temperatures slowly improved the quantum yield for life while limiting the amount of direct UV exposure. This is how the solar ecosystem and the planet work in a quantum fashion. Those who lived in hotter climates joined the tropics and died at a much higher rate from the cytokine storm. This pointed to a direct effect of the sun’s magnetic field in the pandemic. Physicists also found the Earth had a weakened magnetic field in the solar maximum 14. The pandemic of 1918 showed the direct alterations in human beings’ immune function worldwide. Why is this important for us today? Because humans are facing the same situation. It's time to get your redox higher quickly using full spectrum light. The exact circumstances have been happening between the sun and Earth since 2009. In 2013, we experienced a solar maximum, but the sun’s activity has been troubling NASA since March of 2013 because it is decreasing its power while the human virus continues to explode globally in people with poor redox potentials. I believe this why they choose 2020 to unleash their created Wuhan virus. They are studying carefully what is the best time in the solar calaneder to unleash their creations for maximum effect for future power grabs.

**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-eggs.28216/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### healthy2015 — 2023-10-01T20:12:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-eggs.28216/post-326950>

Hi
I wonder if anyone can help me understand something. We gave our 6 year old son fish eggs (with the salt removed); however, when we noticed, he became very rude, naughty and acted up, and we saw a dramatic difference in his behaviour. Does anyone know why this could happen? Thanks

### Inger — 2023-10-03T10:42:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-eggs.28216/post-326984>

> [healthy2015 said:](/goto/post?id=326950)
>
> Hi
> I wonder if anyone can help me understand something. We gave our 6 year old son fish eggs (with the salt removed); however, when we noticed, he became very rude, naughty and acted up, and we saw a dramatic difference in his behaviour. Does anyone know why this could happen? Thanks

Could it be vitamin A overload? Fish eggs tend to be high in vitamin A, in a very bioavailable form. If he´s liver is already filled with it, it could be the drop that makes it spill over. It seems like there is an increase in vitamin A overload happening in our world and this very much can affect the nervous system. Since i overloaded myself with it I have researched a lot about this issue and there is a forum out there too, Grant Generoux is the guy who owns the forum and he cured himself of really bad kidney disease following a almost zero vitamin A diet. You can read up on his blog or forum for more information.
I am not sure yet why this issue seem to increase but I wonder if it has to do **with our technology use**, and of course our unnatural food consumption with tons of beta carotene too, that was not natural to begin with. Now there are liver, cod liver, goji berries, mangoes, sweet potatoes, red peppers, carrot juice, kale, tomatoes, etc. everywhere and touted as health foods, and these have really high amounts of retinol or betacarotene which will be converted to retinol more or less depending upon your genetics.
I know the eye has something to do with vitamin A turnover, I have learned that from Jack.

### TaylorF — 2023-10-03T14:36:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-eggs.28216/post-326987>

I have mast cell activation and have a tough time with roe/fish eggs. I get a histamine response from them and sometimes that results in an emotional change like anger, frustration, irritability. Typically nnEMF, blue light (lack of purple and red), and gut dysbiosis can cause higher histamine levels.

> I am not sure yet why this issue seem to increase but I wonder if it has to do **with our technology use**, and of course our unnatural food consumption with tons of beta carotene too, that was not natural to begin with. Now there are liver, cod liver, goji berries, mangoes, sweet potatoes, red peppers, carrot juice, kale, tomatoes, etc. everywhere and touted as health foods, and these have really high amounts of retinol or betacarotene which will be converted to retinol more or less depending upon your genetics.
> I know the eye has something to do with vitamin A turnover, I have learned that from Jack.

Another possibility is
there is a sum of (Omega3 + Omega6) that our bodies are able to deal with.
All the food around is overloaded with Omega6 by at least 30x or more.
In that situation
adding (good) (Omega3)
maybe the drop that makes it spill over.

////

### healthy2015 — 2023-10-07T20:54:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-eggs.28216/post-327090>

> [TaylorF said:](/goto/post?id=326987)

That’s very interesting, we have now removed the eggs from his diet and within a few days he returns back to normal.

### Jack Kruse — 2023-10-08T03:34:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-eggs.28216/post-327093>

maybe your kid was just being who he is.

### healthy2015 — 2023-10-08T16:43:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-eggs.28216/post-327100>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=327093)
>
> maybe your kid was just being who he is.

Obviously he is not normally like that which is why I questioned it as I really wanted him to eat the fish eggs and his behaviour has really put me off giving them to him. Is it a lack of sun issue although he is outside an awful lot.

### JanSz — 2023-10-08T17:11:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-eggs.28216/post-327103>

> [healthy2015 said:](/goto/post?id=326950)
>
> Hi
> I wonder if anyone can help me understand something. We gave our 6 year old son fish eggs (with the salt removed); however, when we noticed, he became very rude, naughty and acted up, and we saw a dramatic difference in his behaviour. Does anyone know why this could happen? Thanks

Since you have mentioned salt, which is mostly about Na,
I suggest that you also think about potassium K, and magnesium Mg.

Humans started agriculture about 12000 years ago.
Humans evolved from Africa 300000 years ago.
12000 years is a smal part of 300000.
Our bodies are still about the same as it were 12000 years ago.
But
our diet changed drastically, especially about potassium,
and
rather recently (150 years) about Linoleic acid (Omega-6) (seed oils).
This are major changes
and
I assume that even todays purely equatorial sunlight is not able to make sufficient enough corrections.
Have to address the two or even three (Mg) individually (on top of serious caring about proper sunlight exposure).

Add to that also that way back 12000 years or even only 200 years ago most everybody was constantly hungry, so they were lean even in times of "plenty" and then there was seasonal time when there was nothing to eat.
///////////////////////////////////////
While insulin resistance is routinely identified as a root cause of most chronic diseases, it too is only a symptom of a deeper problem. At the root will be impaired cellular energy production in the mitochondria.
**High linoleic acid (LA) levels are the primary factor that drives insulin resistance, and LA is clearly far more damaging than sugar, especially in the long term.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-eggs.28216/post-327118>

### Anthony Smith — 2023-10-03T04:41:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-are-the-haplotype-calculators-again.28217/post-326980>

Since our family tree is 2 parents, 4 grandparents, 8, 16, 32, 64, 128, 256, 512, 1024 etc. It doesn’t take too many generations to run out of people . . . Back in the 90’s a statistician did a study to find out how far back one would have to go for us to be descended from everyone. He calculated that everyone that was alive in Europe 1,000 years ago (that has any descendants alive today) due to inter marriage is related to EVERYONE of European heritage alive today. It is estimated that 20% of the people alive back then have no ancestors today because their line died out. In other words you are related to 100% of the people alive in Europe 1000 year ago whose line did not die out.

Then he took it to the next level and calculated how far back one need to go for everyone on the planet to be our ancestor - 3,500 years. That means for example: Abraham lived 3,600 years ago . . . everyone one on the planet (statistically) is a descendant of Abraham, Isaac, Jacob and ALL 12 boys.

Several year later a geneticist heard about this statistical hypothesis and decided to see if it was true, he found it was. Ergo - our differences are but percentages, Neanderthal included.

# Thread: Glaucoma
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glaucoma.28220/>
**Subforum:** Redox Rx
**Posts:** 26
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Joffrey — 2023-10-04T02:23:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glaucoma.28220/post-326998>

Hi there ☀️,

Can I recommend my 74yo mum to watch the sunrise safely with her glaucoma ?

Thank you

### JanSz — 2023-10-04T02:45:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glaucoma.28220/post-327000>

I am 73+ years old.
Had in both eyes' cataracts surgery.
Had in both eyes narrow angle glaucoma.
Narrow-angle glaucoma is a type of glaucoma that occurs when the structure inside the eye that allows fluid to drain normally from the eye (called the drainage angle) becomes restricted.

Had a holes drilled to relief excessive pressure. laser iridotomy
\======================
I see sunrise directly.
When sun is higher, I look at the sun momentarily.

....

### JanSz — 2023-10-04T20:34:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glaucoma.28220/post-327028>

> [Joffrey said:](/goto/post?id=327001)
>
> Ouah thank you JanSz for sharing this . My mum will feel good about It !

It pleases me when I am able to share (and prevent avoidable pain).
I do not know where your mom stands in a way of cataracts.
I think that I have made good decision by opting to use very simple lenses.
It is few years already since I did that.
I still use eyeglasses.
I am making new eyeglasses yearly.
The thing is every year the glasses are minutely different.
That means my eyes are constantly changing.
Had I opted for beautiful and perfect lenses the first time:
By now I would need eyeglasses anyhow.
Above is not so bad.
Worst would be if the new lenses would prevent me from being able to drive at nights.
Because that was the undesirable possibility of picking those "perfect" lenses.

////

I literally just got my 23andMe report back and it said I had an "increased likelihood" of developing glaucoma and guess what they recommended I do....Always wear sunglasses to protect my eyes from the sun. I couldn't believe what I was reading. It was so absurd and upsetting to see they are recommending this. I am surprised they didn't also recommend to stare at a computer screen all day with naked eyes before we go outside with sunglasses to protect our eyes from the sun (jk). And then not even an hour later, I see this post you put up about glaucoma and the world was right again. Thank you for this. The timing is perfect!

### Jack Kruse — 2024-09-13T15:44:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glaucoma.28220/post-339541>

### [x.com](https://x.com/DrJackKruse/status/1834573584984928753)

 x.com

### sheas — 2024-09-13T16:14:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glaucoma.28220/post-339543>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=339541)
>
> ### [x.com](https://x.com/DrJackKruse/status/1834573584984928753)
>
>  x.com

Thank you. Timing once again.... A friend of mine who lives in N. CA just posted yesterday that she has uveitis. She is a horse trainer and outside all the time but always in dark sunglasses. We see uveitis in horses, which is also known as moon blindness. Not sure why they get it other than I had a horse who got it from a leptospirosis exposure. So I am trying to understand why we see this so much in horses because they are not outside wearing sunglasses and on computer screens indoors. Maybe vaccines weakens them and they become susceptible to different protozoa... I don't know. Gelding them? Anyway, she thought it was weird that she got it. But it makes sense to me with her light environment. But horses I am not sure. Do you have any thoughts on this? Thanks for this information. I am 56 & have genetic short eye +17 prescription wear contact lens, hard, oxygen permeable & zero UV block in them. I had laser irodotomy 5 years ago, no issues from that. My protocol is no lenses in, 30 min sunrise into my eyes, midday take out lenses in sun/daylight outside for 20 mins, sunset contacts back out to view more sunlight.
my eye specialist, keeps telling me on the annual testing, no need for cataract surgery, I want to keep it this way.
always blue blockers and F.Lux on computer & night blue blockers & red lamps at home in the evening. I also have a BlockBlue Light red light panel that has pulse function, I use this in AM first thing for 5 mins into my eyes (no contacts in).
I never wear sunglasses outside.

I get at least 3 -4 hours a day outside all skin exposed to light & have a great tan for Irish genome, don't burn anymore,, grew up in UK but lived in Australia since 1993.

I wear eyeglasses at home so i can allow light to come in around them, but the lenses are too thick and heavy to wear while I am out and about doing business & meetings.

**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/linda-an-italian-champion-and-member-showed-off-how-she-uses-the-sun-because-of-advice-she-got-here.28223/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2023-10-05T16:19:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/linda-an-italian-champion-and-member-showed-off-how-she-uses-the-sun-because-of-advice-she-got-here.28223/post-327042>

**Linda Cerruti** (born 7 October 1993) is an Italian competitor in [synchronized swimming](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Synchronised_swimming) who competed at the [2011 World Aquatics Championships](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2011_World_Aquatics_Championships), [2013 World Aquatics Championships](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2013_World_Aquatics_Championships), [2010 European Aquatics Championships](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2010_European_Aquatics_Championships), [2012 European Aquatics Championships](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2012_European_Aquatics_Championships).[\[1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Linda_Cerruti#cite_note-sref-1) and in [Duet](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artistic_swimming_at_the_2020_Summer_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_duet) at the [2020 Summer Olympics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2020_Summer_Olympics).[\[2\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Linda_Cerruti#cite_note-2)She also competed at the [2022 World Aquatics Championships](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2022_World_Aquatics_Championships) in Budapest, Hungary, and the [2022 European Aquatics Championships](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2022_European_Aquatics_Championships) in Rome, Italy.

Anything in, on or around you that is conductive with attract electric fields and act as an antenna for radio frequencies, anything magnetic will attract magnetic fields (think any clothing - bra wires & belts, jewelry, even makeup, also - bed springs, metal desks etc.) The RF frequencies will depend on the size/shape of the wire/metal. Historically, jewelry was used to stimulate certain energy points on the body (think acupuncture points) for health benefits. It is my understanding that the point in the middle of the ear lobe is to stimulate the pineal gland and the “ring finger” is the heart meridian but they were only dealing with natural energy NOT nnEMF. As Jack says - do what you like - but if your inner voice is giving you pause, maybe listen.

# Thread: Moose Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/moose-journal.28230/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### enyaw — 2023-10-08T19:44:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/moose-journal.28230/post-327106>

I'm a 43 yo male that lives just shy of 43N near Grand Rapids, MI with a H5 maternal haplotype. I've been a forum lurker since I started reading the blogs in 2012. I've dabbled in some of the protocols, but ultimately did not need them at the time (thought I still saw benefits). I had a consult with Jack in 2017 and the key takeaway was that my timing was off. Wouldn't you know it, Jack was right. After a few months of fixing my timing, I was back to where I needed to be in regard to weight and energy levels. I suppose I'll begin my story there.

In 2017 I fixed my timing issues and I was as healthy and felt as great as I have in my adult life. I was 36. I'm 5'8" and weighed around 160 lbs; a weight I had no problems maintaining. Fast forward to October 2020. In those three years, without much change to diet and exercise, I gained 30 lbs. It worked out to be almost exactly 10 lbs per year for three years. I then gained 5 lbs per year for two years, putting me at my current weight at 200. My energy levels are a lot lower, my cognitive ability can best be described as brain fog. In 2021 I passed a kidney stone. I got thrush. I went to a functional medicine doctor in Sept 2021 because my PCP was uninterested in determining why I gained so much weight and lost so much energy in such a short amount of time. They took labs, provided a consult from a dietician, then "prescribed" a bunch of supplements. Some of the lab work was interesting. More on that later.

In 2023 I went to a determatologist to treat athlete's foot. I think I had it for a few years (I thought it was dry feet because OTC meds didn't change anything). It looks like it's healed but the soles of my feet still have an easy ability to peel (dermatologist said a sort of dermatitis and prescribed a steroid cream). I had a yeast growing on my scalp and on my face (t-zone) that I just recently cleared up with a cream from the same dermatologist (for years I thought it was just dry skin; same with feet). In April I had an inguinal hernia repaired. I'm not sure if this relates to everything else.

Labs: I've had high cholesterol (200-300+) since my 20s. HDL has always been in the normal range, but below 60. Vitamin D has always been on the low end of normal (30-40s ng/mL), but I think I've always tested in Autumn. (I think D levels can fall relatively quickly, so I could have had better levels in the middle of summer, but I haven't tested that yet.) My ALT was high last year, but has slooooooooowly been falling. Still high though. My BUN/Creatinine ratio is 11.7 (as of two days ago), but has bounced between 10 and 17 over the last ten or so years. In 2021, the functional doctor labs did show h pylori and some elevated numbers (more than just the usually high cholesterol). hsCRP was good (0.7 mg/L), but my Secretory IgA was low and my Anti-gliadin IgA was at the very low end of the range (43 on a 0-157 scale). The doctor thought this may be indicative of a chronic stress response. I have more numbers and pretty charts if anyone is interested, but my inability to lose weight combined with the ease of putting it on and my lessened cognitive ability are more concerning to me.

I currently eat an epi-paleo diet maybe 70% of the time. I should eat more seafood, especially with winter coming. I have been good about only eating when the sun is up, finishing before 7p (usually closer to 6p), and no snacking. I get morning sunlight in my eyes, though I cannot see the sun as it crests over the horizon. I sit on my front porch and look into the sky, even on rainy days. I may install a radiant heater to make it more bearable when it's 18 degrees and windy outside.

I do spend quite a few hours a day looking at a computer screen, though not all 8 hours of the work day. I use a thermabulb (IR) and two duracell black CFL bulbs (UV-A) while working. I also have daytime blue blockers (TruDark) but don't wear them as often as I should. I also turn on two UV-B reptile bulbs midday. I have a Sperti red and I'm thinking about using it this winter, especially on my gut. I may also use it on my children. (Maybe more about them in subsequent posts.)

I'm unsure if my current health issues have an underlying cause that is concerning or if I'm merely over weight and slower thinking because my timing is still off. I have really struggled in the last couple of years with maintaining a regular bedtime, but I've been better about it this last month now that the kids are in school and I made a semi-firm resolution to do so.

### enyaw — 2023-10-08T19:48:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/moose-journal.28230/post-327107>

Going into winter in Michigan, what should be my goal for timing my day? I am unable to obtain optimal while my kids are in school. We rise between 0600 - 0630 but the sun doesn't rise until anywhere between 0730 (currently) and 0830 (Dec 21). The sun sets early, but that's not a huge issue for us. We don't watch much tv (maybe a football game on Sunday afternoon) and the kids have zero devices. I can turn mine off by 9p at the latest, but I try to sooner. Would it be best to wake up at the same time everyday (~0600) even though it is an hour or two before sunrise? Fasting until the sun comes up seems like it'd be a good idea in that case.

Now, speaking of cortisol.
There is a Randle Cycle.
In the end of its discussion the conclusion is that:

• The Randle cycle refers to an antagonism between glucose and fats as fuel, which explains why you can become insulin resistant when you avoid carbohydrates

• If you’re in chronic ketosis, your tissues decrease their insulin sensitivity, which can lead to or contribute to insulin resistance

• A low-carb diet is best implemented as a temporary strategy to restore metabolic flexibility. Once metabolic flexibility is regained, adding more carbs back in will help to lower cortisol, which is highly inflammatory

### doctorwu85 — 2024-03-14T01:26:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/moose-journal.28230/post-332523>

I think it's easy to go down a paralysis-by-analysis rabbit hole with labs, especially if you're seeing a functional medicine doc. They can be useful, but it's also easy to over-analyze specific results, and the doc will invariably recommend a bunch of supplements that are unlikely to address the root cause of your problems. They're not pushing you into the same Big Pharma sinkhole as allopathic doctors, but remember they have to make a buck too.

You seem to have a number of simultaneous issues, which suggests some sort of systemic problem with redox, hormone levels, circadian rhythms, etc. Have you tried doing the strict Leptin RX for 6 weeks? How careful are you about blocking blue light at night? Are you using artificial light at night in general? Uncle Jack has mentioned on many podcasts that biology is non-linear, and you could be doing 9 things pretty well but it's the 10th that jumpstarts your redox and associated issues in the right direction.

Another thing -- Anthony mentioned this earlier in the thread but I didn't see a reply. What is your EMF environment like? EMFs have been shown to produce a chronic stress response and mess with cortisol (see the last 100 pages or so of Robert O Becker's book), as well as significantly raise triglyceride levels. I would bet that this is part of your problem.

### NikkiYork — 2023-10-08T19:51:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vo2-max-testing.28231/post-327108>

Hi there,
I'm not in the mood to spend 100-200 bucks in a gym for VO2 max testing. There are a bunch of ways to do it by yourself. So I did two types of step testing so far. One with a 16inch stair case and oh wonder, I scored through the roof, like impossible, so I probably did it all wrong.

Just now I did a 12 inch box test and it seemed more reasonable with "above average". I'm thinking, it's morning and my pulse goes way higher in the mornings.
Did anybody do other testings, like the mile walk? I'm going to probably do all of those tests described online and average it out.

I'm grateful to have an amazing heart apparently. The covid area wasn't great for me though, I still can't excercise, but confident to get there. Back before Covid I had a personal trainer, and she said she couldn't believe my heart, and I was like, well, that's what I'm used to. I always hated Cardio and I kinda try not to do it, hahahaha. Maybe it's time to change.

According to the researchers, ivermectin suppressed lung cancer in at least three different ways:
>
> (1) Ivermectin stopped the cancer cells from growing and dividing,
> (2) Ivermectin caused the cancer cells to die (“apoptosis”), and
> (3) Ivermectin increased the process of cells breaking down their own damaged or unwanted parts (“autophagy”).
>
> The researchers found that the way ivermectin works is by inhibiting a protein called PAK1, which is more highly expressed in lung cancers than in normal tissues, and lung cancer patients showing high PAK1 levels have a worse prognosis. PAK1 has been linked to cancer cell growth and survival. So when PAK1 is inhibited, the cancer cells cannot grow or divide and eventually die.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-10-15T00:12:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-and-brownian-motion.28234/post-327276>

Temperature is a measure of the kinetic energy of particles (matter) in a system. Higher temperature means faster moving matter. This is referring to energy in the form of heat.

Higher temperature also means matter is more expanded. Expansion of matter hinders the movement of electrons through matter. Now we are talking about electrical energy. Electrical potential is reduced at higher temps.

Cooler temps condense matter. Less heat energy, but there is an increase in the potential for energy generation in the form of electricity (moving electrons)

### Jack Kruse — 2023-10-15T01:26:36+0300

How about in cold?

>
> Higher temperature also means matter is more expanded. Expansion of matter hinders the movement of electrons through matter. Now we are talking about electrical energy. Electrical potential is reduced at higher temps.
>
> Cooler temps condense matter. Less heat energy, but there is an increase in the potential for energy generation in the form of electricity (moving electrons)

Its worth checking out aluminum as a factor. Both my mother and maternal grandmother had dementia, so I regularly drink a silica rich mineral water to help clear any I haven't avoided. I think its especially important in a microwaved environment (think aluminum in a microwave oven ....). On a biophysics level Al reflects light, so would have an effect on cells in terms of reflecting both natural sunlight and endogenously produced cellular signalling light. Then there's light signalling in the microbiome .....

_PD is a progressive neurodegenerative disorder manifested by motor symptoms such as slowness of movement, tremors, stiffness, postural instability, and non-motor symptoms: depression, anxiety, dementia, and autonomic dysfunction. The ultimate cause of PD is unknown, but studies point to risk factors such as age, family history, and pesticide exposure \[[117](https://www.mdpi.com/1422-0067/24/8/7228#B117-ijms-24-07228),[118](https://www.mdpi.com/1422-0067/24/8/7228#B118-ijms-24-07228)\]. Additionally, long-term exposure to Al creates a risk factor for PD. Al accumulates in the substantia nigra and in Lewy bodies and disrupts the dopaminergic system by affecting the activity of the enzyme involved in the dopamine (DA) biosynthesis pathway \[[119](https://www.mdpi.com/1422-0067/24/8/7228#B119-ijms-24-07228),[120](https://www.mdpi.com/1422-0067/24/8/7228#B120-ijms-24-07228)\]. Additionally, the research carried out on zebrafish showed that exposure to Al not only causes neurodegenerative processes but also influences the regulation of genes related to PD \[[2](https://www.mdpi.com/1422-0067/24/8/7228#B2-ijms-24-07228)\]._

### [Aluminium in the Human Brain: Routes of Penetration, Toxicity, and Resulting Complications](https://www.mdpi.com/1422-0067/24/8/7228)

Aluminium (Al) is the most ubiquitous metal in the Earth’s crust. Even though its toxicity is well-documented, the role of Al in the pathogenesis of several neurological diseases remains debatable. To establish the basic framework for future studies, we review literature reports on Al...

 www.mdpi.com

I'm still down the rabbit hole, but a biologically reactive Al atom is surrounded by 6 water molecules, and I've been wondering if the H is replaced by deuterium on the water molecules, its possibly one of the ways it can get into the brain and damage the deuterium absorbing melanin in the substantia nigra.

Another potential route is if the bones (which are an Al sink) are contaminated with Al. Al is linked to osteoporosis, but I think It is hypothetically possible that contaminated stem cells from the bone marrow destined for regenerating the brain could deliver Al which in turn negatively deflects endogenous light emission ....(In PD the dopamine biosynthesis pathway?) In the book Imagine you are an aluminum atom by Chris Exley, he shows fluorescence microscopy pictures of aluminum in the head, midpiece and tail of a human sperm, a macrophage, a T helper cell, and lymphocytes crossing the meninges in brain tissue with autism, so I don't think its too far fetched that stem cells could also be a source ....

I would not give a thought to what happened to your Dad happening to you unless you have lived his life in the same places he lived his.

I give it a lot of thought because my kids inherited my mitochondrial DNA.  But I'd be interested in how parental imprinting from the male affects risk. Jack has said ([https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/building-a-childs-brain-begins-with-electrons.27475/#post-324111](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/building-a-childs-brain-begins-with-electrons.27475/#post-324111))

_"Another example of epigenetic inheritance, discovered about 10 years ago in mammals, is parental imprinting. In parental imprinting, certain autosomal genes have seemingly unusual inheritance patterns. **For example, the mouse Igf2 gene is expressed in a mouse only if it was inherited from the mouse's father."**_

This kinda makes sense for mammalian biology, in that in many species of mammals, the males don't help raise their offspring. But maybe its a way of passing transgenerational epigenetic _information_ about their own mtDNA inheritance?

My kids lived with me and their father for the first 18 years of life, and if the choices they make or the environments they live in after leaving home are similar its possible they MAY get a similar response to me if I develop or don't develop dementia, but it could increase their risk if their life choices or environment is worse after leaving home. Alternatively they could reduce the risks associated with their mitochondrial DNA (and parental imprinting inheritance?) if they make better choices. For example, I know my mother's likely sources of aluminum exposure, but as a vegetarian for over 20 years before she died, (as an example from just one possible exposure route), she would have ingested far less from dietary sources than I would if I was a meat eater using aluminum foil to roast beef, or taking antacids, etc which if anything I think would raise my risk of developing dementia earlier than she did, because endogenous UV light signalling abnormalities would be higher from an earlier age, particularly in the modern nnemf world, and as skin is an aluminum sink too, I think that would negatively affect my ability to make vitamin D. This could be a factor in someone younger developing a disease that seems to come from nowhere - their parents may be alive and not develop the disease, and their grandparents may have remained healthy into old age.

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=327224)
>
> Have you measured electric, magnetic, and RF levels of your office and home?

I have. With a Cornet.

Surprisingly, office seems okay...

Our home is completely in green at night regarding RF when routers and devices are turned off, except for baby monitor, which was marketed as low EMF (Bebcare). Luckily the camera portion appears safe from device, but the parent unit that is kept in our room turns the meter red. I've contacted them multiple times only for them to ignore/divert my concerns.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-11-01T19:43:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alzheimer%E2%80%99s-parkinson%E2%80%99s-%E2%80%94-but-why.28238/post-327769>

Luckily the camera portion appears safe from device, but the parent unit that is kept in our room turns the meter red. I've contacted them multiple times only for them to ignore/divert my concerns.

Green for RF is great but what about 60Hz AC electric fields? Baby monitor if plugged in should be at least 6 - 8 away from the baby NEVER in the crib/bed. You can put a metallic shield between you and your part of the monitor so the RF doesn’t affect you at night.

### Plural — 2023-11-01T21:27:35+0200

I'll recheck those again. I remember them being green/yellow as well.

I did recently buy a Defender Shield pouch for the monitor, and after testing and discussing with their rep, I better understand that shield affect to divert it from us while we sleep, since the pouch is only 1-sided and not complete faraday.

The cam for child is as high on ceiling as can go. Her bed is also now on floor so she's even further.

### sunman — 2023-10-12T00:35:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/watermellon-sourced-structured-ez-water.28240/post-327176>

I was looking at watermellons as a source of structured/EZ water but am unsure regarding the deuterium content of commercially grown watermellons or semisweet watermellons before they become sweet;
Does anyone have good insight into what the deuterium content of watermellons is and what are the pros or cons of eating them; I was thinking of homegrown and before they get sweet as i assume sweeter ones may have more deuterium;
all insight is appreciated here;
thanks again.

### JanSz — 2023-10-12T03:18:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/watermellon-sourced-structured-ez-water.28240/post-327177>

> Does anyone have good insight into what the deuterium content of watermellons is and what are the pros or cons of eating them; I was thinking of homegrown and before they get sweet as i assume sweeter ones may have more deuterium;

You can test your watermelons for deuterium content.

### [The Center for Deuterium Depletion - Test, Track, and Improve Your Health](https://www.ddcenters.com/)

The Center for Deuterium Depletion focuses on research and heavy hydrogen/protium analysis to improve health and lifestyle outcomes.

 www.ddcenters.com

Let us know the results.

### NikkiYork — 2023-10-12T21:40:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/israel-palestinian-conflict.28241/post-327199>

Since we are talking about it.

One of my familie’s distant friends, a photographer who would take our family photos, died in the Hamas attacks. We think he attended that rave. He was identified by his cameras.

As a German married to a Jew, we have a half/half family, me being more Jewish than my husband  you know, it’s a Mom’s thing to do all that stuff.

Anyways, it is rather lonely right now to be Jewish - as the Christian, that I am.

Every more differentiated person can see how there are two sides to a story, but the siding is what makes it lonely. I am siding with the people. All people, as opposed to a failing leadership. Emphasize failing!!!!

In a lefty state like the one I live in the bullies are silencing pro-Israel voices, but also the natural empathy one would have towards an atrocity that was committed.

It is a very sad state of affairs.

I am positive that Jack’s type of people have the balls to stand up for the people and to have a heart. Showing community goes a very long way. In real life.

I’m sorry, I’m not a virtual person, so I kinda suck at that. If this was offensive, I apologize.

### Johan Lindøm — 2023-10-13T12:12:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/israel-palestinian-conflict.28241/post-327221>

> [NikkiYork said:](/goto/post?id=327199)
>
> siding is what makes it lonely.

I believe we are being manipulated into taking sides in significant global events. When major world events occur, powerful forces are at play, and many of the events we are witnessing may be driven by hidden agendas. These events are often both complex and controversial, leaving ample room for interpretation. We are not encouraged to think critically and independently about these topics. Instead, our discussions and understanding often result in polarization and negative feelings, fostered by limited information, propaganda, illusions, and skewed perceptions. As a result, we are hindered from gaining a deeper understanding and engaging in constructive discussions.

When we examine the roots of this conflict, I firmly believe that the lens through which we must view it is imperialism. Imperialism has a few clear objectives: acquiring land, resources, and amassing power. It's plain and simple. Every available means is deployed to maintain and expand hegemony and power. Religious contradictions, historical events, ideologies, hatred, culture, and more are all manipulated to exert control over people and nations. For instance, in South and Central America, the Spaniards, during their voyages of discovery, skillfully manipulated indigenous communities, inciting conflicts that led to the destruction of each other's lives and societies. I believe this pattern still persists in many places worldwide.

### Sajid Mahmood — 2023-10-29T17:19:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/israel-palestinian-conflict.28241/post-327635>

CONSPIRACY THEORY: Is the funding of Israel against Palestine a US proxy war against Iran to protect the petrodollar?

Historical patterns suggest repercussions for nations challenging the PetroDollar - Venezuela, Iraq, Libya all faced US interventions.

Today, BRIICS nations are contemplating a new reserve-backed currency, with Syria's pivotal role sparking a decade-long war.

Consider these key historical and geopolitical puzzle pieces:

Bretton Woods Agreement, 1944: Established the U.S. dollar as the global reserve currency. The dollar became a fiat currency when Nixon removed the gold standard in 1971, and the petrodollar system emerged.

OPEC's Role, 1970s: The U.S. brokered a deal with Saudi Arabia to ensure oil trades in U.S. dollars, extending to all OPEC countries, reinforcing the PetroDollar's dominance.

Venezuela, 2002: After Venezuela stopped accepting dollars for oil, the US funded a coup, and PetroEuro plans were shelved.

Iraq, 2003: Saddam Hussein's move away from the PetroDollar led to a US invasion following a false WMD report.

Gold Dinars and Gaddafi, 2009: Gaddafi proposed the Gold Dinar, a single African currency for oil trades. This threatened the PetroDollar and led to NATO's intervention in Libya. This USA led intervention resulted in Gaddafi's death. The PetroDollar remained untouched (WikiLeaks also later revealed this was motive for the intervention).

BRIICS, 2010: Nations met to discuss a new reserve-backed currency. Syria, key to pipeline plans, became a war zone, with the US supporting the opposition.

Russia, 2014: Putin secured Crimea, a strategic point for LNG delivery from Syria, leading to an expanded BRIICS+ despite geopolitical tensions.

China's PetroYuan, 2018: China launched crude oil futures contracts in yuan, convertible into gold, challenging the PetroDollar.

The Shanghai Cooperation Organization (SCO, ongoing): This alliance, including China, Russia, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Tajikistan, Uzbekistan, India, and Pakistan, has been discussing trade in local and gold-backed currencies, a potential PetroDollar bypass.

Now as Middle East tensions rise as the USA, alone, vetoes a ceasefire.

Iran plans to ditch the Dollar for oil trades, while unproven claims link it to Hamas.

Lebanon struggles with constant destabilization.

Several nations, including Egypt, Saudi Arabia, the UAE, Algeria, and Bahrain, consider non-dollar trades.

As the USA seeks to protect the PetroDollar, Israel's military fueled by advanced US weaponry, expands.

This could lead to a proxy or direct war against oil-rich Iran.

Are these events coincidental, or calculated moves in a geopolitical game? Only time will tell. Would love to hear your thoughts.

Follow me for more: @xsn

[https://x.com/xsn/status/1715362113747816878?s=48&t=XkU4PTZJUZYg2Sos6TQfFg](https://x.com/xsn/status/1715362113747816878?s=48&t=XkU4PTZJUZYg2Sos6TQfFg)

The Molotov-Ribbentrop Pact, officially known as the Treaty of Non-Aggression between Germany and the Union of Soviet Socialist Republics, was signed on **23 August** **1939** **in Moscow.**

### Chaostheory — 2023-10-13T00:38:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-is-the-lowest-emf-in-us.28242/post-327209>

I remember reading on here that the suburbs below New Orleans has the lowest emf in the country. Is that still true? What is the latitude/longitude and names of the towns/cities? I am planning on moving in a year hopefully with my partner.
I have to consider cost of living but would still feel better living in the suburbs.
I don't believe I can make much progress where I live. It is too far north and probably has too much emf.
I am trying to improve my mental health mainly and have been wanting to make this move for a while. I am finally stable enough to do it.
Thanks in advance for any advice about location, etc.

### NikkiYork — 2023-10-13T00:43:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-is-the-lowest-emf-in-us.28242/post-327210>

I checked all the maps from all the cell phone carriers. You can find those online.
Then I also checked light pollution maps.

And I conclude you are sh\*\* out of luck. Alas, far from cities should be a hint. Not California… not Tenessee. Not anywhere northern because sun light…

I found the East is more lit than the west, but it probably varies from location to location and you can shield off some.

If you find a great spot, let me know. I’m hunting for a US and a Non-US spot.

The mammalian target of rapamycin (mTOR), also referred to as the mechanistic target of rapamycin, allows life to continue in good and bad times.
That is, it is longevity promoter.
That works from bacteria to humans, worth looking into it.
Problem we have now is that we have mostly eliminated bad times.
And that is bad news, because bad times are needed to eliminate cancer, diabetics, high blood pressure and most of other ailments of this type.

### JanSz — 2023-10-21T03:23:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mtor-switch-and-supercentenarians.28243/post-327453>

Body Mass Index (BMI) is a person’s weight in kilograms divided by the square of height in meters1. It is a screening tool that measures the ratio of your height to your weight2. A high BMI can indicate high body fatness1.

The World Health Organization (WHO) and the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) define obesity as follows:

If your BMI is less than 18.5, it falls within the underweight range.
**If your BMI is 18.5 to <25, it falls within the healthy weight range.**
If your BMI is 25.0 to less than 30, it falls within the overweight range.
If your BMI is 30.0 or higher, it falls within the obesity range1.

**_What we conclude in a big picture point of view is that mTOR is the protein that links the availability of nutrients in our environment to whether we’re in a catabolic or an anabolic state_

Anabolism** is growth, and **catabolism** is the breakdown of material

This function accounts for why mTOR has so many different roles

If you think about our evolutionary history, there’s almost nothing in our physiology that shouldn’t be controlled by the availability of nutrients

It’s such a central thing in our lives

**We tend to forget that now because we’re in a overeating stage

### JanSz — 2023-10-23T19:20:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mtor-switch-and-supercentenarians.28243/post-327497>

**Rapamycin=Inhibitor of mTOR**
The phrase **“bench to bedside”** refers to the process of translating scientific discoveries from the laboratory bench into clinical applications that can benefit patients. In the context of longevity studies, this phrase is used to describe the process of taking basic research findings and translating them into interventions that can help people live longer, healthier lives.

**Rapamycin** is a drug that was discovered in 1972 on Easter Island, also known as **Rapa Nui.**
It was originally used as an immunosuppressant for organ transplant patients, but it has since been found to have potential anti-aging properties.
Rapamycin works by targeting a master regulator of cell growth in our cells called mTOR.
When rapamycin targets mTOR, it inhibits cell growth.
This makes it a potent anti-cancer drug, since cancer is marked by uncontrolled cell growth.
The inhibition of mTOR also triggers autophagy, a process by which lysosomes clean up misfolded proteins and damaged organelles.
Researchers have long thought that rapamycin targets more than one cellular pathway.
Now, researchers have discovered which pathway it is—a calcium ion channel on the lysosomal membrane called TRPML.
The discovery potentially expands uses for rapamycin.

Hey, are there other targets that are better than rapamycin? (I guess no answer here yet.)

Since rapamycin was developed (successfully) for use in immune compromised patients, (transplants),
that clouded the issue of side effects if it would be used by healthy peoples.
Because rapamycin was approved and is used in transplant patients, it has rather bad reputation.
\----------

Caloric restrictions have a positive impact on the life span.
..
Rapamycin is a drug that inhibits the activity of the protein complex called target of rapamycin (TORC1).
TORC1 is responsible for a wide variety of cellular activities, including cell proliferation, metabolism, and survival.
Baur and colleagues considered that rapamycin treatment’s reductions in NADH **could decrease cellular ATP production, but their results showed otherwise.**

Scientists have identified 12 hallmarks of aging that accumulate over time and are thought to play a major role in the aging process.
These hallmarks include genomic instability,
telomere attrition,
epigenetic alterations,
loss of proteostasis,
deregulated nutrient sensing,
mitochondrial dysfunction,
cellular senescence,
stem cell exhaustion,
altered intercellular communication,
dysregulated nutrient sensing,
tissue inflammation,
and extracellular matrix dysfunction
.......
Leucine (symbol Leu or L) is an essential amino acid that is used in the biosynthesis of proteins.
It is essential in humans, meaning the body cannot synthesize it: it must be obtained from the diet.
Like [valine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Valine) and [isoleucine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Isoleucine), leucine is a [branched-chain amino acid](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Branched-chain_amino_acid).
..............

mTOR is often thought of as a SWITCH.
And that is not correct.
\==========
0.1 mg/kg day for people
70kg----7mg

8-12mg daily ???
\-------------------------------------------------
rapamycin likely is slowing duration of cells.

structural rejuvenation of the ovary
...........................................................................................
spermatogenesis----fast

ovogenesis---slow

Spermatogenesis and ovogenesis are the processes of producing male and female gametes, respectively.
[The duration of spermatogenesis is estimated to be around **74 days** according to tritium-labelled biopsies and approximately **120 days** according to DNA clock measurements](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spermatogenesis).

[On the other hand, **ovogenesis is a much slower process** that starts before birth and continues until menopause](https://bio.libretexts.org/Bookshelves/Introductory_and_General_Biology/Book%3A_General_Biology_%28Boundless%29/43%3A_Animal_Reproduction_and_Development/43.03%3A_Human_Reproductive_Anatomy_and_Gametogenesis/43.3C%3A__Gametogenesis_%28Spermatogenesis_and_Oogenesis%29).

During this process, a primary oocyte undergoes meiosis I to produce a secondary oocyte and a polar body.
The secondary oocyte then undergoes meiosis II to produce an ovum and another polar body.
[The entire process of ovogenesis takes around **28 days**](https://bio.libretexts.org/Bookshelves/Introductory_and_General_Biology/Book%3A_General_Biology_%28Boundless%29/43%3A_Animal_Reproduction_and_Development/43.03%3A_Human_Reproductive_Anatomy_and_Gametogenesis/43.3C%3A__Gametogenesis_%28Spermatogenesis_and_Oogenesis%29).

### JanSz — 2023-10-25T02:57:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mtor-switch-and-supercentenarians.28243/post-327526>

### The Switch with James Clement-​

### Ignite Your Metabolism with Intermittent Fasting​

\=====================================

twenty BMI healthy twenty year
1:29:25
old of a **hundred and fifty pounds** so that's a very healthy person a BMI of 20 percent this person would have
1:29:33
about a **hundred thirty five thousand calories worth of fat** in their body so
1:29:39
you could go sixty days without eating anything and burning thousands of
1:29:46
calories a day from the fat that you carry alone around alone
\===================================
how many pounds of human fat has 135000 calories

[A pound of body fat is estimated to contain between **3,436 and 3,752 calories**](https://www.healthline.com/nutrition/calories-in-a-pound-of-fat).
[This range is due to the fact that body fat is not just pure fat but also contains fluid and protein](https://www.healthline.com/nutrition/calories-in-a-pound-of-fat).

If we take the average of this range, which is about **3,594 calories per pound**, and divide 135,000 calories by this number, we get:
3594135000≈37.56 pounds
So, approximately **37.56 pounds** of body fat would contain 135,000 calories.
\=================================
Disregarding fluid and proteins.
[Pure fat has a very high energy content, or about **9 calories per gram**](https://www.healthline.com/nutrition/calories-in-a-pound-of-fat). [This amounts to about **4,100 calories per pound of pure fat**](https://www.healthline.com/nutrition/calories-in-a-pound-of-fat).

So, if we divide 135,000 calories by 4,100 calories (the amount of calories in a pound of pure fat), we get:
4100135000≈32.93 pounds
So, approximately **32.93 pounds** of pure body fat would contain 135,000 calories.
\=====================================================
[So, a person who weighs 150 pounds and has a BMI of 20 would be approximately **6.04 feet** tall](https://www.nhlbi.nih.gov/health/educational/lose_wt/BMI/bmicalc.htm?source=quickfitnesssolutions).

Having 32.93 pounds of fat (32.93/150)\*100=**21.95% of fat

Somehow, I do not like those numbers. This would be rather fat person.
I think he overestimated that guy's fat content.
But I still believe that that person could do 60 day's fast.**
\========================================
I have 150# 5'8" BMI=22.8 body fat (as shown on my scale)=14%
150\*0.14=**21 pounds of fat**

### JanSz — 2023-10-25T15:18:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mtor-switch-and-supercentenarians.28243/post-327534>

### [Body Fat Percentage & Body Fat Weight Calculator](https://www.fitwatch.com/calculator/body-fat-percentage)

Easily calculate how much of your weight is body fat vs. lean body mass with this simple-to-use body fat percentage calculator

www.fitwatch.com

American Council on Exercise (ACE)

### [Health and Fitness Certifications, Continuing Education & Resources | ACE](https://www.acefitness.org/)

Explore ACE resources for health and fitness education including personal trainer, group fitness instructor, health coach and medical exercise specialist certification programs, continuing education, career resources, and more.

 www.acefitness.org

According to the American Council on Exercise (ACE),
essential body fat is the minimum amount of body fat necessary for a healthy, functional body.

For men, **essential fat** is typically around 2-5% of total body weight.
For women, who require more body **fat for ovulation**, **essential fat** is approximately 10-13% of total body weight.

**What about post menopausal women?**
ACE does not provide specific guidelines for postmenopausal women.

### JanSz — 2023-10-29T14:24:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mtor-switch-and-supercentenarians.28243/post-327627>

[**Betterhumans** is a non-profit scientific research organization that conducts the world’s most comprehensive genomic study of supercentenarians and their families](https://supercentenarianstudy.com/)[1](https://supercentenarianstudy.com/). [They are particularly interested in individuals who have demonstrated increased or extreme resistance to devastating, age-related diseases such as **cancer, cardiovascular disease, diabetes, Alzheimer’s disease, Parkinson’s disease, organ failure, immune system failure, and neurodegeneration**](https://supercentenarianstudy.com/)[1](https://supercentenarianstudy.com/).

They are currently seeking participants to enroll in their **Supercentenarian Research Study**. [Enrollment is **open to individuals at least 105 years old** and their families](https://supercentenarianstudy.com/)[1](https://supercentenarianstudy.com/). [They also provide a database of supercentenarian genomes where you can explore their variants for cancer, diabetes, cardiovascular diseases, etc](https://supercentenarianstudy.com/)[1](https://supercentenarianstudy.com/).

However, due to privacy laws and regulations, specific data about individual supercentenarians may not be publicly available. [For more detailed information or data requests, you may want to contact Betterhumans directly](https://supercentenarianstudy.com/)[1](https://supercentenarianstudy.com/).

Eventually he became a sort of politician, so he got his wife elected to good position.
Then he worked for her number of years (in Ukraine).
Highly fit, retired, got into biking accident, shook his brains out, year latter was gone.

The bigger question is how many of those 1224 who lived until 100 had been downing metformin or supplements like resveratrol for years.  Eating several locally grown high fructose fruits in season that come with things like the fibre intact and other nutrients that would take time to eat is not the same as getting the same amount of fructose in one hit from drinking juice. If the juice has been pasteurised the effect is probably worse, as a fruit picked straight from the plant will be a source of structured water and information from biophoton release as the food is chewed or digested. If dying cells release UV, hypothetically whether from prey or vegetation, it could be a UV source for nocturnal mammals and they get the whole context of the food including seasonal circadian information, from what is essentially stored sunlight. For me, any anti aging compound extracted from a living food or made in a lab and put in a supplement would be like trying to decypher a computer image based on one pixel.

### JanSz — 2023-11-02T15:14:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mtor-switch-and-supercentenarians.28243/post-327781>

At the moment I am guessing that it could be a good idea to introduce category of people that are EMF super sensitive.
And talk more with them in more specific way.
Addressing everybody as hypersensitive,
prevents more detailed information from being figured out.
Then, attempt to figure out why only that group and not the rest.

There is a girl in NYC that posts here. She appears to me as one of those HSPs.
Wish I could have some advice for her, but the kind that would help her, but must be of very low cost.
She looks like a very low income, can't do much that cost $$.

/////////////////////
Electromagnetic hypersensitivity (EHS) is a condition where people experience symptoms such as tingling skin, body aches, headaches, dizziness/nausea, fatigue, flu-like symptoms, and stress when they are in close proximity to EMF radiation-emitting devices.
Some people are more sensitive to EMFs than others.
Studies have shown that people with high sensitivity often find themselves uncomfortable in places with increased levels of EMF radiation.
The symptoms of EHS can lead people to seek medical treatment or render them incapable of working or living around wireless technology.

There is no strong evidence that proves people can be allergic to Wi-Fi signals.
However, **highly sensitive people (HSPs)** tend to feel the physical, mental, and emotional impact of electromagnetic fields (EMFs) at even higher levels than others do.
These people are also known as Electromagnetically Sensitive People (EMPs).
**HSPs** react much more strongly to everything, including foods, pesticides, chemicals, air pollution, cigarette smoke, heat and cold, sounds, vibrations, and even the emotions of others.

Food allergies and genetic predispositions reveal an important truth about our physiology.
As humans, our physical bodies all generally function in the same way.
We have the same organs, we carry out the same cellular processes, we require the same basic needs to continue living.
Yet despite this surface-level uniformity, no two human bodies are the same.
It’s the same case with Electromagnetic Field (EMF) Radiation: though we all can potentially experience its ill effects, **some of us are more sensitive to EMFs than others.**

Unfortunately, because electromagnetic radiation has only recently been more widely accepted as a health hazard, those who’ve gone to their doctors presenting symptoms of EHS were commonly labeled psychiatric cases.
Not only are these patients getting treatment that entirely overlooks the true cause; there is a certain stigmatization that occurs when you suffer from a condition that most of society does not yet understand or even believe in.

/////////////////
