### Phil — 2023-11-07T23:14:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/infra-red-light.28269/post-328015>

> [Espresso said:](/goto/post?id=327510)
>
> Fitting heat sinks with double sided tape pretty misses the point. In order to get the heat out, one would need to have direct contact with the heat source. Might as well just use blocks of wood to stiffen up the panel.

I used thermal adhesive tape, I could have used thermal adhesive glue. The future models of the improve panel will have heat sinks built in for added support and to dissipate some of the heat. This panel is pretty low intensity so heat sinks are not really needed. Just using blocks of wood is actually a good idea

### Espresso — 2023-11-08T10:16:47+0200

Well, you have taught me something. I didn't know that one could get thermal adhesive tape. Thanks.

# Thread: Raw eggs as a base for Leptin Reset
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-eggs-as-a-base-for-leptin-reset.28270/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Elkim — 2023-10-22T11:00:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-eggs-as-a-base-for-leptin-reset.28270/post-327477>

I'm not a much breakfast person since forever and always had issues to eat large breakfasts. When it comes to animal products alone without a side dish, it's even worse. 3-4 scrambled eggs is max. I can do before a switch flips in my head and I can't swallow it anymore a start retching - and that's only if I have some carbs like sourdough bread. Same thing with meat or fresh cheese without carbs to rotate between bites. Forcing it causes a lot of stress. I suspect it might be a eating disorder caused by the Leptin resistance.

However I found I could hack it with just raw eggs blended a bit and drink full of glass it. The rest of the 50g of protein I could probably finish with bacon and fresh cheese or pecorino.

I can't think reason why would raw eggs be bad choice but asking anyway. Those would of course be pasture-raised eggs from happy hens. It also seems like the least hustle option as it take few minutes to prepare and I can take it outside to A.M. sunlight.

Thanks

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2023-10-22T11:47:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-eggs-as-a-base-for-leptin-reset.28270/post-327479>

> [Elkim said:](/goto/post?id=327477)
>
> I'm not a much breakfast person since forever and always had issues to eat large breakfasts. When it comes to animal products alone without a side dish, it's even worse. 3-4 scrambled eggs is max. I can do before a switch flips in my head and I can't swallow it anymore a start retching - and that's only if I have some carbs like sourdough bread. Same thing with meat or fresh cheese without carbs to rotate between bites. Forcing it causes a lot of stress. I suspect it might be a eating disorder caused by the Leptin resistance.
>
> However I found I could hack it with just raw eggs blended a bit and drink full of glass it. The rest of the 50g of protein I could probably finish with bacon and fresh cheese or pecorino.
>
> I can't think reason why would raw eggs be bad choice but asking anyway. Those would of course be pasture-raised eggs from happy hens. It also seems like the least hustle option as it take few minutes to prepare and I can take it outside to A.M. sunlight.
>
> Thanks

Back when I was doing keto and I was late for dinner, I went into a convenience store and purchased six organic eggs ( it was about 8.30pm and I was hungry )

I then went out side the store and bit the tops off, and drank them down all six of them. Half an hour later I felt replenished.

I would say in my opinion that there's nothing wrong with raw eggs from a recognised source

### JanSz — 2023-10-22T23:16:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-eggs-as-a-base-for-leptin-reset.28270/post-327483>

> [Dr Rohen Kapur said:](/goto/post?id=327479)
>
> I would say in my opinion that there's nothing wrong with raw eggs from a recognised source

I worked in the city, visiting my parent who had little farm in the sticks.
On the way home I would go with the suitcase full of eggs.
No refrigeration, stored in the place that was hot, near roof of the home.
Eggs were sometimes waiting to be eaten for 2 months or more.
I drank them raw.
By the end, 1/4 of the egg was missed, evaporated.
Newer got sick via eating anything, not only raw eggs.

I still think that storing eggs in refrigerator is a waste of refrigerator's space.
...

### Anthony Smith — 2023-10-23T01:40:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-eggs-as-a-base-for-leptin-reset.28270/post-327485>

Eggs fresh from a farm and NOT bleached can be stored out of the fridge just like you did - BUT store bought eggs have been washed in bleach and have lost their natural protective coating and MUST be refrigerated.

### Highlander999 — 2023-11-06T08:02:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-eggs-as-a-base-for-leptin-reset.28270/post-327904>

I read somewhere that raw eggs are fine, the biggest cause for any issue (contaminant) is from the shell; so I guess avoid dropping the shell into the mix - I like my raw eggs in a glass with a small amount of milk. As mentioned, a nice quick meal!

# Thread: Holistic Dentistry
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/holistic-dentistry.28273/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Clydesdale — 2023-10-23T23:35:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/holistic-dentistry.28273/post-327500>

Greetings,

Went to the dentist recently for the first time in several years and have a couple cavities I'm told need to be filled.

Does anyone have experience going to a holistic dentist for this issue and what did they use to fill it? The place I went to wants to use either metal or some sort of composite resin, which I think they said is plastic.

Any advice on what should be used ideally to fill these?

I saw a link in another thread to search for biological dentists, but wanted to ask for any input from those who may know best practices.

Thanks!

### coho3131 — 2023-10-24T02:50:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/holistic-dentistry.28273/post-327502>

I think gold is the best material for fillings. If it was my mouth, I would ask the dentist what will happen if the "cavities" are not filled (besides him making less money).

### Saichi — 2023-10-24T04:42:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/holistic-dentistry.28273/post-327507>

Wouldn't put any metal in the body since it's conductive, composites the way to go. Unfortunately there's hydrogel in lidocaine now though.

### ryantiefen — 2025-03-18T19:14:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/holistic-dentistry.28273/post-348839>

Just went to Jack's recommended dentist in El Salvador and he recommends resin. He recommended to me to replace my mercury amalgams with resin.

### Aaron K — 2025-03-18T23:12:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/holistic-dentistry.28273/post-348863>

worth making sure they are BPA free also... more common than they used to be a decade ago.

### christine_fp — 2025-03-18T23:15:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/holistic-dentistry.28273/post-348864>

> [ryantiefen said:](/goto/post?id=348839)
>
> Just went to Jack's recommended dentist in El Salvador and he recommends resin. He recommended to me to replace my mercury amalgams with resin.

Are you going to remove your amalgams? I've had a couple done and want to do the rest but have been reading that its likely more harm than good taking them out because of how the lighting in dental offices interacts with the mercury vapours.

### Adamo — 2025-03-19T01:45:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/holistic-dentistry.28273/post-348872>

Are there no options for biomimicry, using minerals/stones?
I could have sworn I have seen this, but perhaps not for filling.

### Aaron K — 2025-03-19T02:28:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/holistic-dentistry.28273/post-348873>

there are ceramic fillings... i can't find it in my records, but i think that may be what i had a decade ago to avoid the BPA in composites.

### S Hudson — 2025-03-21T01:46:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/holistic-dentistry.28273/post-349005>

I'm a former dentist with bachelors and masters level education, so I'm biased with flawed thinking

1) If you are getting cavities your diet is shagged and there may also be systemic issues causing low salivary buffering capacity. Get rid of the sugar and drink more water as a preventative. Certain diseases (diabetes and kidney issues) can impact buffering capacity. The evidence for systemic fluoride in water is poor to none existent. There is a plethora of evidence for fluoride in toothpaste, but you can bet none of these studies had light controls. If your body and diet are sound, fluoride isn't even worth considering.
2) Caries is caused by a triad of substrate/time/organic flora. The more frequent sugar(substrate) you eat promotes bacterial growth which forms a biofilm called plaque (flora). This coats the teeth, and as the plaque matures (time), the cariogenic species proliferate (assuming they are in your mouth. Brushing your teeth disrupts the biofilm. Little known belief held by a few of us dentists is that caries is an infectious disease, the bacteria of the newborn acquired from the parents
3) The only material I would consider for most work on back teeth would be gold as it's relatively inert. But then you have to look at what is being used to cement that gold in.

Available materials:
**Amalgam** \- filling
Cons - Metal and contains mercury.
Pros - quick, cheap, durable, relatively low on the "can the dentist fuck it up scale", contains silver so acts as a kind of low grade weed killer for secondary caries

**Composite** \- Filling and cement
Cons - BPA Release, Unpolymerized monomers, Microplastics, requires high skill levels and is high on the "can the dentist fuck it up scale".
Pros - looks like teeth, durable

**Glass ionomer** \- Filling and cement
Cons - Aluminium, fluoride, monomers in resin modified versions, low durability and high failure rate for fillings
Pros - chemically bonds to teeth, quick and good as a temporary option. low on the "can the dentist fuck it up scale", Makes for a good cement for indirect restorations. The fluoride does make it resistant to secondary decay.

**Porcelain** \- Inlay/Crown (seems to be a favourite for the holistic guys)
Cons - abrasive over time, needs to be cemented in, can break, high on the "can the dentist fuck it up scale", expensive and usually requires 2 visits (except for CADCAM)
Pros - looks nice, inert

**non-precious metal** \- Inlay/Crown
Cons - Nickel-Chromium (Ni-Cr), so can corrode if you get a cheap alloy. Silver colour (although can be coated in porcelain for crowns). Two visits. needs to be cemented in
Pros - cheap, strong can be chemically bonded

**Zirconia**
Cons - Can wear opposing teeth, expensive, a complete bastard to remove or cut through, needs cementing, two visits, high on the "can the dentist fuck it up scale",
Pros - strong, tooth coloured, Stable, hypoallergenic, as close to zero-risk with regards to materials as it gets

**Gold** \- Inlay/ crown
Cons - gold coloured, needs cementing in, bloody expensive, uber high on the "can the dentist fuck it up scale", two visits
Pros - If done well, longest lasting restoration. Inert. Durable

Now a thing about crowns... when you cut down a tooth for a crown, unless that tooth is dead, the dentist is cutting into living tissue. The more tooth that is cut away, the more chance there is of causing irreversible change in the health of that tooth. the more aesthetic you go (porcelain/Zirconia) the more tooth surface has to be removed because these materials are not great in thin section. That means the risk of the tooth dying post procedure increases which take you into the mystical realms of root canals.

So for an inlay or a crown, in my flawed and biased opinion, gold is the best bet. It's been used for over a hundred years, it can be made in thin sections and can minimise tooth destruction. It doesn't wear the opposing teeth, and it mechanically retained through the cavity design allong with the chosen cement that holds it in place. the dentist needs to be technically excellent though.

### ryantiefen — 2025-08-15T20:29:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/holistic-dentistry.28273/post-358305>

> [christine\_fp said:](/goto/post?id=348864)
>
> Are you going to remove your amalgams? I've had a couple done and want to do the rest but have been reading that its likely more harm than good taking them out because of how the lighting in dental offices interacts with the mercury vapours.

I had heard the same and therefore had refrained, but the dentist in El Salvador had me reconsidering. He informed me of ways dentists can add extra care to counteract the noxious mercury vapors when removing the amalgam (e.g. completely isolating the tooth in question from the rest of the teeth/mouth, at least to the best of their ability anyway). Only very astute dentists seem to be doing this, like biological dentists.

### Jen Hollywood — 2025-08-15T21:32:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/holistic-dentistry.28273/post-358317>

### DLO — 2025-08-22T02:48:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/holistic-dentistry.28273/post-358602>

Jack says not to mess with them and he hasn't taken his out.
I've had mine taken out with a biological dentist, felt pretty good after not sure if it was the IV they gave me pre/post.

Now onto the gongshow....
My mom with dementia thought she was using toothpaste but rather it was her hand cream. Quickly threw that crap out.
She had the bad habit of not being able to sleep and eating sweets while she was up all night and not brushing.
Got an infection and tooth had to come out, doc said she needs ONE filling. I didn't think that was a big deal.
So she goes and comes back with 5 amalgums and said they gave her a really good deal!
Now I'm debating whether it's worthwhile to go back and ask for the xrays to see the 5 cavities and sueing based on that. Or even showing them that the EU has banned the use of amalgums as of Jan 2025. Quite the dirtbag move of the dentist trying to cash out on her while she is still around. Did these guys not have ethics courses to get through school?
Around the same time she was using a hearing aid for the first time only to find out the thoughtful ear doc connected it to her phone so she could adjust between surround sound and focused sound in front of her. So having wireless crap around her new amalgums are causing them to dump even hard i'm sure.
The fall out is now my mom's dementia has gotten way worse can't remember her passwords or how to use elevators etc etc.
I was going to try stemcells and such for the dementia but i figure why not start with a solid foundation and take the 5 and however many she had previous to that out.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-11-12T15:16:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/holistic-dentistry.28273/post-362546>

Something new I am following.

Regrowing teeth treatments present huge potential

And this important research is going on with some interesting results.

South Korea developed a patch that regrows teeth — ending dentures forever
Scientists at Seoul National University have created a microneedle patch that stimulates dormant tooth stem cells to regenerate enamel and even grow entirely new teeth in adult humans. The patch contains a drug called tideglusib combined with growth factors that activate dental stem cells still present in the jaw.
Clinical trials showed remarkable results:

Small cavities completely healed in 4-6 weeks
Chipped teeth regrew enamel within 8 weeks
In 30% of participants, completely new tooth buds began forming
No pain, no drilling, no fillings needed

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

How it works: The patch is applied to the gum above the damaged tooth for 20 minutes daily. Microneedles penetrate the gum tissue painlessly, delivering the drug directly to the tooth root where stem cells reside. The drug inhibits a protein (GSK-3) that normally prevents tooth regeneration in adults.
This could revolutionize dentistry:

3.5 billion people suffer from oral diseases
Dental tourism generates $10 billion annually
Dentures and implants cost $10,000-$40,000
Regrown teeth are genetically your own—no rejection risk

South Korea plans to commercialize the patches by 2026, pricing them at around $300 per tooth—a fraction of implant costs. The same technology might work for regrowing bone in other parts of the body.
Source: Seoul National University School of Dentistry, Science Translational Medicine 2025

So glad that you are following/posting on this! I find this a very fascinating field.

Ever since learning, through you, about Robert O. Becker's work, I have always wondered why bone in the mouth cannot repair/regenerate similarly to the way bone in the body can. I understand that the teeth are exposed (have a different structure, etc), but I have always had the intuition that teeth could be repaired though the incentives are not there for the industry.

I heard that there were some tests/trials going on using lasers and specific light (colour/frequency) to illicit the same effects.
The Japanese were trialing drugs/pills to regrow the tooth from the bud as well.

### S Hudson — 2025-11-17T15:59:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/holistic-dentistry.28273/post-362731>

> [Adamo said:](/goto/post?id=362582)
>
> So glad that you are following/posting on this! I find this a very fascinating field.
>
> Ever since learning, through you, about Robert O. Becker's work, I have always wondered why bone in the mouth cannot repair/regenerate similarly to the way bone in the body can. I understand that the teeth are exposed (have a different structure, etc), but I have always had the intuition that teeth could be repaired though the incentives are not there for the industry.
>
> I heard that there were some tests/trials going on using lasers and specific light (colour/frequency) to illicit the same effects.
> The Japanese were trialing drugs/pills to regrow the tooth from the bud as well.

The enamel is formed as the tooth is erupting. It is formed by a sheath building up layer upon layer. That sheath is destroyed when the tooth erupts, thus there is nothing there to then repair the enamel when it becomes damaged. Dentine is different as it retains a blood supply through it structure which can allow the dentine to react to external traumas.

As for talk of specific "regrow enamel without fillings" science, the news has been talking about this for the last 40 years, the same way they have been talking about anti caries vaccines. Neither have produced a product that has yet to hit the market

### MattB99 — 2026-04-29T00:29:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/holistic-dentistry.28273/post-372366>

Teeth are neuroectoderm so I guess they would not regenerate like bone as Becker discovered. I am skeptical of conventional wisdom that says enamel can't regrow. Seems like evolution would have found a way by now considering the severe consequences of a tooth infection. On the other hand perhaps cavities are so rare in nature that there was never adequate selective pressure to develop a mechanism to regrow enamel.

### S Hudson — 2026-04-29T00:59:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/holistic-dentistry.28273/post-372370>

> [MattB99 said:](/goto/post?id=372366)
>
> Teeth are neuroectoderm so I guess they would not regenerate like bone as Becker discovered. I am skeptical of conventional wisdom that says enamel can't regrow. Seems like evolution would have found a way by now considering the severe consequences of a tooth infection. On the other hand perhaps cavities are so rare in nature that there was never adequate selective pressure to develop a mechanism to regrow enamel.

well the enamel is laid down in layers by ameloblasts, like someone building a brick wall. Unfortunately when the tooth erupts, the cells doing that layering are destroyed. Odontoblasts exist within the tooth and can lay down fresh dentine and can even cause the obliteration of much of the pulp chamber in some cases

# Thread: How many faces does Quantum mechanics have?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-many-faces-does-quantum-mechanics-have.28274/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2023-10-24T03:30:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-many-faces-does-quantum-mechanics-have.28274/post-327503>

11 different interpretations of Quantum mechanics explained in brief

1\. Copenhagen Interpretation: The "standard" interpretation where quantum systems exist in superpositions until measured, at which point they "collapse" to a definite state.

2\. Many-Worlds Interpretation (MWI): Every quantum event spawns countless parallel universes, with each possible outcome actually occurring in a different universe.

3\. De Broglie-Bohm (Pilot Wave) Theory: Quantum systems are guided by "pilot waves" that determine their behavior, implying that particles have definite positions at all times.

4\. Objective Collapse Theories: Quantum systems spontaneously collapse to definite states over time, without requiring a measurement.

5\. Quantum Bayesianism (QBism): Quantum states are subjective beliefs about the outcomes of experiments, emphasizing a Bayesian approach to probability.

6\. Relational Quantum Mechanics: The properties of a quantum system are relative to the observer and do not exist absolutely.

7\. Transactional Interpretation: Quantum events involve a time-symmetric exchange of "offer waves" and "confirmation waves" between source and detector.

8\. Ensemble Interpretation: Quantum mechanics only applies to ensembles of systems, not individual systems, emphasizing statistical outcomes.

9\. Consistent Histories: Focuses on establishing a consistent framework to discuss sequences or "histories" of quantum events over time.

10\. Quantum Logic: Proposes a modification of classical logic to account for quantum phenomena.

11\. Participatory Anthropic Principle (PAP): Observers play a role in bringing the universe into existence through quantum processes.

None of these interpretations alter the core mathematical formalism of quantum mechanics, but they provide different perspectives on what's "really" happening beneath the calculations. The debate over which interpretation, if any, correctly describes nature is ongoing and remains one of the central philosophical questions in the foundations of quantum theory.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: Robert O. Becker's: "The Body Electric"and a cancer quote...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/robert-o-beckers-the-body-electric-and-a-cancer-quote.28276/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### RisleyDC — 2023-10-25T02:52:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/robert-o-beckers-the-body-electric-and-a-cancer-quote.28276/post-327525>

Halfway through Beckers book and found a provocative cancer theory by G. Andres of Switzerland (pg. 216). "A normal cell becomes cancerous by dedifferentiation. This change is not dangerous per se, according to Andres, but, because it occurs in a postfetal animal, **the controls that would normally hold these neo-embryonic cells in check aren't working."**

"Those animals that regenerate best are least susceptible to cancer. In general, as complexity increases up the evolutionary ladder from worms to humans, regeneration decreases, and cancer becomes more common. (Salamanders) have tremendous regenerative abilities and almost no cancer. Even to give them tumors in the laboratory requires much effort."

When the controls of light are not present or are present in the wrong wavelengths... This brings new insight to comments made about metastasis as cells "looking for light" by Dr. Kruse

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-10-30T21:54:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/robert-o-beckers-the-body-electric-and-a-cancer-quote.28276/post-327681>

Interesting. Thanks for sharing!

### Johan Lindøm — 2023-10-31T08:50:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/robert-o-beckers-the-body-electric-and-a-cancer-quote.28276/post-327710>

# Thread: Just FYI for Travel to Playa del Carmen, regarding EMF
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-fyi-for-travel-to-playa-del-carmen-regarding-emf.28278/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 47
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Evee — 2023-10-26T23:24:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-fyi-for-travel-to-playa-del-carmen-regarding-emf.28278/post-327564>

Hi all, I'm staying in Playa del Carmen for awhile and have my Trifield. Just FYI, magnetic fields, I'm assuming from how various electrical wires are grounded, seem to have wild variations here. Walking around I can get low readings in some spots and then some of the highest I've ever seen in others. If I was to do this again, I might book a place for a week or few days, and then check out where I was trying to stay longer in person. I've walked under hot spots that send the meter into the 40s and above  Where I'm staying seems to average .7, so not great, but not horrid and trying to focus on the positive that I'm not at one of those hotspots. (For context bed at home averages .2) Beach is of course zeros so headed back out there soon. Just figured I post this info though in case it's helpful for anyone else.

### Ronald Waters — 2023-10-27T00:22:47+0300

I'm on the coast in Colombia, not as dense as PDC but I didn't bring my meter, I'm moving outside the city on Sunday so it will be better.
What are you finding for prices on short term rentals there?

### Ronald Waters — 2023-10-28T03:21:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-fyi-for-travel-to-playa-del-carmen-regarding-emf.28278/post-327590>

I'm renting a house next to the beach in a gated community 3 bedroom 2 bath 65 a night. I was surprised to see the prices in PDC are rather reasonable. How are you finding the environment and beach? Food and cost?
Transportation? Can you see yourself staying there for 3 months?

### Evee — 2023-10-29T04:02:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-fyi-for-travel-to-playa-del-carmen-regarding-emf.28278/post-327620>

> [Ronald Waters said:](/goto/post?id=327590)
>
> I'm renting a house next to the beach in a gated community 3 bedroom 2 bath 65 a night. I was surprised to see the prices in PDC are rather reasonable. How are you finding the environment and beach? Food and cost?
> Transportation? Can you see yourself staying there for 3 months?

I've only been here 4 days but so far my impression are: it's very touristy, weather is amazing, people are nice. The Centro beach is very resort/club vibes with narrow public access, but if you walk up to like 77 (I think or ish about) there's a beautiful wild beach. I've only taken 1 cab so far but it was reasonable. Food is also reasonable, even eating in touristy places it's still coming in a bit less than eating out in the north eastern United States. There's a Walmart if you want really cheap groceries etc.. I'm down here for 7 weeks but given this MG thing, I don't think I'd stay longer. Walked a couple blocks with meter at 33 today. (Over by the highway & Ave Constituyentes) Unfortunately I have to work as a means of being down here so I'm stuck inside part of the day and it's bumming me out that I'm sitting in levels worse than what I left. Other than that though, it's great and I'd have no complaints. And for context, I'd say I'm pretty easy going about most stuff. I'm living out of a carry on and backpack for 7 weeks BUT I get a little fanatical about health stuff so that's why I'm hung up on the MG issue
Thoughts on Columbia?

### Ronald Waters — 2023-11-01T02:31:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-fyi-for-travel-to-playa-del-carmen-regarding-emf.28278/post-327741>

I'm staying 20 minutes from Central Santa Marta , a 6 Dollar cab ride each way..
Staying in a gated community on the beach.
Colombia 8s going to be a bigger adjustment than moving to PDC. Little to no inglés and poco gringos.
Also no longer any flights from Miami so would have to travel through a gateway City in Colombia
But good seafood and Sunshine. Andean spring water Plus the Ocean is like a swimming pool most mornings....

### Saichi — 2023-11-05T17:48:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-fyi-for-travel-to-playa-del-carmen-regarding-emf.28278/post-327883>

> [Ronald Waters said:](/goto/post?id=327863)
>
> What will w causing these fields to be present on the beach? Are you referring to cell signal? Military communication? Marine activities? If I'm in the water isn't that creating a faraday cage? What's the best meter to measure these RF signals?

Water's only a faraday if you're scuba diving. If you're swimming or bathing in the ocean your head's above water and the micros are going straight through your head into rest of body. Anyone who's built a faraday room on land knows any little gap in continuity lets in a huge amount of micros and can never achieve safe levels unless the background levels weren't over a 100 micros in the first place.

I do CT in far Northern California mostly deserted beach where the closest cell tower's over a mile away and the dunes separate the beach a good distance from highway 101 and on some parts of the beach I'll measure up to 500 micros.

I just posted the best meters are Gigahertz Solution and SLT's meter. Trifield, Cornet, Acoustimeter, and all the cheap ones are worthless in giving actual readings. When SLT's meter first came on the market I was shocked to see it reading up to 15 times what my Trifield was showing.

I agree with Saichi the SLT (Safe & Sound Pro II) or Gigahertz Solution meters are more accurate but they are of course also more expensive ($400+) just for RF. To measure 5G (20GHz - 40GHz) $1100+. That said, if money is an issue, I would rather someone had the less expensive TriField TF2 which measures up to 8GHz ($180+/-) and be aware of not only high RF but also electric fields & magnetic fields in their home and begin to do something about it and then be able to measure their mitigation attempts success. To me the accuracy of the initial high reading is not as important as the actual second low reading (and the difference between the two) having mitigated. If you can’t afford $400+ don’t wait, get a TF2 and get started. I don’t sell any of these meters so I have no skin in the game. I do sell a Body Voltage Kit (BVK) which helps people measure and locate electric field and electrical circuits in their home/bedroom so they can create an EMF mitigated sleep sanctuary. All EMF is cumulative so removing/reducing the in home EMF you can control can be the best way to deal with the outside EMF you cannot control. Reduce your total daily EMF exposure with a sleep sanctuary. [Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

### Saichi — 2023-11-05T21:57:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-fyi-for-travel-to-playa-del-carmen-regarding-emf.28278/post-327898>

Trifield site doesn't list their RF sampling rate but I remember SLT's head tech made a video years ago mentioning it was something like 20 to 50 ms, whereas SLT's is under 5 ms. Advanced 4G and midband 5G are pulsed in shorter times than traditional 4G which the older meters were designed for. I found the video after looking for an explanation why my Trifield was reading low levels when actuality was high levels.

SLT's meter isn't expensive for how essential it is to know how toxic one's environment is. I believe most people don't really want to know because the implications are life changing. It's not easy to face total depravity of biological and psychological destruction and then do one's best to mitigate it.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-11-05T23:09:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-fyi-for-travel-to-playa-del-carmen-regarding-emf.28278/post-327901>

All the new technology and frequencies certainly are making measuring and mitigation more difficult and expensive but what is your health worth? I say start at home with the stuff that is easy to read and deal with #1 the 60Hz (50Hz) AC field and the WiFi/BlueTooth - just turn it off, especially at night. For most that would remove 70%+/- of their daily harmful nnEMF load/exposure. Check [www.antennasearch.com](http://www.antennasearch.com) for local cell towers and at least shield that side of the bedroom (or house) if you have one close by. How close is close, depends who you ask. For many people their neighbors WiFi could be a far greater issue. That said, if your bedroom is on the ground floor, simply putting a metal mesh (metal window mosquito screen) on your fence between you and them could fix that. If you measure, you will know, ignorance (and lack of action) has it’s consequences.

They have those pesky little bugs there, they call them bora-shuta. Smaller than mosquitos.
They are practically invisible.
Walking on the sandy beach, you may think that it is a sand on your legs.
Some is sand, most of it its bora-shutas.
First, they inject numbing saliva on the legs, then they suck your blood.
After anesthesia wears out, it starts itching.
Scratching the place causes infection.
People are walking on the beach with crudy looking skin on their legs.
Takes time before it fully heals (if you are visitor).
Permanent skin blemishes for locals.
Look up locals' legs, to figure out what place you are in.
\----
Lots of critters to watch for.
In your shower, watch for little frogs, some are poisonous.
On the benches salamanders.
On the grass tarantulas.
Snakes that you watch their colors.

Which one is going to kill you, this or that?

....

### Anthony Smith — 2023-11-06T22:27:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-fyi-for-travel-to-playa-del-carmen-regarding-emf.28278/post-327954>

Fiber optic cables transport LIGHT not electricity . . . That said - when they lay them in the ground they put a bunch of other things/wires in that do carry electricity . . . And THAT’s the problem, not the actual fiber optics. Mute point I guess! But the Fiber Optic infrastructure is something else: This from Jeromy Jonson - [https://www.emfanalysis.com/fiber-optics-increasing-electrical-sensitivity/](https://www.emfanalysis.com/fiber-optics-increasing-electrical-sensitivity/)

There can be a significant problem with the high-speed fiber optic converters out at the street (or in the home with some newer fiber systems) that create these electrical data signals. This is because the converters are not designed with low-EMI emissions in mind. Thus, their power supplies and operation can generate high amounts of wide-spectrum EMI (electromagnetic interference). This inadvertent EMI then piggybacks on the copper cable and phone lines into our homes where it can radiate from every copper wire. This EMI from the fiber optic infrastructure is a primary reason why electrical sensitivity is increasing when high-speed internet is installed in our communities.

I say start at home with the stuff that is easy to read and deal with #1 the 60Hz (50Hz) AC field and the WiFi/BlueTooth - just turn it off, especially at night. For most that would remove 70%+/- of their daily harmful nnEMF load/exposure. Check [www.antennasearch.com](http://www.antennasearch.com) for local cell towers and at least shield that side of the bedroom (or house) if you have one close by. How close is close, depends who you ask. For many people their neighbors WiFi could be a far greater issue. That said, if your bedroom is on the ground floor, simply putting a metal mesh (metal window mosquito screen) on your fence between you and them could fix that. If you measure, you will know, ignorance (and lack of action) has it’s consequences.

Great point! I used antenna search, and Google maps to make sure there were no cell phone towers right around where I was staying when booking my Airbnb, they don’t give you the address, but often the approximate pin on the map is close-ish, and most places allow you 24 hours to cancel once you get the actual address if you want to cross reference it then

### Evee — 2023-11-06T23:11:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-fyi-for-travel-to-playa-del-carmen-regarding-emf.28278/post-327966>

>
> There should not be too much magnetic field unless close to power lines. Electric fields again close to power lines (in the air and underground) and within 8 feet of buildings.

Yeah, it’s shocking to me how bad some magnetic in PDC are. There must be wiring errors or poorly grounded poles everywhere.
I literally looked at the electrical wires outside the Airbnb on Google maps before I booked it, thinking that I could avoid high magnetic field by understanding where the wires were, but there must be some really weird grounding situations here, because there are high magnetic fields all over the place. And not just an obvious spots where you see massive high tension wires. I had to go to an appointment near some of the big pylons by the highway and the magnetic fields were like 30. But I’ve also seen that level on streets without massive pylons for wires, so TBH so I’m confused. Can poles just be grounded that badly? The worst I had seen in New Jersey was 16 and I thought that was a horror show. On average, it’s nothing to walk through like 2 to 6 here. Quinta Avenida, depending on where you are, can run anywhere almost 0 to over 10.

And I don’t think I have a meter error for two reasons, one this is a new meter that checked out normal levels for my home before I left too. I see the same reading of predictable patterns over and over for the same spots here in PDC so there’s nothing erratic that I’m getting for each location.

But I’ve mapped out some of the low spots, and I work at the same café every day because it’s got a nice .1-.3 depending on where you sit

> [Evee said:](/goto/post?id=327966)
>
> Yeah, it’s shocking to me how bad some magnetic in PDC are. There must be wiring errors or poorly grounded poles everywhere.

In a properly wired home the magnetic field from the power wire is cancelled out by the magnetic field from the neutral. On service power lines there is no neutral which is why they all have magnetic fields.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-fyi-for-travel-to-playa-del-carmen-regarding-emf.28278/post-328278>

> [coho3131 said:](/goto/post?id=328216)
>
> What is "SLT's meter" called?
> Is it Safe and Sound Pro II RF meter?

Yes, it's essential for everyone here. The most recent version measures up to 3.18 million micros, the GS meter with a 20 dB attenuator only measures up to 2 million. I was going to purchase the GS meter but GS doesn't offer a 30 dB attenuator to measure up to 20 million. Of course once you max out at 2 million you should leave that area immediately, but since I still spend time at ground zero in NYC I'd like to be able to measure up to 10 or 20 million so I know what the actual peak levels are for apts in close proximity to macro cells.

I no longer fly through LAX after their renovations since some of their gates maxes out the SLT meter.

If for some reason you need a directional antenna to locate an RF source then you'd want to get the GS meter along with the SLT.

### JanSz — 2023-10-30T02:39:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/other-food-options-for-chitin-iodine-and-carotenoids.28281/post-327653>

**Food options for chitin, iodine, and carotenoids

Chitin:** Chitin is a type of fiber found in the shells of crustaceans such as shrimp, crab, and lobster.
It is also present in the cell walls of fungi.
Some food items that contain chitin are shrimp, crab, lobster, mushrooms, and yeast.

**Iodine:** Iodine is an essential mineral that is required for the production of thyroid hormones.
Some food items that are rich in iodine are seaweed, cod, yogurt, milk, and eggs.

**Carotenoids:** Carotenoids are organic pigments that are found in plants, algae, fungi, and bacteria.
They are believed to be linked to a lower risk of chronic disease and cardiovascular illness and could even potentially help prevent or fight some cancers.
Some food items that contain carotenoids are:
Carrots are an excellent source of carotenoids.
Just half a cup of cooked carrots contains 6499 mcg of β-carotene and 2945 mcg of α-carotene.
Spinach: Spinach is another great source of carotenoids.
Half a cup of boiled spinach contains 6875 mcg of β-carotene and 14906 mcg of lutein + zeaxanthin.
Kale: Kale is a cruciferous vegetable that is packed with nutrients.
Half a cup of boiled kale contains 5735 mcg of β-carotene and 12803 mcg of lutein + zeaxanthin.
Pumpkin: A single cup serving of pumpkin, boiled and mashed, has 6652 mcg of α-carotene, 5135 mcg of β-carotene, and 2484 mcg of lutein + zeaxanthin.

I hope this helps!
/

### JanSz — 2023-10-30T02:45:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/other-food-options-for-chitin-iodine-and-carotenoids.28281/post-327655>

**I’m sorry to hear that you cannot eat shellfish**. Here are some other sources of chitin:
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

-   [**Mushrooms**: Chitin is present in the cell walls of fungi, including mushrooms](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chitin).

-   **Insects**: Insects are another source of chitin. [Some insects that contain chitin are **crickets**, **grasshoppers**, and **beetles**](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chitin).
-   **Algae**: Some species of algae contain chitin. [For example, the cell walls of diatoms, which are a type of algae, contain chitin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chitin).
-   [**Fungal mycelium waste sources**: Chitin can also be derived from fungal mycelium waste sources](https://link.springer.com/chapter/10.1007/978-1-4614-1247-2_4).

I hope this helps!
[](https://www.mushroomrevival.com/blogs/blog/chitin-an-important-component-in-mushrooms)

# Thread: Hi From Flagstaff
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-flagstaff.28284/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Emily Burgoon D.O. — 2023-10-31T00:00:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-flagstaff.28284/post-327694>

Hello, I'm happy to be here finally. Been listening and reading for a few months now and putting the light changes and Wim Hof breathing into practice, seeing significant changes in energy levels that took a solid 2 months to kick in.
The thing that has me so excited about all this is that it's taking me back to what I have always loved: nature. I have always had an alternative, outsider's view of the centralized system, and as a teen and college student I read constantly, eventually finding Tom Brown Jr's books. This lead me to his Tracker School, where in the 2000s I spent countless weeks/months taking classes, volunteering, interning etc.. in primitive wilderness survival skills, tracking, nature observation and spirituality/awareness training. Foundational to the nature observation, spiritual awareness and tracking I learned there was always two things that are central here as well: 1. morning "sit spot" out in nature to watch the sunrise, prayer and thanksgiving, set intentions for the day, nature awareness in wide angle vision as a doorway to spirit and 2. getting out in the elements away from the insulated comfort zone of society, including entering cold water as a practice.
I've always known how hopelessly corrupt medicine is, and this is partly what lead me to osteopathy as a specialty. It was the only place I felt I could train and maintain my integrity and conscience. Since Tracker School I've been through osteopathic medical school, residency, starting my own practice in osteopathy, health crisis with lyme, autoimmunity and trauma, raising my daughter in spite of her father etc.. In the insanity of survival I lost track of spending time in nature, started wearing sunglasses and keeping my corrective lenses on out on the trail... Now I'm learning the science of why I need to get out again with the glasses off for my health and survival, and am so glad to be integrating this into all the other skills I've pursued in alternative medicine over the past 10 years.
My question is for other practitioners here - tips on integrating it into your practice, creating educational guidelines for patients etc..

Glad to hear. I love osteopathy as well. I feel like I'm part of a beautiful and sacred lineage.
One of my favorite quotes from A.T. Still:
“An Osteopath is taught that Nature is to be trusted to the end.”
also:
“To find health should be the object of the doctor. Anyone can find disease.”
In biodynamic cranial osteopathy we make a lifelong study of interfacing therapeutically, perceptually with all the subtle rhythms and rates and forces in the human organism, and its relationship to the environment. In service to the patient and as students of Health or Source. I wonder sometimes how much of that will someday be explained by biophysics.
I love looking at the old pictures of Still - always studying his books out in the yard, outdoor cadaver dissection etc.. They lived a very different life from our modern lives.

# Thread: Magnetico Sleep Pad - EMF Shielding

**Subforum:** The EMF Rx

### Mike117 — 2023-10-31T14:24:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-sleep-pad-emf-shielding.28286/post-327714>

Hi Jack,

When purchasing an Magnetico Sleep Pad, is it necessary first to shield the bedroom completely from EMF since the pad contains metal? Or is the benefits from the Magnetico going to offset any negative effects from EMF in the environment?

Thanks!

### Anthony Smith — 2023-10-31T18:46:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-sleep-pad-emf-shielding.28286/post-327725>

I know you asked Jack but I went to the Magnetico web site and in the Q&A they say no electronic devices should be close to the pad. Since any interference comes from the electric and magnetic fields put out by these devices and the wires in the wall next to or floor under the bed put out 60Hz AC field 6 - 8 FEET in all directions, it might be a good idea to turn off any circuits in and around the bedroom, which EVERYONE should do anyway, Magnetico or not. With electric and magnetic fields it is much easier to just turn off the source rather than shield. Measure and know exactly what nnEMF you have - [www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

From the Magnetico web site: “As per our FAQ, if you choose to use any electronic devices near or on the Sleep Pad, you do so at your own risk. Magnetic fields can interact with electronic devices, and we cannot guarantee no temporary or permanent damage won’t happen with your device. Dr. Bonlie also does not suggest using other devices such as ground pads/sheets, PEMF devices, or or other electronics near you while you sleep as these can interfere with the function of the Sleep Pad.”

### RitaC — 2023-10-31T20:19:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-sleep-pad-emf-shielding.28286/post-327730>

I would like to know if a Magnetico pad could be used in combination of a EMF protection bed canopy.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-11-01T01:54:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-sleep-pad-emf-shielding.28286/post-327737>

> [RitaC said:](/goto/post?id=327730)
>
> I would like to know if a Magnetico pad could be used in combination of a EMF protection bed canopy.

Great question. Magnetico do not recommend ground pads/sheets, I am guessing because these are metallic and go between you and the magnets so could interfere. A bed canopy is also metallic but would not be between you and the magnets but you would be sleeping inside a metallic “envelope”. Could be worth a call to them to find out.

### Brent Patrick — 2023-11-01T03:15:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-sleep-pad-emf-shielding.28286/post-327743>

> [Mike117 said:](/goto/post?id=327714)

> Hi Jack,
>
> When purchasing an Magnetico Sleep Pad, is it necessary first to shield the bedroom completely from EMF since the pad contains metal? Or is the benefits from the Magnetico going to offset any negative effects from EMF in the environment?
>
> Thanks!

I have had my 20 Gauss Magnetico Sleep Pad since late 2017 ...I have kill switches installed so no EMF is coming through from the walls.
The other thing I done was remove the spring mattress and replace with a springless mattress, Bonlie has said that a spring mattress increases the effect of the Magnetico. I am of the opinion this is not correct and only acts as an antenna for nnEMF.

The Magnetico is going to offset nnEMF ,however to receive maximum advantage with the Pad it would be beneficial to increase your Seafood if you are receiving negative effects from the pad when first sleeping on it as some members of this forum have experienced.

When I do not sleep on the Magnetico I notice my sleep is fragmented!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-sleep-pad-emf-shielding.28286/post-327813>

I personally dont follow what Bonlie has preached about the Magnetico ..I really don't think he understands the science
I would actually be knuckling down and learning everything Jack has taught regarding the Magnetico.

The Magnetico is an excellent product but the science delivered from the company is basic to say the least and does not explain much.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-11-03T05:29:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-sleep-pad-emf-shielding.28286/post-327814>

It is my understanding that static magnetic fields can be very beneficial for everyone - the Magnetico is one option. As Jack says they can help people who have been exposed to nnEMF. That said, the best way to deal with nnEMF exposure, whenever possible, is to turn off the source and either not be exposed in the first place or at least significantly reduce your exposure. Leaving the 60Hz AC electric & magnetic fields in your space as they are and thinking ANY product is going to compensate for their negative affect on you is . . . counter productive & lazy. KILL, turn off, reduce - ALL the nnEMF in your life, especially your home, that you can - then add these beneficial devices. Remember Jack spent thousand$ extra building his place to be low nnEMF and then added these things.

A kill switch and magnetico cover my hours of sleep.

Much talk of faraday cages but one of the best faraday cages is the one inside you.....**Metabolic Water.**

# Thread: Cold and hour a day, living in the sun, skin improving
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-and-hour-a-day-living-in-the-sun-skin-improving.28288/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shanti — 2023-10-31T22:19:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-and-hour-a-day-living-in-the-sun-skin-improving.28288/post-327735>

It’s my last month in California. Soon I’ll return back to my home in Costa Rica. The hardest part of leaving is that my husband chooses to stay in California.

I’m maximizing my cold exposure (and sun) with long exposures of floating and swimming in the ocean and sleeping with just a flannel sheet covering me. It feels like my blood is made of coolant. I can surf without a wetsuit for an hour too without getting cold. But I’m the only one I know that’s doing this. Most everyone around me except my husband is pale. But this is also a similar experience for me in Costa Rica. I hope to find my peeps.

Yesterday I had a dexa scan. My fat levels increased from 19% to 26% over last year and my bone density dropped from 1.3 to 1. I wonder if this test is a centralized scam because perhaps my WAT changed to BAT and it’s all good?

I don’t understand the bone mineral loss. Perhaps this is just a variation in testing and insignificant? My skin has become better at healing the “precancerous “ swollen and tender solar keratosis and atrophic skin. So I’m seeing overall progress at age 58.

Grateful for Jack and our tribe.

Even if that picture is one year old.
You look much less than 19% fat.
Check your BMI compare with the desirable.
But you look like you are well within where you are supposed to be.
\===============
Since you are 59yo, so more than 35 or 40 yo,
You may want to try NO2U.com lozenges.
If that makes a difference that you could easily recognize (after about month of their use), then you would likely keep using them and additionally use about 10mg/day of tadalafil.
Overall, that should significantly improve your blood circulation.
But from looking at you and the way you describe yourself, just enjoy it.

# Thread: Why all centralized MDs must learn about the non visual photoreceptive system.  The Public's health is at risk until they do.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-all-centralized-mds-must-learn-about-the-non-visual-photoreceptive-system-the-publics-health-is-at-risk-until-they-do.28289/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2023-11-01T14:49:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-all-centralized-mds-must-learn-about-the-non-visual-photoreceptive-system-the-publics-health-is-at-risk-until-they-do.28289/post-327752>

Why all centralized MDs must learn about the non visual photoreceptive system. The Public's Health is at risk until they do.

Did you know plant life and animal life are linked by their absorption and emission spectra? I discussed this with Bill and Rick in Tetragrammaton Part 3 not yet released. Here is how I figured this out in the museums of NYC as a kid. Today I am walking the same halls in the Hayden Planetarium in NYC. New blog out.

The distinctive increase in the red portion of Earth's global reflectance spectrum is called the vegetation red edge in astrobiology literature and is one of the proposed surface biosignatures to search for on exoplanets and exomoons. Earth's surface vegetation has only been widespread for about half a billion years, providing a surface biosignature for approximately one-ninth of our planet's lifetime. However, as chlorophyll is present in many forms of life on Earth, like cyanobacteria, algae, lichen, and corals, as well as leafy vegetation, such a spectral red edge feature could indicate a wide range of life, expanding its use for the search for surface biosignatures beyond vegetation alone to a time long before vegetation became widespread on Earth. The implications for this and the Cambrian explosion is long and entangled.

[https://www.patreon.com/posts/quantum-59-part-92079106](https://www.patreon.com/posts/quantum-59-part-92079106)

### JanSz — 2023-11-01T14:54:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-all-centralized-mds-must-learn-about-the-non-visual-photoreceptive-system-the-publics-health-is-at-risk-until-they-do.28289/post-327754>

**A non-visual photoreceptive system
is a mechanism that enables organisms to sense light without the use of eyes or visual perception.**
This system is present in both vertebrates and invertebrates, and it is responsible for a variety of physiological and behavioral responses.

In mammals, this system is based on intrinsically photosensitive retinal ganglion cells (ipRGCs) that use the photopigment **melanopsin** to detect light.
These cells are responsible for regulating circadian rhythms, pupil constriction, and other non-visual responses to light.

In invertebrates, non-visual photoreception is not limited to eyes or eye-like structures.
Instead, it occurs through a variety of mechanisms such as the use of extraocular photoreceptors.

.............

### JanSz — 2023-11-01T15:40:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-all-centralized-mds-must-learn-about-the-non-visual-photoreceptive-system-the-publics-health-is-at-risk-until-they-do.28289/post-327756>

### [NOC | Remote Sensing: Principles and Applications](https://archive.nptel.ac.in/noc/courses/noc21/SEM1/noc21-ce11/)

 archive.nptel.ac.in

Course abstract
Remote sensing (RS) is the technology that helps to gather information about objects and phenomena from a distance.
There has been a radical transformation in the technology from the early application of â€˜image interpretationâ€™ to the paradigm of quantitative RS.
The advancement in sensors and data processing algorithms have led to multiple applications of RS in various domains.
To perform quantitative RS, one must understand the basic nature of RS sensors, the interaction between electromagnetic radiation and earth surface features and the assumptions and limitations of the algorithms applied.
This course will enable the participants to learn about the necessary physical concepts involved in different phases of RS which will help in better appreciation of algorithms and existing datasets.
The concepts will further be strengthened through explanation of selected applications.

### JanSz — 2023-11-01T17:49:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-all-centralized-mds-must-learn-about-the-non-visual-photoreceptive-system-the-publics-health-is-at-risk-until-they-do.28289/post-327762>

Oceanic anoxic events or anoxic events describe periods wherein large expanses of Earth's oceans were depleted of dissolved oxygen, creating toxic, euxinic waters.

**UVA vasodilators** are medications that help to widen blood vessels by relaxing the smooth muscles in the walls of the arteries and veins.
**This results in increased blood flow and reduced blood pressure.**
They are used to treat a variety of conditions, including high blood pressure, heart failure, and pulmonary hypertension.
Some examples of UVA vasodilators include hydralazine and **minoxidil**.

**Minoxidil** is a medication that is used to treat hair loss.
It is applied topically to the scalp and works by increasing blood flow to hair follicles, which can stimulate hair growth.

Like **all medications**, minoxidil can **have side effects.**
Some of the most common side effects include skin irritation, itching, and unwanted hair growth.
In rare cases, minoxidil can cause more serious side effects such as chest pain, rapid heartbeat, and difficulty breathing.
If you experience any of these symptoms while using minoxidil, you should seek medical attention immediately.

It’s important to note that minoxidil is not recommended for everyone.
For example, it should not be used by people with certain heart conditions or those who are pregnant or breastfeeding.

# Thread: Where to move?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-move.28291/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### 1oevets — 2023-11-02T08:20:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-move.28291/post-327776>

Little background, We are a military family stationed in Alaska. We love it here, life style wise. My wife is a ob nurse and a few years from retirement. My wife is halotypr j1ci Fitzpatrick 1 blonde blue eyes. My youngest is the same complexion. These two have all the health issues living here. Not sure my halo type but olive toned 100% Italian that tans even when it’s cloudy and my oldest is a spitting image of me. We found we were living in emf hell next to HV transmission lines, 3 5g antennas surrounding us and very out of touch circadian biology living here as you could imagine.

We’ve moved to a new emf friendly house, bought magnetico sleep pads, I pulled my youngest from school started homeschooling, use no artificial lights after sunset, We see early morning sun everyday (sunrises more now that’s it’s actually setting), grounding daily, We also eat surf and turf style carnivore.

Anyway the wife is up for reassignment and need to rank our top 3 choices. We will probably turn it down if we don’t get a favorable location and stay here for a couple years. So I need suggestions/feedback on where we are looking.

In all locations we would likely live up to 50 miles outside of base

Our top 3 are currently

1 Colorado Springs Air Force Academy- we both lived in Colorado Springs as well as the Breckinridge area before the military and love Colorado.
We would mitigate the flouride problem in the water here but love the weather and recreation opportunities. I’m wondering how altitude would affect my son?

2 San Antonio - Lackland Afb a bit hotter then we would care for b I haven’t looked into the water quality yet and not sure if this

3 Destin Elgin Afb I know Jack likes the gulf but this is where my wife’s problems began while stationed in biloxi. We are a bit apprehensive of moving back to the south. We both have a hard time coping with humidity but would try to do things differently this time.

### JanSz — 2023-11-02T16:00:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-move.28291/post-327783>

Not answering your question, but
since you have mentioned
“surf and turf style carnivore”
which means Steak and Shrimp or lobster
but likely you are eating animal meat and seafood (bought in the stores) (any place, organic or not)
For a moment I thought to turn to venison.
Then quickly found that people raise those animals of farms too.
So, the venison does not mean that wild animal was killed for it.
With above in mind:
If you could arrange that you or your friends could kill actually wild animals or birds, they may be a good food source.
From the stores just eggs, and wild fish, animal protein vise.
\--------------
Your problems may not only be due to nnEMF but also,
or primarily from insanely high Linoleic Acid, eventually it or its metabolites creating inflammation.

You may consider taking the most complaining, health vise, in your family,
and get him/her this test:
Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum
Then
figure out LA/ALA ratio.
The ideal would be about 1 (one)
Commonly is about 30, thirty.
Laboratory will tell you that about 34 is right in their opinion.
But
sky is the limit for the most complaining.
I have already seen 180 in a girl on the 200 acres farm that is far from nowhere, and about electricity read in the book.
.............

### JanSz — 2023-11-02T20:16:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-move.28291/post-327799>

> [1oevets said:](/goto/post?id=327797)
>
> The thing that concerns me with altitude and my son is his **bun 19/creatinine .3 ratio is 63.** Bristol is 4, urine normal color not frothy. Skin turgor is good. All other labs are excellent. The **drs say it’s just his high protein diet** and aren’t concerned.

The **BUN/Creatinine ratio** is a blood test that can reveal a lot about your metabolism, kidney, liver, and overall health. BUN measures the amount of urea in your blood, while creatinine is a waste product created from the normal wear and tear of muscles.
[The normal range for BUN/Creatinine ratio is anywhere between **5 – 20 mg/dL**](https://labs.selfdecode.com/blog/bun-creatinine-ratio-high-low-levels-normal-range/).
The ideal ratio is between 10:1 and 20:1

A high BUN/Creatinine ratio indicates an underlying disease/disorder and will usually be accompanied by the symptoms of this underlying disorder.
[The causes shown here are commonly associated with high BUN/creatinine: **decreased blood flow to the kidneys, such as congestive heart failure or dehydration**](https://www.medicinenet.com/what_causes_a_high_bun_creatinine_ratio/article.htm)**.**
If he drinks less than about 2.5 liters of water daily, that may be one of the problems.
How to check blood flow to the kidneys, do not know.
**I am not a doctor.**
Thinking of checking heart
**Coronary Calcium Scan, Agatston score.
In young boy it should be zero.**
Insurance does not cover that.
In my place in NJ I pay $100 for that test.
It is done in places where they do MRI.
\--------------------
Other symptoms:

-   Fatigue
-   Shortness of breath
-   Swelling in your legs, ankles, or feet
-   Nausea
-   Vomiting
-   Loss of appetite
-   Confusion
-   Irregular heartbeat

////////////

Decreased blood flow to the kidneys can be caused by a variety of conditions, including renal artery stenosis.
Renal artery stenosis is the narrowing of one or more arteries that carry blood to your kidneys.
This narrowing can prevent enough oxygen-rich blood from reaching your kidneys, which can lead to kidney damage and **high blood pressure.**

Symptoms of renal artery stenosis may include high blood pressure that begins suddenly or worsens without explanation, **high blood pressure that begins before age 30** or after age 50, and worsening kidney function during treatment for high blood pressure.
\--------------------------------
If he has high blood pressure
I would consider NO2U.com
lozenges.
Likely using Cialis (Tadalafil) could help too.
But blood pressure reducing pills will just make things worse.

In this case using toothpaste, mouth wash, would make things worse.
Same goes for upset stomach, any PPI would make things worse.

//////////////////

### JanSz — 2023-11-03T18:54:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-move.28291/post-327828>

Astragalus is an herb that has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries.
[It is believed to have many health benefits, including immune-boosting, anti-aging,](https://microsoftstart.msn.com/en-us/health/ask-professionals/expert-answers-on-astragalus/hp-astragalus?questionid=r2ncqfnj&type=nutrition&source=bingmainline_nutritionqna)
[and anti-inflammatory effects](https://microsoftstart.msn.com/en-us/health/ask-professionals/expert-answers-on-astragalus/hp-astragalus?questionid=r2ncqfnj&type=nutrition&source=bingmainline_nutritionqna).
Some of the potential benefits of astragalus include:

-   **Boosting the immune system**: Astragalus contains beneficial plant compounds that may enhance your immune system. [It may increase your body’s production of white blood cells, which are responsible for preventing illness](https://microsoftstart.msn.com/en-us/health/ask-professionals/expert-answers-on-astragalus/hp-astragalus?questionid=r2ncqfnj&type=nutrition&source=bingmainline_nutritionqna) .
-   [**Reducing inflammation**: The active compounds in astragalus may help reduce inflammation in the body](https://www.healthline.com/nutrition/astragalus).
-   [**Improving heart function**: Some studies indicate that astragalus might improve heart function in individuals with heart failure](https://microsoftstart.msn.com/en-us/health/ask-professionals/expert-answers-on-astragalus/hp-astragalus?questionid=r2ncqfnj&type=nutrition&source=bingmainline_nutritionqna).
-   [**Treating hay fever**: Astragalus can be used to treat hay fever](https://microsoftstart.msn.com/en-us/health/ask-professionals/expert-answers-on-astragalus/hp-astragalus?questionid=r2ncqfnj&type=nutrition&source=bingmainline_nutritionqna).
-   [**Treating diabetes and kidney disease**: Astragalus may help treat diabetes and kidney disease](https://microsoftstart.msn.com/en-us/health/ask-professionals/expert-answers-on-astragalus/hp-astragalus?questionid=r2ncqfnj&type=nutrition&source=bingmainline_nutritionqna).

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/parathyroid-hormone.27122/post-314831Humans](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/parathyroid-hormone.27122/post-314831Humans) at the bare minimum need to get 25(OH) Vitamin D up to 50ng/ml 125nmol/l to optimize PTH.

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=314831)
>
> Most people can avoid calcium stones by reducing/eliminating high calcium food sources or making sure they eat natural foods with Vitamin K2 (raw milk cheeses and charcuterie). We can easily increase our citrate intake UP to a limit TO PREVENT CALCIUM AND URIC ACID STONES.
> Citrate is an intermediate in the TCA cycle which is the central metabolic pathway for animals, plants, and bacteria. Citrate synthase catalyzes the condensation of oxaloacetate with acetyl CoA to form citrate. Citrate then acts as the substrate for aconitase and is converted into aconitic acid. Aconitase is where metabolic water is recycled and this makes it vulnerable to deuterium kinetics. This is why processed food is dangerous for humans because its hydrogen atoms have no protection from the photosynthetic pathways to make sure of the fidelity of the isotope of hydrogen around specific carbons. The cycle ends with the regeneration of oxaloacetate in the TCA cycle.
> [https://kidneystones.uchicago.edu/citrate-to-prevent-stones/](https://kidneystones.uchicago.edu/citrate-to-prevent-stones/)
> My choice is to use lemon-lime in my water and use shellfish with high citrate concentrations like Royal Red Gulf Shrimp. You can also use a half teaspoon of Magnesium citrate powder is simple to dissolve in 2 liters of water and if consumed throughout the day with food. It usually does not cause diarrhea in my experience. Most people consume less than the magnesium RDA daily so have too much calcium in relation to magnesium. Most people who are Mg deficient have a high BUN/creat ratio from melanopsin dysfunction dehydration. This implies they also have significant thiamine issues. If we all consumed more thiamine/magnesium we would see fewer people complaining about problems with vitamin D levels because it would support water formation in the matrix and skin to perform the photoisomerization step of LDL to Vitamin D intermediates. Magnesium speeds up the raising of 25(OH)D and reduces the rate of increase over 30ng/ml so protects you from the results of excess calcium.

> Most people who are **Mg deficient have a high BUN/creat ratio**

Hey,

So I'm wondering about supplementation. I saw on the website that here is a Top Ten Paleo Supplements list. But I also heard Uncle Jack saying he is not a big fan of supplementation. I eat mostly animal protein/products (organic, grass-fed, etc.), seasonal fruits (max. 2 pieces a day) and sometimes low-oxalate vegetables. But is this enough? Or is it smart to supplement? And what kind of supplements? I found one company that provides these in one bag: Omega 3, Vitamine D3, Selenium, Jodium, Zink, Vitamine B, Vitamine K2, Magnesium and Vitamine A. And what about the probiotics? Is that also smart to supplement like AG1? Or get my body too lazy then?

Thanks!!

We do not need sunscreens.

Sunds interesting.
Wonder if it eventually will be available for a purchase as a separated cream?
Wonder if it makes sense using it (regardless of all that positive sounding research)?
And at this time, I do not worry about using it for any wound healing,
but only as a way to deal with Pro-opiomelanocortin (POMC) that seems to get lower in most of us.

### [“Super melanin” cream can protect skin from sun damage and heal wounds](https://newatlas.com/science/super-melanin-cream-skin-protection-sun-damage-heal-wounds/?fbclid=IwAR3UHJKbcIQXnBnLH2s6y89KyK4m7vrbD_9MPKmuZtu2Dz_agUoqqyQetFU)

Researchers have created a cream containing a souped-up, synthetic version of the free-radical-removing melanin we produce naturally to protect skin from sun damage and accelerate the healing of existing damage. They see it being added to sunscreens and moisturizers and as a treatment for burns,…

 newatlas.com

### [Can you have your cake and eat it too? The sunlight D‐lema](https://academic.oup.com/bjd/article/175/6/1129/6698419?login=false&utm_source=TrendMD&utm_campaign=British_Journal_of_Dermatology_TrendMD_1&utm_medium=cpc)

In this issue of the BJD, Felton et al.1 report the results of their elegantly designed study to determine the impact on vitamin D status and skin DNA damage fo

 academic.oup.com

////////////////
Maasai herders who have skin type 6 have circulating levels of 25(OH)D on average of 48 ng mL−1.
Achieving these levels requires the ingestion of 3000–5000 IUs daily.
It is likely that our hunter‐gatherer equatorial ancestors exposed to daily sunlight were making this amount of vitamin D3 and at the same time their high melanin skin content prevented significant DNA damage.
\------------------------
UVB is penetrating into the epidermis to form vitamin D3, it is also absorbed by DNA resulting in the formation of CPD and other pyrimidine photoproducts that if unrepaired have been associated with increased risk for **nonmelanoma** skin cancer.
\-------------------------
