# Thread: LF meter
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lf-meter.28338/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### coho3131 — 2023-11-17T02:04:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lf-meter.28338/post-328357>

The first Gigahertz Solutions LF meter I am looking at goes from 16 Hz to 2,000 Hz.

The second Gigahertz Solutions meter I am looking at is a little more professional, but only goes from 16 Hz to 1,000 Hz.

If I get the second meter, what sources of waves will I be missing in the 1,000 Hz to 2,000 Hz range?

Thank you.

### JanSz — 2023-11-17T02:49:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lf-meter.28338/post-328358>

> [coho3131 said:](/goto/post?id=328357)
>
> The first Gigahertz Solutions LF meter I am looking at goes from 16 Hz to 2,000 Hz.
>
> There second Gigahertz Solutions meter I am looking at is a little more professional, but only goes from 16 Hz to 1,000 Hz.
>
> If I get the second meter, **what sources of waves will I be missing in the 1,000 Hz to 2,000 Hz range?**
>
> Thank you.

**sources of waves 1000 Hz to 2000 Hz**

[The frequency range of 1,000 Hz to 2,000 Hz falls within the **audible range** of human hearing](https://phys.libretexts.org/Bookshelves/University_Physics/Book%3A_University_Physics_%28OpenStax%29/Book%3A_University_Physics_I_-_Mechanics_Sound_Oscillations_and_Waves_%28OpenStax%29/17%3A_Sound/17.04%3A_Sound_Intensity).
[This range is also known as the **midrange** and is responsible for the perception of sounds such as musical instruments, human speech, and birdsongs](https://blog.sonicbids.com/understanding-frequencies-how-to-describe-what-youre-hearing-to-your-sound-tech).
Sources of waves in this frequency range include:

-   **Musical instruments**: The midrange is where most musical instruments produce their fundamental frequencies.
-   [For example, the piano’s middle C note has a frequency of 261.63 Hz, which is within this range](https://phys.libretexts.org/Bookshelves/University_Physics/Book%3A_University_Physics_%28OpenStax%29/Book%3A_University_Physics_I_-_Mechanics_Sound_Oscillations_and_Waves_%28OpenStax%29/17%3A_Sound/17.04%3A_Sound_Intensity).
-   **Human speech**: The fundamental frequencies of most human speech sounds fall within the midrange.
-   [This range is crucial for the perception of consonants and vowels](https://phys.libretexts.org/Bookshelves/University_Physics/Book%3A_University_Physics_%28OpenStax%29/Book%3A_University_Physics_I_-_Mechanics_Sound_Oscillations_and_Waves_%28OpenStax%29/17%3A_Sound/17.04%3A_Sound_Intensity).
-   **Birdsongs**: Many bird species produce songs that fall within the midrange.
-   [For example, the American Robin’s song has a frequency range of 2,500 Hz to 3,500 Hz, which overlaps with this range](https://blog.sonicbids.com/understanding-frequencies-how-to-describe-what-youre-hearing-to-your-sound-tech).

### Saichi — 2023-11-17T04:31:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lf-meter.28338/post-328360>

Package 1 in here: [https://www.emfanalysis.com/recommended-emf-meters/](https://www.emfanalysis.com/recommended-emf-meters/)

I have the ones in package 1 except for the ME3851A, I use the ME3951A instead. Both are single axis gauss meters though which is why you also need the Alpha Labs UHS2.

### coho3131 — 2023-11-17T16:23:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lf-meter.28338/post-328372>

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=328360)

### Anthony Smith — 2024-01-17T06:06:48+0200

Ditto, Alpha Labs UHS2, Safe & Sound Pro II but for electric field my personal choice is the EMFSafe Body Voltage Kit (BVK) . . . But full disclosure - the BVK is my product: [www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

# Thread: Samantha's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/samanthas-journal.28341/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kalikaye — 2023-11-19T01:38:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/samanthas-journal.28341/post-328428>

MY HISTORY 1992-NOW

Pregnancy – no complications

1992 Birth – born almost 3 weeks early, no issues

Infancy – no jaundice, terrible cholic 2 months, ear infection in first year (maybe 2) – oral amoxicillin given to treat

Toddler/early childhood – frequent ear infections (1-2x per year), out of the blue, otherwise pretty healthy, not often sick; ear infections stopped by school-age; regularly took oral/liquid amoxicillin to treat (occasional sinus or ear infection thereafter)

Had FULL schedule of vaccines and boosters through 18 years old.

1998-1999 - 6-7 years old

– diagnosed with asthma, allergy testing indicated environmental allergies (mold, pollen – no food on testing, but lima beans caused hives as a child);

-   Prescribed Advair and Astellin nasal spray to treat, later switched to Symbicort, with albuterol rescue inhaler, later switched to Xopenex.
-   Zyrtec then Claritin daily until around age 15
-   lazy eye correction surgery age 6 or 7
-   Play soccer and dance competitively

2000 – 8 years

– stop competitive dance, gain weight as child – “chubby” rather than fat, plus I was very tall for my age, stayed “chubby” until puberty at age 12, lost “baby fat”

2002 - 10 years old

– migraines begin, tried some medicine (don’t remember – Relafen?), did not work so stopped taking it, migraines disappeared sometime during next 3 ish years.

2004 - 12 years – menstruation begins

2005 - 13 years – took Gardasil vaccines (series of 3), psoriasis appears shortly after

2006 - 14 years – psoriasis on legs, repeat ankle sprains from playing competitive sports (soccer, basketball, softball)

2007-2011 (high school) 15-18 years

-   Stop taking allergy medication regularly.
-   Continue asthma medicine (Symbicort daily, rescue as needed), growing out of asthma symptoms throughout this time
-   Had surgery to remove large wart from heel
-   At 17 – torn labrum and injury to rotator cuff – no surgery to repair to date, continued playing sports
-   At 18 – torn MCL of knee – no surgery to repair to date, continued playing sports
-   At 18 - Psoriasis worsens, lesions largely on legs, some on scalp;
    tested for psoriatic arthritis due to joint pain and stiffness/locking;
    went to specialist who prescribed Methotrexate, took from late high school through sophomore year of college (2011-late 2012).
-   Throughout high school: no dietary restrictions, not a big consumer of dairy products or typical junk foods. Fairly regular binge drinking at weekend parties, clubs, etc. 3-sport athlete played for high school and for competitive travel/club teams, always training and stayed active and fit; summers spent at the neighborhood pool; a lot of time spent outside playing sports, playing with friends, running, etc. Many nights with less-than-optimal sleep due to rigorous school and sports schedule. Very much a “night owl” type.

2011-2016 college

2011-2012 – 18-20 years

– Start birth control, start regular yoga and meditation (2011-present), continue playing competitive soccer

\- Freshman to soph year – psoriasis explodes from on legs and elbows, to stomach, breast, ears, scalp – stopped drinking alcohol, cut out dairy, eggs, sweets/carbs, never really ate a lot of junk food to begin with, limited red meat, ate fish, chicken, and occasional lunch meat, vegetables. Poor sleep (21 credit hours + social life); training for half marathon, ran half-marathon December 2012; played club soccer throughout; despite training and activity level, gained weight (about 10 pounds – from ~125 lbs. at 5’4 to ~135 lbs.).

\- Stopped Methotrexate in late 2012

2013 – 19-20 years

\- Study abroad in Morocco: lifestyle changed to drinking, smoking, eating lots of carbs + sweets (bread and desserts), limited sleep, covered skin outdoors (Muslim country); significant weight gain (from ca. 135 lbs. up to 155 lbs. in approx. 4-5 months).

\-Psoriasis still present on legs, some on elbows

Fall 2013 – 20-21 years

– move to Northern VA, transfer universities

-   Lifestyle: heavy drinking/partying, poor sleep, smoking weed, limited red meat, eating fish and chicken, limited carbs, riding bike everywhere, playing club soccer, taking heavy course load (18-21 credit hours)
-   Weight drops back to around 130-135 lbs.
-   Begin taking Humira biologic injections to treat psoriasis – food allergies begin to appear by following summer; experience occasional “locking” of ankle joints (side effect), psoriasis skin lesions clear away
-   October 2013 - severe concussion, stopped school for one semester to recover, long and short-term memory loss; stopped drinking for short time, but re-started before fully recovered.

2014 – 21-22 years

– return to school, experiment with vegan diet in two week “cleansing” intervals at first, becomes more consistent over time; still in school (18-21 credits) and working part time (paid internship), plus social life, drinking/partying, late nights, poor sleep; return to playing soccer, still riding bike everywhere

\-Taking biologic injections, no psoriasis symptoms externally

2015 – 22-23 years

-   get car for transportation (no longer riding bike frequently); playing club soccer
-   Fully vegan by summer of 2015
-   still in school and working full time hours (office internship during week, restaurant job on weekends) plus social life and partying in between, minimal sleep; late nights

2016 – 23-24 years

-   Still vegan
-   graduate school in May, spend summer outside at pool and working part time, still partying, still taking biologic injection (by this time, might have switched to Taltz)
-   start work at Trader Joe’s in August 2016, evening shift (4 pm – midnight)
-   Change to raw vegan diet winter 2016-2017 for about 8-9 months, plus doing master cleanses, and mostly sober (no alcohol at all, minimal and rare other substances)
-   September 2016 – acute depression over a bad breakup led to not eating for about a month, severe weight loss from ~130/135 to 115 lbs. in about 2 weeks; between Sept 2016 and December 2017 slowly gain weight back to about 120-125 lbs.
-   Consistent yoga/meditation/kriya practice continues

2017 – 24-25

-   stop birth control, continue Taltz injections, first cold sore outbreak
-   Menstruation changes after stopping birth control – 35-day cycle, normal at first, but between 2017-2021 symptoms change – brown spotting 1-2 days prior to bleeding, blood darkens over time and by 2021 blood is sometimes brown with presence of tissues and clots; bleed for 2 days, prior 2 and after 3 are more like spotting; no other menstrual symptoms
-   cold sore outbreak in January (sexually abstinent at this time for several months, don’t know where caught), moved to new townhouse around the same time
-   dancing/going out frequently, but sober
-   Continue working at Trader Joe’s, but move to morning shift (5-1 or 6-2) and working evenings and weekends as a painting instructor at an art studio (weekdays work ~5pm-9 or 10pm weekends work 10 am-11pm or midnight); on days of only one job, spend it outside - exercising, meditating, hiking, tanning, etc.
-   Trip to India for yoga training in fall – very active and outdoors daily for about 2 months, including 1 week trip to beach; gain about 5 pounds back to circa 130-135 lbs., lose weight once returned to US back to 125 lbs. by February
-   Continue dancing multiple nights a week, very active and long work days under artificial lighting; still primarily nocturnal

2018 – 25-26

-   Lifestyle continues from 2017 until August – move to a very rural farm in WV
-   Another cold sore outbreak occurs in July 2018
-   Stop biologic injections cold turkey (withdraw symptom of joints locking, no others, all symptoms disappear within 4 weeks) – psoriasis lesions return on elbows and a little on ears/scalp by winter
-   outside all the time, naked or scantily clad
-   go to NOLA for 1 month, working in open-air warehouse on art projects; moderate sleep, minimal partying but no alcohol; continue vegan diet
-   back on farm in winter – spending a lot of time outside but fully clothed every day; still mostly a night owl
-   winter 2018-2019 – Ayurveda castor oil cleanse and anti-inflammatory diet for 2-3 weeks eliminates all psoriasis and joint pain (stays in remission until spring 2023)

2019 – 26-27

-   spring and summer spent outside all day every day gardening and doing farm chores – naked or minimally dressed
-   summer spend 1 month in STL - outside, active, social, naked/bikini
-   fall 2019 return to STL for mural commissions - mostly night owl, painting murals, socializing indoors and clothed; frequent recurring cold sores begin and endure through late 2019 into 2020

2020 – 27-28

-   start taking Valtrex 500 mg early 2020, appears to work and cold sores mostly stop until spring 2021
-   outdoor life on farm continues (naked yoga outdoors gardening, etc.), until October
-   October – work deployment in MS – severe sleep deprivation over 3 weeks’ time, spending 20 hours a day on a computer, not eating enough and eating poor calories when eating, minimal movement, minimal time outdoors
-   NOLA after for 1 week - tread through flood water (ew) and three weeks later ringworm rash develops around neck, spreads to cover about 75% of body from waist to neck by Jan/Feb; try many herbal, natural, home remedies to alleviate and nothing works; doctors prescribe ketoconazole cream – appears to help at first, but stops helping; rash comes and goes in severity from winter 2020 through December 2021
-   Cold sore outbreak in October during work deployment, appears after eating large amounts of peanut butter (plus, I know now, lots of blue light, etc.).

2021 – 28-29

-   ringworm rash continues on upper body, comes and goes/increases/decreases severity;
-   May 2021 - cold sore breaks through Valtrex after having a beer at dinner and Valtrex never works again; cold sores recur frequently from here to present (2023)
-   Attempt to treat cold sores with lysine, dietary changes, tinctures, and herbs – nothing seems to work very effectively or for very long
-   December 2021 start anti-candida diet with oregano supplement and probiotics, “ringworm” disappears in 2-3 days – likely to have been severe candida expressing on skin from gut; also supplementing with glutamine to help treat candida until May/June 2022
-   Began eating wild game while at home (deer)

2022 – 29-30

-   spend January through May in Centroamerica, “ringworm” stays away, no cold sore outbreaks when on beach in El Salvador Jan-Feb, later outbreaks in Guatemalan highlands (March) and in Oaxaca city in Mexico (May)
-   slow weight gain (eating tortillas, beans, cheese) up to around 130 ish
-   return to US, immediately go to work in NE – always indoors, seated at computer for 20+ hours a day; sometimes outside but in car and professionally clothed; add dairy back into diet with aged cheeses, monitor macros and see calorie deficient/undernourished
-   body composition significantly changes within two weeks from fit to flabby, development of cellulite for first time, first wrinkles on face (forehead and around eyes) and puffy undereyes upon waking
-   work all summer - no sun, poor diet, lots of computer time, sedentary, no or poor sleep
-   return to the farm in August - relax outside, garden, tan, make decision to de-vegan
-   return to work in FL in Sept-November – outside everyday but professionally clothed, lots of screen time, sleep deprivation; weight gain and body composition issues (losing muscle/gaining fat up to 135, but compositionally looks VERY different than at same weight before).
-   Continue diet restrictions for cold sores (no alcohol, chocolate, peanut butter, corn, tomatoes, minimal breads/wheat, minimal fermented foods, no or rare citrus, minimal coconut, no nuts), also treating with turkey tail mushrooms – successful for about 1 year before becoming as ineffective as anything else I’ve tried

2023 – 30-31

-   \-winter at home, indoors, resting and sleeping, computer research and work
-   get outdoors in March to begin gardening, but mostly clothed; CT in creek every day
-   go to work again in March & April – long hours seated, clothed, indoors, computer; improve diet with more seafood, primarily wild caught salmon, still including some dairy (aged cheeses), sometimes eggs
-   get chickens; spring – working outside, minimal clothing while gardening (always barefoot), catching up on sleep still but still not great sleep cycle; CT in creek daily
-   spring – two small (pencil eraser) psoriasis lesions appear on legs, spots appear on elbows
-   June – eating local organic regenerative grass-fed beef and pork
-   leave for CO to work again – indoors in hotels at a computer 18-20 hours a day. Try to balance with better diet and hydration, daily movement, but still largely sedentary and poor light environment.
-   Cold sores recur.
-   Psoriasis spots on elbows, some very small spots on legs
-   August – return to farm, lots of travel to NY and OH, dancing, partying, substances, no alcohol, sunshine but clothed
-   stay on farm throughout fall – grounding, yoga, minimal clothing outdoors; travel to STL in October – suburban poor light environment
-   regularly eating seafood, eggs, and grassfed meats when available (not consistently available)

September 2023 – find this information and immediately implement changes – sleep earlier, rise for sunrise, barefoot and minimally clothed or naked in mornings – walk, breathe, yoga; change indoor light environment, change electronics habits; adding in more protein, but no consistent sources of animal products – have consistent source of daily eggs from chickens; eating salmon primarily and deer tallow and pork fat, grassfed butter; eating homegrown vegetables, which include potatoes and squashes; adding in grassfed beef, shrimp, cod, wild boar, wild elk, and wild deer when/as available, but so far no consistent sources. Supplementing with pea protein powder and grassfed collagen. Unable to consume 50g protein at once in morning – building up, currently around 35g before feeling very ill/bloated and sluggish until afternoon

\-begin CT in creek or tub 2-3 times per week, plus cold exposure to morning air while wearing as little clothes as possible, even when cold

Now

– cycle appears to be regulating somewhat, three months of 28–30-day cycle with no pre-spotting, first day still darker and scant, still some presence of tissues/clots, but more “regular” rich red blood appearing, lasts pretty much a full 7 days with still only 2-3 days of “regular” flow. No other menstrual symptoms.

\-Cold sores still recurring despite still not eating any or rarely any “trigger” foods, still attempting to treat with turkey tail supplements, plus St. john’s wort tincture and licorice root tincture, added back in some lysine supplementation – these measures seem to stop a sore from fully emerging and quicken its healing time significantly, but do not appear to prevent emergence

\-Psoriasis present on scalp (can’t see it but can feel it) and elbows

\-Feeling more energy returning, feeling libido increase slightly but not fully “ON,” feeling a strength slowly return to my body, no weight loss yet by pounds, but seeing subtle shift in body composition/appearance, sleep dramatically improved

\*Through 2019-2023 change diet to prevent cold sore trigger foods (chocolate, beer, coconut, fermented foods, wheat/bread, nuts, corn, tomatoes, peanut butter, popcorn, citrus fruits) – diet is primarily vegan with occasional wild game when hunted or gifted and eggs when gifted from neighbors

\*Throughout 2020-now decrease in energy level and decrease in libido and quality/frequency of arousal – seeing some small changes within the last two months of making conscious shifts in light diet and circadian rhythm

> \- Freshman to soph year – psoriasis explodes from on legs and elbows, to stomach, breast, ears, scalp – stopped drinking alcohol, cut out dairy, eggs, sweets/carbs, never really ate a lot of junk food to begin with, limited red meat, ate fish, chicken, and occasional lunch meat, vegetables. Poor sleep (21 credit hours + social life); training for half marathon, ran half-marathon December 2012; played club soccer throughout; despite training and activity level, gained weight (about 10 pounds – from ~125 lbs. at 5’4 to ~135 lbs.).
>
> \- Stopped Methotrexate in late 2012
>
> 2013 – 19-20 years
>
> \- Study abroad in Morocco: lifestyle changed to drinking, smoking, eating lots of carbs + sweets (bread and desserts), limited sleep, covered skin outdoors (Muslim country); significant weight gain (from ca. 135 lbs. up to 155 lbs. in approx. 4-5 months).
>
> \-Psoriasis still present on legs, some on elbows
>
> Fall 2013 – 20-21 years
>
> – move to Northern VA, transfer universities
>
>
> -   Lifestyle: heavy drinking/partying, poor sleep, smoking weed, limited red meat, eating fish and chicken, limited carbs, riding bike everywhere, playing club soccer, taking heavy course load (18-21 credit hours)
> -   Weight drops back to around 130-135 lbs.
> -   Begin taking Humira biologic injections to treat psoriasis – food allergies begin to appear by following summer; experience occasional “locking” of ankle joints (side effect), psoriasis skin lesions clear away
> -   October 2013 - severe concussion, stopped school for one semester to recover, long and short-term memory loss; stopped drinking for short time, but re-started before fully recovered.
>
> 2014 – 21-22 years
>
> – return to school, experiment with vegan diet in two week “cleansing” intervals at first, becomes more consistent over time; still in school (18-21 credits) and working part time (paid internship), plus social life, drinking/partying, late nights, poor sleep; return to playing soccer, still riding bike everywhere
>
> \-Taking biologic injections, no psoriasis symptoms externally
>
> 2015 – 22-23 years
>
>
> -   get car for transportation (no longer riding bike frequently); playing club soccer
> -   Fully vegan by summer of 2015
> -   still in school and working full time hours (office internship during week, restaurant job on weekends) plus social life and partying in between, minimal sleep; late nights
>
> 2016 – 23-24 years
>
>
> -   Still vegan
> -   graduate school in May, spend summer outside at pool and working part time, still partying, still taking biologic injection (by this time, might have switched to Taltz)
> -   start work at Trader Joe’s in August 2016, evening shift (4 pm – midnight)
> -   Change to raw vegan diet winter 2016-2017 for about 8-9 months, plus doing master cleanses, and mostly sober (no alcohol at all, minimal and rare other substances)
> -   September 2016 – acute depression over a bad breakup led to not eating for about a month, severe weight loss from ~130/135 to 115 lbs. in about 2 weeks; between Sept 2016 and December 2017 slowly gain weight back to about 120-125 lbs.
> -   Consistent yoga/meditation/kriya practice continues
>
> 2017 – 24-25
>
>
> -   stop birth control, continue Taltz injections, first cold sore outbreak
> -   Menstruation changes after stopping birth control – 35-day cycle, normal at first, but between 2017-2021 symptoms change – brown spotting 1-2 days prior to bleeding, blood darkens over time and by 2021 blood is sometimes brown with presence of tissues and clots; bleed for 2 days, prior 2 and after 3 are more like spotting; no other menstrual symptoms
> -   cold sore outbreak in January (sexually abstinent at this time for several months, don’t know where caught), moved to new townhouse around the same time
> -   dancing/going out frequently, but sober
> -   Continue working at Trader Joe’s, but move to morning shift (5-1 or 6-2) and working evenings and weekends as a painting instructor at an art studio (weekdays work ~5pm-9 or 10pm weekends work 10 am-11pm or midnight); on days of only one job, spend it outside - exercising, meditating, hiking, tanning, etc.
> -   Trip to India for yoga training in fall – very active and outdoors daily for about 2 months, including 1 week trip to beach; gain about 5 pounds back to circa 130-135 lbs., lose weight once returned to US back to 125 lbs. by February
> -   Continue dancing multiple nights a week, very active and long work days under artificial lighting; still primarily nocturnal
>
> 2018 – 25-26
>
>
> -   Lifestyle continues from 2017 until August – move to a very rural farm in WV
> -   Another cold sore outbreak occurs in July 2018
> -   Stop biologic injections cold turkey (withdraw symptom of joints locking, no others, all symptoms disappear within 4 weeks) – psoriasis lesions return on elbows and a little on ears/scalp by winter
> -   outside all the time, naked or scantily clad
> -   go to NOLA for 1 month, working in open-air warehouse on art projects; moderate sleep, minimal partying but no alcohol; continue vegan diet
> -   back on farm in winter – spending a lot of time outside but fully clothed every day; still mostly a night owl
> -   winter 2018-2019 – Ayurveda castor oil cleanse and anti-inflammatory diet for 2-3 weeks eliminates all psoriasis and joint pain (stays in remission until spring 2023)
>
> 2019 – 26-27
>
>
> -   spring and summer spent outside all day every day gardening and doing farm chores – naked or minimally dressed
> -   summer spend 1 month in STL - outside, active, social, naked/bikini
> -   fall 2019 return to STL for mural commissions - mostly night owl, painting murals, socializing indoors and clothed; frequent recurring cold sores begin and endure through late 2019 into 2020
>
> 2020 – 27-28
>
>
> -   start taking Valtrex 500 mg early 2020, appears to work and cold sores mostly stop until spring 2021
> -   outdoor life on farm continues (naked yoga outdoors gardening, etc.), until October
> -   October – work deployment in MS – severe sleep deprivation over 3 weeks’ time, spending 20 hours a day on a computer, not eating enough and eating poor calories when eating, minimal movement, minimal time outdoors
> -   NOLA after for 1 week - tread through flood water (ew) and three weeks later ringworm rash develops around neck, spreads to cover about 75% of body from waist to neck by Jan/Feb; try many herbal, natural, home remedies to alleviate and nothing works; doctors prescribe ketoconazole cream – appears to help at first, but stops helping; rash comes and goes in severity from winter 2020 through December 2021
> -   Cold sore outbreak in October during work deployment, appears after eating large amounts of peanut butter (plus, I know now, lots of blue light, etc.).
>
> 2021 – 28-29
>
>
> -   ringworm rash continues on upper body, comes and goes/increases/decreases severity;
> -   May 2021 - cold sore breaks through Valtrex after having a beer at dinner and Valtrex never works again; cold sores recur frequently from here to present (2023)
> -   Attempt to treat cold sores with lysine, dietary changes, tinctures, and herbs – nothing seems to work very effectively or for very long
> -   December 2021 start anti-candida diet with oregano supplement and probiotics, “ringworm” disappears in 2-3 days – likely to have been severe candida expressing on skin from gut; also supplementing with glutamine to help treat candida until May/June 2022
> -   Began eating wild game while at home (deer)
>
> 2022 – 29-30
>
>
> -   spend January through May in Centroamerica, “ringworm” stays away, no cold sore outbreaks when on beach in El Salvador Jan-Feb, later outbreaks in Guatemalan highlands (March) and in Oaxaca city in Mexico (May)
> -   slow weight gain (eating tortillas, beans, cheese) up to around 130 ish
> -   return to US, immediately go to work in NE – always indoors, seated at computer for 20+ hours a day; sometimes outside but in car and professionally clothed; add dairy back into diet with aged cheeses, monitor macros and see calorie deficient/undernourished
> -   body composition significantly changes within two weeks from fit to flabby, development of cellulite for first time, first wrinkles on face (forehead and around eyes) and puffy undereyes upon waking
> -   work all summer - no sun, poor diet, lots of computer time, sedentary, no or poor sleep
> -   return to the farm in August - relax outside, garden, tan, make decision to de-vegan
> -   return to work in FL in Sept-November – outside everyday but professionally clothed, lots of screen time, sleep deprivation; weight gain and body composition issues (losing muscle/gaining fat up to 135, but compositionally looks VERY different than at same weight before).
> -   Continue diet restrictions for cold sores (no alcohol, chocolate, peanut butter, corn, tomatoes, minimal breads/wheat, minimal fermented foods, no or rare citrus, minimal coconut, no nuts), also treating with turkey tail mushrooms – successful for about 1 year before becoming as ineffective as anything else I’ve tried
>
> 2023 – 30-31
>
>
> -   \-winter at home, indoors, resting and sleeping, computer research and work
> -   get outdoors in March to begin gardening, but mostly clothed; CT in creek every day
> -   go to work again in March & April – long hours seated, clothed, indoors, computer; improve diet with more seafood, primarily wild caught salmon, still including some dairy (aged cheeses), sometimes eggs
> -   get chickens; spring – working outside, minimal clothing while gardening (always barefoot), catching up on sleep still but still not great sleep cycle; CT in creek daily
> -   spring – two small (pencil eraser) psoriasis lesions appear on legs, spots appear on elbows
> -   June – eating local organic regenerative grass-fed beef and pork
> -   leave for CO to work again – indoors in hotels at a computer 18-20 hours a day. Try to balance with better diet and hydration, daily movement, but still largely sedentary and poor light environment.
> -   Cold sores recur.
> -   Psoriasis spots on elbows, some very small spots on legs
> -   August – return to farm, lots of travel to NY and OH, dancing, partying, substances, no alcohol, sunshine but clothed
> -   stay on farm throughout fall – grounding, yoga, minimal clothing outdoors; travel to STL in October – suburban poor light environment
> -   regularly eating seafood, eggs, and grassfed meats when available (not consistently available)
>
> September 2023 – find this information and immediately implement changes – sleep earlier, rise for sunrise, barefoot and minimally clothed or naked in mornings – walk, breathe, yoga; change indoor light environment, change electronics habits; adding in more protein, but no consistent sources of animal products – have consistent source of daily eggs from chickens; eating salmon primarily and deer tallow and pork fat, grassfed butter; eating homegrown vegetables, which include potatoes and squashes; adding in grassfed beef, shrimp, cod, wild boar, wild elk, and wild deer when/as available, but so far no consistent sources. Supplementing with pea protein powder and grassfed collagen. Unable to consume 50g protein at once in morning – building up, currently around 35g before feeling very ill/bloated and sluggish until afternoon
>
> \-begin CT in creek or tub 2-3 times per week, plus cold exposure to morning air while wearing as little clothes as possible, even when cold
>
> Now
>
> – cycle appears to be regulating somewhat, three months of 28–30-day cycle with no pre-spotting, first day still darker and scant, still some presence of tissues/clots, but more “regular” rich red blood appearing, lasts pretty much a full 7 days with still only 2-3 days of “regular” flow. No other menstrual symptoms.
>
> \-Cold sores still recurring despite still not eating any or rarely any “trigger” foods, still attempting to treat with turkey tail supplements, plus St. john’s wort tincture and licorice root tincture, added back in some lysine supplementation – these measures seem to stop a sore from fully emerging and quicken its healing time significantly, but do not appear to prevent emergence
>
> \-Psoriasis present on scalp (can’t see it but can feel it) and elbows
>
> \-Feeling more energy returning, feeling libido increase slightly but not fully “ON,” feeling a strength slowly return to my body, no weight loss yet by pounds, but seeing subtle shift in body composition/appearance, sleep dramatically improved
>
>
>
> \*Through 2019-2023 change diet to prevent cold sore trigger foods (chocolate, beer, coconut, fermented foods, wheat/bread, nuts, corn, tomatoes, peanut butter, popcorn, citrus fruits) – diet is primarily vegan with occasional wild game when hunted or gifted and eggs when gifted from neighbors
>
> \*Throughout 2020-now decrease in energy level and decrease in libido and quality/frequency of arousal – seeing some small changes within the last two months of making conscious shifts in light diet and circadian rhythmV

Great work
Glad to hear some things turning around quite quickly for you.

### kalikaye — 2023-12-02T18:30:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/samanthas-journal.28341/post-328860>

I am seeing more and more connections and things clicking into place as I read through the blogs and the Epi-Paleo Rx book. What is funny, is I see how even though I was eating really "clean" - all organic, local or regional produce when possible - hormonally and chemically, I've ended up in a similar place to someone who spent those same years eating doritos and hos hos. I'm finding humor in this rather than beat myself up. I was young. I was naive. I know better now and I am doing better now.

The past week we had company come to visit and I got out of my routine - not spending as much time outside at sunrise, not making it exactly for sunrise, staying up past my bedtime, eating later than normal in the evening, and on their last night here (Thursday), I started to see a cold sore develop.

I also recently started taking monolaurin and BHT to support my journey to decrease these outbreaks. In some ways, I was surprised that this sore started to appear - even though I was out of my routine, I was still spending the days outside hiking and was not around phone/laptop/blue light. In the evenings, we lit candles and lamps and played games and painted, so the light diet and emf exposure was actually less than in a normal day when I am using my phone or my laptop, even intermittently or sparingly. I have been staying away from typical trigger foods as well. and yet...one started to appear. It seems that with all these changes and the supplements, I am able to prevent the sores from fully emerging and they heal very quickly. Sometimes in just 1 day, sometimes up to 3. I would like to get to where they are not emerging at all.

I am a bit torn about focusing only on my environment with this issue, and focusing on diet and other factors. As I have been reflecting, I can see where even when I have been in other states, cities, countries, houses, and climates, in different seasons, with different exposures to blue light and higher/lower EMF (assuming - no gear so never tested), I have still had cold sore outbreaks. So currently, I'm feeling challenged by this puzzle of the relationship between environment and this virus. Perhaps, as I sit with it more and keep learning, I will see a pattern emerge.

The environment at the farm seems like it should be (almost) ideal - super rural, no cell service, electrical lines are not buried for miles and miles around, changed all lightbulbs in the house to no blue light and low emf, only use lights at night when absolutely necessary and keep them low and few, turn off wifi at night, windows are 120 year old single pane (not sure how they filter light, but thinking they let more in than new double pane argon gas windows), and I spend most days outside away from all that junk and in the sun anyway. I eat homegrown produce, and recently been adding wild caught seafood, grass fed butter, local wild game, our own farm fresh organic free range eggs, and locally sourced grassfed grass finished beef and pork when available. Aside from latitude, there's not really much more I would change about this environment. So the fact that I'm still experiencing makes me wonder if there are things going on I can't see, or about which I am making incorrect assumptions (bad emf/dirty electricity exposure, hidden mold, etc).

At the same time, I'm also recognizing I have 10+ years of un-doing to do. So maybe it's not a riddle or puzzle I need to crack, it's a clock and I need to wait on it.

I'm reading through Time and letting a bit of that sink in. I need time. Time, commitment, and patience, and things will change. I trust my body to do its thing once given the right "tools" it needs, the right inputs. The patience is a bit difficult for me, because this cold sore journey has been rough on my self-esteem and my relationship. I've been in the trenches with it trying to "figure it out" for years now, and my patience is much thinner. I'm feeling more an urgency to conquer it or to surrender. But I'm also seeing more expansively that there has been a lot more going on in the complex network of my body and really...the cold sores are more a symptom of a malfunctioning system (hormones, gut, immunity).

I know the changes I am making will help to correct and heal some of these places that are in freak out mode - it will take time. So I'm asking myself every day how patient, compassionate, and GENEROUS can I be with myself in this place?

The psoriasis on my scalp has disappeared. The spots on my elbows are shrinking and the texture/color drastically improving. I have noticed even more subtle changes in the shape and appearance of my body in the last two weeks. No new noticeable changes in libido or energy. I've had a few nights of poor sleep, but seems related to my not-so-great bed situation and comfort/temperature than body-based disturbances. I am working on that, as well. My menstrual cycle will be starting again early next week and I will pay close attention to that process as well. Today, I am feeling pretty slow, restful, a bit tired. It is also a cold rainy/cloudy day and I still feel as if I am recovering a bit from having company all week, along with the hormonal changes of being 2-3 days before menstruation. So maybe slow today is warranted and normal.

I am trusting this whole process to keep unfolding as long as I stay the course. I am learning about labs now and am considering having some testing done this winter so I can have a better understanding of my situation and what is really going on.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-12-02T19:18:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/samanthas-journal.28341/post-328862>

Kalikaye - So the fact that I'm still experiencing makes me wonder if there are things going on I can't see,

If you live in a 120 year old house has it been rewired with modern romex wire or does it still have any of the old “knob and tube” wiring? In knob & tube the power and neutrals are separate resulting in high magnetic fields. You turn off WiFi at night but do you turn off the 60Hz AC power in your bedroom at night? I would recommend measuring your environment for EMF before you start with a bunch of medical tests. Check electric and magnetic fields in your home especially your bedroom at night, this is when your body heals. TriField TF2 is a decent gauss meter (Amazon) and get a Body Voltage Kit (BVK) for electric fields here: [www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

### kalikaye — 2023-12-02T21:42:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/samanthas-journal.28341/post-328867>

The house was rebuilt about 20 years ago. I'm not sure about the wiring, but I can find out from my partner who completed the work. The windows were re-used as well as the exterior. From the outside it looks the same as it did when it was built circa 1901 but from the inside it is updated (minus original wood floors). We do sometimes flip all the breakers to the house when we don't have anything in the freezer. I am currently in a disagreement with my partner about doing this every night for AT LEAST the bedroom. I definitely need to get some meters. Thank you for the recommendations, I will look that up today!

### kalikaye — 2024-02-04T19:12:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/samanthas-journal.28341/post-330999>

Long overdue update:
By Early December I was able to increase myorning single-meal protein intake up to 65g (accidentally went over 50). Yay! Ready to start Leptin rx - just in time for a run of hosting company, travel, and solar winter where I live. I did not make the commitment fully. I went to bed most nights between 9-10 pm, with some exceptions while having company of staying up late (midnight or a few exceptions even later). I woke every morning around 6 or 7 at the latest and got outside immediately. I went as little clothed as possible and barefoot when I could for as long as I could. I found I could do yoga in the yard nude and barefoot as low as 38F.

Near the mid-end of November I began two supplements - monolaurin and Bht to treat the recurring cold sore outbreaks. I started the Bht at 500mg taken as an anal suppository. I continued for about 2 weeks before having an outbreak. I decided the anal suppository was too difficult to time well for proper/full absorption and switched to taking it orally encapsulated in coconut oil on an empty stomach before bed. I continued this for about 3 weeks before having a breakout. I switched to taking the same dose in the same way after breakfast on a full stomach. I had a breakout. I switched back to before bed, empty stomach and increased the dose to 1g. I continued this for about a 4-5 weeks before having an outbreak.
The current outbreak is still healing and occurred about 8 days into a trip to a Caribbean island. The island had one 3g cell tower located on the opposite end of where we stayed (about 2 or 2.5 miles). There was no Wi-Fi where we stayed and the electricity on the whole island was out between 6am and 2pm daily. I was up at sunrise grounding on the beach and in the ocean every day, doing the same at sunset. I ate the hardiest breakfasts I could find (did not have a kitchen) but they were a far cry from Leptin reset quality and quantity of protein. I spent most of every day in the sun or outdoors but in the shade. Very little screen time, some days none at all. I got very slightly sunburnt one day (lightly pink and it faded to tan by the next morning) and the following morning is when the sore appeared. The worst one in years. I also consumed two cacao balls made from raw cacao, nuts, coconut oil, maca, spices, with chocolate historically being a trigger as well, but usually only in very large or consistent amounts.

We left the island and went to the pacific coast. First place we stayed did not have wifi. Very little screen time, most days none. Sunrise and sunset on the beach. Walking and yoga on the beach every day plus swimming. Outdoors all day either in sun or shade. The second place we stayed had wifi and electricity and I got more screen time, but at most I would say 1 hour; one day I did a yoga class online and got and extra hour of screen time (though I was outdoors and not close it or consistently staring at the screen). The screen on my phone is filtered deep red and I have blue blocking glasses. I also travelled with biobulbs (no blue light, low emf) which we used at every stay except this last one. Still spending every sunrise and sunset on the beach and/or in the water, usually from 6-10am and again from 4-7pm with bursts of sun and activity throughout the day in between (walking, swimming, yoga, surfing). Consistency in quality and quantity of protein for breakfast has been a challenge on this trip. Seafood for every dinner. This last week we have had a kitchen and I found decent (but not great) quality meat which I've been eating with eggs for breakfast, seafood at dinner. I have had a little ice cream the past four days.

Today, before the last sore that appeared on the island has finished healing, I feel and see another one forming. I ate something yesterday that had a sprinkle of crushed peanuts on top. I began my menstrual cycle yesterday as well. (Which I will note has been quite regular since September and has improved in quality and cycle time (dropped from 35 days to 30 and less or no pre-menstrual spotting)).

Historically, the most obvious trigger for me for the cold sores has been food. Chocolate, nuts, peanut butter, beer, sometimes wine, fermented foods, citrus, corn, tomatoes, tobacco. I'm trying to reframe my experience to look at food as less of the picture, but at the same time, these cold sores have plagued me repeatedly over the last 6 years and the one consistent thing has been reactions to food. During that time I have lived and travelled in many different environments throughout the year, each year, at different latitudes, typically with good daily exposure to sun, even in winter (with travel). I can't speak to the emf ratings of these myriad places as I did not have any gear throughout this time period. However, I am having a hard time getting over the thought that it's ME. It's my body, my diet, something I am doing or not doing, or not seeing that is causing these sores. Even since consciously changing my schedule, my sleep, my diet, and being more conscious about electricity, wifi, Emf exposure since August-September I cannot seem to hack this cold sore response.

I know it is likely pointing to a low redox potential and high inflammation. The obvious hormone imbalances associated with that. And the fact that in recent months, a pattern of sores emerging more readily just before or during my menstrual cycle (also pointing to hormone issue). It seems my system is super sensitive and not able to handle change or stress well. I should say that aside from the cold sores, I feel healthy and stronger and have not been sick with any other illness in over two years. The two years prior to that I was also not sick, though I caught c0v!d in the middle of the last four years (extremely mild and short lived, only knew I had it bc I lost smell and taste for about 5 days). I have not committed more to this question of my health and the cold sores over the last two months. Excuse, excuse, busy life travel blah. But after these sores emerging through what I would have thought to be an ideal environment to not have any breakouts, my mind is turning again. At the same time that I feel a renewed vigor, I also feel a bit lost. Over the past month in Nicaragua, I've had what feels like lower exposure to emf, no 5g, and at times electricity at all. I have had many many days with no screen time at all and the days with screen time, very little. I have been going to bed early and rising early, and eating acceptably though not fully or cleanly epi-paleo. Lots of grounding and lots of sunlight, no other pinks or burns since the first mentioned above. Gentle exercise/movement every day, and no stress. What more could I ask for? What am I not seeing?

We return home Tuesday and I will be sitting with this. Diving back into the blogs and reading and research. Committing to the Leptin reset once home and settled with consistent access to quality protein in the mornings. Investigating the emf situation in our house and on our land and making changes if and where necessary to mitigate or limit exposure. And other than that, in this precise moment, I can't think of much else. I will continue experimenting with the Bht and the monolaurin. I will give the monolaurin one year. I will try to give the Bht more time as well, and consider changing to the higher dose of 1g but with food once back home. There is not a ton of info I've been able to find about this, and a lot of what I have found has been forums and none too recent. What I read was promising but also seemed it didn't work for everyone and of course with disturbances at the root level it cannot function as a solution. I know I have not gotten to the root. It feels I'm circling a bit, not quite honed in yet. I need better thinking. Using the last of winter for this contemplation.

### doctorwu85 — 2024-02-19T20:21:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/samanthas-journal.28341/post-331666>

> [kalikaye said:](/goto/post?id=330999)
>
> Even since consciously changing my schedule, my sleep, my diet, and being more conscious about electricity, wifi, Emf exposure since August-September I cannot seem to hack this cold sore response.

Have you tried a high-dose Vitamin C protocol to help with the cold sores? I get them very infrequently, but I have had some success with megadosing either powdered (good) or liposomal (better) Vitamin C when I feel one coming. I shoot for about 1000mg every hour during the day, or to whatever your bowel tolerance is. I've found it both prevents it from forming and reduces the severity and time necessary to heal.

# Thread: PT in El Savador? You better believe it!

### Dr. Mimi — 2023-11-19T19:42:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pt-in-el-savador-you-better-believe-it.28343/post-328443>

Physical therapy in El Salvador? The beaches south of Libertad are flat. We’re staying in Playa San Diego seven steps from the beach. My son walked only 10 ft. with a walker in the blue lit PT facility on Tuesday in Philadelphia. After an exhausting day of travel, he rolled around in the ocean for a couple of hours in the afternoon and then walked about ¾ of a football field with the walker on Thursday. And took 20 steps in the ocean without the walker on Friday. The healing is palpable! On our way to Los Pelicanos for lunch…

# Thread: Greetings from the Desert Southwest in the US
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-the-desert-southwest-in-the-us.28346/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mtnhiker — 2023-11-20T19:32:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-the-desert-southwest-in-the-us.28346/post-328483>

Hi Everyone,

I recently encountered Dr. Kruse's work through Rick Rubin's podcast and that opened my eyes to the beautiful landscape that you all are exploring here.
I've been exploring red light therapy in recent months and honestly not making much headway due to my mysterious reactions to it along with natural sunlight exposure. I'm finding that even short doses of red light + NIR from a RLT panel or natural sunlight can kick up significant irritability and depression. This reaction seems to have developed in the last two years after a confirmed mold exposure. I also have a lingering heavy metal issue as well. Couple that with imbalanced methylation at present and I'm not the best detoxifier.

Putting aside speculation on the underlying cause, I'm trying to find a way in that doesn't magnify the irritability / depression too much and thought I'd see if anyone has suggestions. With the red light panel (RedRush 400), a 1 minute dose of continuous red light and NIR at a 3 feet distance still seems to be too much. The next experiment I'm thinking of trying there is to focus solely on the NIR.

With sun exposure, I was trying 20 minute doses toward the end of the day when UVB was gone but UVA was still present. The next step there might be to simply dial that dose way back.

I've been struggling with gut issues for a long time and am curious about how sunlight exposure might help with gut motility. If I'm reading the literature on that correctly, it seems like midday sun could have a profound effect on the microbiome and I hope on motility as well. Curious if anyone has seen a relationship between motility and light exposure on the gut.

That's enough for now. Grateful to be here and for the opportunity to learn in community!

Chris

### Saichi — 2023-11-20T20:40:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-the-desert-southwest-in-the-us.28346/post-328484>

# Thread: Medical Examination Requirements for Former Astronauts
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/medical-examination-requirements-for-former-astronauts.28348/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JanSz — 2023-11-20T22:35:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/medical-examination-requirements-for-former-astronauts.28348/post-328492>

### [Medical Examination Requirements (MER) for Former Astronauts - NASA](https://www.nasa.gov/directorates/esdmd/hhp/medical-examination-requirements-mer-for-former-astronauts/)

Tests: Complete Blood Count – hemoglobin, hematocrit, red blood cell count, red blood cell indices, white blood cell count, differential count, platelet

 www.nasa.gov

### Medical Examination Requirements (MER) for Former Astronauts​

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

#### Robert E. Lewis​

#### Web Community Manager​

MAR 16, 2023
ARTICLE

### CONTENTS​

-   [Laboratory Testing](https://www.nasa.gov/directorates/esdmd/hhp/medical-examination-requirements-mer-for-former-astronauts/#section-1)
-   [Musculoskeletal](https://www.nasa.gov/directorates/esdmd/hhp/medical-examination-requirements-mer-for-former-astronauts/#section-2)
-   [Dermatology](https://www.nasa.gov/directorates/esdmd/hhp/medical-examination-requirements-mer-for-former-astronauts/#section-3)
-   [Ophthalmology/Optometry](https://www.nasa.gov/directorates/esdmd/hhp/medical-examination-requirements-mer-for-former-astronauts/#section-4)
-   [Audiology](https://www.nasa.gov/directorates/esdmd/hhp/medical-examination-requirements-mer-for-former-astronauts/#section-5)
-   [Cardiopulmonary](https://www.nasa.gov/directorates/esdmd/hhp/medical-examination-requirements-mer-for-former-astronauts/#section-6)
-   [Gastroenterology](https://www.nasa.gov/directorates/esdmd/hhp/medical-examination-requirements-mer-for-former-astronauts/#section-7)
-   [Reproductive Health](https://www.nasa.gov/directorates/esdmd/hhp/medical-examination-requirements-mer-for-former-astronauts/#section-8)
-   [Behavioral Health](https://www.nasa.gov/directorates/esdmd/hhp/medical-examination-requirements-mer-for-former-astronauts/#section-9)

Bioanalytical Core Lab
NASA

### Laboratory Testing​

#### MER1001 Hematology (annually)​

**Tests:** Complete Blood Count – hemoglobin, hematocrit, red blood cell count, red blood cell indices, white blood cell count, differential count, platelet count.
**Rationale:** The results of this test are used to diagnose conditions and diseases such as anemia, infection, and thrombocytopenia. There currently is no occupational surveillance activity, associated with an exposure-identified occupational health risk, that requires the information from this test. This screening is a standard of care and is included in the annual Lifetime Surveillance of Astronaut Health (LSAH) examination. These data are also needed to support Epidemiology’s astronaut surveillance activities for detection of unknown adverse health conditions that may be related to occupational exposures.

#### MER1002 Biochemistry (annually)​

**Tests:**

-   Liver function – AST, ALT, GGT, bilirubin, ALP, LDH
-   Renal function – urea, creatinine, electrolytes (Na, K, Cl), uric acid
-   Endocrine – TSH, free T4
-   Fasting blood glucose and hemoglobin A1c
-   Cardiovascular profile – fasting total cholesterol, HDL, LDL, non-HDL
-   cholesterol, triglycerides
-   HS-CRP
-   Calcium, magnesium, inorganic phosphate
-   Prostate-specific antigen (males) beginning at age 50
-   Iron, total iron-binding capacity, percentage saturation of transferrin, ferritin

**Rationale:** A chemistry panel determines an astronaut’s general health status, elucidating the body’s electrolyte balance, arbohydrate, lipid status, and status of several major body organs, such as the kidney, liver, and thyroid gland. The long-term effects of radiation and microgravity exposure are poorly understood. Although the short-term health effects of radiation exposure seem benign in low Earth orbit, the long-term effects of exposure to relatively low doses of radiation on various organ systems are unknown and need to be monitored. The United States Preventative Services Task Force (USPSTF) Guideline: The USPSTF strongly recommends screening men aged 35 and older for lipid disorders. The USPSTF strongly recommends screening women aged 45 and older for lipid disorders if they are at increased risk for coronary heart disease.

View of Dan Burbank,Expedition 30 Commander; and European Space Agency (ESA) Andre Kuipers,Expedition 30 Flight Engineer (FE),during Integrated Immune Blood Sample Draw at the Human Research Facility (HRF),in the Columbus Module. Photo was taken during Expedition 30.
NASA

#### MER1003 Urinalysis (annually)​

**Test:** Standard urinalysis test strip.
**Rationale:** There currently is no occupational health risk surveillance activity associated with an identified occupational exposure, that requires the information from this test. This screening is a standard of care and is included in the annual LSAH examination. These data are also needed to support Epidemiology’s astronaut surveillance activities for detection of unknown adverse health conditions that may be related to occupational exposures.

View of personnel at work with InVia Raman Microscope in Bldg 13 Chemical Analysis lab rm 258.
NASA

### Musculoskeletal​

#### MER2001 Bone Health (Every 3 years)​

**Test:** Dual-energy X-ray absorptiometry (DXA) and bone health serologic markers.
**Rationale:** CHS manages an astronaut-specific bone health program through the JSC clinic. This aspect of long-term astronaut monitoring is unique to NASA and the astronaut corps. Concerns exist that exposure to the space flight environment (microgravity, space radiation, and elevated CO2 levels) may cause a decrease in bone strength and, thus, an increase in the risk of non-traumatic fractures for astronauts relative to healthy non-astronauts. A major risk to crew health is the change in bone ultimate strength with respect to mechanical loads; it is possible that a fracture may occur from activities that would be unlikely to induce fracture before space flight. From an occupational health perspective, occupational surveillance needs to be conducted throughout the lifetime of astronauts to detect health issues and develop mitigation strategies to reduce the risk.

### Dermatology​

#### MER3001 Clinical Evaluation (Annually)​

**Test:** Visual exam of the skin with photo documentation of any abnormalities.
**Rationale:** Although the difference is not statistically significant, the rate of melanoma skin cancers are 59% higher in astronauts than in a control group. The incidence rate of diagnosed non-melanoma skin cancers appears to be increased post-flight. The incidence of non-melanomas skin cancers is not reported to cancer registries; therefore, the literature regarding its incidence in the general population is limited. Because of the limited documentation, this exam is needed to gather data to better understand the incidence rate in the astronaut population. Participants are referred to a dermatologist when results of the exam indicate the presence of potential skin cancer.

View of Urine Collection Device (UCD) – Male Bellows Catheter,Serial Number (S/N): A5577,Barcode: HRF07140J,and Kona Coffee drink container in the Columbus Module. Photo was taken during Expedition 30.
NASA

### Ophthalmology/Optometry​

#### MER4001 Assessment of Visual Function (Annually)​

**Test:** Visual acuity, color vision, and extraocular muscles.
**Rationale:** An ophthalmologic examination measures the astronaut’s ocular health and visual status, in order to detect abnormalities in the components of the visual system and to determine how well the person can see. It may also reveal the presence of hypertension, increased intracranial pressure, or diabetes. The lens of the eye is recognized as one of the most radiosensitive tissues in the human body, and it is known that cataracts can be induced by acute doses of less than 2 Gy of lowLET ionizing radiation and less than 5 Gy of protracted radiation. Although much work has been carried out in this area, the exact mechanisms of radiation cataractogenesis are still not fully understood. In particular, the question of the threshold dose for cataract development is not resolved. Cataracts have been classified as a deterministic effect of radiation exposure with a threshold of approximately 2 Gy. Studies indicate the threshold for cataract development is less than was previously estimated, on the order of 0.5 Gy, and that radiation cataractogenesis may, in fact, be more accurately described by a linear, no-threshold model. There appears to be no relationship between radiation exposure and the progression rate of an aggregate area of posterior subcapsular cataract or nuclear cataract. However, longer follow-up is needed to further understand any impact of space radiation on progression rates for these types of cataracts and to characterize changes in visual acuity. Cataract formation rates are being characterized by the Epidemiology group.

#### MER4002 Refraction Manifest/Cycloplegic (Annually)​

**Test:** Manifest/Cycloplegic refraction test.
**Rationale:** Refraction tests are used to measure refractive errors (myopia–nearsightedness, hyperopia – farsightedness, and astigmatism), and determine a person’s prescription for eyeglasses or contact lenses. As reported in the postflight exam vision questionnaire, 50 percent of astronauts living on the International Space Station have reported vision changes related to flight. However, long-term effects of vision changes secondary to microgravity are not well understood. From an occupational health perspective, occupational surveillance needs to be conducted throughout the lifetime of astronauts to detect ocular health issues and develop mitigation strategies to reduce the risk.

Buzz Aldrin – ST105.012
NASA

#### MER4003 Intraocular Pressure (IOP) (Annually)​

**Test:** Tonometry test
**Rationale:** Elevated IOP has been observed during parabolic flight and during microgravity exposure. The clinical significance of this rise in IOP in microgravity is unknown. However, it is theorized that a prolonged increase in IOP during space flight could put astronauts at risk for optic nerve damage. Those at risk for glaucomatous optic nerve damage would most likely be those who were exposed to microgravity for months to years. From an occupational health perspective, occupational surveillance needs to be conducted throughout the lifetime of astronauts to detect ocular health issues and develop mitigation strategies to reduce the risk.

#### MER4004 Ocular Health Assessment – Imaging (Annually)​

**Test:** Dilated fundus exam, retinal photographs, and optical coherence tomography (OCT)
**Rationale:** There are many concerns related to the ocular system, cephalad fluid shift during microgravity exposure and potential increased intracranial pressure being chief among these concerns. Permanent hyperopic shifts have been documented after long-duration flights. Additionally, optic disc edema, globe flattening, and choroidal folds have been documented in these astronauts. Some individuals experienced transient changes post flight while others have reported persistent changes with varying degrees of severity. The exact cause(s) and long-term effects of these phenomena are not known at this time. From an occupational health perspective, occupational surveillance needs to be conducted throughout the lifetime of astronauts to detect health issues and develop mitigation strategies to reduce the risk.

NASA astronaut Karen Nyberg, Expedition 36 flight engineer, conducts an ocular health exam on herself in the Destiny laboratory of the Earth-orbiting International Space Station. (NASA)
NASA

### Audiology​

#### MER5001 Clinical Evaluation/Audiometry (Annually)​

**Test:** Hearing questionnaire and pure-tone audiometry test
**Rationale:** NASA is concerned about strategies to assess and reduce acute, chronic, and delayed effects of hearing loss due to terrestrial (ground-based) and space flight noise exposures experienced by astronauts. There is a significant concern about synergistic interactions between noise, vibration, workload, and comorbidity factors of radiation, toxicology, and microgravity on auditory status. The long-term effects of these occupational exposures are unknown. A hearing questionnaire and pure-tone audiometry test can also help identify progressive changes in auditory function that warrant additional diagnostic evaluations or treatment. As a result, annual audiometric tests will continue to be included as part of the annual exams.

NASA astronaut Barry Wilmore conducts a Tonometry exam on NASA astronaut Terry Virts. Tonometry measures the intra-ocular pressure of the subject.
NASA

### Cardiopulmonary​

#### MER6001 Hypertension Screening (annually and as clinically indicated)​

**Test:** Hypertension screening using a sphygmomanometer.
**Rationale:** This screening is based on the United States Preventative Services Task Force (USPSTF) guidelines. There currently is no occupational health risk associated with hypertension. This screening is a standard of care and is included in the annual Lifetime Surveillance of Astronaut Health examination. These data are also needed to support Epidemiology’s astronaut surveillance activities for detection of unknown adverse health conditions that may be related to occupational exposures. USPSTF Guideline: The USPSTF recommends screening for high blood pressure in adults aged 18 and older.

#### MER6002 Resting 12-lead ECG (Annually)​

**Test:** Resting 12-lead electrocardiogram.
**Rationale:** There are concerns that astronauts may have subclinical cardiac abnormalities that could be exacerbated by the adaptive responses of the cardiovascular system to microgravity. Currently, the long-term effect of these adaptive responses is not known. This test is required to obtain the data needed to determine whether exacerbation of subclinical cardiac abnormalities is a valid long-term impact of occupational exposure to space flight. Knowing this may have implications for long-duration space missions in the future.

Mission Specialist Acaba used the shuttle’s ergometer.
NASA

#### MER6003 Cardiovascular Health Screening (Annually)​

**Test:** Cardiovascular health screening.
**Rationale:** The cardiovascular health screening documents cardiovascular risk factors (blood pressure, age, gender, cholesterol, smoking status) and includes CIMT calculation. These risk factors are used to calculate a Framingham Risk Score. The Framingham Risk Score is a gender-specific algorithm used to estimate the 10-year cardiovascular risk of an individual. Astronauts may have subclinical cardiac abnormalities that could be exacerbated by the adaptive responses of the cardiovascular system to microgravity and exposure to space radiation. We do not currently know the long-term effect of these adaptive responses. This test is required to obtain the data needed to determine whether exacerbation of subclinical cardiac abnormalities is a valid long-term impact of occupational exposure to space flight. Knowing this may have implications for long-duration space missions in the future.

### Gastroenterology​

This medical exam is done as clinically indicated.

### Reproductive Health​

This medical exam is done as clinically indicated.

### Behavioral Health​

This medical exam is done as clinically indicated.

////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////

..

### JanSz — 2023-11-20T22:50:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/medical-examination-requirements-for-former-astronauts.28348/post-328493>

[**The Framingham Risk Score** is a sex-specific algorithm used to estimate the 10-year cardiovascular risk of an individual](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Framingham_Risk_Score)[1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Framingham_Risk_Score). [It was first developed based on data obtained from the Framingham Heart Study](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Framingham_Risk_Score)[1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Framingham_Risk_Score)[, which began in 1948](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Framingham_Risk_Score)[2](https://ukcab.net/resources/factsheets/framingham/). [The score is considered a reliable, accurate benchmark for assessing cardiovascular risk in the general population](https://ukcab.net/resources/factsheets/framingham/)[2](https://ukcab.net/resources/factsheets/framingham/).

[The ‘Hard’ coronary Framingham outcomes model is intended for use in non-diabetic patients aged 30-79 years with no prior history of coronary heart disease or intermittent claudication](https://www.mdcalc.com/calc/38/framingham-risk-score-hard-coronary-heart-disease)[3](https://www.mdcalc.com/calc/38/framingham-risk-score-hard-coronary-heart-disease). [It estimates the 10-year risk of heart attack based on factors such as age, sex, cholesterol levels, HDL levels, blood pressure, and smoking status](https://www.mdcalc.com/calc/38/framingham-risk-score-hard-coronary-heart-disease)[3](https://www.mdcalc.com/calc/38/framingham-risk-score-hard-coronary-heart-disease). This calculator was created by Dr. Peter W.F. [Wilson, a leading expert in coronary heart disease epidemiology and genetics](https://www.mdcalc.com/calc/38/framingham-risk-score-hard-coronary-heart-disease)[3](https://www.mdcalc.com/calc/38/framingham-risk-score-hard-coronary-heart-disease).

# Thread: Does red light or IR light help with Osteoarthritis?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-red-light-or-ir-light-help-with-osteoarthritis.28349/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### annelies_1 — 2023-11-21T16:58:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-red-light-or-ir-light-help-with-osteoarthritis.28349/post-328504>

I read a lot about RLT or IR and osteoarthritis. I know that the disease ultimately stems from low redox, but can RLT or IR help in any way? And if yes, should the person then do red light or infrared light?

Thanks

### tallweeds — 2023-11-21T17:59:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-red-light-or-ir-light-help-with-osteoarthritis.28349/post-328505>

Yes, and CT or whole body cyro therapy works great too.

### annelies_1 — 2023-11-22T00:03:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-red-light-or-ir-light-help-with-osteoarthritis.28349/post-328522>

Thanks! Would you say red light or IR light is better? Looking to buy a Christmas present for someone

### Anthony Smith — 2023-11-22T01:35:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-red-light-or-ir-light-help-with-osteoarthritis.28349/post-328527>

> [annelies\_1 said:](/goto/post?id=328522)
>
> Thanks! Would you say red light or IR light is better? Looking to buy a Christmas present for someone

Red light (that you can see) is for skin, it only penetrates a few mm. NIR 850nm +/- goes in 2-3 inches.

### Michalis — 2023-11-23T05:17:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-red-light-or-ir-light-help-with-osteoarthritis.28349/post-328560>

Sun helps my joints a bit however no artificial light has done much of a difference. It won't fix or reverse osteoarthritis though. You can tell the person in question to add other modalities like CT and the effect multiplies but again it has not fixed it for me and I don't think it will at least in my current environment. The only thing I haven't tried is moving long term somewhere with low population density, low blue lit and low emf. Maybe this is the variable you need to be cognisant about and let the person know. Then it all boils down to the person's obligations and if they can easily leave everything behind and move in order to give that a go. Which is my opinion the biggest hurdle especially if there are family matters involved.
Again on paper and in theory everything looks good from what we read here. In practice it is a completely different story as everyone is differently built and sometimes the scales have also tipped so I believe no reversal can be achieved. So timing is very important.

### JanSz — 2023-11-23T16:13:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-red-light-or-ir-light-help-with-osteoarthritis.28349/post-328575>

> [annelies\_1 said:](/goto/post?id=328504)
>
> I read a lot about RLT or IR and osteoarthritis. I know that the disease ultimately stems from low redox, but can RLT or IR help in any way? And if yes, should the person then do red light or infrared light?
>
> Thanks

Osteoarthritis is a degenerative joint disease that happens when the tissues that cushion the ends of the bones within the joints break down over time. It is the most common form of arthritis that affects joints in the hand, spine, knees and hips.
Symptoms of Osteoarthritis include pain and stiffness of joints (hand, knees, hip), tender and warm joints, swelling, limited range of movement.
\---------------------------------------------------------
Use joints less, especially the leg joints and lumbar vertebra, by weighting less.
Keep in mind that walking or running creates dynamic load (not static).
That dynamic load is up to six time higher than static.
Somebody who is 150# and runs, 150# time six=900 pounds
Same person, same bones, same joints but when the weight is 250#
250\*6=1500 pounds
If you have a car that is able to pull 3000# trailer and you hook up 6000# trailer, it will not go very far.

More than two-thirds of U.S. adults in the United States are overweight or have obesity.

Why Jack broke his tendon by just walking, and talks about it every time he speaks?
He was 350# (or more).
\--------------------------
Happy Thanksgiving

Nitric oxide synthases (EC 1.14.13.39) (NOSs) are a family of enzymes catalyzing the production of nitric oxide (NO) from L-arginine.
\-----------------------------
cGNP guanylyl cyclase (GC-C) is an enzyme that belongs to the family of guanylyl cyclases.
It is encoded by the GUCY2C gene and is expressed in the intestinal epithelium.
The enzyme catalyzes the conversion of GTP to cyclic GMP (cGMP), which is a second messenger molecule that regulates various physiological processes.
The EC number of cGNP guanylyl cyclase is 1.14.13.39.

\------------------------------

There are at least two different ways NO is produced in human bodies.
but
After about 40 years of age NO about 50% peoples see large reduction in NO production.
With time reduction of NO progresses.
That leads to
many diseases.
High blood pressure and cardiovascular and many others.

**S****unlight, good sleep, sex, lean and relatively mobile (no excessive musculature, but moving, running often), cold, already checked----but what else?**

# Thread: Our Ark/El Salvador 1 week findings...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/our-ark-el-salvador-1-week-findings.28353/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### tomstaplez — 2023-11-21T21:15:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/our-ark-el-salvador-1-week-findings.28353/post-328511>

Nov 12-20 2023.
Us 2 hick Canadians went down for a vacation and secondarily an ark research mission. We had a blast.
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Naturally beautiful. The rural air and water feels clean and pristine. We drove ~500km stayed in 6 different locations different elevations. We learned our first dozen or so spanish words on the flight down . The language barrier was heavy of course, but everyone was so helpful, so happy, and often even amused by our communication incompetency. The food is way better than we even hoped for. I wouldn't say the food is cheap, but good value for the quality.

Our hotels/Airbnb's were as advertised. No hot water which surprised us but not a big deal.

Tbc..

### tomstaplez — 2023-11-21T21:22:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/our-ark-el-salvador-1-week-findings.28353/post-328513>

We aren't huge beach or night life people, but did experience both.

Kerri was likely mad at me thinking I was constantly looking for bikini's but I was actually looking for Jack.

### tomstaplez — 2023-11-21T21:36:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/our-ark-el-salvador-1-week-findings.28353/post-328514>

We think the only way we could board this ark is if we could acquire some accessible south facing land, 2500-4000' up, <1hr from ocean, <2hr from airport.

I'd build a few smaller airbnb style short term rentals. And cater to Canadians.

I'm currently going thru a midlife crisis, detest our government because they detest me, we currently host an airbnb here on the farm, we love to host know what it takes, I'm a confident builder, built a many houses from the ground up, we MIGHT have adequate capital to make it all happen.

I was talking to an Australian entrepreneur on the beach, he has been there for a year or two I think. But he didn't have the kindest words for Latin American culture and so on. Mildy told me I should look elsewhere.

Anyone care to talk about this opportunity we are considering? Thoughts? Suggestions? Experience? Legality of it all? Anyone try to build anything down there?
Gracious. Tom.
[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/img-20231115-wa0004-jpg.26139/)[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/pxl_20231115_232354040-jpg.26140/)[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/pxl_20231115_120412728-long_exposure-01-cover-jpg.26141/)[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/pxl_20231115_011411773-jpg.26142/)[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/1000030186-jpg.26143/)

### tomstaplez — 2023-11-21T21:45:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/our-ark-el-salvador-1-week-findings.28353/post-328515>

O, also we ran into miss universe 2022, miss El Salvador probably hasn't stopped thinking of me since!
